attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so this is… hard to even type, honestly. Last night was… a lot. The bridge club, you know? It’s just… everyone’s so *together*. Like, they’re laughing about things I don’t even understand, and they seem to just *know* what to say. I tried to chime in about Mrs. Henderson’s new rose bushes – I know she’s been working on them for months – and it just kind of… fizzled out. I don’t even remember what I said.

I feel like I’m watching a movie of everyone else’s lives and I’m just stuck behind a pane of glass, you know? Like I’m supposed to *feel* something, but I just...don’t. It’s not that I’m not *trying*, I really am. I wanted to be pleasant, I wanted to be a good member of the group, but it just feels… exhausting.

And then there's the small talk. Seriously, who talks about the weather for twenty minutes? I’d rather just… not. I felt this tightness in my chest the whole time, like a balloon slowly inflating. I kept wanting to just, like, excuse myself and go home. Maybe I should’ve. I don't know, maybe I should have just said I wasn't feeling well. I just… I don't want to seem rude. It's stupid, I guess. It's just… everything feels so amplified, like I'm hyper-aware of every little awkward moment. I dunno. It's just a lot, and I’m starting to feel really drained. Like I’ve run a marathon in my head. I should probably just… stay in tonight, yeah? Watch something mindless. Just… avoid.

okay seriously i don't understand how people just...do it. like, go to parties or dinners or even just a coffee shop and it's just…fine. everyone’s laughing and talking and it’s all just normal. and i just…freeze. it’s like my brain just shuts down completely. i practiced saying hi to sarah from work today, just a simple ‘hi’ and i completely blanked. my mouth went dry and my hands started sweating and i think i actually mumbled something incoherent. she just smiled and said ‘hi back’ and then i wanted to crawl under a rock.

it's not even the talking part, i think. it’s the *being seen*. the feeling like everyone is judging me, like i'm a walking, talking mistake. i keep replaying everything that went wrong with mark, you know? every argument, every awkward silence, every time i said the wrong thing. and it's all swirling around in my head and it just makes everything feel…too bright. too loud. like i need to just disappear.

i tried going to that book club thing last week, the one lisa invited me to, and i spent the whole time staring at my shoes. i couldn't make eye contact with anyone, and i felt like everyone was noticing how quiet i was. i just kinda nodded along when they were talking about the book and then i made an excuse to leave early. i feel so pathetic. i know i *should* be over it, it’s been six months, but i just can’t seem to shake this feeling that i’m a burden, that i’m just…wrong. like i’m not meant to be around people. maybe i'll just stay home tonight. just…stay home.

Ugh, can’t sleep. Just…racing thoughts. Like a broken record. Everything feels so heavy tonight. I keep tossing and turning, and it’s just making me more anxious. Seriously need to just switch off, but my brain won't let me.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, I just… I don’t even know why Mom is yelling again. It’s always something, isn't it? My grades, my job search, the fact that I’m still living in my childhood bedroom. It’s like she just can’t stand that I haven’t figured everything out yet, you know? Like I’m supposed to just *know* what I’m doing and be, like, successful and independent. It's just… a lot. My chest feels tight, like someone’s wrapped me in a really heavy blanket, and I can’t breathe properly. Dad just looks at me, like he’s trying to understand, but he never really does, does he? I keep replaying everything in my head, all the things I said, all the things I didn’t say. It’s exhausting. Just… need to go lie down, I think. Maybe if I close my eyes it will just… stop.

Ugh, deadlines are a monster. Just staring at this spreadsheet and it’s like it’s actively judging me. My stomach is twisted into knots. I keep rereading everything, convinced I’ve messed it up somewhere. Need caffeine. Seriously, *need* caffeine.

okay seriously i don't even know what's happening anymore. it's like a constant hum in my head, just a low thrum of…everything. mom’s yelling again, mostly about dinner, always about dinner. it’s stupid, i know, but it just…it just sets everything off. like a domino effect. i tried to make her the roast she likes, the one with the rosemary and garlic, and she said it was dry and tasteless and that i don’t *try* anymore. i did try! i spent two hours on it! and then my brother, mark, he just…he just rolled his eyes and said “well, that’s disappointing” like it’s some huge personal failing. he doesn’t even *look* at me when he talks to me, ever. it's like i'm invisible.

and dad…he just sits there, silent, watching. like he’s waiting for something awful to happen. i feel like i’m walking on eggshells all the time, afraid to breathe too loud, afraid to say the wrong thing. i keep replaying everything in my head, picking apart every word, every gesture, wondering if i could have done it differently, if i could have prevented this…this mess. my stomach is tied in knots, i feel dizzy sometimes, and my hands shake. i just want it to stop. i just want a moment of quiet, a moment where i don’t feel like i’m failing at everything. i just... i just wish they could see how much this is affecting me. it's like a weight, just crushing me. i don't even know how i'm going to get through tonight. i just need to breathe. please just let me breathe.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Okay, seriously, I can’t. Just…can’t. Mom keeps asking about job prospects and it’s like a weight just crushing me. Every time someone asks me what I’m doing, I just kinda freeze. Like my brain shuts off. It’s stupid, I know, I have a degree. But everyone else seems to have it all figured out. They’re out there, you know? Building careers, moving out, having *lives*. And I’m here, eating cereal for dinner and feeling like a complete failure. My chest feels tight, like someone’s sitting on it. I just want to disappear, honestly. It’s not that I *want* to hurt myself, it’s just…this constant feeling of being stuck. Like I'm drowning in everything and nothing at once.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: Everyone else seems to be studying together, laughing, actually *doing* something. I'm just...here. In my room. Surrounded by books. It’s so quiet, and it’s freaking me out. Like, I can't even breathe properly.

Okay, seriously, I don’t get it. I just… don’t. Everyone else seems to just *do* these things. Like, they walk into a room and instantly know what to say, how to act. I spent an hour planning my outfit for this book club meeting – dark jeans, that blue sweater, sensible shoes – and it felt like I’d built a fortress around myself. And then I was *there*, surrounded by people talking about… books. And wine. And apparently, their grandkids.

It’s not that I don’t *want* to talk. It's just... the words get stuck. Like they're coated in cement. I tried to say something about the plot of the book, and I just mumbled something about it being “interesting.” Interesting! Seriously? That’s the best I could come up with?

My husband keeps saying I’m being too hard on myself, that people are just being friendly. But it doesn't feel friendly. It feels like everyone's judging me, like I’m the weird one. Like I’m failing at being a normal adult. God, I feel so stupid. I just want to go home and curl up on the sofa with a cup of tea and pretend the world doesn’t exist. This constant feeling of… wrongness. It’s exhausting. I keep replaying it in my head, every awkward pause, every mumbled sentence. It's like a record stuck on repeat. I just... need to breathe. Just need to get out of here.

it's just...the bills. they keep piling up and i can’t even breathe. everything feels so tight and wrong like a knot in my stomach i just want it to stop. i don’t know how i’m going to manage honestly i just keep spiraling.
Ugh, tonight’s party was awful. Everyone just… talking. Like, actually talking. I couldn’t even look at people. Just wanted to disappear. Seriously need a bubble. My head’s buzzing.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, I just... I don’t even know why I agreed to go to Sarah's party tonight, it’s just so loud and everyone’s laughing and I feel like I’m going to spontaneously combust, like I’m breathing too hard or something, and I just want to disappear, honestly, it’s always like this, isn’t it?

Okay, seriously, I don’t even know what’s happening. It’s 3am and I’m staring at this spreadsheet, just… swimming. They keep piling on assignments for the presentation, like, *more* data to analyze, more slides to build. I told Mr. Harrison yesterday I was feeling slammed, he just said, "Pull yourself together, kid. This is a competitive program." Like that’s *helpful*.  It's not pulling myself together, it's like my brain is just… fizzing.  I keep re-reading everything, making tiny changes, then re-reading it again.  It’s a loop. And the pressure from my team, they’re all so confident, so sure they’re going to nail it, and I just feel like I’m going to fall apart. I keep picturing myself freezing up in front of the whole class, totally blanking.  It’s not even about the grade, it’s just… the *feeling*. This constant knot in my stomach. I keep washing my hands, like I’m trying to scrub away the anxiety, but it just gets deeper. My eyes are burning. I need coffee, but I’m terrified of jittering and making a bigger mess. I keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong, every possible way I could fail. It’s exhausting. Just…exhausting. I wish I could just disappear for five minutes, just breathe. Seriously, just five minutes.

Okay, seriously, I don’t even know where to start. It’s just… constant. Like a low-level hum of dread that’s always there. I feel jittery all the time, even when I’m just sitting down. My hands shake sometimes, especially when I’m making dinner. It’s stupid, I know, but it makes me feel like I’m failing at everything.

He’s… he’s doing okay, mostly. But it's *so* much. I’m his only support. And I feel like I'm constantly on edge, waiting for something bad to happen. Like a broken record playing in my head. “Is he sleeping okay? Did he take his meds? Is he hungry? Is he comfortable?” I check him constantly. It’s exhausting. I haven’t slept properly in weeks. Just tossing and turning, replaying everything, worrying about everything.

My husband thinks I’m stressed, which, yeah, I guess I am. But it’s more than that. It’s like… my brain just won’t shut off. It’s replaying everything, every little thing that could go wrong. I tried to go for a walk today, just to get out of the house, but I couldn’t even do it. I just sat on the porch and couldn’t breathe. My chest felt tight. I had to come back in. I just… I just want it to stop. I just want a few minutes of peace. Is that too much to ask? I feel so useless. Like I'm drowning and nobody can hear me.  It's just... too much.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay so like seriously, I can’t even think straight. It’s just… everything feels so heavy, you know? I keep staring at the ceiling and it’s just, a blur, like it’s swimming around and I can’t focus on anything. I tried to watch a movie last night, a really dumb rom-com, and I literally fell asleep halfway through. Like, fully out, snoring. My roommate didn't even say anything, which is weird because she usually judges my terrible taste in movies. But honestly, I don’t even care about the movie anymore, or what anyone thinks. It’s just… the sleep is awful. It comes in waves, like, I’ll be wide awake for hours just staring into the dark, and then I’ll fall asleep instantly and wake up in the middle of the night completely drenched in sweat. It’s like my body is just trying to escape something, I don’t know what.

And the worrying... oh god, the worrying. It’s just a constant hum in the background, louder and louder. It started the minute they told me I was let go, you know? “Restructuring,” they said. Like I’m some kind of spreadsheet. I keep replaying everything in my head, all the things I could have done differently, all the mistakes I made. Did I not network enough? Was my presentation bad? Did I not seem enthusiastic enough? It’s just a loop, a horrible, endless loop. I keep telling myself it’s temporary, that I’ll find something else, but it feels so…permanent. Like this is it. Like this is how my life is going to be. It’s just… exhausting. I feel like I’m drowning, slowly, silently, and no one even knows I’m struggling. I just wish I could just... stop thinking. Or sleep. Or *feel* something other than this awful, hollow dread. I just keep going over and over it, like if I just repeat it enough times, it’ll go away, but it doesn't. It just gets worse.

okay seriously, this is a mess. i’m just… swirling. like, everything feels too loud. we moved like, two weeks ago and i haven’t even unpacked half my stuff. boxes are just… everywhere. it’s like a physical representation of how i feel, you know? a jumble. and everyone keeps saying “it’s a fresh start!” and “you’re going to love it here!” but it’s not. it’s just… different. and different is terrifying.

my room is the only place i can actually breathe sometimes. i keep staring at the walls, just trying to… i don’t know, stop the feeling. it’s like my brain is constantly running a simulation of everything that could go wrong. like, what if i can’t make any friends? what if i fail all my classes? what if i just… disappear? i keep replaying every awkward thing i ever said, every time i stumbled over my words. it’s ridiculous, i know, but it’s just… a constant loop.

Mom keeps asking if i’m ‘settling in’ and Dad just gives me this really polite, ‘everything’s fine’ look. they don't get it. they don't feel like my chest is a cage, or that my hands are always shaking. i tried to call Sarah, but i just… froze. couldn’t even say hi. it’s stupid, right? just a new town, new people, but it feels like the whole world is shifting underneath me and i’m just… stuck. i just need to lie down. please.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
okay seriously i can’t breathe. like, actual panic. it’s been three days since leo…you know. three days and it feels like a lifetime of everything awful all crammed into one really long, blurry afternoon. i keep replaying everything we said, every little thing, trying to find where it went wrong but there’s just…nothing. it wasn’t a huge fight, not really. just…quiet. him just looking at his phone and not talking. and then he said it. he said he needed space. space from *me*. like i was a problem or something.

i’m drowning in textbooks and caffeine and the constant feeling that i’m going to fail every single exam just because i can’t focus on anything except him. it’s stupid, i know, right? he’s gone and i have finals next week. but it's not just the exams, it’s everything. it’s the way my hands shake when i try to write, the way my stomach twists when i think about him, the way i just…can’t sleep. my roommate keeps asking if i’m okay and i just nod and say yeah yeah everything’s fine but it’s a lie. a big, ugly, desperate lie.

i keep scrolling through old pictures of us, stupid silly ones from that trip to the coast, and it just makes it worse. like a tiny little knife twisting in my chest. i just want to disappear, honestly. just vanish and not have to face any of this. i feel like i’m losing my mind a little bit, you know? like i’m floating in this endless ocean of worry and sadness and i don’t even know how to swim. i keep telling myself it’ll get better, that i’ll be okay, but right now…right now i just feel completely and utterly lost. this exam season is a nightmare but this is just…a whole other level of awful.

Dad's on my case about everything, Mom's crying again, and little Leo just keeps asking why everyone's so mad at him. text: Okay, so like, this is just… a lot, you know? I keep thinking about tonight, the work party, and it’s already making my chest tighten. I don’t even *want* to go, really, but my sister keeps saying it’ll be good for me to, like, get out, reconnect, you know? Which is totally fine, but then I start picturing everyone talking about my job loss, or even just… looking at me, wondering what happened. It’s stupid, I know, completely irrational, but the thought of small talk just… drains me. I’ve been practicing saying “things are changing” over and over in my head, but it just feels so hollow and fake. I just want to disappear into my couch and watch bad movies, honestly, but I feel like I *have* to go. What if I say something wrong? What if I just… freeze? Ugh, I’m so tired of feeling this way.

text: Okay, so this is just… a lot, you know? Work’s been insane. Mrs. Henderson keeps needing help with everything, and Mr. Davies just… doesn’t understand how to use the TV remote anymore. Just needed to get it *somewhere* I guess.

okay like seriously i don't know what to do. my parents are being so weird lately and it’s all about the bills and stuff. they keep saying we need to cut back on everything and i just… i don’t get it. like, we’re not *that* broke, are we? it feels like everything is just piling up. i keep thinking about how much i need that new laptop for school, and it’s just… gone. it’s like a weight on my chest i can’t shake off. i keep replaying conversations in my head, trying to figure out what i did wrong, but there’s nothing. i just feel so useless and stressed. i just wanna disappear sometimes, you know? it's all just too much to handle. i’m so tired of worrying about money.

I keep tossing and turning, heart racing, trying to breathe, but it’s no use. It’s just… a lot. I feel like I’m drowning in everything and nothing at the same time. Just wanna close my eyes and disappear, but then I’ll be even more stressed about that. I just need… quiet. Please.

text: Okay, so this is just… a lot. I don’t even know where to start. Everything feels kind of blurry, you know? Like looking through a rain-streaked window. I’m just… constantly on edge. It’s not like anything *specific* happened, it's just... everything. The phone keeps buzzing, I keep checking if they’re okay, even though they’re sleeping. I’m supposed to be relaxing, going for a walk maybe, but I just… can’t. My chest feels tight sometimes, like there’s a weight on it. I feel like I should be doing more, taking care of myself, but then I worry about them, and it just spirals. I just… idk. Maybe it’s the weather? Or maybe it’s just me. It’s exhausting, honestly. I just wish it would quiet down a little.

Ugh, just…can’t breathe right. Mom and Dad are at it again. Loud voices, slamming doors. It's always something, isn't it? Like a record stuck on repeat, this constant fighting. My head is just buzzing, you know? Like static. I keep replaying everything they said, picking at it, trying to figure out what I did wrong, even though I didn't. It’s stupid. Everything feels too bright, too loud. I keep needing to go to the bathroom, just to splash water on my face. Seriously, I feel like I’m going to vibrate out of my skin. Just need to get through this shift. Just need it to be over.  Wish I could just disappear for a minute.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: okay seriously i don't know what's happening i just feel like i'm drowning like a really slow drowning where you're just gasping for air and can't quite get it right it's like this huge exam next week for intro to urban planning and i haven't even started properly i've been just staring at the textbook for like three hours now it’s just a blur of maps and zoning regulations and i keep skipping sections because i’m so overwhelmed i moved here like six months ago completely alone and i thought being independent would be great you know exciting but it’s just… loud and lonely and i keep expecting someone to call me or text me or anything but nobody does i keep replaying every single conversation i've had since i got here wondering if i said the wrong thing if i'm just… awkward and unlikable. and then the exam comes up and it’s like a second layer of panic. like if i fail this i’m going to have to figure out how to get back home which is a ridiculous thought but it keeps popping into my head. i keep telling myself to just breathe but it feels like my chest is tightening up and i’m sweating even though it’s only like 6pm. i feel like i’m going to throw up. i need to just do something anything to feel like i’m not completely falling apart maybe i’ll just order pizza and watch something mindless but even that feels… exhausting. i just wish i could just be calm for five minutes. just five minutes of not feeling this constant, buzzing anxiety. this city is amazing and beautiful but right now all i feel is terrified.

text: Ugh, just… can’t breathe sometimes. This presentation is tomorrow and I’m staring at the slides and it’s just a swirling mess in my head. Everyone keeps saying “you got this” but it feels like they don’t understand. Like, they don’t get that I’m already drowning in everything else. The new job is weird, my roommate’s gone, and now this. I keep replaying every little mistake I made in the proposal, like it’s going to somehow explode and ruin everything. Just need to… like, just get through it. Seriously, just get through it. I keep feeling this tightness in my chest, like I'm holding my breath and it’s not enough. I should probably go for a walk, but I'm too afraid to leave the apartment.

Okay seriously, I’m losing it. Like, completely losing it. It’s just… everything. The bills are piling up, you know? I thought after… after everything, things would be simpler. Stupid, right? I was so convinced that being single would be this amazing, liberating thing. Turns out it’s just… more responsibility. More loneliness. And more money disappearing.

I checked my bank account again. Again. It’s like watching my life savings bleed out one tiny decimal point at a time. I keep staring at the numbers and they just… swim. I can’t even bring myself to look at my credit card statement. It’s just a visual representation of me failing.

I tried to call Mark – no dice. He’s… busy. Which is, you know, understandable. But it just makes it worse. It’s like he’s actively avoiding me. And honestly, I kind of get it. I’m a mess. A complete, utter mess. I keep replaying everything, every argument, every stupid thing I said or didn't say. It’s a loop. A horrible, repeating loop.

I just… I keep feeling this tightness in my chest, like I’m breathing through a straw. My hands are always clammy. I keep pacing. I can't sit still. It’s like my brain is stuck on repeat, just buzzing with worry. I keep thinking about how I’m going to pay the rent, how I’m going to eat, how I’m going to… just keep going. It’s exhausting. Just… exhausting. I wish someone would just tell me it’s going to be okay. But they won’t, will they?

it's just… quiet. too quiet. used to be a constant stream of texts, calls, something. now it’s just me and the rain. i keep expecting him to just… show up. like it hasn’t been six months. feels like i’m fading away a little bit every day.
Everything just feels…heavy. Like I’m carrying this giant weight around and I don’t even know where it came from. Just constantly on edge, waiting for something bad to happen. Ugh, I hate this.

text:
Okay, so this is probably dumb, but I just… I can’t sleep. Like, seriously can’t. I know, I know, I should be excited about, you know, being back home and having *time* – which is a weird thing to say, right? Like, I *should* be enjoying it. But it's just… a lot. I keep lying in bed staring at the ceiling and my thoughts just… spiral. It’s not like specific things, it's just… everything. I keep replaying conversations from college, things I said, things I didn’t say, and it’s like, did I do the right thing? Did I say the right thing? I don’t even remember what I was thinking, but I’m convinced I messed up.

And then I start worrying about finding a job, obviously, which is terrifying. Everyone keeps saying ‘the market is tough’ and honestly, I just feel like I'm going to be stuck here forever, flipping burgers or something. Ugh, that’s awful to think about. I tried reading, but I keep getting distracted by, like, the dust motes in the sunlight or the sound of the fridge. It's stupid, I know. I've been up for hours, just pacing and then going back to bed and then just… feeling like I’m going to throw up. Maybe I should just call Mom? But she’ll just tell me to ‘get a hobby’ or ‘exercise.’ Like that’s the solution to everything. I don't even know what to do. I just feel… overwhelmed, I guess. Like I’m drowning in a sea of ‘what ifs’ and ‘could have beens.’ I just want to feel normal, you know? Like, a little bit calm. Idk, maybe it’s just a bad week. Or maybe it’s… more than that.

text: okay so like, this party tonight was just... a lot, you know? i spent basically the whole time hovering near the doorway, just watching people talk and laughing and it felt like everyone was having the *best* time except me. i tried to say hi to a couple of people i kinda know from classes, but my voice just came out all squeaky and i totally stumbled over my words, like i was speaking another language. and then i just started sweating, seriously, i could feel it dripping down my back. everyone seemed to be paired up in little groups, you know? and i just... froze. i wanted to disappear, honestly. it’s like, i’m suddenly acutely aware of every single awkward silence and every glance that isn’t directed at me. i keep thinking about tomorrow’s exam, and how if i fail, this feeling will just keep looping. it's stupid, i know, but i just need to breathe, i think.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
it’s… it’s today. october twenty-seventh. feels like a punch to the gut every single time i see the date. honestly i don’t even know why i’m typing this. it just… keeps happening. this feeling. like a record stuck on repeat. it’s been six months since… well, you know. six months since everything just… shattered. i keep expecting it to be different, you know? like maybe if i just… breathe, or eat something, or call someone, it’ll fade. but it doesn’t. it just sits there, this heavy blanket draped over me.

i keep replaying it all in my head, like a broken film reel. the arguments, the silence, the way he looked at me. it’s awful. i should probably try to distract myself. go for a walk, i guess. but i can’t even bring myself to put on shoes. my legs feel like lead. everything feels… sharp. like little knives digging into my skin. i keep thinking about all the things we *didn’t* say. all the things we *should* have. it’s stupid, i know, to still be obsessing over it this much. but it’s just… so much.

i keep imagining him calling. just a normal, casual hello. and then i freeze. i just… stop. it’s like my brain short-circuits. and then i have to remind myself that he doesn't. and that's supposed to make me feel better, right? it doesn't. it just makes me feel… emptier. i just wish i could just… not feel this. like, just for one day. is that too much to ask? i don’t even know. i just… i just need to stop thinking.

Okay seriously, I'm spiraling. It’s just… everything. I keep staring at this essay, this stupid history paper due Friday, and it’s like it’s mocking me. Like it *knows* I'm already a mess. He’s gone, obviously, and now this. It's not even a big deal, really. It’s just a ten percent grade. But it’s *everything* right now. My brain is just a constant loop of “what if I fail? What if I completely blank? What if everyone thinks I’m an idiot?”

I keep re-reading the prompts, highlighting things I *think* I need to say, and then deleting it all. It’s pointless. I just keep getting more and more panicked. My chest feels tight all the time, like someone's sitting on it. I tried to do some reading, but I just kept zoning out, seeing flashes of… well, you know. Things I don’t want to think about. It’s not fair. Why does everything have to be so hard?

I should probably just drop the class, right? Seriously. But then I’ll feel even worse about letting everyone down. And then I’ll feel like *I* let him down. Which is… a lot. I just need to breathe. Deep breaths. But they don’t really help, do they? Just a little buzzing in my ears and a really strong urge to just… hide under the covers and not come out. I hate this. I really, really hate this. I just need to finish this essay. Or maybe I don’t. Maybe I just need a nap.

okay okay just breathe i think i’m actually going to be sick. it’s just a weird ache in my side but it feels like…like something’s wrong really wrong. i keep checking my pulse and it’s just…fast. too fast. they said it was probably nothing just muscle strain from hauling boxes all night but it’s not nothing is it? it feels like a warning. everything feels too loud tonight the fluorescent lights are buzzing and the air smells weird like disinfectant and panic. i keep replaying that phone call from the doctor about my aunt’s test results, just a little thing but it’s stuck in my head and i can’t get it out. i should probably go lie down but i can’t just lie down i need to know if it’s okay if everything is okay. seriously freaking out a little.

Seriously, they just don’t *get* it. It’s like talking to a wall. Trying to explain how exhausted I am, how everything’s piling up… it’s just… gone. My chest feels tight, like a vise. I just need a minute, please.

text:
Okay seriously, I don't even know what's happening anymore. It's just… so much. Like, this project at school is completely overwhelming and Professor Eldridge keeps emailing me about it, and it’s not even due for like two weeks but I feel like it's due *today*. And then at work, Mr. Harrison keeps piling on more and more responsibilities, saying it’s “good experience” but it’s just draining me, you know? I keep staring at my laptop screen and everything just blurs together, and my stomach is always tied in knots. I tried to go for a walk last night, but I just ended up pacing around the apartment, just thinking about all the things I *haven’t* done, all the things I *should* be doing, and I just felt this huge, crushing weight on my chest.

This apartment… it’s nice, I guess, it’s bright and clean, but it just feels so empty, you know? Like a really expensive box. And being alone here, especially when I’m feeling like this, it’s… intense. I keep expecting someone to just pop in and say something, anything, but it’s just me and the silence. I keep replaying conversations in my head, things I said, things I *didn’t* say, and picking them apart until they’re nothing but jagged edges. It’s exhausting. I keep telling myself to just breathe, to focus on one thing at a time, but my thoughts just keep racing and racing and racing. I just wish I could just… disappear for a little while, just to escape it all. It's like I'm drowning in to-do lists and expectations and I can’t even remember how to swim. I just want to curl up and not feel anything, but then I’m afraid of not feeling *anything* at all. Ugh. I just need a giant hug and a really strong cup of tea, that's all.

text: Okay, so like, this deadline is just… looming. It’s huge, you know? I haven’t really started, I’ve just been, like, staring at the screen and it’s just this blank white void and then I start thinking about all the things that could go wrong, obviously. Everyone else seems to be, like, totally on top of it and I’m just… here. This city is so weird too, I don't know anyone and every street looks the same and it just makes me feel even more isolated. My stomach is doing this weird flip-flop thing and my hands are clammy. I keep replaying everything I should have done differently, little stupid mistakes that now feel like massive failures. I just need to breathe, I guess, but it’s hard when my chest feels like it's full of cotton wool and my thoughts are just… buzzing. Seriously, I think I might just hide in my room until it’s over.

It’s just… quiet. Too quiet. Everyone else is asleep, dreaming, probably laughing. I’m here, staring at the monitors, watching numbers flicker. It's 3 am again. Feels like I'm the only person left in the world. My phone’s dead, of course. Stupid. I keep scrolling through old messages, just looking at people who *were* here, you know? Like it’ll somehow bring them back. This shift is always the worst. Just this endless grey. I keep telling myself it'll be over soon, but it doesn’t feel like it. Just… this hollow feeling. Like I’m slowly fading away. I just want someone to talk to, honestly. Just anyone.

text:
Okay, so like, this is really hard to even type, honestly. It’s been… a week, I think? Since everything officially ended. And I just… I keep replaying everything in my head. Specifically, Mom. She keeps calling, you know? Just… saying she misses us, that she’s worried about me. Which, okay, I appreciate it, I really do. But it just makes it worse. It’s like, I feel guilty for being… me. Like I should be okay, right? Everyone says divorce is supposed to be a fresh start, but it just feels like adding another layer of complication. Dad’s not answering his phone either, which is… not great. We haven’t spoken properly in, like, months. It's mostly just clipped messages about bills and stuff. I’m trying to be polite, I guess? But it feels so forced.

And then there's my sister. She’s sending me these really lovely cards, with little inspirational quotes and everything. Which is nice, but it also makes me feel like I'm failing. Like I’m not handling this well enough. Maybe I should just block her for a bit? No, that’s dramatic, right? I don’t want to be dramatic. I just… I feel like everyone expects me to be strong, to be moving on, to be *fine*. But I’m not. I genuinely don’t think I’m fine. My chest just feels tight sometimes, like there’s a knot there. And I keep getting these little waves of just… panic. It’s stupid, I know. Just a lot of family stuff swirling around and making everything feel so overwhelming. I don’t even know what I’m trying to say, honestly. I just… I feel so lost. Idk, maybe I should just go for a walk? Or… just hide under the covers. It’s exhausting, you know? Just… exhausting.

Okay, so this is… a lot. Like, seriously a lot. I thought having a baby would be, you know, amazing. Like, all the Pinterest pictures and everything. But it’s just… quiet. Too quiet. Liam’s asleep, finally, and I’m sitting here staring at him, just… feeling it. This hollow feeling. My husband’s at work, which is fine, he’s a good guy, really, but it’s just… him. And me. And this tiny, demanding human who needs everything *all the time*.

I keep expecting someone to pop in, you know? Like, a neighbor, or a friend, just to say hi. Or maybe just to offer a bottle or a diaper wipe. But no one does. I texted Sarah yesterday, asked if she wanted to come over, but she said she's swamped. That's... okay, I guess. But it just feels like everyone else is living their lives and I’m stuck here. Like I’m watching a movie and I’m not in it.

I feel like I should be *doing* something. Like I need to be feeding him, or changing him, or sterilizing everything. But I just… can't. I just sit here. And the silence just gets louder and louder. It’s not even a bad silence, it's just... there. A constant reminder that I'm alone. I keep thinking about how much easier it would be if I didn't have this kid. I know, I know, that's stupid. But it’s just… hard. Really hard. I just want someone to talk to. Or even just… be here. Is this what it’s like all the time?

okay okay i need to finish this report by tomorrow morning and little elara just decided to have a massive meltdown. seriously what is happening to me i feel like i’m going to completely lose it. just breathe just breathe ugh

Just...can’t shake this feeling. Like static in my chest, you know? Everything feels too loud. The lights in the breakroom are blinding, and i keep checking the clock. It’s stupid, i know, but i just need to breathe. Seriously need to breathe.

text:
okay seriously, i can't breathe. like, not even a little. it’s just…constant. it started a week ago, i think? maybe two? i don't even really know. it's not like one thing happened, you know? it's just…everything. all the exams coming up, like, it's a mountain and i'm just a little ant trying to climb it and i’m already exhausted. and my roommate’s playing music super loud when i’m trying to study and then she’s being all moody and it just…builds.

my hands are shaking even when i’m just holding my pen. i keep checking the time, even though i know i should be studying. it’s just…a need to know how much longer i have to suffer, i guess. i keep replaying everything i did wrong in the last lecture, like if i could just go back and *do* it differently, it would be okay. but it’s not. it never will be.

i keep feeling this tightness in my chest, like someone’s sitting on it. and my stomach is all twisted up. i tried to go for a run yesterday, which is usually good for me, but i just…couldn’t. i had to just sit on my bed and stare at the ceiling. it's like my brain is stuck on repeat, just playing the same awful scene over and over. i feel so numb, but also like i'm vibrating.  i just need to...stop. Just stop feeling this.  It’s not fair, you know? Just...why? I just want it to be quiet.

text: It’s just… quiet now, you know? Like, really quiet. Used to be buzzing with everything, all the people and the work and the chats, and now it’s just me and the dust motes dancing in the sunlight and i feel like i’m disappearing a little bit with each day that goes by, honestly it’s awful and i don’t even know what to do anymore.

Okay, seriously, this is insane. My mom called again. Said she got a weird result on her bloodwork. Nothing major, she said, just “elevated.” Elevated what? I don't even know. She’s been… fine, I guess. Mostly. But she's always been a little… fragile, you know? And now this. And it's just… it’s everything. Like, I spent the last six months trying to build something, trying to be a good husband, trying to figure out this whole adulting thing, and now this. It’s like the universe just decided to throw a giant wrench into the gears.

I keep replaying everything in my head. The arguments, the silences, the way she used to just… stare. It's like she was always waiting for something to go wrong. And now it has. My mom’s health scare. It's triggering everything. The divorce was bad enough, felt like my whole world just shattered into a million pieces. But this… this is a slow, creeping dread. Like a fog rolling in. 

I keep checking my phone, waiting for a text, a call, anything. Just to know. To hear that everything is okay. But it's not. It can’t be. It feels like a weight, just sitting on my chest. Like I’m breathing through concrete. I just want to disappear, honestly. Not like, permanently. Just… somewhere quiet. Where there aren't any blood tests or elevated numbers or… anything. I just need a minute. A real minute. To breathe.

text: okay so like, this is embarrassing even thinking about it but… i’m seriously freaking out about money. it’s not like a huge amount, like, I’m not about to lose my house or anything, but it’s just… piling up. I keep getting these little bills, you know? the internet, the phone, gas… and then there’s the student loans, which i swear are mocking me every time i look at my bank account. it’s just a slow, constant drip, drip, drip, and it’s making me feel so overwhelmed. i tried making a budget, like, seriously, i did, and it looked amazing on paper – all neat and organized – but then i just… couldn't stick to it. i’d buy something small, just because i needed a little something, and then the whole thing would fall apart. idk, i just feel like I’m constantly failing at being responsible. my boss mentioned something about a bonus maybe, if i hit certain targets, but it feels so far away, like a completely different lifetime. i keep telling myself it'll be okay, that i'll figure it out, but then this weird tightness comes in my chest, and my stomach just flips. i haven't been sleeping well, and i keep replaying every expense in my head. maybe i should talk to someone? but what if they think i'm just being dramatic? it's stupid, really. just money. but it feels like everything, you know? like if i can’t get a handle on this, then what even is the point? i just want to breathe normally, without this constant, low-level panic.

ugh just thinking about tomorrow’s party makes my stomach flip. everyone’s gonna be talking and laughing and i’ll just freeze. i don’t even want to go, it’s just… too much. i feel like i'm gonna panic again.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, seriously, I’m just… spiraling. It’s like a record stuck on repeat, this horrible, buzzing feeling in my chest. I haven’t slept properly in days, just tossing and turning, replaying everything in my head. It’s stupid, I know, but it feels like if I just *think* about it enough, I can somehow fix it, you know? Like if I just really, really focus on the data analysis report for Mr. Henderson’s project, it’ll magically become perfect and the whole layoff thing won’t feel so… pointless. But it doesn’t. It just gets worse. The deadline is looming, like this huge, dark cloud, and it’s just getting closer and closer. I keep staring at the spreadsheet, just a jumble of numbers and formulas, and I can’t seem to make sense of anything. I feel like everyone else understands it, they’re just effortlessly gliding through, but I'm stuck wading through mud. 

And it's not just the report, it’s everything. It’s the fact that I don't have a job anymore, that I’m relying on savings that I’m starting to worry are dwindling, and that my mom keeps calling to ask if I’ve “found something.” God, it’s just… a lot. My stomach is always tied in knots, and I feel this constant pressure behind my eyes. Like I'm about to explode. I keep snapping at Liam, which is awful, he’s trying to be supportive but I just want to scream. It's like my brain is a tangled ball of yarn, and every time I try to pull at one strand, it just makes the whole mess worse. I keep telling myself to breathe, to just take it one step at a time, but it's like trying to hold back the tide. Just... overwhelmed. Completely and utterly overwhelmed. I just want to disappear for a little while, just to quiet it all down, but then I think about the report and the deadline and... ugh.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably stupid to even write about, but I’m seriously freaking out. It’s only been, like, three days since we moved and I already feel completely…lost. Everything is just…different. The school’s huge, and everyone already seems to know each other, you know? Like, they’re all laughing and inside jokes and I'm just standing there, completely invisible. I tried to say hi to a girl in my English class – she just kinda glanced at me and kept reading. Maybe I said something weird? I don’t even remember what I said.

My room is…okay, it's fine, I guess. It’s just…empty. Like, it doesn’t *feel* like my room. Everything’s just…new. I unpacked everything last night, I think? It feels like a blur. My parents are trying to be really positive, telling me it’s an adventure and that I’ll make new friends in no time, but…it’s just hard. I keep imagining everyone looking at me, judging me, thinking I’m the weird new kid. Which, I probably am. 

I keep checking my phone, hoping for a text from my old friends, but nothing. It’s just…quiet here. Really quiet. And when I close my eyes, I just see boxes and hallways and…nothing. I keep feeling this tightness in my chest, like I’m breathing too shallow. I tried to go for a walk earlier, but I just turned around and came back. I don’t even know why. It’s like…the thought of actually *going* somewhere and having to talk to people just makes my stomach flip. I dunno. Maybe I’m just being dramatic, right? Probably. It’s just…a lot. I wish I could just go back to how things were. Just…idk. I’m just so overwhelmed.

okay seriously i’m losing it. i haven’t slept properly in like three days and it’s just… spiraling. leo’s been amazing of course he is, the sweetest little guy but he’s also… demanding. and this presentation for work is due friday and i just… i can’t focus. like i keep staring at the slides and nothing comes to me. it’s just a bunch of blurry shapes. and i keep checking him, making sure he’s okay, feeding him, changing him, burping him… and then i feel this huge wave of guilt like i’m failing at both things. i should be enjoying this, right? this is supposed to be the happiest time of my life. but it feels like i’m drowning. i keep replaying everything i could have done differently, every tiny mistake i made today, it's just... ugh. i feel so shaky all the time. my hands are always clammy and my chest feels tight. i tried to take a bath last night, but i just sat there staring at the bubbles and feeling completely overwhelmed. i keep thinking about my mom, she always said i was a worrier and now i totally get it. she’d probably be yelling at me to just breathe, but i can't even seem to do that properly. i just… i need to get through this week. i just need to finish the presentation. and maybe get a little sleep. but it all feels so impossible. please let me get through it.

text: Ugh, just trying to say hi to people in the cafeteria and my brain is just… a mess. idk if I’m even making eye contact, or if they can tell I’m freaking out. maybe I should just go back to my desk? It’s probably better that way, honestly. just feels so overwhelming, you know?

Ugh, seriously, I can’t sleep. Like, at all. It’s 3 am and my brain is just… buzzing. I keep picturing all the deliveries I have to make tonight, the stupid traffic, the grumpy customers. It’s not even *really* happening yet, but it’s already here, just swirling around in my head. I tried counting sheep, but they just kept multiplying and yelling about late fees. Honestly, I feel like I’m vibrating. My eyes are so heavy, but I can’t just shut them off. Every little sound – the hum of the refrigerator, the rain on the roof – it’s amplified a thousand times. 

I keep telling myself to just relax, take a deep breath, but it’s like trying to hold water in my hands. Everything just slips away. I keep replaying conversations from earlier today, nitpicking every word, every pause. Did I say the right thing? Did I look confused? Did they think I was being rude? It's exhausting. I feel like I’m constantly walking on eggshells, anticipating some disaster that hasn't even happened. My stomach is tied in knots. I just want to be asleep, just to… stop. This shift is going to be brutal tomorrow. I’m already dreading it. I wish I could just disappear for a night. Just… vanish.

Okay, seriously, I just had this weird dizzy spell in chemistry. Like, everything went blurry for a second. Mom’s freaking out and saying I need to see a doctor. Ugh, this is just… too much. Everything feels so shaky lately.

text:
Okay, so like, this is…weird. I don’t even know how to start this. I’ve just been…on edge, you know? For weeks? Maybe longer. It’s not like anything specific happened, which is, like, the worst part, I think. I keep expecting something to *happen* and then it just…doesn’t. And then I feel even more anxious, because it’s like, what am I even worried about?

Liam’s sleeping, thankfully. He’s a good sleeper, mostly. But when he *isn’t* sleeping, or when he’s fussy, or even just when I’m holding him and he’s…quiet… it just ramps up. My chest feels tight, like someone’s sitting on it. And my hands are always clammy. I keep touching them and it’s so weird. I feel like I’m gonna throw up, sometimes, but I don’t. It’s just…a constant, low-level hum of panic.

My husband, Ben, he’s so sweet and he’s trying, I know he is. He keeps asking if I’m okay, and I just say “yeah, fine” even though I’m not. It feels stupid to tell him, like he wouldn't understand. It's new parenthood, right? Everyone’s supposed to be exhausted and overwhelmed, but this feels…different. It’s not just the sleep deprivation. It’s…deeper. Like something’s stuck.

Iidk, maybe it's just the whole responsibility thing? Having this tiny human completely dependent on me? It's a lot. Or maybe I'm just not cut out for this. I just… I wish I could just breathe normally. Like, just *breathe*.  I keep replaying everything, every decision I made, every little thing I said, wondering if I did something wrong.  It's exhausting.  I just want a normal day, you know?  A normal, quiet day.  Maybe tomorrow will be better. Probably not, but…maybe.

Okay, seriously, I can’t sleep. Like, at all. Been up for hours staring at the ceiling. Everything feels… amplified. The hum of the machines in the facility is driving me nuts, and I keep thinking about all the patients I’m supposed to be monitoring. It’s just a loop. My heart’s pounding and I keep needing to take deep breaths but it doesn’t even help. I keep replaying stupid conversations in my head, picking apart every word. I just want to be still, you know? Just… quiet. But it’s impossible. My thoughts just keep racing and racing and I’m so tired, but I can’t shut them off. I feel like I'm going to crack.

it's just… constant. like a low hum underneath everything. i keep telling myself it’s just getting older, you know? but it’s more than that. my chest feels tight sometimes, like there’s a weight there. i’m trying to do everything, everything for him, and i just… i don’t know if i’m doing it right. he needs me to be strong, to be calm, but i feel like i’m fraying at the edges. i keep replaying conversations in my head, wondering if i said the wrong thing, if i’m missing something. i just need a minute, just five minutes to breathe and not feel like i’m failing. it’s exhausting. honestly, it’s just exhausting.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is really weird to even write down, but… I’ve been having the *worst* time sleeping. Seriously. It’s like, every time I try to close my eyes, my brain just… explodes. Not literally, obviously, but you know? It’s all just… *stuff*. Just swirling around. I keep replaying everything that happened at the office, you know? Like, maybe I should have said something differently, or maybe I didn’t say anything at all and that was the right thing to do. It’s just… it’s hard to tell, isn't it? Especially when you’re unemployed. It’s like, you're supposed to *know* what to do, but you don’t.

I keep tossing and turning, and then I start worrying about money, obviously. Like, how am I going to pay rent? I checked my bank account, like, five times this morning. It's… not great. I don’t want to ask my mom, she’d be so worried. And it's embarrassing, honestly. I feel like a failure. I was *good* at my job, I really was! I always met my deadlines, I always tried to be helpful. So, what happened? It’s just… baffling.

And then the worry about the job itself just keeps creeping back. What if I never find anything else? What if I’m just… useless? I know, I know, that's stupid. I'm not useless. But it’s hard not to think that way when you're staring at the ceiling at 3 am. I tried meditating, but it just made me more aware of how anxious I am, which is… not helpful. Maybe I should just try counting sheep? I don’t even know. I just... I just feel so restless and jittery all the time. It’s like my body is permanently on high alert. I wish it would just… stop. I don't even know what to do.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay, so like, this is really hard to even write down, i guess. it feels…weird, just putting it out there. it’s been weeks since Liam…you know. and it’s not even really *sad*, not like, a big, dramatic, crying-into-a-pillow sad. it's more… a constant low hum of just…wrongness? i don’t even know. i keep replaying everything in my head, like, every little thing we ever said, every time we didn't quite look at each other. maybe i should just pretend it didn’t happen? but then i’d be lying to myself, right? and i hate lying.

i’m supposed to be…well, i *was* supposed to be taking care of everything, you know? his meds, his meals, getting him to appointments. he's been so withdrawn since it ended, just… staring at the wall. i try to talk to him, but it's like he’s hearing me, but not *really* hearing me. or maybe he just doesn't *want* to. it’s just… a lot. i feel like i’m drowning a little, just keeping his head above water. 

idk, i just feel so…jittery. my chest gets tight sometimes, and i have this weird feeling like everything is about to fall apart. i keep checking his medication levels, even though the doctor said it's fine. it’s stupid, i know. it’s probably just the stress, or maybe i'm just exhausted. i’m trying to distract myself with gardening, but even that feels overwhelming. the weeds just seem to grow faster, you know? like they’re mocking me. i just want things to be…normal. maybe that’s a ridiculous wish. maybe i should just accept it and… and just keep going, i guess? but it feels like a weight, a huge, heavy weight. i just… i don't know what to do.

it’s just… everything feels too loud today i think. like the walls are closing in a little even though i’m just in my room. my stomach is all twisted up and i keep needing to go to the bathroom but i don’t even know why. i keep replaying conversations in my head, like i said the wrong thing or didn’t say anything at all. it’s stupid little stuff, i know, but it just keeps building. i just want to disappear for a second, just breathe. my hands are shaking a little and i feel like i can't even focus on my homework. i keep checking the time like five times a minute, just waiting for something to happen, i don't even know what. it’s like a constant hum in my head that i can’t turn off. i just want it to stop.

I just feel this constant pressure in my chest, you know? Like my heart is trying to beat its way out. And my hands are always shaking, even when I’m not doing anything. Maybe I’m just tired? I’m so tired all the time, it’s hard to tell. I just… I wish I could just chill, you know? Like, just be normal. Idk. It's just a weird, heavy feeling, and it doesn’t go away. I’m gonna go lie down, maybe that’ll help… maybe not.

text:
Okay, so like, this is embarrassing even to write down, but… it’s the anniversary. Of… you know. The thing. It’s just… today. And I keep, like, replaying it in my head. Not like, screaming or anything, just… looping. It’s stupid, right? I should be focusing on finals, which are, like, looming and terrifying as always, but my brain just… won’t shut up. I’m supposed to be studying for this huge history exam and I haven't even opened the textbook. I keep catching myself staring out the window at the rain, and it just makes me feel… heavier. Like everything is just… gray.

I don’t even know why it’s hitting me this hard *now*. It’s been a year, right? Shouldn’t I be… over it? I try to distract myself, I really do. I went to the library, but I just kept fidgeting and feeling this weird tightness in my chest. I tried to read, but the words were just… blurring. Maybe I should just go home? But then I’ll just end up pacing and worrying about the exam. It’s a vicious cycle, I guess. I feel so useless. Like I’m failing at everything. I just… wish it hadn’t happened. I know, it's a cliché, I’m so sorry. It’s just… a lot. And I don’t know how to make it stop. I just want to be normal, you know? Like everyone else. Idk, maybe if I just keep telling myself it’s okay, it will eventually be okay. But it doesn’t feel okay. Not at all. Just… a really, really bad feeling.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a dumb post, and I'm probably overthinking everything as usual, but I just… I don’t know what to do anymore. It’s been, like, three weeks since Liam… since we broke up, and it still feels… wrong. Not even sad, exactly. Just…off. Everything feels off. I keep going through the motions, you know? Going to work, making coffee, staring at the monitors all night, and it’s just…grey. Really grey.

I keep replaying everything in my head, like a broken record. That stupid argument about the dishes last Tuesday, or the way he didn’t text me back that night. It’s probably nothing, I know it is. He said he needed space, and stuff. But space feels like…nothingness. Like I’m just floating in this huge, empty room.

I keep expecting him to just…show up. Like he’ll be standing at the door with flowers and a stupid apology. But he won’t. And I’m starting to think I’m just going to be waiting forever. I tried calling him a couple of times, just to, idk, hear his voice, but it just went to voicemail. Maybe he's busy. Maybe he doesn't want to talk to me. It’s probably a good thing, honestly. Maybe it's what I needed. But then I feel this really sharp pang, like a little knife in my chest, and I just… want him back.

I keep checking my phone every five minutes. Just… waiting. It's ridiculous, right? I’m probably being pathetic. I just wish things could go back to how they were. Even the little annoying things. Maybe I'm just scared of being alone. It’s stupid, I know. I should just move on. But… I don't think I can. Not yet, anyway. This whole thing just feels…heavy. Like I'm carrying a boulder. Ugh. I need a nap.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: Okay, seriously, this apartment is just...too quiet. Like, really quiet. And I keep expecting someone to be here, but nobody is. It's making my chest feel tight and I just want to go home. Wish I hadn't moved.

okay seriously i don’t even know why i’m typing this but i need to just get it out before i completely lose it. tonight was… tonight was a mess. i work the night shift obviously and i usually kinda blend into the wallpaper you know? just quietly do my job and nobody notices me. but last night, mr. henderson from accounting came over to the break room and started talking about his daughter’s graduation party. a graduation party. and he was so enthusiastic, like it was the most important thing in the world, and i just… i froze.

i tried to be polite, i really did. i mumbled something about congratulations and then started rambling about the weather. it was raining, it’s always raining here, it’s just… it’s something to say, right? but he kept talking and talking and i could feel my heart starting to pound like crazy. like someone had stuck a jackhammer in my chest.

then ms. davis from HR walked by and started chatting with him about the new coffee machine. and suddenly i was hyper-aware of every single awkward silence, every little glance, every tiny sound. like everyone was staring at me and judging me and waiting for me to say something stupid. i just wanted to disappear. my palms were sweating and my throat felt like it was closing up.

i just kept repeating “that’s nice” and “good for them” over and over until i felt dizzy. i just need to go home now i think. i’m going to be a nervous wreck all night. i hate these things, these little interactions. they just make everything feel so much bigger than it is. it’s like a tidal wave coming on and i can’t swim. i just wish i could just be invisible for five minutes. honestly i just want to curl up in a ball and never come out.

Everything just feels...wrong. Like the world shifted and I’m stuck in a slightly tilted room. I keep replaying everything, you know? It’s stupid, but I can’t stop. Just…a constant buzzing in my head.

Ugh, tonight was… a lot. Just got out of this party and i feel like everyone was staring at me. Like, judging. I know it sounds dumb, but it just felt so heavy. Trying to make small talk about the weather, about *anything*, and it just felt impossible. My hands were shaking, and i couldn't really look anyone in the eye. I kept thinking about all the things i said wrong, all the stupid things i probably said. Just want to go home and hide under the covers. Seriously, I just need five minutes of quiet. Everyone seemed to be having such a good time, and I just felt...completely disconnected. Like i’m watching a movie of someone else’s life. I just wish i could turn it off.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
Okay, seriously, it’s been a year. A whole freaking year since… you know. And I just… I can’t shake it. Like, I try to do normal things, like, I went to basketball practice today, and it was okay, I think. But every time Coach yelled, or someone tripped, or even just when the ball bounced weirdly, I just… froze. Like my brain just short-circuited. Everyone else seemed to just… keep going. Playing. Laughing. And I’m just… here. Watching.

My hands are clammy all the time, even when it’s not hot. And I keep replaying everything in my head. Not like, trying to change it, just… replaying it. Over and over. Like a broken record. It's stupid, I know, but it's the only way I can make it through the day sometimes.

Mom keeps asking if I’m okay, which is nice, I guess, but it just makes me feel worse. Like I'm supposed to be *okay*. Like I should just… forget. But I can't. It's like this weight, this constant, dull ache, just sitting in my chest. It doesn't go away. Even when I try to distract myself with music or, like, scrolling through TikTok – which, honestly, feels pointless – it’s still there. It’s just… louder. Everything feels louder. The lights, the people, the music… everything just vibrates. I just wish it would stop. I wish it would just… go away. I don’t even know what to do anymore. Just… this.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay so like, I don't even know where to start, seriously. Last night was… a lot. We had Sarah and Ben over for, like, dinner, which sounds totally normal, right? But I just... couldn’t really be present. I was just, you know, watching everyone and feeling like I was failing at everything. They were talking about this amazing trip they took to Italy, and I just wanted to disappear, I swear. Like, it’s not that I didn’t *want* them to be happy, it’s just… it felt like I should be having a better time too, you know? And I wasn’t. I kept rearranging the silverware, and checking if the baby was okay every five seconds, and just generally feeling like a total mess.

Then, when Liam started fussing, I just panicked. Like a full-blown, sweating-through-my-shirt panic. I tried to soothe him, of course, which is what you’re *supposed* to do, but I just kept thinking, "Is he getting enough? Is he warm enough? Is he hungry? Are *we* doing this right?" It’s a constant loop. And Ben was trying to make small talk, asking about my day, and I just kept nodding and saying ‘fine’ and ‘good’ but it felt so… empty. It’s like I’m trapped in this little bubble of worry, and it’s just getting bigger and bigger. I feel so bad, like I’m ruining everything, and that everyone is judging me, even though I know they’re not. I just wish I could just… breathe, you know? Just be normal. It’s exhausting, honestly, just trying to be a good mom and a good friend and… just existing. I keep thinking about what I should have said, or done, or maybe just *been*, and it just makes me feel even worse. I just need a nap, a really, really long nap.

okay seriously i don’t even know what’s happening right now my brain is just… buzzing like a million bees trapped in a jar. i have this huge history project due next week and mr. henderson keeps staring at me like i’m personally responsible for the decline of the roman empire or something. and then i have to work at the bookstore on saturday and sunday and it's always so busy especially with all the tourists coming in for the festival. i keep messing up the checkout system and i feel like i'm failing at everything. like i'm just not smart enough or capable enough or anything. it’s just… too much.

i keep replaying everything in my head like a broken record. what if i get a bad grade? what if i lose my job at the bookstore? what if i just… completely fall apart? it’s like this constant low-level hum of panic that won't go away. my stomach feels weird and i keep needing to go to the bathroom even when i don’t have to. i tried to do some studying but i just kept getting distracted by all these random thoughts. like, did i lock my locker? did i leave the stove on? i don’t even remember what i had for breakfast. i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. i feel so overwhelmed and like i’m drowning. i just wish it would stop. it's like a weight sitting on my chest i can't shake off. i just need a break. a real one.  i just want to breathe normally again. it’s exhausting.

text: Ugh, today’s…today’s just…heavy. It’s the anniversary, you know? Five years. I don’t even want to think about it, honestly. Just…everything feels amplified. Like the air is thicker, and the colors are duller. I keep replaying it all in my head, little snippets, you know? Like, did I say the right thing? Could I have done something differently? I feel like I should be doing *something*, like acknowledging it properly, but I just…freeze. It’s weird. My chest feels tight, like I’m breathing through a straw. I keep telling myself it's just the rain, and it's just a memory, but…maybe it's more than that, idk. I just wish it would all go away. Maybe if I just stay busy, it’ll fade a little? I don’t know. I just…feel so lost.

I keep replaying everything in my head, every email, every meeting, making sure I didn't mess up. My apartment just feels… heavy. I keep thinking about how I don't even know anyone here, and I’m already feeling like I'm failing. Just gotta push through, right? Ugh.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay so like, this is really embarrassing to even write but i’m just… i’m a mess. seriously. it's exam season, obviously, which is already awful, but the anxiety is just… amplified. i haven't slept properly in like, a week? maybe longer? idk. i keep waking up at, like, 3am with my heart pounding and just… thinking. just *thinking*. it’s not even about the exams, not really. it’s just… everything.

i keep replaying conversations, things i said, things i didn’t say. and then i start worrying about what people think of me, if they’re judging me, if they’re secretly disappointed. it’s a stupid loop, i know. and i keep telling myself to just relax, to breathe, but it’s like trying to turn off a record that’s stuck on repeat. my roommate keeps asking if i’m okay, and i just say “yeah, fine” because i don’t want to bother them with my stuff. it feels like a personal failing, you know? like i *should* be able to handle this.

i tried meditating, like, yesterday, but i just ended up stressing about whether i was meditating *correctly*. and now i'm even more anxious. i keep checking the clock, which makes it worse, obviously. maybe i should just drop out? no, that’s dramatic. but… what if i’m just not cut out for this? what if i’m constantly going to be like this, just perpetually on edge? it’s probably ridiculous to even worry about it this much, but i can’t seem to stop. i just feel so… fragile. like one tiny thing could just shatter me. i just want to be normal, i guess. is that too much to ask?

Okay, seriously, I can’t. Like, I just… can’t. Went to Sarah’s birthday dinner last night. Everyone was laughing, you know? Just… *laughing*. And I felt like I was watching a movie of everyone else's lives and I wasn’t in it. Like I was a ghost. And then, like, I had to talk to Mark and his new girlfriend. He kept looking at me, like, assessing me, I guess? It was awful. I stumbled over my words, I think, probably said something stupid. I just wanted to disappear.

It's always like this, isn't it? Any kind of gathering, any conversation, it’s just… a pressure cooker. Like I’m constantly on display and everyone’s judging me, even if they aren't. Or *especially* if they aren't. I keep replaying everything in my head, picking apart every awkward pause, every glance. It’s exhausting. I feel like I’m failing at being a normal person. A normal person doesn’t feel this way, right?

I keep telling myself it’s because of… you know. It’s stupid, I know, but it’s just… everything feels magnified. Like if someone smiles at me, it’s a huge, monumental thing. Like if I accidentally bump into someone, it's the end of the world. I just want to hide. Just curl up in a ball and not talk to anyone. I don't want to go out again. Not for a while, anyway. Just… ugh. I feel so raw, like my skin is too sensitive. It’s just… too much.

the fluorescent lights are buzzing and it’s just me again. everyone’s asleep, or gone, or whatever. it feels like the walls are closing in a little bit, you know? just… quiet. too quiet. i wish someone was here.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, just…everything feels so loud. Like a constant static. I don't even know why, honestly. Just…a weird pressure in my chest. Maybe it's the job thing? idk. It's just…a lot, you know? I feel like I’m spiraling a little, maybe?

Okay seriously, I can’t sleep. Like, at all. It’s 3 am and my brain is just… buzzing. It’s not even *thinking* about anything specific, you know? Just… everything. All the stuff. The house is too quiet, it’s like he’s still here, even though he’s not. And then I start thinking about the arguments, the little things I said, the things *he* said… it’s a loop. A horrible, endless loop.

I keep tossing and turning, and every time I close my eyes, I just see his face. Not a happy face, you know? Just… blank. And then I start worrying about what he’s doing, if he’s okay, if he’s found someone new. Stupid, right? He doesn't even *want* to be found. But it just… comes. Like a pressure in my chest, building and building.

I tried counting sheep, but they just kept jumping over me, and it made me feel even more frantic. I read for an hour, but the words blurred together and I couldn't focus. My hands are shaking. I keep checking the locks on the doors, even though I know it’s pointless. It’s like my body is screaming at me to get out, to run, but I’m just stuck here, paralyzed by this… this feeling. Just a constant, dull ache in my stomach and a tightness in my throat. I just want it to stop. I want to feel… normal. Like before. God, I miss normal.

just… everyone else seems to be having such a good time, you know? like, they’re all laughing and posting about amazing dinners and trips and just… living their lives. and i’m here, eating microwave mac and cheese and staring at my laptop screen. it’s like i’m watching a movie about someone else’s life. i haven’t really talked to anyone properly in days. my phone just sits there, silent. i know i should probably do something, like call someone, but then i just get more anxious. it’s stupid, i know, just feeling this way. like i’m invisible or something. it’s just… heavy. i wish i could just… disappear for a little while. not like permanently, just… away.

Okay, seriously, I can’t do this. Like, I know everyone is just having a good time and laughing and stuff, but it just feels…loud. And everyone’s eyes are on me, even if they’re not really. I keep replaying everything I said in my head and it’s just… awful. I feel like I tripped over my words or something, you know? My palms are sweaty and I’m pretty sure my face is hot. Just wanna disappear. Everyone else seems so chill, like they actually *enjoy* being around people. I just want to go home. This is stupid. Why is everything always so hard for me? I don’t get it. I just wish I could just...not feel this.

text:
Okay, so like, this is really hard to even type, but I need to say something, I think. I’m just… spiraling, honestly. It’s exam season and it’s supposed to be, you know, stressful but like, a normal kind of stressful? But it’s not. It’s… different. I keep checking my bank account, like, obsessively. It’s just… gone. Seriously gone. I thought I'd have, like, a little buffer left after rent, but it’s just… vanished. I don’t even know where it went. I think I bought too many textbooks, maybe? Or that fancy coffee I needed to, like, *think*? It doesn't really matter, does it?

Everything feels so urgent, like if I don't figure something out, *something bad* will happen. Which is probably ridiculous, I know, but my chest feels tight sometimes, and my hands shake when I try to look at my notes. I keep replaying every lecture in my head, trying to cram it all in, and it’s just… overwhelming. I keep telling myself to breathe, but it’s like my brain is a broken record, playing the same panicked thoughts over and over. I don’t know what to do. I’m probably overreacting, right? Maybe it's just the stress of exams, but it feels… bigger than that. I just... I just want it to stop. I’m trying to be responsible, I really am, but I feel like I’m failing at everything. I don’t even know where to start. I’m just... a mess. Idk. Anyone ever feel like this? It's just… a lot.
