text: Okay, so like, this is probably dumb, but I just… I can’t breathe sometimes. Not like, a big panic attack thing, but just… tightness. In my chest. And my head is just *full* of numbers, you know? Bills, and interest rates, and how much I *maybe* overspent on that stupid plant last week. It doesn’t seem like a big deal, right? But it’s just… piling up. I’m trying to be responsible, I really am. I’ve been cutting back on everything – takeout, streaming, even just buying coffee. It’s just… it feels like I’m constantly swimming upstream. I don't even know where to start. I looked at my budget spreadsheet again tonight, and it just looked like a giant, angry mess. Like, I *know* I need to talk to someone about this, but who even *does* that? It feels so embarrassing. My parents would just tell me to "pull myself together," which, like, duh, I'm trying! It's just… hard. I’m working overtime, which is great, but then I feel guilty because I don’t have time for anything else. My friend Sarah mentioned a financial advisor once, but idk, that feels super fancy and intimidating. Maybe I should just stick to Googling “how to save money” until I spontaneously combust from stress. Seriously, this is spiraling. I just want to feel… normal. Like, not constantly worried about whether I can actually afford to pay rent next month. Ugh. I just… I just wish it would stop.

okay seriously i can’t breathe. it’s like a constant tightness in my chest you know? everything feels so…urgent. like i need to be doing a million things at once and i’m failing at all of them. they said “we’ll see how things go” after i left but it feels like they’re just waiting for me to fall apart. i keep replaying everything that happened at work, every email, every meeting, like i missed some huge clue that would have told me it was all going to end. my head just spins and spins. i just want to disappear for a minute, just a little bit, to not feel this pressure. it’s not fair, is it? just… too much. i keep checking my phone waiting for a message but nothing. just…nothing.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: Okay, okay, just breathe. My chest feels weird, like it's trying to… I don’t know, expand or something? It’s stupid, I know, probably just stress. Moving here was a disaster, a total mess, and now this. Mom keeps texting, asking if I'm okay, but it’s hard to explain. It’s not *bad*, exactly, just… off. Like a lightbulb flickering. I keep checking my pulse, it’s racing, obviously. This city is so loud, even when it’s quiet. Everything feels amplified. I just want to go home, but I can’t. Just gotta keep telling myself it’s nothing, seriously nothing. Maybe I should call a doctor? Nah, that’s just making it worse. Ugh.

okay so like seriously this is a mess i just moved into this tiny apartment and it’s…it’s just so empty you know? like i unpacked everything i own and it still feels like there’s a hole in the middle of the room. i keep going back and rearranging things just to try and fill it somehow but it doesn’t work. everyone keeps saying “it’s a fresh start” and “you deserve this” which is…nice i guess but it's not feeling like that right now. it feels more like i’m just…floating. like i’m watching my life happen from behind a pane of glass.

i keep expecting him to just…pop in, you know? like he’ll magically reappear and everything will be okay. which is ridiculous i know but the thought just keeps circling around and around. it’s stupid i should be happy i’m finally free but i feel this constant tightness in my chest and my stomach is just a knot. i keep checking the door and the windows and then i feel even more anxious about it.

my phone keeps buzzing with messages from my sister and my friends but i just…ignore them. i don’t want to talk about it. i don't want to explain how i feel like i'm drowning in quiet and loneliness. and the neighbors are loud i swear they’re playing music really loud sometimes and it just makes me want to curl up in a ball and hide. i just wish i could feel…settled. like this is actually *my* place. not just a temporary stopover. i’m just so tired of feeling this way. it’s like the anxiety is just…coating everything. like i’m wrapped in cotton wool all the time. maybe i should just go back to where i was. no i can't. i promised myself i wouldn't.

Okay seriously, I can't sleep. Like, not even close. It’s been… weeks? Months? Time just feels weird when you’re staring at the ceiling like this. He’s gone, you know? Officially gone. Paperwork, lawyers, the whole nine yards. It should be…good, right? Freedom? But it’s just…loud. My own head is a broken record skipping on the same awful phrase over and over. ‘Maybe I should have…’ ‘Maybe I did the wrong thing…’ Just…noise.

I try to distract myself. I read, I watch stupid shows, I even tried to bake something – ended up burning the cookies. It doesn’t matter. My brain just keeps…replaying everything. Little things, big things, things we never even said out loud. It's like he’s still here, sitting next to me, and I’m desperately trying to ignore him, but he just keeps…breathing.

My chest feels tight, like someone’s wrapped a band around it. And my stomach – always a mess. I keep tossing and turning, pulling the covers up to my chin, but it’s no use. The darkness just makes the thoughts louder. I keep thinking about how quiet it was when he left. Just…gone. And now it’s just *me* and this emptiness. It's like a hole in my chest that won’t stop growing. I just want to shut it all out. Seriously, is it even possible to just…switch it off? I feel like I’m drowning in my own thoughts and I can’t even catch my breath. Ugh. Just…can’t sleep.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, I just… I can’t sleep. Like, at all. I’ve been staring at the ceiling for like, an hour now. My thoughts are just…racing. It’s stupid, I know, but I keep imagining all the things that could go wrong tomorrow – school, the history test, if Liam even notices I exist. It’s probably nothing, right? Maybe I’m just stressed about everything. I keep checking my phone, even though I know I shouldn't, just in case there's a message or something. I feel so jittery. I tried to read, but I kept getting distracted. I don’t even know why I’m so worried. It’s just… a lot. I just want to be calm. Idk, maybe if I just count sheep… but they keep turning into monsters, I swear.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: okay so like seriously, I just had to go to that study group thing tonight and it was…a lot. I’ve been trying to avoid everyone, honestly, because the thought of talking to people just makes my chest tighten up, you know? like a knot. it’s not even anything specific, it’s just…everyone’s so *together*, right? Like, they’re all buzzing about algorithms and theories and have these brilliant, confident answers and I’m just sitting there, staring at my notes and feeling like a complete idiot. I keep scrolling through my phone, pretending to read something, but my thumbs are just moving in circles. And then I feel this pressure in my head, like it’s going to explode, and I just have to get out. I excused myself to go to the bathroom like, three times. Seriously, the bathroom felt safer. I keep replaying everything in my head, every little thing I said, every awkward silence, wondering if everyone noticed how anxious I was. I know, I know, it sounds pathetic, but it's just... exhausting. It’s like my brain is constantly on high alert, scanning for threats, and social situations are the biggest threat of all. I keep thinking about all the exams coming up and it’s just piling on, you know? Like, I can’t even focus on the material because I’m just so worried about *everything*. I wish I could just disappear for a day, just breathe, but then I'd be even *more* worried about missing something important. It’s a vicious cycle, I swear. Just…ugh. Send coffee and maybe a therapist’s number, please?

just... everything feels so heavy. like a blanket i can’t shake off. i keep replaying things in my head, pointless stuff, and it’s just… too much. i don’t even know why i’m feeling like this, it’s just there.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, just… everything feels so loud here. Like the walls are closing in, you know? Mom and Dad are fighting again, and it’s like a low hum in my ears, even when they’re not talking. I idk why it hits me so hard, this new city, this alone-ness, it’s maybe too much.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so this is…weird. Moving here was supposed to be *good*, right? A fresh start. But it’s just…quiet. Like, really quiet. I keep expecting someone to pop out of their apartment or something, you know? Like, I should be hearing music or the clatter of dishes or *anything*. It's just…me. And the rain. Lots and lots of rain. I tried calling my sister, but she’s busy, I guess. Or maybe she doesn’t want to talk? I dunno. I just… feel like I’m floating. Like a balloon, and I'm drifting further and further away from everything. Maybe I should just order pizza and watch something dumb. I don’t even know what I *want* to watch. Idk. It’s just…a lot. I just feel so alone. Maybe I’ll try to go for a walk later, but honestly, probably not.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text:
Okay, seriously, this city is just...empty. Like, I walk down the street and everyone's in a rush, talking on their phones, and it feels like I’m just a ghost. I moved here for a fresh start, you know? To get away. But now it just feels like I’m stuck in a really bad movie where the main character is completely invisible. I keep telling myself it’ll get better, that I’ll make friends, find a routine, something. But then I just stare at the walls of this apartment and it’s just…more empty.

I tried to call Mom last night, but I just couldn’t. It felt…wrong. Like I was admitting I messed up, that I needed her. And honestly, I don’t want to admit that. It’s easier to just be alone, even though it’s awful. It’s a weird kind of comfort, I guess? Like a thick blanket that suffocates you. 

I keep replaying every conversation I’ve had this week, picking apart every word, every pause, wondering if I said the wrong thing. It’s exhausting. My stomach is tied in knots all the time, and I keep feeling this…tightness in my chest. Like I’m breathing too shallow. I don't even know why I'm writing this, it just…needed to get out. It's just...a lot. Just...so much. I wish I could just turn off the noise, you know? Just for a little while.

just got a call from the hospital. it’s my mom. something about her blood pressure. my stomach is twisting into knots i can’t even describe. everything feels so wrong and blurry, like i’m underwater. i just keep replaying everything in my head and i can't breathe.

it's just… a lot. i don’t even know how to say it. everything feels like it’s about to just… collapse. mark keeps saying i’m overreacting about lisa and the kids, like i'm making a mountain out of a molehill. but it's not a molehill, is it? it’s a whole avalanche of resentment and slammed doors and barely audible shouting. she won't even look at me sometimes, just glares and then turns back to her phone. and the kids… they’re mirroring everything. constant bickering, slammed drawers, the whole nine yards. i try to mediate, i really do, but it’s like yelling into a hurricane.

i feel this constant tightness in my chest, like someone’s squeezing the air out of me. my hands are shaking, and i keep biting my lip. i just… i just want things to be normal. i miss normal. i remember when we used to have dinners together without someone complaining about being hungry or how they don’t like what’s on the table. i remember when mark would actually *talk* to me, not just issue instructions.

i’m exhausted. bone tired. i feel like i’m constantly walking on eggshells, afraid to say or do the wrong thing and trigger another explosion. i keep thinking about how much work i put into keeping this family afloat, all the little things, the appointments, the school lunches, the endless laundry. and for what? to be met with this constant, low-grade panic? i just wish someone would just… understand. i feel so utterly alone in this, you know? like i'm drowning and nobody's throwing me a lifeline. i just need a quiet corner and maybe a cup of tea, but honestly, even that feels like too much effort right now.

okay seriously i can’t breathe. it’s like a tightness in my chest, you know? not like, a big panic attack thing but just… constant. i’m staring at the flickering fluorescent lights again and they’re just making everything worse i think. the shift’s almost over but i’m so behind on everything. rent is due next week and i don't even know where to start. it's just a pile of bills and numbers and it’s crushing me. i keep replaying everything, like maybe if i’d taken that overtime shift, things would be different. stupid stupid stupid. i just want to crawl into a hole and disappear. i feel so useless and like everyone else is fine and i’m just… falling apart. i just need a minute. just a minute to not feel this.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
okay seriously i can’t sleep. like at all. it’s 3 am and my brain is just… buzzing. it’s like a thousand tiny wasps are building a nest in my skull. i know i should be happy, you know? i graduated like, a month ago, everyone’s saying ‘now what?’ and i’m supposed to be excited about ‘the future’ but it just feels… heavy. i keep replaying everything, every interview, every small conversation, wondering if i said the wrong thing or if i didn’t say enough.

and then it’s just the small stuff, you know? like if i’ll ever find a job i actually like. or if i’ll ever be able to afford an apartment that isn’t my mom’s basement. or if i’ll ever actually feel like an adult. it's stupid i know but i just keep spiraling. i toss and turn and my sheets are soaked. i keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong, every single one of them. 

mom keeps asking if i’m okay, which is sweet but it just makes me feel worse. like i’m failing at being a functional adult. i tried reading, but my mind just keeps jumping. i tried meditating, but it just made me feel more aware of how anxious i am. i just want to shut it all off, just… be still. i just wish i could just… breathe. i feel so disconnected from everything, like i’m watching my life from behind a really thick pane of glass. this is exhausting. i just want it to stop. i don’t know what to do anymore.

okay so like seriously i don’t even know what’s happening anymore my head is just… buzzing. it’s been three days since he just… left. didn’t even say goodbye properly, just a text saying he needed space and then nothing. i keep replaying everything in my head like a broken record. what did i do wrong? was it something i said? i keep picking apart every conversation, every little thing, trying to find some clue, some explanation. it’s ridiculous i know but i can’t stop.

my friends are trying to be supportive, telling me it’s over and i should move on but it just feels… impossible. like if i stop thinking about it, it’ll just come back. everything feels amplified, you know? like the sun is too bright and the music is too loud. i keep having these little panicky moments where i just freeze and can't breathe and my stomach just drops. it’s like a constant low-level hum of dread.

i went to school today and it was awful. everyone was just… normal. laughing, talking, going about their day and i just felt like i was watching a movie and wasn’t part of it. i tried to focus on my classes but i couldn’t. i just kept thinking about him and how he probably doesn't even care. i keep checking my phone hoping for a message, even though i know it’s stupid. i just need… something. i feel so lost and empty and like a part of me just disappeared. i just wish it hadn't happened. i just want things to go back to how they were, even though i know that’s not possible, right? it’s just so overwhelming and i don't know what to do anymore.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, I just… I just had to leave that thing at the office tonight. You know, the team meeting? It was… it was just too much. Everyone was laughing, talking about their weekends, and honestly, I just felt like I was watching a movie of someone else’s life. Like I was stuck behind a pane of glass and they were all having this amazing time and I was just… here. And it wasn’t that they were being mean, not really, but it was just… the volume of everything. Like, you have to be *on* all the time, you know? Even when you’re trying to be quiet. It’s exhausting. I keep thinking about what I said, and what I didn't say, and whether it sounded stupid, or if they noticed I was just… fading into the background. I tried to make eye contact, like, really tried, but it felt so awkward, and then I'd look away and then I’d feel even *more* awkward. My stomach is doing this weird flip-flop thing, and I just want to crawl into a hole and disappear. It’s stupid, I know, everyone else seems so comfortable, so confident, and I'm just... a mess. I keep replaying it in my head, like a broken record, and it just gets worse and worse. I don’t even know why I went, honestly. It just felt like I *had* to, like if I didn’t show up, something bad would happen. It’s ridiculous, right? Just… a lot. A really, really lot. I should probably go lie down, but I feel like if I lie down, I’ll just keep thinking about it. Like it's stuck in my head. Ugh. Just… need to breathe.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, this is officially the worst. Seriously. I’m sitting in my unpacked bedroom – which, by the way, is still mostly boxes and half-covered with plastic – and I just feel… suffocated. Everything smells wrong, you know? Like, not *bad* wrong, but just… unfamiliar. It’s like the air itself is different here. My parents keep saying “new beginnings” and “fresh start” and it’s just grating on my nerves, honestly. They’re both being all sunshine and rainbows, which is nice, I guess, but it’s also incredibly irritating because *I’m* not feeling any of that. I keep expecting something bad to happen, like a glitch in the matrix or something. I went to the grocery store earlier and it was just… too many people. And the checkout line was ridiculously long and everyone was talking so loudly and I just wanted to disappear. Like, I felt this huge pressure in my chest, like it was going to burst. My hands were shaking and I had to buy a giant bottle of water just to calm down. I tried to talk to Liam, but he just kept saying “it’ll be fine” and honestly, that's not helpful. It's not *just* that I don’t know anyone, it's like... I'm anticipating everything going wrong. I keep replaying every single conversation I’ve had with people here, looking for signs that I’m going to offend someone or say the wrong thing. It's exhausting. I keep thinking about packing everything back up and just driving away, but then I’d disappoint my parents, and… and I don't know. I just feel so lost and small and like I’m drowning in a sea of unfamiliar faces and furniture. I need a blanket and maybe a lot of ice cream. Or maybe just to scream. Seriously.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, okay, breathe. Just…breathe. It’s just a new city, right? That’s all. Except it doesn’t *feel* like just a new city. It feels like…like being thrown into a washing machine full of static and bad decisions. I mean, seriously, I’ve been unpacking boxes for, like, twelve hours straight and I’m pretty sure I’ve just reorganized the same pile of clothes three times. And I keep expecting to find something I need, but then I realize I already put it somewhere and now I’ve completely forgotten where. It's just...too much. Everything is just *too much*. I keep picturing my roommate, Elara, back home, laughing with her friends and eating pizza and not having to worry about whether or not the heating works or if the internet is going to drop out mid-essay. I feel like I’m failing already, you know? Like I’m not cut out for this whole ‘adulting’ thing. My stomach is doing this weird, tight thing, like it’s trying to escape. And I keep checking my email, just hoping for, like, a message from my mom or something, anything to reassure me that I’m not utterly, hopelessly lost. This place is just…loud. Not like, physically loud, although the sirens are definitely a factor. It’s a mental kind of loud. Like all the thoughts in my head are shouting at each other and there’s no off switch. I just want to go back to my dorm room, to my old life, to anything that feels even remotely familiar. I keep second-guessing every single choice I've made, from picking this city to choosing this apartment. It’s stupid, I know, but it’s just…a constant, buzzing feeling that something is terribly, terribly wrong. I just wish it would stop.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay seriously, I don't even know how I'm going to get through this week. It’s just…so much. Like, I haven't slept properly in days, just tossing and turning, worrying about the presentation on Monday and then trying to get Leo to sleep, which is basically a full-time job in itself, you know? Everyone keeps saying "you're doing great," and I *want* to believe them, I really do, but it just feels like I'm constantly failing at everything. My boss keeps emailing me, asking for updates, and I’m just trying to keep my head above water, honestly. And then there's this whole project deadline looming, and I keep imagining myself freezing up in the meeting, just completely blanking. It’s a weird pressure, like I’m supposed to be this calm, collected, amazing parent *and* employee, but I just feel so overwhelmed and shaky all the time. I just need a quiet corner and maybe a really strong cup of tea.

seriously freaking out about this history test tomorrow like i haven't even looked at the notes. my stomach is just doing flips and i keep re-reading the same paragraph over and over. wish i could just disappear, it's so overwhelming.

text: okay okay okay this is ridiculous i’m seriously losing it. i just keep staring at this essay and it’s like… a giant blank wall. the deadline is tomorrow and i haven’t even started properly. it’s just sitting there mocking me. i keep telling myself “just write one sentence” but then i get stuck and then i feel this wave of panic and i just want to hide under the covers. i don't even know if i understand the material anymore. everyone else here seems so calm and together, like they’ve got it all figured out. i’m just… so overwhelmed. i keep re-reading the instructions and it just makes it worse. i feel like i'm going to fail, like everyone will see how stupid i am. i need to breathe. i just need to breathe. i should probably just call mom but she’ll just tell me to “study harder” which isn't helpful. ugh.

Just... sitting here. Again. The house is so quiet. It’s like a big, empty room and I’m just... stuck in it. Wish someone would just, you know, *be* here. It’s getting hard to breathe sometimes, honestly.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay so like, it’s… it’s the anniversary today. of, you know. the thing. and i didn’t even really *think* about it until like, an hour ago. i was trying to cram for this midterm, and it’s just… a lot. honestly, my brain feels like it’s full of cotton wool, you know? everything’s just… fuzzy. and i keep replaying it all in my head, like, a thousand times. i don’t even want to, but i *do*. it’s stupid, right? it’s just a date. but it feels… bigger. like, the world shifted on that day and i’m just… stuck here, in this slightly tilted version of reality.

i keep telling myself it's okay to not remember every detail, that it was awful and traumatic and i don’t need to relive it. but then i start to worry that if i *don’t* remember, then it didn’t really *happen*, you know? like, it’s less real. and that’s even scarier, i think. i just… i don't know how to deal with it. i tried to distract myself with studying, which is probably the worst thing i could have done, obviously. it’s just making me feel even more useless and like i’m failing at everything. my heart keeps skipping beats. like, little jumps. and i keep breathing really fast. it’s just… a constant low-level hum of anxiety, buzzing underneath everything else. idk if i should call someone. my roommate said it’s probably just the stress of exams, but it feels… different. maybe it’s nothing. maybe i’m just being dramatic. but it feels like… like a big, heavy weight on my chest. i just need to get through this midterm, i guess. just get through it.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: Ugh, today’s the anniversary. Stupid little reminder. Just…everything feels heavier somehow. Like the weight of it all is just sitting on my chest. I keep checking on the baby, but it doesn’t help. Just feels…wrong.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: it’s just… too quiet here. like, really quiet. i moved to this city for him, you know? thought it would be exciting, a fresh start. now it’s just… empty. every little noise makes me jump, like he’s still here, about to say something stupid or just… leave again. my chest feels tight all the time, like i’m breathing through a straw. i keep replaying everything, every fight, every little thing we didn't say. i should probably call my mom, but i just… can’t. it feels like a huge effort, like i'm trying to hold something up that’s already falling apart. i just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. this new place feels like a really bad dream.

Ugh, seriously, my parents are at each other again. It’s like a constant low-level hum of yelling, and it’s just… draining. I tried to say something, like, “Can you guys just *not*?” but it just made it worse. Mom got all snippy and Dad just glared. I feel like I’m stuck in the middle, you know? Like I have to pick sides, but I don’t *want* to pick sides. It’s just… loud. And my chest feels tight, and i keep checking the clock, like if i just keep looking at it the arguing will stop. I just need to disappear for a minute, just breathe. Everything feels so overwhelming. I don’t even want to go to dinner.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, everything feels so… heavy lately, like I’m just wading through mud and it’s getting deeper and deeper, you know? It’s stupid, really, this constant buzzing in my chest and the way i keep replaying everything that went wrong, like if i could just change one tiny thing and it would all be okay, but it never is, is it? Just… a mess.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Ugh, seriously, I can’t breathe. This internship is just… too much. Like, everyone expects me to know everything, and I’m just starting out. My boss keeps giving me these little side projects that aren't even in my job description, and I just feel like I’m constantly failing. Mom keeps asking if I'm eating enough, which is just… ugh. It’s not that I don’t *want* to do well, it's just... this pressure is building up and I feel like I'm going to explode. I keep replaying every mistake in my head, you know? Just... a knot in my stomach all the time. I wish I could just disappear for a day, just one day.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, seriously, I don't even know where to start. Like, I thought moving back home after graduation would be...nice? Relaxing? A soft landing? It's anything but. It’s just…a lot. I keep expecting everything to be *settled*, you know? Like I’m supposed to just…snap my fingers and suddenly have a job, an apartment, a life that isn’t just staring at my parents’ mismatched furniture and trying to avoid eye contact. It’s like my brain is stuck on repeat, playing this awful loop of “you should be doing something, you’re failing, everyone else is moving on.” And honestly, I just feel…heavy. Like I’m wading through molasses. I went for a walk earlier, just trying to clear my head, and the leaves were all just…rustling, you know? It felt like they were judging me, like they knew I was failing at being an adult. Mom keeps asking about job interviews and I just… I freeze. I can’t even formulate a coherent sentence. It’s not that I *don't* want a job, it's that the thought of it, of actually *applying* and being rejected, just… paralyzes me. I keep picturing myself back in my dorm room, surrounded by textbooks and lukewarm coffee, and it feels strangely safer than anything here. I’m supposed to be excited, right? About starting a new chapter? But all I feel is this prickly, anxious feeling, like there's a thousand tiny spiders crawling under my skin. I just wish I could breathe normally. Or, you know, not feel like I'm constantly on the verge of a panic attack. I just...I just want it to stop.

Okay seriously, I’m just…a mess. Lost my job last week, which was bad enough, obviously. But then Mom called yesterday, freaking out about her bloodwork. Nothing serious, she says, just a weird number. But she’s convinced it’s cancer. And honestly, it’s just…it’s like a switch flipped. Everything feels magnified. Like, I used to be able to handle a bad day, you know? Just a rough patch. Now, just *breathing* feels like this tight, awful pressure in my chest. I keep checking my phone, refreshing the email, hoping for a call from HR or something, anything, but it’s just silence. It's so quiet here.

I keep replaying everything in my head. That meeting where I messed up, the email I sent, all the things I should have done differently. It’s a loop. And then, of course, Mom’s cancer. It's like two separate disasters colliding. I keep telling myself to just *think* about something else, to watch a stupid show, but my brain just…won’t. It’s just this constant hum of dread. I feel like I'm drowning, but there's no water, just this sticky, suffocating feeling. I keep wanting to hide under the covers and just not move. I just... I don’t even know what to do. I feel so utterly useless. Like I’m failing at everything.  Seriously, I just need to calm down, but I can’t.  It's just... too much.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay, seriously, i’m freaking out. like, a full-blown, can’t-breathe kind of freakout. it’s the history midterm next week and i haven’t even started studying properly, not really. i keep starting chapters and then just… staring at the page. it’s like my brain just freezes. like, it’s not even *trying* to understand anything, you know? and it's not even like i'm not *wanting* to understand it, i really do, i just… i can’t. it’s this weird blockage, like a really thick mud in my head.

i keep replaying all the lectures in my head, trying to piece it together, but it’s just a jumbled mess. i saw Sarah yesterday, and she was talking about how she’d made flashcards and is totally prepared, and it just made me feel even worse. it's not that i'm lazy or anything, i swear, i just... i get so overwhelmed so easily. like, everything feels important, and urgent, and if i don't do one thing perfectly, then everything will fall apart. which, like, is probably totally irrational, but it *feels* real.

i tried to do some practice questions tonight, but i just kept getting stuck on the first one and then i just started scrolling through TikTok and now it’s almost midnight. my mom keeps asking me about it, and i just give her this vague “it’s okay, i’ll get to it” kind of answer, but it’s not okay. it's not okay at all. i feel like i'm drowning in details and dates and historical figures and i just want to disappear, like, just vanish into thin air and not have to deal with any of it. i keep thinking about failing, like spectacularly failing, and then everyone will think i’m stupid and i won't be able to get into college and then… i don’t even want to think about it. i just… i need to breathe. i really, really need to breathe.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so it's… it’s the anniversary today. Like, the one I don’t really want to think about. I’m supposed to be feeling… good, I guess? Celebrating? But it’s just… a lot. Everything feels blurry, you know? Like watching a movie through frosted glass. I keep replaying it in my head, even though I *know* I shouldn’t. It’s just… a constant hum, this anxiety. My hands are shaking a little. I don't even know why. Maybe it's the shift, maybe it's just… everything. I just want to disappear, honestly. Like, just fade away into the quiet of the night. I keep checking the clock, it feels so slow. Idk, maybe if I just distract myself with something, it'll be okay? Probably not, though. Just… a really bad day, I guess.

Just…can’t sleep. Every time I close my eyes, I see his face. Or the empty space where his side of the bed used to be. It’s like my brain’s stuck on repeat, just… buzzing. I feel like I’m drowning in blankets.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: okay seriously, i don’t even know where to start. it’s just… a lot. like, a *massive* amount. i keep staring at these bank statements and they just swim before my eyes you know? it’s like looking at a bunch of black numbers and realizing they’re all me, all my money, and it’s just… gone. i thought, i really thought, that after everything, after the divorce, things would be… easier, right? i mean, less responsibility, you know? but it’s not. it’s just… more. and the bills keep coming. the electricity, the internet, the damn phone – i need to be able to, like, *talk* to people! how else am i supposed to keep in touch with my sister? she’s the only one who really understands, i think. and now i feel like i’m failing her too, by being such a mess. i keep replaying everything in my head, every argument, every stupid little thing, and it’s like a record stuck on repeat. i keep wondering if i should have just… stayed. i know, that's ridiculous, right? but it's just... so overwhelming. i feel this constant tightness in my chest, like i'm holding my breath all the time. and then the worry starts, about everything. can i pay the mortgage? will i have to sell the car? what if i can't even afford groceries next week? it’s just… a horrible, spinning, endless cycle. i just… i wish someone could just tell me it's going to be okay. i just need a little bit of calm, a little bit of space to breathe. i’m so tired of feeling like i’m drowning.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, seriously, I don’t even know how to start this email. It’s just… looming. Like a giant, gray cloud that’s slowly filling up the whole sky and I can’t seem to move it. This project, the Reynolds account, it's due Friday and it’s just… so much. I’ve been working on it, like, obsessively, since Monday, and I still feel like I’m missing something huge. Everything feels fragile, you know? Like one wrong word, one misplaced comma, and the whole thing just collapses. I keep re-reading the brief, and it's just… more brief. More requirements. More things to worry about. My stomach is doing this weird flip-flop thing, like it’s trying to escape. I tried to focus, I really did, I put on some music, made a huge pot of coffee, even attempted a quick yoga session (which mostly consisted of me awkwardly trying not to fall over). But it's just… amplifying. It's like the music is mocking me, the coffee is just making me jittery, and the yoga is just reminding me that I’m failing at being calm. I keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong, and then I start thinking about all the things that *have* gone wrong in the past with similar projects. It’s a vicious cycle. I feel this pressure building, this tightness in my chest, and it's not just from the caffeine. I just need to finish it, I really do, but the thought of it is… paralyzing. Maybe I should just call in sick? No, that's just avoiding the problem. Ugh. I just want to disappear for a few hours, just breathe. This is so overwhelming.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
Okay, seriously, I'm spiraling. It’s like… everything’s just wrong. Like a record skipping constantly. I keep replaying everything, you know? Every conversation, every stupid thing I said, every time I didn’t say anything. It’s exhausting. And the exams are starting next week – finals week, the absolute worst. I haven’t slept properly in days, just tossing and turning, thinking about him. It’s stupid, I know, but it feels like if I don't think about it, it’ll just… explode.

My hands are always shaking, even when I’m just trying to write an essay. I keep staring at the page and nothing comes out. Just blank. It's like my brain is full of cotton wool. I tried to distract myself with music, but it just makes me feel… louder. Like the sadness is amplifying.

Everyone keeps saying “it’ll get better” and “you’ll move on,” but it doesn’t *feel* like it’s getting better. It just feels… heavier. Like I’m carrying this giant weight everywhere I go. And the worst part is, I don't even know why it ended. We just… stopped. Just drifted apart. It’s not like there was a fight or anything dramatic. Just… quiet. And now everything feels quiet, too. Just a constant, buzzing quiet that’s driving me insane. I just want to disappear, honestly. Just fade away and not have to deal with any of this. I feel like I'm drowning, but there's no water. Just this... nothing.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, I haven’t been sleeping well at all lately. Seriously, it’s… a mess. I keep waking up, like, three or four times a night, just staring at the ceiling. It’s not even necessarily *bad* dreams, just… restless. My brain just won’t shut off. I tried, you know? Reading, chamomile tea, that whole thing. Maybe it’s the project at work? It’s kind of stressful, a lot of pressure to get everything done. I dunno. I feel like I should be doing something productive, but then I just feel this huge, heavy anxiety, like a weight on my chest. It’s making it hard to even get out of bed sometimes. I just want to feel… normal, I guess? Like, not constantly on edge. Maybe I should call someone? But idk, that feels like a big step.

my brain just won't shut up. like it’s stuck on repeat playing all the worst things that could happen. i can’t even fall asleep, just tossing and turning, heart racing. it’s so exhausting, honestly. i just want it to stop.

Okay, seriously, I can’t sleep. Like, at all. I keep tossing and turning, just… thinking. It’s stupid, I know, but it’s like my brain just keeps replaying everything. The interview, mostly. It wasn’t even that bad, I guess? But then I start thinking about all the other interviews I didn’t get, and why I didn't get them, and then I start thinking about… well, everything. My job, my apartment, if I'll ever find another one. It's just a constant loop.

I keep staring at the ceiling, just watching the dust motes dance in the light, and it’s not relaxing. It’s just… lonely. Really lonely. I haven’t talked to anyone properly in days, just stupid texts with my mom about the weather. She doesn’t understand, she always says “you’ll find something” but it’s not that simple. It’s like, what *is* simple anymore? Like, what even *is* a normal day?

I tried reading, but I kept getting distracted by every little thought. And music just makes it worse, like it's amplifying everything. I feel like I'm drowning in my own head. I just want to be… quiet. Just for a few hours. Is that too much to ask? I keep telling myself to just breathe, but it’s hard. Really hard. I just feel… heavy. Like I’m carrying the weight of the whole world on my shoulders. Ugh. I need a nap. Or a vacation. Or a new planet. Seriously.

text: everything feels so tight lately. bills are piling up and i just… i don’t know how i’m gonna manage. it’s like a knot in my stomach that won’t go away. just breathing feels exhausting. i just want it to stop.

Everything feels so…loud. Like the world is just buzzing around me and I’m underwater. He just…gone. It’s stupid, I know, but I keep replaying everything, wondering what I did wrong. Just need to breathe, I guess. Or maybe not.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
it’s… it’s the 14th. seriously. i keep checking the date, like it’s going to magically change. it’s just… everything. it’s like a dull ache, you know? a constant thrumming behind my ribs. i haven’t really done anything, just, like, scrolled through my phone. mostly looking at pictures from before. before everything fell apart. it's weird, seeing those happy moments, like they were actually *real* happy. not just… filtered. i think i’m just trying to convince myself it was all worth it, that it wasn’t as awful as it feels now. which, honestly, idk if it was. i mean, we had good times. but… the way it ended, it just… it feels like a hole. a big, gaping hole where everything used to be.

and everyone keeps saying “time heals all wounds” and i’m just sitting here thinking, “sure, but what if the wound is just… expanding?” i keep replaying conversations in my head, things i should have said, things i didn’t. little stupid things, big things. it's exhausting. i tried to call, you know? just to say… something. but i couldn't. i just hung up. i don’t even know why. it just felt… pointless. maybe i should have sent a card? that feels too… nice. too hopeful. maybe it’s just better to pretend it didn’t happen. maybe that’s the only way to survive this. i just… i feel like i’m drowning in memories, and i can’t seem to find a way to breathe. it's just... a lot. a really, really, *a lot*.  i just wanted a normal day, i guess.

okay just… ugh. i keep replaying everything in my head like it’s a broken record. it’s stupid i know, but i can’t stop analyzing every single thing we ever said, every little thing. like maybe if i just said something different, if i’d reacted differently, it would have been… different. it’s probably pointless, right? but it feels like if i don’t dissect it all, i’ll just… i’ll just keep feeling this awful tightness in my chest. my stomach is a knot and i can’t breathe properly sometimes. everyone keeps saying “it’s over, move on” but it’s not that simple is it? it’s like a part of me is still there, stuck in this loop and i can’t seem to escape it. i just want it to stop. i just want to feel normal again.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: okay seriously, i can’t even. like, i haven’t slept properly in a week. it’s just…constant. my brain is like, a record stuck on repeat of me failing. i keep picturing the exams, you know? the professor’s face, the questions, just blank white paper staring back at me. and then it spirals. i can’t stop thinking about it. i scroll through my notes, trying to cram in more, but it’s just making it worse. my eyes burn, my head is throbbing. i keep blinking, but it’s still there. this morning i woke up at 3am, just staring at the ceiling, feeling this…pressure. like something’s about to explode. i tried to read, but i just kept zoning out. i keep telling myself to just relax, to breathe, but it feels impossible. it’s like there’s this wall between me and everything. i’m so tired, genuinely, bone-deep tired. not just physically, but… mentally. everything feels so heavy. i keep wanting to just… disappear, i don’t know. like if i just close my eyes and don't wake up, maybe it will all stop. it's stupid, i know, but that thought keeps popping up. i just need a few hours of sleep. just a few. but it feels like this is going to be my life now. endless sleepless nights, fueled by coffee and panic. i just want it to be over. i just want to feel… normal again. this is ridiculous, i’m being ridiculous. but i can’t shake it.

text: everything feels so…tight. like i’m wrapped in bubble wrap that’s slowly shrinking. i just wanna disappear into my notes, but mom keeps saying i’m not trying hard enough. and dad…just sighs. it’s all just too much, i can’t breathe.

Ugh, just staring at the monitors all night. Feels like everything’s blurry, not just the screens. This place is usually… quiet. But tonight it’s just echoing with this awful buzzing in my head. It’s stupid, right? Just a breakup. But it feels like the whole world shifted. Like I’m standing on a crumbling cliff and everyone just walked away. I keep replaying everything, little things, big things, everything. Why? Why did it have to end like this? I keep checking my phone, hoping for a text that isn’t there. Just… numb. Coffee’s not helping. Maybe if I could just fix something, *anything*, this would go away. It doesn't. Just the buzzing.

text: it’s… it’s today. like, a whole year. i keep scrolling through my phone and it’s just…everything feels blurry. like a faded photo. i don’t want to think about it, really i don’t, but it just keeps popping up. little things, mostly. a song we used to listen to, a picture of us at the lake. stupid stuff. my stomach is just a knot. it’s always like this, you know? a constant tightness. i tried to distract myself with homework, but i just keep zoning out. it’s like my brain is stuck on repeat. i just want it to stop. i wish i could just fast forward to tomorrow. this feeling… it’s awful. honestly, it’s just… too much.

text:
okay okay okay just breathe i’m trying to breathe but it’s like my chest is a balloon about to pop like seriously i hate being here tonight. it’s just…everyone’s laughing and talking and it feels like they’re all speaking a different language. like i’m watching a movie i don’t understand and i can’t turn it off. this apartment is so small too, you know? i keep feeling like i’m trapped in a box and everyone’s just…outside.

i went to that little cafe downtown earlier and it was awful. just trying to order a coffee and i completely blanked on what i wanted and i just stared at the menu for like five minutes and then the barista looked at me like i was insane. she just said ‘uh…what can i get for you?’ and i mumbled something about a latte and then i spilled some on my sleeve and then i just wanted to disappear.

i told myself i’d be okay, that i’d meet people, that this new city would be exciting but it's not it's just…loud and overwhelming. everyone seems to know each other and i don't and i don’t *want* to, i just want to curl up on the couch and not feel this. i keep replaying everything in my head, every awkward moment, every misinterpreted glance. it’s like my brain is stuck on repeat. i wish i could just teleport back home for like five minutes, just to be somewhere familiar. it’s getting harder and harder to even get out of bed in the morning, honestly. this is ridiculous i’m being ridiculous. just gotta keep going i guess right? just gotta… *deep breath*  it’s just too much.

Just...everyone seems to be having a good time. Like, genuinely happy. And I'm just here. Watching. It feels like I’m underwater, you know? Just breathing, but not really living. Everything feels so loud when nobody’s around.

seriously freaking out again. this project is due tomorrow and i just...can't breathe. the baby’s crying and i keep thinking i’m failing at everything. like, everything. need to just lie down but i feel like i have to keep going.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so this is just… a lot. Like, seriously a lot. I’m back here in my childhood bedroom, which, you know, is supposed to be comforting, right? But it’s just… everything. My parents are, like, constantly picking at me about the job search. It’s not that they don't *want* me to have a good job, obviously they do, but it’s this relentless pressure. They keep saying “you should be further along by now” and “have you even *tried* networking?” and honestly, it just makes me want to crawl under the covers. I know they mean well, I really do, but it feels like they don’t actually *hear* me. Like they just see a failure, a disappointment.

And then my brother… he’s just… being his brother. Loud, opinionated, making jokes that land wrong. Last night he said something about my “lack of ambition” and it just… hit me hard. I don't even know why it bothered me so much, it was just a stupid comment, but it just made everything feel so heavy. I keep replaying it in my head, you know? Analyzing every word. It's like my brain is stuck on repeat. I’m trying to focus on applying for stuff, I really am, but it’s impossible. Every time I open my laptop, I just feel this wave of… tightness in my chest. Like I’m suffocating. I just want to disappear, honestly. I don’t know what to do. Maybe I should just call them and tell them I'm feeling overwhelmed? But then they’ll just tell me to “pull myself together.” I idk, it just feels exhausting. I just… feel anxious all the time lately. Like a constant hum of worry.  It's probably nothing, right? Just stress. But it feels… bigger than that.

okay seriously this is just… awful. i keep staring at the walls you know? like, just watching the paint. it’s beige. a really boring beige. and it’s just… there. i haven’t left the apartment in three days. three days. that’s insane right? i keep telling myself i need to do something but then i just… don’t. it’s like my brain just shuts down. i know i should call someone but i don't even know who. my mom? she’d just say "get a job" which is, like, the last thing i need to hear right now. it’s not like it’s easy to find something, you know? everything’s online now and i’m not good at that stuff. i used to be good at my old job, at least. it feels like a different lifetime. i remember laughing with sarah, talking about lunch, just… being around people. now it’s just… quiet. really quiet. and i think i’m starting to forget what normal sounds like. it’s just this low hum, like a refrigerator running all the time. and i just want to turn it off but i can’t. i don't even know how. i just feel… empty. like a really, really big, empty space. and it’s getting colder in here somehow. i’m wearing two hoodies but it still feels… wrong.  i wish i could just… go somewhere. anywhere. but i don't even have the energy to make a cup of tea. seriously, just a cup of tea. that feels like a mountain right now.

text:
Okay, so like, this is really hard to even write about, honestly. It’s just… everything is piling up, you know? I haven’t been sleeping properly, which, duh, when you’re already a mess. But it’s not just the sleep, it’s this… this constant buzzing in my head. It’s the project deadline for Professor Eldridge’s history paper, like, it’s due next week and I have *maybe* two days to finish it, and it's, like, a huge chunk of my grade. And I’m just… I’m completely stuck. I keep staring at the screen, and all I see is a blank page. Or, like, a half-written page filled with sentences that don't make sense. I've tried to outline, I’ve tried to just start writing, but my brain just… freezes.

I keep thinking about how I messed up with, you know, everything. The divorce, the job… and now this. It feels like if I can't even handle a stupid history paper, what’s the point of anything? I keep replaying conversations in my head, things I said, things I *didn’t* say. It's awful. I keep telling myself to just breathe, to take a break, but then I feel this tightening in my chest, like I’m going to panic. I don't even know why this paper is so important, it’s just… a deadline. But it feels like it represents so much more. Like I'm failing at everything. I dunno, it's just… a lot. I feel so incredibly vulnerable and fragile. I just want to disappear, I guess. Maybe I should just email Professor Eldridge and ask for an extension? No, that’s probably just asking for more trouble. I don’t even know what to do. I just... I feel so overwhelmed.

text:

i keep re-reading the prompt and it just looks so complicated. i don’t even know where to begin. i should probably start with the research, right? but then i’ll get bogged down in sources and citations and it’ll just make me feel even more overwhelmed. maybe i could just, like, write a really bad first draft and then fix it later? but what if it’s *terrible*? i just… i don’t think i can handle the stress.

my hands are shaking a little bit. i keep checking the time. it’s only 3 pm, but it feels like the end of the world. i tried to tell my roommate, Liam, but he just said “you’ll be fine” which is the opposite of helpful, honestly. he doesn’t really get it. he’s always so chill. i don’t even know what i’m doing, like, truly. i’m just spiraling. i’m starting to feel nauseous. idk, maybe i should just go lie down? But then i’ll just lie there and think about it more. i just want it to be over, you know? maybe if i just breathe… but it’s hard to breathe when you feel like you're drowning.

text: okay okay okay just breathe i think i’m losing it seriously i went to the pharmacy this morning to pick up my prescription for the cough and the pharmacist just asked me about my blood pressure and now i’m convinced it’s sky high like dramatically high and i’m going to die alone in this tiny apartment somewhere in portland and nobody will even notice. it's so weird because i haven't felt sick really except this cough but it’s just... building. like a pressure in my chest. i keep checking my pulse it’s fast but is that normal? i don’t know anything about pulses. i just moved here like three months ago and it’s been a mess already. everything feels so overwhelming. i keep picturing doctors and hospitals and needles and then i just start to sweat. it’s ridiculous i know but i can’t shake this feeling. i just want to call someone but i don’t want to bother them with this stupid thing. it's probably nothing. probably. but what if it's not? what if i'm just… falling apart? i should probably drink more water maybe that would help. or maybe i should just hide under the covers and not come out until tomorrow. it's so loud in here anyway, all the traffic and the sirens and just… everything. i just feel so small and exposed and like i’m constantly on the verge of something bad happening. it’s exhausting. i just want a normal day. just a normal, quiet day.

Seriously can’t sleep. Just staring at the ceiling. It’s like my brain is a broken record, skipping over all the worst things. Like, the job, obviously. It's been three weeks and it just… keeps playing. I keep replaying everything I said, everything I didn’t say. Did I mess it up? Should I have pushed harder? It’s stupid, I know. Everyone tells me to “just relax,” but how do you relax when you’re constantly worried about money and if you’re even any good at anything? My chest feels tight all the time. And I’m so tired, like bone tired, but I can’t shut off. Just… ugh. Wish I could just turn it off.

text: Okay, so like, this whole moving thing is just... a lot. I’m back here, in my childhood room, and it’s weird. Really weird. Everyone keeps saying “settle down” and “you’ll adjust,” but I just feel like I’m drowning a little. I don’t even know where to start with the job hunt, honestly. I applied to, like, a million things, and idk if any of them are even real. My stomach feels like it’s permanently tied in knots. The apartment is nice, I guess, but it just feels... empty. Like a stage set. Maybe I'm overreacting, maybe this is just normal post-grad jitters. But what if it's not? What if I'm just… stuck? I keep picturing myself just staying here, you know? It's just... overwhelming.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, so like, this is officially a disaster. I haven’t slept properly in…I don’t even know how long anymore. It’s just…layers. Like, I try to fall asleep and then this *thing* just starts. It’s not a monster, not exactly, but it’s like a hundred tiny, insistent questions just buzzing around my head, all at once. Mostly about whether I’m good enough, you know? Like, I finished my degree, which is…good, I guess? But then it’s immediately followed by, “Did I really *do* good enough? Did I even *try* hard enough? What if everyone was just being polite?” And then it’s followed by a whole avalanche of ‘what ifs’ about job applications, about moving out, about…everything.

And then the physical stuff hits. My chest gets tight, like someone’s wrapped a band around it, and my stomach just flips. I toss and turn, desperately trying to quiet it all, but it’s like trying to hold back the tide. I’ve been staring at the ceiling for hours, just watching the shadows shift and thinking about how everything is probably going to go wrong. My mom keeps asking if I’m okay, which is nice, but it's also so incredibly embarrassing. Like, I feel so *visible* with my anxiety, you know? Like I’m wearing a neon sign that says “I’m a mess.”

I keep telling myself it’s just stress, that it’ll pass, but it feels…different. It’s not just the pressure of finding a job, it’s this underlying, constant dread. It’s like I’m expecting something bad to happen, even though I don’t know what or when. I just wish I could just…switch it off. Just be able to relax and not feel like I'm perpetually on the verge of a panic attack. Seriously, is this what adulting is supposed to be like? Just a relentless, exhausting battle against my own thoughts? I feel so incredibly alone with this, like nobody understands how it feels to just…not sleep.

Everything feels…wrong. Like the whole world tilted when they left. My baby’s beautiful, but I just… I can’t breathe sometimes. Just a constant tightness in my chest. It's like I'm drowning, even when I'm holding him.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like seriously, I don’t even know why I’m typing this out but I feel like if I don’t just… get it all out there it’s just going to build up and build up and I’ll explode or something. Mom keeps talking about my job search, you know? Like it’s the only thing that matters, and Dad just nods along, completely silent, which is worse, honestly. It’s not that they don’t care, I think, they really don’t, they just… don't *get* it. Like they think getting a “real” job, a stable job, is the same as *me* being happy. It's not. It's like, I’m trying, okay? I’m sending out applications, I'm networking (which is terrifying, by the way, everyone just seems so… confident) but it’s just this constant pressure, this feeling that I'm failing and disappointing them. And then they just… comment. About how I should be further along, how I need to ‘pull myself together’ which is just… exhausting. I feel like I'm walking on eggshells around them all the time, saying the wrong thing, doing the wrong thing. It’s like living in a constant state of low-grade panic. I just want to curl up in my room and not talk to anyone, but then I’d feel even worse, right? Like I’m pushing everyone away. It's just... a lot, you know? A huge, overwhelming amount of ‘shoulds’ and ‘musts’ and I can’t breathe sometimes. This whole moving back home thing was supposed to be temporary, a little breathing room, but it’s just amplified everything, it feels like the air is thick with unspoken expectations and judgments. I just wish they'd just... be quiet for five minutes. Just let me *be*.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: It’s the anniversary. Feels like yesterday, actually. Everything just…smells the same. Like the rain before it hits, you know? This tightness in my chest isn't going away, and honestly, I don’t even want to go outside.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is… a lot. I moved into this apartment yesterday. It’s…small. Really small. And beige. Like, aggressively beige. I didn’t pick it, obviously, my lawyer just said “something affordable” and, well, here we are. I just… I don’t know. Everything feels wrong. It’s not even *that* bad, I guess, but it’s just… a constant little hum of unease, you know? Like static on a radio. I keep expecting something to happen, something awful, but it never does. Which is maybe even worse, honestly.

I tried to unpack, but I just stared at the boxes for like an hour. It felt pointless. Like if I unpack, then I’m admitting it’s real, that I actually *did* this. That I’m here. And that’s… scary. I mean, I told myself it would be good, a fresh start, right? Get away from everything. But it just feels like… trading one set of problems for another.

I keep checking the locks, even though the landlord said they're brand new. And I keep thinking about all the noises, like, the pipes, or the neighbors, or just… the wind. I don’t even *like* the wind. It’s just… unsettling. I had a weird dream last night where the walls were closing in. It wasn’t a *bad* dream, just… intensely unsettling. I dunno. I just feel… exposed, I think. Like everyone can see right through me, and I’m just a mess. Maybe I should just call my sister? But she’ll just tell me to “look on the bright side,” and that’s… not helpful. I just… I need to breathe. I don’t even know why I’m typing this, it’s probably stupid.  I just felt the need to say *something*.  It's just... everything feels so fragile right now. Like it could all just crumble.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, this whole thing with my aunt… it’s just… a lot. She went to the hospital yesterday, and they found something weird, I think? They wouldn’t really tell me much, just said “monitoring.” And now I’m just… obsessing. Seriously. I keep Googling everything, which I know is a terrible idea, but I can’t help it. My heart is just racing all the time, even when I’m sitting still. I don’t even know what’s normal, you know? Maybe I’m overreacting, probably. It’s just… everyone keeps asking me how she is and I just want to say everything’s fine but it’s not. I just feel this constant, low-level dread. I should probably call my mom, but idk, it feels pointless. Maybe I’ll just watch TV.

okay okay okay just breathe i keep breathing but it feels like my chest is gonna explode i don't even know what to do anymore the doctor said it was probably just a virus something to do with the baby’s immune system but he kept looking at me like he was questioning if i was even capable of taking care of him seriously? like i’m some kind of idiot? i just… i just wanted him to be okay. he was so quiet last night just sleeping and i was staring at him and i felt like i was failing him somehow like i wasn’t giving him enough or anything or if i was doing it wrong and he’d get really sick and it would be all my fault. and then i started thinking about everything else everything i have to do the house is a mess the laundry is piling up i can barely shower and i just keep replaying everything in my head what if i missed something what if i didn’t do it right what if what if what if i just feel so overwhelmed like a wave of panic just washing over me and i can’t stop it. i keep checking his temperature and his breathing and i feel like i’m going to burst. i just want everything to go back to normal back to before the baby back to before i felt like this. i don’t want to be a bad mom i just want to be… calm. i keep telling myself he’s fine he’s fine but it doesn’t feel like it does it?

text: Okay, it’s… it’s today. Three years. I keep expecting him to just, you know, *be* here. Like it was just last week we were arguing about the thermostat, which sounds ridiculous now, doesn’t it? It just feels… wrong. Like a piece of me is missing. I tried to make his favorite lasagna – the one with extra ricotta – but it just tasted… bland. Everything does, really. I keep replaying it all in my head, the hospital, the waiting room, his face… God, his face. I shouldn’t think about that. I just… I can’t stop. It's like a loop, this awful, heavy loop and I’m just spinning and spinning and I don't know how to stop. Just breathing feels exhausting, honestly. I just need to get through today, I guess. Just… get through it.

text: Ugh, just… everything feels so heavy lately, like I’m carrying this giant invisible weight around all day. Work is a disaster, I keep messing up these assignments and my boss just sighs, you know? And then I have to help my mom with stuff, it’s just a constant swirl of things needing to be done and I can’t even breathe sometimes, seriously.

Ugh, just… can’t shake this feeling. Like static on the radio, you know? Everything feels too loud. The fluorescent lights are driving me crazy, even though I’m used to them. It’s just… a lot. Like my brain is trying to juggle a thousand things at once and it’s all slipping away. I keep replaying conversations in my head, saying the wrong thing, probably. Or not saying enough. I don’t even know. Just feeling jittery and on edge. Coffee’s not helping. Seriously, just need to… breathe, I guess. But it’s hard. Really hard. Hoping this shift goes fast. Maybe if I can just get through it…

seriously freaking out about this month’s bills. my parents are already stressed and i just… i don’t know how we’re gonna make it. everything feels so heavy and i can’t breathe. like a constant pressure in my chest. i just want it to stop.

It's just…quiet. Too quiet. Everyone else is out doing stuff, you know? Friends, family, even just random people at the park. And me? I’m staring at this little face, feeding him, changing him, and it’s…fine. I guess. But it feels like I’m drowning in it. Like I’m watching everyone else’s lives from behind a really thick pane of glass. I keep wanting to just…call someone. Anyone. But then I feel this wave of panic, like I’m failing at being a mom, and I just freeze. It's not that he’s a bad baby, he’s a good baby, just… isolating. I feel so alone with it all. Just wishing someone would just, like, acknowledge I'm here.

Okay seriously, I’m spiraling. Like, a full-blown, colors-bleeding-into-everything spiral. This presentation is tomorrow and I haven’t slept properly in three days. I keep going over the slides, adding, deleting, adding *more*, and it’s just… making it worse. It’s like my brain is a broken record, skipping back to all the potential ways I could mess it up. My boss keeps saying “confident” and “clear,” but what does that even *mean*? Like, how do you just *be* confident when you’re convinced you're about to embarrass yourself spectacularly?

Everyone else seems so calm, like they’ve got this whole thing figured out. They’re just casually tossing around ideas and talking about next steps, and I’m just… sitting here, paralyzed. My stomach feels like it’s full of lead. I keep imagining myself stumbling over my words, forgetting key points, just generally failing. It’s stupid, I know, but it’s not *stupid* to feel this way, right? It's just... a lot. I keep checking my email, even though it’s practically midnight, just hoping for some kind of reassurance that I'm not completely useless. I just need to breathe, I think. Just... breathe. But it feels impossible. I just want it to be over.  I keep telling myself it's just pressure, just a presentation, but it feels like so much more than that. Like it's a test of my worth, or something. Ugh.

text: Okay, seriously, I don’t even know where to start. This whole thing with, like, getting let go… it’s just… a lot. I keep replaying everything in my head, you know? Like, what did I say? Did I do something wrong? It’s probably nothing, right? But my brain just won't shut off. I keep obsessing over all the deadlines I missed, the emails I didn't respond to, just this constant feeling of failure, like I just completely messed up everything. I feel so behind, and everyone else seems to be moving so fast, and I’m just… stuck. I keep imagining them talking about me, wondering why I wasn't good enough. It’s stupid, I know, but I just can’t shake this feeling that I’m going to be forever behind, like I’ll never catch up. I just need to breathe, I think, but it’s so hard.

Ugh, just staring at the ceiling again. It’s 3 AM and my brain is just… a mess. Bills are piling up, you know? Like, seriously piling. I keep checking my bank account and it just keeps going down. Feels like I’m drowning, honestly. Every little thing feels huge right now. Like, buying coffee is a massive decision. Or if I should actually call my mom about it – that’s a whole other level of stress. My chest is tight and I keep breathing too fast. Just… everything feels too loud. I wish I could just shut it all off, just for five minutes. Seriously considering just not going to work tomorrow. I don’t even know why I bother. This feeling… it never really leaves, does it?

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, this is just…a lot. I keep staring at the walls, you know? They’re beige. Really beige. It’s the color of…nothing. It’s just…beige. And it’s all I see. It’s been, I don’t even know how long now, since…well, since everything. It feels like a lifetime, honestly, even though it's only been six months. Six months of just…this. This quiet. I used to hate quiet, I loved the noise, the arguing, the chaos, even the yelling – it felt…connected somehow, even if it wasn't good connected, you know? Now? Now it’s just a heavy blanket draped over everything. I keep expecting to hear his voice, just a random thing, like “Did you remember to…” or “I’m making dinner,” or something, anything, but there's just…silence. Thick, suffocating silence. I tried calling a friend yesterday, Sarah, you know, the one who always makes me laugh, but I just couldn’t. I started talking and then I froze, like, completely froze, and I just hung up. It felt…wrong. Like I was intruding. Like if I really started talking about it, about *him*, about how empty it feels, it would just…explode. And I don't want to explode. I just want to…not feel this. It’s not sadness, not really, it’s more like this constant, buzzing anxiety, like there’s a swarm of bees trapped in my chest and they're just…waiting. Waiting for something, I don't even know what. Just waiting. And the worst part is, I don’t even know how to get them out.  I keep replaying everything, every conversation, every fight, wondering if I could have done something differently, anything differently, and it just keeps spiraling.  It's exhausting.  Seriously exhausting. I just want to curl up and disappear, but then what would I even *do*?

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, this is officially the worst. I graduated last month, remember? The big thing? And everyone’s posting about their amazing jobs and apartments and trips to Europe and I’m…here. Still in my childhood bedroom. Mom keeps asking if I’ve “thought about dusting” which, honestly, is the most depressing thing ever. It’s just…everything feels so loud, you know? Like, not actually *loud*, but like my brain is just constantly cranking up the volume on everything. I went to the grocery store yesterday and just… froze. Like, three people bumped into me and I just stood there, staring, convinced everyone was judging me, judging my messy hair and the way I was clutching my shopping basket. It's stupid I know, but it just felt… overwhelming. I tried to call Liam – he moved to Seattle like six months ago – but I couldn't even get through to him. His voicemail just kept repeating, which was, like, the most terrifying thing. Seriously. I keep replaying all the conversations we had, wondering if I said something wrong, if I wasn't funny enough, if he just doesn't care anymore. I just feel…invisible, I guess. Like I’m fading away. It’s not even like I *want* to do anything, I just…don't.  I keep scrolling through social media and it’s just a highlight reel of everyone else's perfectly curated lives and I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. I don’t even know what to do anymore. I feel like I'm just… stuck. Like wading through mud. And the mud is getting thicker.  I just wish I could just… breathe. Properly.

Okay, seriously, I can’t breathe. Little Finn had a fever spike last night – like, a *huge* one. The pediatrician was all calm and said it was probably just a virus, but I’m a mess. Just…constantly checking his temperature, listening for every little cough. I haven’t slept properly in days, just staring at the monitor. My husband’s trying to be supportive, bless him, but I feel like I’m failing him, failing Finn. Everything feels so fragile right now, like one tiny thing could just…break it all. I keep replaying everything – did I give him enough formula? Was the room too cold? I just need to stop thinking, but I can’t. It’s just…too much.

text: Okay, so like, this is really hard to even write about, honestly. I just… I don’t know what’s happening. We’re, um, struggling. Seriously struggling. The bills are piling up and I keep staring at them and it's just… a blur. I feel like I’m failing, you know? Like, a really bad parent. I should be happy, obviously, with Leo, he's the sweetest little guy, but all I can think about is how we're going to pay for everything. Maybe we should just move back in with my parents? I don't want to, it feels so embarrassing. I just feel this constant, low-level panic, like a weight on my chest. I keep replaying everything, wondering if I could have done something differently. Idk, I just need to breathe. Maybe if I just keep busy, it'll go away? But it doesn’t, does it?

text: Ugh, seriously, I’m just… stuck. It’s like my brain won't shut off. I keep replaying everything from tonight, even though it’s, like, over. Just little things, you know? Did I turn off the lights right? Did I lock the door? Idk. And then it spirals. I’m supposed to be on tonight, again, and I’m already exhausted. I haven’t slept properly in days, I think. Maybe weeks? It’s just… a constant buzzing. My heart feels weird, like it’s trying to beat its way out of my chest. I keep telling myself it’s just the shift, the graveyard shift, but it feels… deeper. Like it’s not just tired. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. Maybe if I just stare at the ceiling a little longer…

text: Okay seriously, I don’t even know how to explain this to anyone, it’s just… a knot. Like a really tight, prickly knot in my stomach that won’t loosen. It started last week with Mrs. Henderson’s medication schedule and just kind of snowballed. I’m supposed to be helping her transition to the assisted living place and it feels like I’m drowning, you know? Everything is so complicated, the paperwork, the appointments, getting her to eat… and then Mr. Davies called this morning about the tutoring sessions – he’s so particular about the timeline and the exercises, and I just… I just can’t keep up with it all. My head is spinning, I keep forgetting things, and I just feel this overwhelming need to just disappear for a little while, just to breathe, but I can't, because someone has to do this. It’s just too much.

text: Okay, so like, this is ridiculous, seriously. I keep replaying everything, you know? Every. Single. Thing. It's not that I’m blaming him, not entirely anyway, but… it’s just… a lot. Like, I keep seeing the way he looked at me last Tuesday, right before he said it, and it's just… stuck. Like a record skipping. And I keep thinking about the argument we had about the dishwasher – stupid, I know, but it felt huge at the time, like a little crack in the foundation of everything. And now the foundation’s just… gone, isn’t it? It’s like the whole house just crumbled. I tried to be a good partner, I really did, I cooked his favorite meals, I listened to him talk about his stupid stamp collection (seriously, stamps?), I even tried to learn how to fix the leaky faucet – which, by the way, I still can’t do. I feel like I poured everything into this, this… this *thing*, and it just wasn’t enough. It’s not even like I wanted it to be perfect, I just wanted… something solid. Something to hold onto. And now I'm just floating, you know? Like a balloon that’s lost its string. I keep feeling this tightness in my chest, like someone’s wrapping it up with barbed wire. My stomach is doing this weird flip-flop thing, and I keep needing to breathe, just breathe, but it’s hard to even remember how. Everything feels… amplified. Like the volume on the world is cranked up to eleven. It’s exhausting. Just… exhausting. I keep expecting him to call, to text, to *do* something, anything, but it's just… quiet. A really, really loud, empty quiet.

text: Ugh, I just… I don't even know how I'm going to get through this week, honestly. They keep piling on the assignments, and I feel like I'm drowning, just constantly trying to keep everything straight and not mess up, you know? It's just a lot, a *huge* lot and I feel this tightness in my chest all the time now, like it's stuck.
