text: Okay, seriously, this is just… a lot. Moving here was supposed to be this fresh start, right? Like, totally new, reinvent myself. But now? I’m staring at my bank account and it's just… red. Everywhere. Rent’s due in two days and I’ve barely eaten anything decent this week. I keep replaying every conversation, every stupid thing I said, wondering if I could have gotten a better job, saved more, anything. It's just this constant, buzzing worry in my head. I feel like I’m drowning, just trying to keep my head above water. I keep checking my phone for texts from my mom, hoping she hasn't noticed I haven’t called. Just… ugh. I don’t even know what to do.

seriously i can’t breathe. the baby just… won’t stop. he’s been crying for like an hour straight and i just keep rocking him and he just… cries. it’s like a little screaming machine. i feel so useless. i thought having a baby would be all cuddles and milky smiles but it’s just constant panic. my chest is tight and my hands are shaking. i keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong, you know? like what if he’s sick? what if he’s not feeding right? what if i’m doing something wrong? i just want to sleep but i can’t even close my eyes without him starting up again. i feel like i’m going to completely lose it. i just need five minutes. five minutes to just… be.

okay just… wow. went to the doctor this morning and it’s not good. nothing serious seriously but the tests came back showing something weird in my chest. my heart’s been pounding like crazy ever since. i keep checking my pulse like five times a minute just to make sure it’s still there. i feel like i'm going to hyperventilate just thinking about it. everything feels so overwhelming, like a giant weight is sitting on my chest. i’m trying to breathe but it’s hard, really hard. i keep imagining worst case scenarios and it’s just… a mess. i just want this to be over and to feel normal again. i feel so useless and just… frozen. i just need a minute. maybe two.

Okay, seriously, I don’t even know what to do anymore. It just... happened. Like, one minute we were arguing about dishes, the next he was just… gone. Packed his bags and left a note. A stupid note. It says he needs to “find himself,” whatever that even means. I just feel… hollow. Everything feels wrong. My kitchen looks messy, my plants are drooping, and I can’t stop replaying everything we said. I keep expecting him to walk through the door, you know? Like this is some terrible, elaborate joke. My chest just feels tight all the time. I keep telling myself I’m fine, that I’m strong, but… I just feel like I’m falling apart.  It’s just… a lot.  Too much.

just... ugh. went to the grocery store with little leo today. felt like everyone was staring. like i was failing at being a mom. my heart just started racing and i had to bolt. it’s stupid i know but i can’t even look at another shopping cart.

okay seriously i don’t get how everyone else just *does* this. like, it’s like they’re born knowing how to talk to people and not freak out. i was at sarah’s party last night and it was… bad. really bad. i tried to say hi to like, three people and i just froze. my throat closed up and my face felt hot. i think i actually mumbled something about the weather because i literally couldn't think of anything else. and then sarah started talking about her new boyfriend and i just stared at my shoes. i wanted to disappear. everyone else was laughing and chatting and it felt like everyone else was having the best time ever and i was just… stuck.

it’s not that i don’t *want* to be part of things, you know? i do. i really do. but it’s like there’s this wall in my head that just slams shut whenever i’m around people. and then all these thoughts start going around and around and around. what did i say? did they think i was weird? are they judging me? what if they realize i’m completely clueless and awkward and everyone just wants to walk away? i keep replaying everything in my head and it just gets worse and worse. my stomach is still churning. i just want to be alone, curled up in my room with my headphones, but i can’t just leave. i have to pretend like everything is okay. it’s exhausting. i just… i just wish i could just be normal. is that too much to ask? i feel like i'm drowning a little bit every time i try to talk to someone. ugh i need a nap.

text: Okay, so like, I don't even know where to start, honestly. It’s just… everything feels so tight, you know? The bills are piling up, and it’s not even like I’m spending more, it’s just that everything’s gotten so much more expensive, like the price of bread or something, but it’s everything. I keep going over the numbers in my head, a million times, and it just gets worse. I feel this awful pressure building up, like a balloon about to pop, and it’s all about the money, really. I keep telling myself it'll be okay, that we’ll figure it out, but then I just… I just feel so helpless, like I'm drowning and there’s no shore in sight. It’s exhausting, you know? Just… exhausting. I just want a little peace, a little quiet, but there’s just this constant worry buzzing around, like a persistent fly.

Okay seriously, I’m losing it. Like, completely losing it. I just… I don’t know how we’re going to do this. Leo is amazing, of course, he’s the sweetest little guy, but everything is just…expensive. I swear, diapers alone are eating my entire paycheck. And the formula? Don’t even get me started. It’s like, a tiny fraction of his weight in money every single time. My mom keeps sending those little gift cards, which is nice, but it’s not enough. It feels like a band-aid on a gaping wound.

I tried to talk to Mark about it, but he just says "We'll figure it out." Like, yeah, *we’ll* figure it out. He’s working overtime, which is great for *him*, but it’s also making me even more exhausted and stressed. I’m constantly worrying about bills, about not being able to afford to take Leo to the doctor if he gets sick, about whether we’ll be able to afford anything, you know? Christmas is coming up and I just want to give him something, anything, but I feel like I’m failing already. I keep replaying every single decision that led us here, every impulse buy, every time I said “oh, it’s just this once.” It's stupid, I know, but I can't shake the feeling that I messed everything up. Just… so much pressure. I just need a quiet moment, a minute to breathe, but it’s never quiet. It’s just…everything.

Ugh, just… can’t breathe sometimes. Mom and Dad are at each other again. It’s like a constant low-grade hum of yelling in the background, even when I'm trying to work. My stomach is twisted into knots. I just want it to stop.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: It’s… it’s the anniversary. Seriously, how is it already? I keep checking the calendar, like if I can somehow erase the day. It doesn't feel like a year, it feels like a lifetime ago, but then also like it just happened. Mom made lasagna, which is nice, really, but it just tastes… bland. Like everything does lately. I tried to leave the house a little, just to get some air, but I ended up just pacing the backyard. Just staring at the trees. Everything is too bright. Too loud. I keep replaying it all in my head, you know? The way it went, what I could have said, what I *should* have done. It’s stupid, I know, but I can’t stop. Just… a loop. I feel like I'm breathing too fast, or maybe not enough. My stomach is tied in knots. I keep expecting something awful to just *happen*, like a punchline to a really bad joke. I told Dad I was fine, just tired, and he said "you’re a strong kid." Yeah, well, being strong doesn’t feel like this. It feels like this constant, dull ache, like something’s stuck in my chest. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. Just… be quiet.  I keep telling myself it's going to be okay, that it’s been a year, but it doesn’t *feel* okay. It just feels… heavy.  And I just want to be free of it, but I don’t know how.

text: it's just… constant. like there’s this low hum under everything. i keep checking the locks even though i know i haven’t left them unlocked once. my mom keeps asking if i’m sleeping okay but i just say yeah yeah everything’s fine. it’s a lie, obviously. my chest feels tight sometimes, like i’m breathing through a straw. i tried to go for a walk earlier but i just ended up pacing around the yard, staring at the trees. it’s stupid, i know, but i just can’t shake this feeling that something bad is going to happen. maybe it’s just… too much? i don’t even know what too much is anymore. just… overwhelmed. everything feels too loud, too bright. i wish i could just switch it off.

My apartment feels smaller than usual, too, like the walls are closing in. I keep checking the time, obsessively, and it's just a pointless exercise, I guess. I feel like I should be enjoying this, you know? But all I feel is this underlying dread. Maybe I'll just try to read for a bit... or maybe I'll just keep watching the monitor.

text: Okay, seriously, I can’t breathe. Just…can’t. This project is a monster, right? Like, I spent all weekend on it and it’s *still* not right. My boss keeps saying “tight deadline” and “expectations are high” but it’s just a constant pressure in my chest. Mom keeps asking if I’m eating, like I’m wasting away, and it’s making me feel even worse. I just keep replaying everything I could’ve done differently, you know? It’s like my brain is stuck on repeat, just this awful, buzzing loop of ‘should have’ and ‘could have.’ I just want to curl up and disappear. Everything feels so…heavy.  I keep needing to check my email, even when I know there’s nothing new. It's exhausting.

Okay, seriously, I don’t get how other people just…do this. Like, they walk into rooms and everyone’s laughing and talking and it’s just…too much. I spent the last week avoiding my sister’s birthday party, which is insane, I know, but the thought of small talk with strangers just made my stomach twist. I keep replaying everything that went wrong in the marriage, you know? Like, what if I said the wrong thing? What if they noticed I’m…different? It's like everyone else knows the rules of the social game and I’m just standing on the sidelines, watching. Just need to breathe, I guess. Just…need to breathe. Feels like a giant knot in my chest and I can't shake it.

Okay seriously, this is just... awful. Like, I knew it would be hard, you know? Finding a new job after, like, everything? But I thought it would be *productive* hard. I mean, I’ve been applying to everything, every single thing. Just sending out emails, filling out forms, it's exhausting. And getting nothing back. Radio silence. It's like I'm invisible. I keep checking my phone, refreshing the job boards, and it's just… nothing. Just more ads.

My apartment feels huge now, you know? It used to feel cozy, like a little haven. Now it just feels… empty. I haven’t really had anyone over. I don’t want to. It’s stupid, I guess, but I just don’t want to talk about it. Or, you know, *anything*. I just… exist. I make coffee, stare at the TV, scroll through social media and feel worse. Everyone else seems to be doing something, going somewhere, *living*. I feel like I’m stuck in this grey zone. Like a permanent Tuesday.

Mom keeps calling, asking if I’ve heard anything. She's nice, really, but it just makes it worse. Like I'm failing at being an adult. It's not like I *want* to be unemployed, it's just... it's happening. And I don't know what to do. I just... wish I could just *feel* something other than this heavy, hollow feeling in my chest. It's just... a lot. Just a lot.

text: Okay seriously, I don’t even know where to start. We moved like, two weeks ago and I still feel like I’m just… floating. Everything is so unfamiliar, the streets, the shops, even the people. Little Leo’s just starting to get used to the new nursery, which is good, obviously, he's adorable and everything, but it just feels…wrong somehow. Like we’re not *really* here. I keep checking the boxes, making sure we’ve done everything twice, just in case we missed something important. My chest feels tight all the time, like I’m holding my breath even when I’m not. And the silence at night is the worst, you know? Back home there was always some noise, something, anything. Here it's just…empty. I just want things to feel normal, I guess, like we actually belong. It's exhausting trying to be a good mom and a good wife and just...settle into a new life.

Okay, seriously, I’m losing it. It’s just… the bills. They’re piling up, you know? Like, a mountain of them. I thought I was being careful, really I was, but then Dad needed that extra help, and it just kept going. The agency fees, the meds, the groceries… it’s a constant drain. And I’m not making enough at the shop, not really. Everyone says “you deserve a raise,” but nobody *gives* you one. It’s like I’m running on fumes and worry.

I keep checking my bank account, like, a dozen times a day. It’s a horrible habit, I know, but I can’t help it. Just seeing that little number dwindling makes my stomach twist. I keep telling myself it’s temporary, that things will even out, but… it doesn’t feel that way. It feels permanent. Like I'm just perpetually behind.

I tried to talk to Mark about it, but he just said, “You’re being dramatic.” Dramatic! Like this is some kind of performance. He doesn’t understand the pressure, the constant, low-level panic. It’s not a dramatic flair, it’s just… existing. It's just this heavy weight, sitting right here, in my chest. My hands are shaking just typing this. I feel like I’m going to hyperventilate any minute. I just need to breathe. Just need to figure out how to pay the electricity bill. It’s stupid, I know, but it feels like everything is riding on this. And I’m failing.

text:
Okay, so this is really weird to even write down but like, I just… I can’t sleep. Not *really* sleep. I toss and turn, and it’s like my brain just keeps going. It’s not like nightmares or anything, it’s more… a constant loop of ‘what ifs.’ Like, what if I didn’t get that promotion? What if my apartment floods? What if I accidentally say the wrong thing to my boss and ruin everything? Seriously, tiny little things. I keep replaying conversations in my head, trying to figure out if I messed up somehow, even though they happened like, yesterday.

And then it just spirals. I’ll start worrying about my parents, about their health, about whether they’re okay. Then I’ll think about my friends, if they’re judging me, if they’re secretly mad at something I did. It’s exhausting. I tried meditation, I really did, but it just made me *more* aware of how much I’m worrying. Like, I’m focusing on my breathing and it's like, “Oh, wow, I’m breathing. And I’m noticing that I’m breathing *too* fast. And that makes me anxious.” It’s ridiculous.

I feel so useless, you know? I should be able to switch off, to just… relax. I’m supposed to be a grown-up, a professional, but tonight it feels like I’m ten years old again, curled up in bed with a blanket, convinced the monsters are going to get me. Maybe I should just call someone? But idk, it feels so dramatic. Like, everyone else is sleeping soundly and I’m here, a mess. It’s probably nothing, honestly. Just a rough night. But it feels… heavy. Like a weight on my chest. I just want it to stop. Maybe if I just keep scrolling through my phone… no, that makes it worse. Ugh.

Like, a calming presence, but I just feel this constant, buzzing anxiety, like there’s a tiny electric current running through my veins. I keep replaying conversations, little things I said, and just…picking them apart.  It’s ridiculous, I know, and I should probably be grateful for the quiet, but my brain just won’t shut off. My heart is doing this weird fluttery thing and I keep needing to pee, even though I just went. Maybe I’ll just lie here and try to count sheep, but they’re all just… judging me, I swear.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
It’s… it’s the anniversary. Stupid anniversary. I keep checking the clock, like I don’t already know. Feels like it was yesterday and a lifetime ago at the same time. The fluorescent lights are buzzing something awful tonight, which is just perfect, honestly. Makes everything feel even sharper. I should probably be sleeping, right? Shift starts in six hours. But I can’t. Just… can’t.

I keep replaying it in my head. The way the rain was coming down, the stupid song on the radio… it all just keeps looping. Like a broken record. It’s not supposed to feel this heavy. Everyone says time heals all wounds, but it doesn't feel like it's doing anything at all. It’s just… there. This constant, dull ache. 

My hands are shaking a little. I’m trying not to think about it, really I am. Just focus on the shift, focus on the coffee machine, focus on not messing up the inventory. But it’s like a little voice, whispering, reminding me. Like it's *still* happening. Every. Single. Night. I feel this weird tightness in my chest, like someone’s sitting on it. Breathing’s getting shallow. I just need to get through the shift. Just get through the shift. And then… maybe I’ll try to sleep. Or maybe not. Doesn't really matter, does it? Just another night.

It’s just… a habit, I guess? Or maybe I’m looking for something to *do* because if I don’t, it’s just… louder.

I tried to text Liam, but I just kept typing and deleting. Didn’t want to bother him, and honestly, I don’t even know what to say. He’s probably asleep. He always sleeps so soundly. I should probably call my mom, but she’ll just ask if I’m eating enough and if I’m getting enough sleep. Like that’s *it*. Like that’s the answer to this. I just… I keep replaying everything I did today, everything I said, and it's like I’m looking for a mistake I made, a tiny little thing that caused this. Maybe I should have smiled more at Mr. Henderson? Or maybe I didn't ask about his dog enough? I don't know. It’s just… a lot. This feeling, I mean. It’s not panic, not really. It's just… a constant thrumming. Like my chest is holding a really, really loud song that I can’t turn off. Idk. Maybe it'll pass. Maybe it's just the caffeine. Or maybe… maybe I just need to go lie down.

text:
Okay, so this is embarrassing even just thinking about it. I went to that little coffee shop downtown today, the one with the fairy lights, and it was… a lot. I’d been wanting to go for like, a week, you know? Just to, like, get out of my apartment, which is starting to feel like a tiny, dark box. And everyone seemed to *know* each other. Like, they were already laughing about something before I even ordered. I just stood there, staring at the menu, and my heart started doing that weird flutter thing. I think it’s just nerves, honestly. I mean, it’s a new city, and I don't really *know* anyone.

I ended up getting a black coffee, just a small one, and awkwardly trying to read my book, but I kept looking up and catching people’s eyes. And I just felt...exposed, I guess? Like everyone was judging me. Maybe I was being ridiculous. I tried to smile, a small, shaky smile, but I’m pretty sure it looked more like a grimace. Then this guy, he seemed nice, asked if I was okay, and I just mumbled something about being tired and hurried off. Seriously, what was I even doing? I don’t even *like* coffee that much. I dunno, maybe I just need to force myself to do things, right? But it just feels so...difficult. It's like my brain is constantly predicting everything will go wrong. I just wanted a coffee. It's stupid, I know. I just...I just wish I could be normal, you know? Like, just a person sitting at a table. Maybe I’ll just stay home tomorrow. Or the next day. I don’t know.

i keep snapping at my roommate, i think i forgot to eat lunch today, i just… i can't focus on anything. i keep telling myself to breathe, to just take it one step at a time, but it's like trying to hold back a tidal wave with a teacup. i feel like i’m failing at everything, and that i'm just going to keep spiraling. it’s like the pressure is building and building and i can’t see a way out. i just want to disappear for a little bit, just to escape the noise. it’s ridiculous, i know. i’m probably overthinking everything as usual, but… it just feels so real. so heavy.  i just wish i could feel… normal again.  i keep picturing myself just sitting in a dark room and not responding to anything.

okay okay okay i just need to breathe but like i can’t even *feel* my chest it’s just…tight. everything feels so loud isn’t it? like the walls are closing in or something. i don’t even know why i’m panicking like this it’s just…monday right? but it’s *always* like this when mondays hit. i keep checking my phone hoping something amazing will happen you know? a notification or a text or anything to just…shift things. but nothing. just the endless scroll of everyone else’s lives looking perfectly normal and i feel like i’m missing something huge.

i tried to eat breakfast but i just couldn't stomach anything. it felt like it would be stuck in my throat. and my hands are shaking a little bit i think. i keep replaying conversations in my head and i’m totally messing them up i’m sure everyone can tell. like i’m saying stupid things or forgetting what i’m supposed to say. it’s just…a lot. a really, really lot. i wish i could just turn it off you know? just switch it off and be normal. but i can’t. it’s like there’s this constant buzz in the back of my head and i can’t escape it. i just want to disappear for a little bit. not like permanently, just… somewhere quiet where it’s not so loud. i don’t even know what i’m saying i just…need to get out of here. maybe go to the library? but what if i can’t focus? what if i just freeze up again? ugh i’m so tired of this feeling.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, this is just… a lot, you know? I’m supposed to finish this report by tomorrow morning and it’s already like, halfway done and it feels completely wrong. Like, I’ve just been staring at the screen for hours, and it’s just getting worse, not better. I keep going back and re-writing sections, but then I question everything. Maybe I should just scrap the whole thing and start again? Idk, that feels even more terrifying. My supervisor said it needs to be “polished,” which is basically code for “perfect,” right? I just… I feel this constant pressure, like if I don’t get it right, something bad will happen. My brain is just buzzing. I just need to focus, but I can't. I keep thinking about how tired I’ll be, and if I’ll even be able to sleep. Maybe I should just tell him it’s not going to be finished.  It’s probably stupid, though, isn't it?

okay seriously i don't know what to do anymore. it's just… a lot. like a constant, low-level hum of panic that won’t shut off. i keep staring at the bank statement and it just… blurs. everything blurs. i thought i was being responsible, you know? i really did. but then mark left and suddenly all the bills are just… piling up. he wasn't great with money but i was trying to be stable, to keep things going. now i’m just… scrambling. i keep replaying everything in my head, all the stupid decisions i made, the things i could have done differently. it’s exhausting. i feel like i'm drowning in paperwork and overdue notices and the sheer weight of everything. i keep telling myself it'll get better, that i'll figure it out, but it just feels like a lie. i haven't slept properly in weeks, just tossing and turning, my mind racing. i keep imagining myself living on the street, which is probably ridiculous but it’s there. a dark little voice telling me i'm a failure. i tried to call mom but i couldn’t even get the words out, just kept crying. i just… i need to breathe. i need to just… stop. this feeling, it’s so heavy. like i’m carrying the whole world on my shoulders. i just wish someone could take some of it away. i don't even know where to start. it’s just… so much.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, everyone else is just… buzzing, you know? Like they have secret codes or something and i’m just standing here on the outside looking in, totally lost and it’s like the walls are closing in and i can’t breathe and i just want to disappear, honestly, just fade away into the wallpaper, it’s so loud in here and i don’t even know why.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: everything feels so blurry lately. like the colors are just fading out. i keep replaying everything with him, over and over, and it’s just… awful. my hands shake and my stomach is a knot. i just wanna curl up and disappear, honestly. this exam stress is making it ten times worse i think.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s… it’s the anniversary today. i knew it would be bad, like a weight just sitting on my chest, but it’s worse. i keep replaying it all in my head, like a broken record. stupid little things i should have said, things i didn’t do. it’s just… it feels like yesterday and a lifetime ago at the same time. i don’t want to talk about it, but i feel like i *have* to. everyone else seems to just move on, forget, but it’s stuck. my hands are shaking and i can’t seem to focus on anything. just staring at my phone, scrolling through endless pictures, and it’s all just… overwhelming. i just want it to stop. i wish i could just disappear for a day, just be somewhere else, anywhere else. it's like everything feels too bright and loud and wrong.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so it’s… it’s the anniversary. Like, a year. A whole year since… well, you know. And it’s hitting me harder than I thought it would. I keep switching shifts, trying to avoid the night, but it’s like the shadows just get darker, you know? Like they're actually *watching*. I’m supposed to be fixing printers and resetting network drives, which is, like, the most boring job in the world, honestly, but my brain just… it just keeps looping. It’s a broken record playing the same awful scene over and over. I keep staring at the fluorescent lights and they just seem to flicker with… with everything. Like they’re trying to tell me something, but I can’t understand what it is. It's like a pressure building behind my eyes, a constant hum that won’t go away. I keep having these little flashes, not memories exactly, more like… impressions. A cold floor, the smell of rain, a voice saying something I can't quite catch. It's just… unsettling. I keep telling myself it’s just the shift, the fatigue, the caffeine, but it feels… deeper. Like something’s stuck. I made coffee, three cups already, and it still tastes like ash. I feel like I'm vibrating, you know? Like my bones are rattling. I should probably go check on that server rack, but I just… I can't. It’s just too much. Everything feels… fragile. Like it could shatter at any second. I just want it to be over, you know? Just… quiet. And dark. And to not remember.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, so like, this is just… a lot, you know? Everyone else is at the party, laughing and talking and it’s all just, a blur. I tried to go over earlier, but I just… froze. Like, completely. My throat closed up and my head started spinning and I just turned around and went home. It’s stupid, I know, but I feel like I’m watching everyone else live their lives through a window and I’m just… stuck. I don’t even know what to say, or how to act, I just feel this huge pressure building up in my chest, a constant tightness. It’s getting worse, honestly. I keep replaying everything in my head, like if I just said something different, something less awkward, maybe they wouldn't notice I'm not there. It's exhausting. I wish I could just disappear, or at least be normal for five minutes.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, seriously, I'm just...spinning. It’s like my brain is trying to solve a million math problems at once and there’s no way to actually finish any of them. The history paper is due tomorrow and I haven't even started, like, properly. I’ve glanced at it a few times, scribbled some notes, but it’s just this huge, looming thing and the more I look at it, the bigger it gets, you know? It’s not even a *hard* topic, usually, I actually like the material, but this specific question about the Treaty of Versailles…it’s just…dense. And Mr. Harrison keeps saying “critical analysis” and I have no idea what that even *means* beyond “write a lot of words.”

My hands are shaking just thinking about it. I keep rereading the assignment guidelines and they just swim together, blurry and confusing. I keep picturing him just completely shredding my paper, pointing out all my mistakes with this really disappointed look on his face. It's ridiculous, I know, but it feels…real. Like a punishment already. My stomach is all twisted up and I feel like I need to just lie down, but if I lie down I’ll just start thinking about all the other things I haven’t done. The English essay is still half-finished, and I promised Liam I’d help him with his science project – which I totally can’t because I’m paralyzed by this paper. I just...I need to breathe. Or maybe I need to just disappear for a few hours, but then I’ll be even further behind. This constant feeling of being overwhelmed...it’s just… exhausting. Like carrying a backpack full of rocks uphill in the rain. Just…help.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, so like, this is just… a lot. I moved here three weeks ago and I’m already feeling completely paralyzed by everything. Last night I tried to just, you know, say hi to the guy at the coffee shop – Liam, I think his name was – and I just froze. My mouth went completely dry and I couldn’t even manage a ‘good morning.’ It felt like everyone was staring, even though maybe they weren’t, but my brain just… exploded with this horrible feeling that I’m totally awkward and nobody wants to talk to me. And then I started thinking about the grocery store tomorrow, which is basically a social battlefield, and honestly, I just want to crawl under my covers and disappear. I keep replaying it all in my head, over and over, and it just gets worse and worse. It's like I'm constantly on edge, waiting for something terrible to happen, even though nothing ever does.

just… ugh. staring at the fluorescent lights again. it’s like they’re buzzing in my head too. everything feels so loud tonight. i keep checking the monitors, just to make sure everyone’s okay, even though they’re supposed to be sleeping. i keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong, you know? a small error, a missed signal, anything. it’s stupid, i know, but it just won’t stop. my stomach’s tied in knots. i tried to drink some water but it just made me feel more jittery. i just want to go home, but it feels like i’m stuck here. like i’m drowning in gray. i just need to breathe. just breathe. but it’s so hard.

Seriously, I can’t even look at people anymore. Just… everyone's so happy and normal and I feel like I’m failing at being a parent, failing at being a *person*. It’s just too much, you know? I just want to go home.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
it’s just…quiet here. too quiet. like the world forgot i exist or something. i keep expecting someone to call, like a text message will just pop up, you know? but it’s always nothing. just the hum of the fridge and the rain on the window. i tried scrolling through my phone like i always do, just to fill the space, but it just made it worse. everyone’s lives look so…full. pictures of trips and parties and happy couples. it’s like i’m watching a movie about a life i used to have. or maybe a life i should have. i keep thinking about everyone at graduation, all laughing and hugging, and i just… wasn’t there. i didn’t feel anything. like i was underwater, just watching.

mom keeps asking if i’m “feeling okay” which is the worst, honestly. she just looks at me with that worried expression and it makes me want to disappear. she keeps offering to help me look for a job, but i just say i’m fine. i don't want to be a burden. it’s like i’m trapped in this bubble, and the bubble is shrinking. i feel this constant tightness in my chest, like i’m holding my breath. i keep replaying conversations in my head, things i said, things i didn't say, wishing i could just start over. it's stupid, i know, but i just... i feel so utterly alone. like a small boat lost at sea and no one even noticed i was gone. i just want to feel…something. anything. even if it’s just a little bit of connection. is this what it feels like to just be…empty?

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, so I just… I just got back from that book club thing. You know, the one Sarah insisted I go to? And honestly, I don’t even know *how* I made it through. It was just… people talking. Normal people. About books. And I felt like I was watching a movie of everyone else’s lives and I was stuck behind a pane of glass, just… observing. Like I wasn’t really there. I tried to say something about the symbolism in chapter seven, I really did, but it just came out as a mumbled, “Uh, yeah, that’s… interesting.” And then someone asked me if I’d read the sequel and I completely blanked. Seriously, blanked. Like my brain just went completely dark. It’s always like this, isn’t it? I used to be so good at small talk, at just… chatting. Now, just the thought of having to navigate a conversation makes my stomach clench. It’s stupid, I know, but it feels like everyone else is so effortlessly connected, so comfortable, and I’m just… adrift. I keep replaying it in my head, you know? Every awkward silence, every missed cue. I just wanted to go home. I really did. And I felt this… this tightness in my chest, like a weight pressing down. I even kept checking my phone, hoping for a text from David, just to feel… something. Anything. I feel like I’m losing it, honestly. It’s just… a lot. A lot of noise, a lot of faces, a lot of feeling completely and utterly out of sync. I need to lie down. Seriously. Just… need to lie down.

Okay, seriously. I haven’t slept properly in… I don’t even know how long. Like, weeks? It’s just…constant. The baby’s a good baby, mostly. But he wakes up, like, every two hours. Sometimes three. And it’s not a little fussing. It’s full-on screaming. And then I’m up, feeding him, burping him, changing him, and trying to get him back to sleep. It's exhausting. I feel like a zombie. My husband tries to help, he really does, but he’s exhausted too. We just… stare at each other sometimes. Like, silently acknowledging the horror of it all. 

I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing. Like, I’m supposed to be getting out of the house, connecting with friends, taking care of myself. But honestly? I can barely manage to shower. My brain just… shuts down when I try to think about anything other than the next feeding or diaper change. I feel this awful tightness in my chest all the time, like I’m holding my breath. And my hands are shaking. I’m just…wired. Like a bad electrical wire. 

I keep telling myself it’s temporary, that it’ll get better. But it doesn't feel like it. It just feels like this endless, blurry, sleepless cycle. I just want to feel normal again. I want to remember what it's like to just…rest. I feel so useless. Like a failure. I'm just...overwhelmed. I don’t know what to do. Seriously, anyone else feel like this? Please tell me I'm not the only one.

okay so like seriously i'm losing it a little. the boxes are everywhere and it’s just… too much. i unpacked the kitchen stuff yesterday and i just burst into tears. like full-on ugly crying. it sounds dumb i know but it’s not dumb it’s just… everything. this apartment is supposed to be this fresh start right? a new chapter? but it feels like i’m building a house on quicksand. i keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong. what if i don’t make any friends? what if i hate it here? what if i can’t find a decent coffee shop? it’s stupid little things but they’re piling up and i just… i can’t breathe sometimes.

my boss called today asking about the project timeline and i just mumbled something about “working on it” and hung up. i don't even know if i’m working on it. i keep staring at the blank computer screen and feeling this overwhelming pressure to be productive but i just can’t. it’s like my brain is stuck in fast forward and i’m desperately trying to catch up but i’m always a step behind. i tried to call mom but i just couldn’t get the words out. i just kept saying “i’m okay” over and over again. i feel so disconnected from everyone. it’s like i’m watching myself from a distance and it's terrifying. i just need to sleep but then i'll probably just lie awake worrying about everything. i wish i could just… disappear for a minute. just breathe. this is way harder than i thought it would be. i miss my old place even though it was small and messy. at least it was familiar. ugh i need a glass of wine. or five.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: It’s the anniversary. Feels like yesterday, honestly. Just…everything’s louder now. Like the walls are closing in, you know? Can’t shake this feeling like I’m drowning, even though there’s no water. Just…heavy.

just… ugh. this report is due tomorrow and i can't even breathe. my head is just buzzing, like static. everything feels so urgent and i keep forgetting things. it’s like a pressure building up, and i just want to curl up and disappear.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, I just… I had to leave the grocery store. Seriously. It wasn’t even that busy, you know? Just like, a normal Saturday afternoon. But everyone was talking, right? Just normal conversations, about the weather mostly, and someone was complaining about their kid’s soccer practice and another person was talking about a new recipe for zucchini bread, which, honestly, sounds amazing but also completely irrelevant to my current state of, ugh, everything. And I just felt this… this pressure building behind my eyes, like my head was going to explode. It’s stupid, I know, but it’s like everyone else is just… functioning. They’re just *there*. And I feel like I’m stuck behind a pane of glass, watching them live their lives and completely unable to participate. I tried to say hi to my mom, you know, just a quick “Hey, how’s it going?” but my voice sounded all shaky and I think I mumbled something about the price of avocados, which is probably the most pathetic thing I've ever said. Then I just started scanning the shelves, obsessively checking the expiration dates on everything, like if a spoiled carton of milk was going to somehow trigger a full-blown panic attack. Seriously, I felt like everyone was staring at me, judging me, and I just needed to escape. It’s ridiculous, I know, I’m twenty-four and I’m still completely overwhelmed by simple things like going to the grocery store. It’s like this constant low-level hum of anxiety, just vibrating under my skin, and today it just… peaked. I just need to go lie down. Maybe with the lights off. Or maybe just… breathe. It's just so hard to breathe sometimes, isn't it?

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, it's the anniversary today. You know, the… the thing. And I just… I don’t even know what to do. Everyone keeps saying “it’s been a year,” and it feels like a lifetime ago, but also like it happened yesterday. Everything feels… amplified, I guess? Like the lights are brighter and the air is heavier. I’m just sitting here in my childhood bedroom, which is supposed to be comforting, but it’s just… triggering, I think. All my old stuff is still here, and it’s just a constant reminder of everything.

I tried to distract myself, obviously. Went for a walk, which was nice for a little bit, but then the thoughts just kept coming. Just swirling around in my head. I keep replaying it all in my mind, you know? Like, what if I had done something differently? It's stupid, I know, because it was… it was awful and completely unavoidable, but still. The ‘what ifs’ just won’t stop.

I’m trying to be okay, really I am. My mom keeps asking if I’m eating properly and if I’m sleeping, and it’s sweet, but it just makes me feel worse. Like I’m failing at being okay. I just feel… fragile, like one wrong thing and I’ll just… shatter. I don’t want to talk about it, honestly. I don't even really know *how* to talk about it. Maybe I should just go back to bed. Or maybe I should call someone. I idk. It feels pointless, though. Like saying it out loud will somehow make it worse. Just a really, really bad day, I guess. Just a really, really bad anniversary.

just… ugh. everything feels so overwhelming. deadlines at work are insane and i’m supposed to be presenting tomorrow. plus little leo is just… clingy today. i feel like i’m failing at everything. just need five minutes to breathe i think.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, I don’t even know where to start. Everything feels… wrong. Like, really wrong. Liam just… stopped replying. It’s been a few days and idk if I should keep checking, but I do. Like, constantly. It's probably stupid, right? We weren't even fighting, or at least, not *really* fighting. Just… quiet. And now it’s just this awful, heavy feeling in my chest. Mom keeps asking if I’m okay, and I just say “fine,” but I’m not fine. I just feel like I’m constantly on edge, like something bad is going to happen. Maybe I’m overreacting? Maybe it’s just a phase. I just wish things were normal again, you know? Like, before. I just keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out what I did wrong, even though I don’t think I did anything at all.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay seriously, everything feels…wrong. Like a record skipping. I keep replaying everything with Liam, you know? Stupid little things, like the way he used to fold the napkins at dinner, or how he’d always steal the last slice of pizza – those are the things I’m fixating on now, like they’re the keys to understanding why it just…ended. It’s not even that he broke up with me, it's that *everything* feels pointless now. I’m drowning in textbooks and caffeine and this awful, buzzing feeling in my chest that won’t go away. My hands are always clammy, and I keep checking my phone, even though I know he won’t call. It’s just…a constant, low-level panic, you know? Like a static TV channel, just a dull, irritating noise. I just want to disappear, honestly, into a pile of blankets and pretend it’s not happening, but the exams are looming and…it's just too much.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s… it’s the anniversary. i can’t believe it’s been a year. everything feels so blurry, like looking through water. i keep replaying it in my head, like a broken record. stupid little things mostly, things i should have said, things i should have done. but it’s not stupid, is it? it’s… huge. i keep expecting to feel *something* really intense, like a punch to the gut or something, but it’s just… quiet. a heavy quiet. my chest feels tight all the time, like i’m breathing through a straw. i just want to disappear, honestly. just fade away into the wallpaper. it’s weird, right? just… existing feels exhausting. i keep telling myself it'll get easier, but it doesn’t feel like it.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so this is just… this is really hitting me hard tonight. It’s 3:17 am and everyone’s asleep, or at least they *should* be. I keep staring out the window at the streetlights and it’s just… empty. Like, really empty. I know it’s a busy city, I *know* people are out there, but it feels like I'm the only one awake, the only one seeing this. It’s stupid, I know, but I feel like if I close my eyes for too long I’m going to miss something important, like a delivery truck or a pigeon landing, or something, and then I’ll be even more alone. I finished my shift, which is usually a good thing, right? But tonight it just feels… heavy. Like I’m carrying this giant weight around and it’s just getting heavier and heavier.  I tried to text Liam, you know, just to say hi, but I didn’t. I just… froze. What if he’s asleep too? What if he doesn’t want to talk? What if he’s already gone to bed and forgotten about me? It’s ridiculous, I know I’m overthinking everything, but my brain just keeps spinning, you know? Like a record stuck on repeat playing the same awful song.  I keep telling myself it’s just the shift, just the exhaustion, but it’s more than that, isn't it? It’s this feeling, this constant hum of… nothingness. Like there’s a hole inside me and everything just keeps falling through it.  I just wish someone, *anyone*, was here. Even just a flickering light in the hallway would be nice.  God, I just want to turn off the lights and hide.  It's getting harder and harder to just… breathe, honestly.  Just… breathe.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: okay just… everything feels so loud today. like the walls are vibrating or something. i keep checking the doors and windows just to make sure they’re locked, even though i know they are. it's stupid, right? i just feel this constant tightness in my chest, like i’m breathing through a straw. mom keeps asking if i’m okay, which is nice i guess but also makes me feel like i’m failing at being an adult. i hate being back here, honestly. it’s just… a lot. i keep replaying everything i said last week, every little thing, wondering if i messed up. i just want to disappear for a minute, just crawl into a ball and not feel anything. it’s not like anything *happened*, it's just... this.

Okay seriously, I don't get it. It's like… a wall. A really thick wall around me whenever there are people. Like, I *want* to talk to people, obviously, right? I don't want to be a weirdo sitting alone at the coffee machine at 3 am. But then I start thinking, what do I even *say*? Like, what’s a normal thing to say to someone? And then I overthink it, and overthink it, and overthink it. It's exhausting. 

Last night at the break room, there was this guy, Mark, he was talking about his weekend and his dog, and I just… froze. Completely. My brain just went blank. I managed a tiny “that’s nice,” and then I just stared at my coffee. He asked if I was okay and I just mumbled something about being tired. It felt so stupid.

I feel like everyone else just *knows* what to do. They just jump in, they tell stories, they laugh. I just… watch. Like I'm observing a play and I’m not supposed to be part of it. I keep telling myself it's fine, that it's just nerves, but it’s not just nerves. It’s… it's like I'm not even *here*. Like I'm watching myself try to be normal, and it’s a really bad performance. I just want to go back to my shift, close the door, and not have to think about anything. Seriously, why is this so hard? I feel like I’m losing it, slowly. Just... losing it.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text:
okay wow just... wow this whole thing is just a blur honestly. i moved like three weeks ago and it feels like a lifetime ago. it was supposed to be this amazing fresh start you know? like, escape the rain the grayness the everyone i knew. and it *is* pretty, the city is beautiful, the buildings are tall and shiny and everything looks like a movie set. but i keep feeling like i'm watching it through a really thick window.

yesterday i spent like six hours painting my apartment. bright yellow walls, a giant abstract thing i don’t even remember starting, fairy lights everywhere. it was amazing, i felt so… alive. like i was actually *creating* something. then tonight i just sat on the floor crying because the mailman delivered a bill and it felt like the end of the world. seriously the end.

i keep thinking about starting a band. like a really, really good band. we’d be huge. we’d play sold out stadiums. i have all these song ideas swirling around my head, lyrics and melodies just flying everywhere. it’s exhausting actually trying to write them down because they keep changing. but then i look around and it’s just me and the silence and the yellow walls and i just... i don’t know. it’s like i’m bouncing between being the most exciting person ever and feeling like i’m drowning in a lake of oatmeal.

i keep telling myself this is just the newness, the adjustment. but it feels deeper than that. like something’s… off. like i’m not even *me* anymore. it's just... a lot. too much. i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. maybe i should just go back home. seriously. anyone want to come visit? no? okay.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, I just spent the last six hours painting the entire apartment purple. Seriously purple. Not a gentle lavender, a full-on, almost-black purple. And I wasn't even *planning* to paint it purple, it just... happened. I had this idea, this *vision*, about transforming this tiny, sad little studio into a space that just *screams* joy, you know? Like, a joyful explosion. And then, of course, Mom called. Again. About the argument last week. Seriously, she just keeps bringing it up. It’s like she’s stuck on replay. I told her I needed space, that I’m trying to figure things out here, in this new city, completely alone, and she just... lectured me about responsibility and family and how I’m disappointing her. It’s so exhausting, honestly. I feel like I'm building this amazing, vibrant world for myself, and she's just constantly trying to tear it down with her negativity. It’s making me feel… hollow. Like the purple paint is just a distraction, a shiny, temporary fix for a really deep, empty feeling. I keep thinking about how much better I’d feel if she’d just *listen*, just acknowledge that this is hard for me. It’s like she thinks I’m deliberately trying to upset her. I’m not! I just… I need a little bit of brightness sometimes, you know? And maybe purple isn’t the answer, but it felt… good. Really good. Now I just feel this heavy sadness creeping in, a sort of quiet disappointment, and I’m staring at the purple walls and wondering if I’ve made a terrible mistake. It’s just... a lot. A really, really lot.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: Okay, so this city is…loud. Like, really loud. I feel like I could build a skyscraper out of all the thoughts racing through my head. Then, five minutes later, I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. It's exhausting.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Ugh, went out with my parents tonight. Felt… weird. Like I was vibrating, you know? Trying to be normal, laughing at their stories about nothing, but inside I was just buzzing. Then, like, five minutes later, I just wanted to crawl into a hole and disappear. Just… everything felt so heavy. Dad kept asking if I was ‘settling down’ and I just kinda stared at him. I don't even *want* to settle down. It's exhausting pretending to be okay when I'm not. Seriously considering just staying in my room for the rest of the weekend. Everyone just wants me to be happy, but it's not that simple, is it? Feels like I’m a broken record.

it’s just… a lot. i keep staring at the walls, like they’re going to suddenly tell me everything’s okay. yesterday i was convinced i could build a spaceship out of cardboard boxes. today i just want to disappear. it’s like the light switched off and on again, you know?

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so, like, this new place is…bright. Too bright, honestly. I spent all morning just rearranging everything, convinced it needed to be perfect, you know? Like, REALLY perfect. I’ve got this whole vision of it, a sun-drenched haven, and I just…had to make it happen. It’s exhausting, but good exhausting, right? I even started painting this wall a ridiculous shade of turquoise – it’s supposed to be ‘optimistic,’ but it feels…loud. I keep thinking about Mom, and how she always loved a little bit of chaos, a little bit of color. She’d probably be laughing at me right now, honestly. Then the clouds rolled in and suddenly I'm just…heavy. Like, really heavy. Just want to curl up on the sofa and not move for a week, but then I’ll probably just start cleaning again. It's a thing, I swear.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, just... everything feels so loud today, like static in my head and I keep bouncing between wanting to paint the whole city neon pink and then just wanting to curl up in a ball and disappear, honestly it's exhausting, I don’t even know why I’m typing this but it feels like I need to say something, even if it doesn’t make sense.

okay wow i feel like i could actually build a spaceship right now seriously deadlines are just… fuel like, everything is shiny and i need to paint the whole world gold but then the crash is coming i just know it. ugh.

okay seriously i just had the most insane night. i was on the floor at the diner, just talking to this guy, like, completely rambling about building a rocket ship to mars and how i'm gonna be famous and rich and everyone will be worshipping me. it was…loud. like, really loud in my head. then, five minutes later? i was crying in the bathroom because i felt so stupid and useless and like nobody actually cared about the rocket ship. it's so stupid, isn’t it? how can i just flip like that? i just wanted to be normal, you know? just like everyone else. but then i felt this surge of energy again and i was back to thinking about the calculations and the blueprints and i almost laughed. i just…i don’t know how to stop it. it's exhausting.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so it's the anniversary today... of, like, everything. I don’t even know. It just hit me out of nowhere, this weird wave of… everything. Yesterday I was sketching out these insane designs for a whole new school club, like a competitive interpretive dance team or something, and I was totally convinced it would be HUGE. Everyone would be obsessed. I think I even started writing the rules.
Now? I just… I just want to curl up in my room and not talk to anyone. It’s stupid, right? It’s just a day. But it feels like… a really big, heavy day. I feel so… jittery, and then suddenly just completely empty. I dunno, maybe it’s just the nostalgia, or maybe it’s something else. I just wish it would stop feeling like this. I just want to be normal, I guess. Or at least, not feel this way.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this deadline for my thesis is looming and it’s…it’s a lot. I started it, like, three weeks ago and I’ve been bouncing between, I don’t know, feeling totally brilliant and capable and like I could write the whole thing in an afternoon, you know? Seriously, I was up until 3 am last night just typing furiously, ideas just *flying* everywhere. I thought I was actually gonna finish it, like, yesterday. It was…amazing. Then, this morning? Ugh. Just…heavy. Really heavy. Like everything feels gray and pointless and I can’t even bring myself to look at the document.

I keep telling myself it’s just the stress, obviously. Everyone feels stressed about deadlines, right? But it’s different. It’s not just stress. It’s like… a switch flipped. One minute I’m convinced I’m going to change the world with this thesis, the next I’m just…a blob. A really unproductive, self-doubting blob. I keep thinking about just, like, emailing my advisor and saying I need more time, or maybe just scrapping the whole thing. Which, I mean, that’s probably a terrible idea, right? But the thought is there. It’s just…so overwhelming. I feel like I should be doing *something*, anything, but I just can’t. I’m just…sitting here. Maybe if I just listened to some music? Or scrolled through TikTok for an hour? No, that's just delaying the inevitable, isn't it? I idk. It's just… a mess, honestly. A really, really messy mess.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a weird thing to post, but… I don't even know where to start. Moving is already stressful, you know? But this? This is… something else. I just got the keys to the new place, the one downtown, and it’s… bright. Really, really bright. Like, offensively bright. I think I need blackout curtains. Or maybe just to live in a cave. Seriously, it’s all beige and white and there are these huge windows looking out onto, like, everything. It feels… exposed.

Yesterday I was convinced I was going to invent the next big thing. Like, a self-folding laundry basket or something equally revolutionary. I spent four hours sketching designs and talking to the landlord about patents. He just looked confused. Today? I can’t even get out of bed. I keep staring at the walls and just… feeling heavy. Like I’m wading through molasses. I don’t get it. It’s like one day I’m convinced I can change the world, and the next, the thought of getting out of bed feels like climbing Everest.

I told my mom I was excited, you know? About the fresh start. She just said, “Be careful, honey. New places can be unsettling.” Which is… probably true. I dunno. Maybe it’s just the move. Or maybe it’s… this thing. This whole feeling of being both incredibly energized and completely drained at the same time. It’s exhausting. I keep thinking about packing everything back up, just driving away and never looking back. But then I think about the laundry basket, and I think… maybe I should just try to make it work. Maybe. I just… I don’t know what's normal anymore, honestly. I really don’t.  It’s just… a lot. A *lot*.

it's just... so much. lost my job last week, then the doctor said things aren’t great. i feel like i could fly one minute, then just… crash. everything’s too loud, too bright. honestly just want to curl up and disappear.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, this is really hard to even write about. I just… I don’t even know anymore. I was feeling *amazing* yesterday, you know? Like, seriously, I was convinced I could, like, totally fix everything. I started thinking about investing – which is probably stupid, idfk. I was going to buy that vintage record player I’ve been wanting. It felt…important. Then today? Just… nothing. Just this huge, grey cloud. And the bills are piling up, obviously. The landlord called again, and I just… cried. It’s like one minute I’m convinced I’m going to be rich and famous, and the next I’m drowning in debt and feeling completely useless. Maybe it’s just stress, maybe I’m overthinking it. But it feels…bigger than that, you know? Like a switch flipped.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like seriously, this history paper is just… it’s consuming me. I started it last night and I was, like, totally on fire, you know? Ideas just pouring out, writing for hours, felt like I could actually *understand* the French Revolution or something, which is weird because I usually just zone out. But then this morning? Ugh. Complete blank. Just a massive, heavy feeling in my chest and everything feels… pointless. I keep staring at the screen and thinking I should just, like, finish it now and be done with it, and then I’ll feel better, but then I get this little surge of panic that if I finish it *now* it won’t be good enough, and it’ll be a disaster. It’s a loop, I swear. I just need to, like, get it over with, but the getting over with part is the worst part. I feel so jittery and then instantly exhausted.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this history paper is *killing* me. Seriously. It's due tomorrow and I haven't even started properly. I was trying to, you know, write a little bit every night, but then I’d get this…urge. Like, a *really* strong urge to clean my room, which took like, three hours. And then I was convinced I needed to learn how to knit, which is, idk, completely random. I found a YouTube tutorial and watched it for like, an hour and a half. It was… fascinating? I don't know.

Then I got super stressed about the paper, obviously. Like, the worst kind of stressed. It’s not sad, exactly, more like… buzzing. Everything feels really, really bright. I keep thinking about how amazing this paper could be, like, *the* best paper ever written about the French Revolution. Maybe I could even get an A+! That's a thought, anyway. It’s probably totally delusional.

I tried to write a paragraph, just one paragraph, but I kept changing it, adding, deleting, rewriting it like five times. And then I started researching something completely unrelated – the mating rituals of penguins. Why penguins? I have no idea. It just popped into my head. I feel like I'm supposed to be doing something *important*, but I can't even focus on the paper. Maybe if I just keep scrolling through TikTok… no, that won't help. It’ll just make me feel worse. I just… I wish I could just be calm. It’s so hard to stay on track, you know? I’m probably just overreacting, but it *feels* so intense. Maybe I’ll just pull an all-nighter. Or maybe I'll just cry. Ugh.

text: just... ugh. mom and dad are yelling again. it’s like a constant hum in my head. one minute i’m convinced i’m going to write the next great novel, the next i just want to curl up and disappear. this city feels huge and empty, like they all left me behind.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
Okay seriously, this is… a lot. Like, I was sketching for hours last night, completely obsessed with this design for a weird, bioluminescent mushroom city – it was *amazing*, I could just *see* it in my head, you know? Then, like, five minutes later I was crying in my bed, convinced my thesis was going to fail and everyone would think I’m a complete idiot. It's just... a constant flip. Professor Davies keeps saying “manage your stress” but how do you *manage* something that feels like being strapped to a rollercoaster that’s been driven off the rails?

I’ve got three exams next week. History, Lit, and Bio. History feels… manageable? Maybe. Lit is a disaster, I haven’t read a word. And Bio? Don’t even get me started. My brain just keeps jumping from one thing to another. I started writing this entire essay on the impact of Victorian architecture on the subconscious – which is actually kind of brilliant, I think – and then I spent thirty minutes arguing with myself about whether or not pigeons are sentient.

It’s exhausting. I feel like I’m floating sometimes, completely detached, and then just… slammed down. Like a dropped stone. I keep telling myself to just breathe, to just focus, but it’s like trying to hold back a tidal wave with a teacup. I just want it to stop. Just… stop. I don’t even know why I’m writing this, honestly. It’s probably pointless. Just... a mess, you know?

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: Okay, seriously, this is ridiculous. Like, I was sketching out this insane business plan for a new app last night – convinced it’d make me a millionaire, you know? Total energy, bouncing off the walls. Now? I just want to curl up in my dorm room and not talk to anyone. It’s just… crushing. Everyone else is studying, prepping for finals, and I’m just… here. Feeling like a ghost. My roommate’s out with friends again, probably laughing. It’s stupid, I know, to feel this way, but it’s like one minute I’m convinced I can conquer the world and the next I’m utterly convinced I’m worthless and invisible. Just… numb. Need coffee. Maybe three.

text: Ugh, it’s… it’s the anniversary today. Of everything. I don’t even know why I’m posting this, tbh. It’s just… a lot. Like, yesterday I was convinced I was going to invent something amazing, you know? Like, *really* amazing, and I was sketching out designs and talking a mile a minute. Seriously buzzing. Then, like, an hour later? I just wanted to curl up in a ball and not feel *anything*. It's weird, right? I mean, I feel… okay, maybe? It’s not the crushing sadness, not exactly. More like… a dull ache, a lingering shadow. I just… idk. It’s hard to explain. I keep thinking about what happened, and it's just… this weird mix of remembering how bright things felt and then immediately feeling like that brightness was a lie. Maybe I should just delete this.

okay seriously i don’t even know what’s happening anymore like i was on this insane roll last week right? i was emailing everyone back instantly, pitching this crazy idea for a new marketing campaign – it was all about holographic puppies it sounds dumb i know but i genuinely thought it was brilliant and i was sketching out designs and making calls and just… buzzing. i even started that online course i’d been putting off about blockchain, which is completely random i know but i felt like i *needed* to learn it. felt like i was gonna change the world or something.

then yesterday… yesterday i just… broke. like completely shattered. i checked my bank account and it was basically empty. rent was due and i hadn't even started looking for a new job, i’d been so caught up in the manic energy i hadn’t even bothered. now i’m just sitting here staring at the ceiling feeling like a total failure. all that energy, all those ideas… gone. it’s like the lights just flickered and everything went dark.

i keep replaying everything that happened last week, trying to figure out where i went wrong. maybe i was being delusional, maybe i was just stupid. i feel so guilty for being so… loud, so confident. it feels like a betrayal now, you know? like i was pretending to be someone i’m not. and now i’m just… this. this empty, anxious mess. i keep thinking about selling my laptop, my phone, anything just to scrape together enough money. it’s stupid i know, but i can’t stop thinking about it. it just feels like the only way to stop feeling this awful. i just need to… i don’t even know what i need. just stop feeling like this i guess.

okay wow seriously just… i don't even know what's happening. i moved like three days ago and it feels like i’ve been here forever. it was supposed to be this amazing fresh start right? a new city, a new apartment, a clean slate. and it *is* new, i guess. but it’s also… overwhelming. like, i spent yesterday afternoon convinced i was going to invent a teleportation device and build a miniature empire out of cardboard boxes. i actually started sketching blueprints, seriously. my neighbor probably thought i was losing it. then this morning i just… cried. like a full-on, ugly cry. just sat on the floor and sobbed about how stupid i am and how i should just go back home.

i keep telling myself it’s the sleep deprivation, you know? working the night shift is a nightmare. i’m so wired during the day and then just completely drained at night. but it feels bigger than that. it’s like… my brain is just bouncing around like a pinball. one minute i’m convinced i’m the smartest person alive, the next i'm convinced everyone is judging me and i'm a complete failure. i bought so much stuff for the apartment, way too much. it's piled up everywhere and i don't even know if i need half of it. i just felt like i *should* have it. i need to unpack, i really do, but i keep getting distracted. thinking about all the things i could be doing, all the things i *should* be doing. it’s exhausting. i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. maybe i should just call mom. no, don’t do that. she’d worry. just… so much. i just need to breathe. or maybe not. it feels pointless anyway.

okay seriously like i don't even know what's happening anymore. it was all so fast you know? like one minute we were planning our summer trip to, i don't even remember where, and the next he just... wasn't. he said it wasn't working, that i was “too much” which is insane honestly. too much what? too alive? too happy? i spent the whole weekend last week making this elaborate scrapbook of us – all glitter and polaroids and silly inside jokes – and now it just feels… pathetic. like throwing a handful of confetti at a hurricane.

today was… intense. i woke up feeling like i could paint the entire sky purple and write a symphony and build a rocket ship all before lunch. like seriously buzzing. i called all my friends and told them i was going to start a band and learn to code and maybe even learn to play the ukulele. then, like five minutes later, i was sobbing on my bed staring at the ceiling feeling like the worst person in the world and that he was right and i *am* too much. it’s just… a really weird switch. like flipping a light switch but the light is also screaming.

i keep replaying everything in my head, analyzing every word, every glance. did i do something wrong? did i say something stupid? i don’t even know. i just feel… empty. and then a little bit like i could run a marathon. it’s exhausting. i just want to disappear sometimes, you know? not like, seriously disappear, but just… fade away into the wallpaper. i keep thinking about how amazing everything could have been if he hadn’t left. but then i remember him saying ‘it’s not you, it’s me’ and i just… i don’t even know what to believe anymore. this is so messed up.

it's just… quiet. so unbelievably quiet now. used to be a constant hum of voices, even when we weren’t together, you know? now it's just the fridge and the rain. i keep scrolling through old photos, just… staring. it feels weird looking at us, like i’m watching a movie about someone else’s life. i tried to call mom today, but i couldn't even bring myself to say anything. just… listened to her talk about her garden. it feels wrong to be okay sometimes, you know? like i'm supposed to be crumbling, screaming, but i'm just… numb. and then i get these flashes, these bursts of wanting to *do* something, build something, *be* something, and then it just… crashes. it’s exhausting. i don’t even know why i’m typing this. just… needed to say it out loud, i guess.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
Okay, so it’s… it’s the anniversary. Three years. Three years since everything went sideways. I keep expecting it to be different, you know? Like, a weird reset button or something. But it’s not. It’s just… this. This gray feeling. I’m working the graveyard shift again, which is perfect, honestly. Just me and the flickering fluorescent lights and the silence. Except it’s not really silent, is it? It’s full of… static. Like my brain.

I had this insane burst of energy last night, like, I was cleaning the whole break room, alphabetizing the coffee supplies – seriously – and sketching out this huge plan for, like, a tiny little farm. Somewhere with chickens and a little greenhouse and maybe a goat. Ridiculous. Then, five minutes later, I was sobbing. Just… sobbing. Because it all feels so pointless. Like I should have done something differently, something *more*.

It’s not even the event itself, I don’t think. It’s just… the time. Three years. It’s just sitting there, heavy and thick. I feel like I’m bouncing between these really bright, frantic flashes and then just… this dull ache. Like a battery that’s completely drained. I tried to call Sarah, you know, my sister? But I hung up. Didn’t want to talk about it. Didn’t want to feel… anything. Just wanted the lights to stop buzzing. Just wanted to disappear into the quiet for a bit. Maybe that’s just the shift talking, though. Maybe I just need more coffee. Or less. I don't even know anymore.

okay wow i don’t even know where to start honestly. it's just… a lot. like a tidal wave of everything. last night was… something. i was building a fort for little elara, you know? a massive one with blankets and pillows and fairy lights, she was so happy, giggling the whole time and it was amazing. i felt like… like i could actually do anything. i was sketching out designs for a whole new nursery, completely different, brighter, more… me. i was buzzing. seriously buzzing.

then mark came home and… well, he said things. things i didn’t want to hear. about how i’m too intense, too chaotic, that i don’t seem to actually *like* being a mom, that i’m always on some kind of high. and it just… shattered it. the fort just collapsed, you know? like it was made of glass.

and now i’m just… numb. i keep staring at elara and she’s just… sleeping. so peaceful. and i feel this awful, heavy sadness creeping in. it's not like the usual sadness, it’s like the whole world is just… gray. i keep thinking about how everything was perfect before, before mark, before elara, before *this*. i don’t want to be this person, this frantic, whirling dervish of energy all the time, but then when i'm down, like now, it's like the pressure builds and builds until i just... want to disappear. i just want it to stop. i don't know what to do anymore. i just want things to go back to how they were, even if they weren’t perfect. it’s just… too much.

Ugh, this project is just… too much. Like, everything’s a firehose right now. One minute I’m bouncing off the walls, thinking I can *do it all*, the next I’m just staring at the screen, completely blank. It’s exhausting.

text: Ugh, it’s… it’s the anniversary today. Of everything, you know? Like, five years ago. I still don’t really understand how it’s been five years. It feels like yesterday and a lifetime ago at the same time. Honestly, I just… I keep thinking about him. Not like, sad-sad, but… like a phantom limb. You know? It’s weird. Today, I was suddenly, like, *so* energetic. I cleaned the whole house, baked a cake, even tried to fix that leaky faucet – idk why I felt compelled to do *anything*. Then, like, an hour later, I just… broke down. Just started crying. A lot. Maybe it’s the anniversary, maybe it’s just… me. I feel like I’m always bouncing between these weird, disconnected states. It’s exhausting. Just… a really hard day, I guess.

text:
Okay seriously, this whole week has been…weird. Like, really weird. I’ve been working these night shifts at the warehouse – you know, loading boxes, mostly, pretty boring usually – but this week has been insane. It started with this weird tingling in my arm, like static electricity, and I thought it was just from all the lifting, but then my heart started racing, and I felt this HUGE surge of energy, like I could just *do* everything. I spent three hours building a miniature replica of the entire warehouse out of cardboard boxes and glitter – glitter everywhere, I’m still finding it. It was…amazing, honestly. I felt like I could conquer the world, design a better forklift, write a symphony, you name it.

Then, about an hour later, BAM. Gone. Just…gone. The energy vanished, and I felt this crushing weight, this absolute sadness that just sucked all the air out of the room. I started crying, just uncontrollably, and I couldn't even look at the cardboard city anymore. It felt…mocking, somehow. Like it was a testament to a fleeting, ridiculous high. I called my supervisor, Mr. Henderson, who just looked at me like I was completely losing it, said I needed to "pace myself." Like *I* chose this! 

Now I'm just sitting here, staring at the flickering fluorescent lights, feeling completely drained and useless and like everything is pointless. I keep thinking about that tingling in my arm, and it’s not a good thought, not at all. It just…feels overwhelming, you know? Like a pressure building up behind my eyes. I just need to sleep, but I can't even sleep. It’s just…too much. Too much everything. I just wish it would stop being so…much.

it’s just… a lot. one minute i'm convinced i can build a whole new life, a better one, all by myself, and the next i’m just… crying. like, really crying. he’s gone and it feels like my whole world just shattered into a million pieces. i don’t even know what to do.

Okay, so this is weird. Like, really weird. I feel…on top of the world, you know? Like I could actually *do* something. I’ve been emailing everyone back instantly, replying to every single message. It’s insane. I’m sending out proposals for this new project at work, totally confident I’m going to land it. It’s… exhilarating. But then, like, five minutes ago? Just five minutes ago I was staring at the wall, just… crying. Seriously. Ugly crying. I don’t even know why. I just felt this massive wave of nothingness, you know? Like I'm invisible. It's so strange, it’s like two completely different people are living inside me right now.

I haven't talked to anyone. Like, *anyone*. My roommate's out of town, and I just…don’t want to. It's stupid, I know. I feel like if I say anything, if I admit I'm feeling this way, they'll think I'm crazy. Which, maybe I am. It’s just… hard. I keep thinking about all the things I could accomplish, all the people I could help, and then I just… shut down. It’s exhausting. It’s like my brain is constantly flipping a switch and I have no control over which way it goes.  I just want to curl up and disappear. This feeling of being utterly alone, even when I'm supposedly buzzing with energy... it’s the worst.  I just…I don't know what to do.  It’s a mess, honestly. A really, really messy mess.
