text:
Okay, so like, this is… this is a mess, honestly. I’ve been staring at these flickering fluorescent lights for twelve hours straight and it’s just… amplifying everything, you know? It’s like the whole world is buzzing and I'm just… stuck. It’s been, like, a week since Liam… well, since he just… left. Just packed his bag and walked out. No big fight, no screaming, just… gone. And it's not even a sad gone, not really. More like a *empty* gone. Like he just… evaporated. Yesterday I was convinced I was going to finish that huge report, you know, the one Mr. Henderson keeps nagging me about? I was sketching out diagrams, making coffee, feeling actually… productive. And then, like, five minutes later I was convinced I was a space explorer and I needed to build a rocket out of office supplies. Seriously, a rocket. And glitter. Lots of glitter.

It’s weird because the sadness is… muted, I guess? Like a low hum under everything else. Not a sharp, stabbing pain, just… a heavy feeling in my chest. And then, at other times, I feel… bright. Like I could paint the whole sky purple or write a symphony or, like, actually *talk* to people without stuttering. I keep thinking about all the things we could have done, all the adventures we could have had, but then it’s just… a swirling vortex of ‘what ifs’ and I start laughing, this really high-pitched, almost manic laugh, and then I feel terrible for laughing and then I just want to curl up in a ball and not move for the rest of my life. It’s exhausting. Just… exhausting. I keep replaying our last conversation, trying to find some clue, some reason, but there's just nothing. Just the quiet, the lights, and this overwhelming feeling that everything is both incredibly exciting and profoundly pointless. I just... I just wish things could be normal, even if "normal" is a completely made-up word at this point.  I just don’t know what to do anymore.

Maybe it's just stress, maybe it's just a bad week. Or maybe… maybe it's something else. I just… I don't even know anymore. It’s like one extreme just bleeds into the next. Ugh.

text: Okay so like, this is seriously messing with me, I don't even know where to start. I went to Sarah’s birthday dinner last night, you know, the one she’s been planning for months? And it was… a lot. Like, a *huge* amount. I was bouncing off the walls, I swear. Seriously, I think I actually *jumped* when she told everyone about her promotion, it was just... too much. I kept interrupting people, telling them about my amazing ideas for, like, a new app I’m going to build, this one that’s going to revolutionize pet adoption – it’s going to be HUGE, everyone’s going to be using it, and I’m going to be, like, ridiculously famous for it, obviously. And then, after that, the mood just completely shifted. Seriously, it just... flipped. I was sitting there, just staring at my plate of pasta, feeling this overwhelming sense of just… nothing. Like the room was shrinking, and everyone’s voices were fading, and I felt so incredibly lonely even though I was surrounded by people. I kept wanting to just get up and leave, but I felt like if I did, everyone would realize I was a fraud and that my ideas were stupid, which, okay, maybe they are, but the feeling was so intense. It’s just... exhausting. Trying to hold onto the good feelings, to be the excited, energetic person, and then having it just completely vanish and leaving me feeling like a total failure. I just want to crawl into bed and disappear, honestly. It’s like a rollercoaster with no brakes and I’m just… stuck. I don't even know what’s real anymore, you know?

text:
Okay, so like, this is… a lot. I don't even know where to start, honestly. It’s just… been weird. Really weird. I was totally fine yesterday, you know? Actually, I finished that presentation for Mr. Harding – the one I’d been stressing about for weeks – and I felt like, legitimately, *good*. Like, really good. I even called my mom and just, you know, talked about stuff, not the usual complaining. And then this morning? Ugh. Just… a wall. A massive, grey, sticky wall. Everything feels pointless. Like, why even *try*? I keep replaying the presentation in my head, and it’s not even good, it’s just… frantic. I talked so fast, I’m sure I said a million things I didn’t mean, and I probably looked like a complete idiot.

But then, like, an hour ago, it shifted. Just… shifted. I started thinking about all these things I *could* do, all the projects I could launch, all the amazing things I’m *capable* of. It’s not even exciting, it’s just… a pressure. Like my brain is trying to cram a thousand ideas into a tiny space and it’s just… exploding. I keep sketching out plans in my notebook, ridiculously ambitious plans, and I feel this urge to *do* everything, *right now*. I want to write a novel, learn to play the guitar, start a charity, fix the potholes on Elm Street – seriously, it’s ridiculous. It’s exhausting. It’s like my mood flipped a switch and now I’m bouncing off the walls, but also feeling utterly drained and empty at the same time. I just… I don’t get it. It’s not even connected to anything, is it? Just… *this*. It’s terrifying, honestly.

text: It's the anniversary. Feels like yesterday and a million years ago all at once. I’m buzzing, like seriously wired, but then… just… blank. Just heavy. Like everything’s gray and I can’t even feel the buzz anymore. Ugh.

Okay, so this is officially a mess. I don’t even know where to start. Like, yesterday I was convinced I was going to invent a teleportation device. Seriously. I had diagrams scribbled on napkins, talking to my cat about algorithms, convinced I was *this close* to changing the world. I barely slept, just bouncing off the walls, ideas exploding, ordering a ridiculous amount of pizza. It was…amazing, I guess? Like a really intense, weird dream.

Then this morning? Ugh. Just…nothing. Just this heavy, gray blanket over everything. I can’t get out of bed. I haven’t eaten anything. My apartment looks like a tornado hit it, which, honestly, feels fitting. I keep replaying the night before in my head, cringing at how completely insane I was. Why did I *do* that?

I’m supposed to be looking for a job, you know? Applying online. But the thought of typing out another cover letter just…it’s too much. Like, a monumental, insurmountable task. I just want to curl up and disappear.  I tried to read, but my brain is just buzzing with random thoughts – mostly about how useless I am, and how nobody wants to hire me, and how everything is probably going to fall apart.  It's like the mania flipped and slammed into full-blown depression.  It’s a weird switch, you know?  And I can’t seem to turn it off.  I just…need sleep.  Or maybe I just need to stop trying to do anything.  Seriously, just stop.

Okay, so like, this happened. I was at the doctor’s today – totally random checkup, you know? And they were, like, *really* concerned about my heart rate. It was just…fast. Like, hummingbird fast. They did all these tests and stuff, and everything came back weird. Not bad, exactly, but…off. The nurse was being super nice, but she kept saying “we need to monitor you” and “are you feeling okay?” I just kept nodding. Honestly, I felt kinda amazing, actually. Like I could run a marathon or something. I was bouncing my leg and telling them about this crazy idea I had for a school play – a robot Shakespeare! It was seriously epic. Then, the doctor came in and was all serious and started talking about “mood fluctuations” and “potential triggers.” And suddenly, everything just...shifted. Like the floor dropped out. I started crying, just really hard. I told them I felt just…empty. Like I’d drained all the color out of the world. They gave me some pamphlets and told me to call if I needed anything. I just wanted to disappear. It’s stupid, right? A fast heart rate and then a complete meltdown. It’s like my brain just decided to throw a party and then immediately shut down. I don’t even know what’s happening to me. Just…a lot. And now I’m sitting here, feeling completely useless and like I’m messing everything up. It's just... exhausting.

text: Okay seriously, this is… a lot. Like, a *massive* amount. I keep staring at this History paper and it’s just… swirling. I started it, like, three days ago and I’ve rewritten the introduction three times, and the conclusion is just a bunch of frantic scribbles about how “everything is connected” which is totally not true but it *feels* true, you know? My mom keeps asking if I’m okay, and I just keep saying “fine” because what else do I say? Explaining that I’m convinced I’m going to unlock the secrets of the Roman Empire while simultaneously feeling like I’m drowning in laundry and instant ramen doesn’t exactly roll off the tongue. It’s like, I had this incredible burst of energy yesterday – I was building a miniature replica of the Colosseum out of sugar cubes and cardboard, convinced it was going to change the way people understood ancient architecture. Then, this morning, I woke up and it all just… evaporated. Like a puff of smoke. Now I just feel this overwhelming anxiety about failing, about disappointing everyone, especially myself. It’s ridiculous, I know, but I feel like if I don’t get this paper in by tomorrow, the world is just going to end. And then I’ll be stuck here, back in my childhood bedroom, eating cereal for dinner, and it’ll be all my fault. I just need to *focus*. Seriously, focus. But my brain is just… buzzing. It's like a hummingbird trapped in a jar, you know? Just frantic, buzzing, wanting to escape. I keep thinking about all the things I *could* be doing, all the brilliant ideas I have, if I wasn’t so utterly paralyzed by this stupid deadline. Ugh. I just... I need a nap. Or maybe three.

okay so this was… a lot. i just got back from dinner with sarah and mark. they were so excited about their new business venture, you know, the artisanal dog biscuit thing? and i was too, at first. really excited for them. like, genuinely happy for them. then… then it just spiraled. i started talking about my garden, you know, the roses i’ve been nurturing for years. and i was going on and on about the perfect pH balance and the specific fertilizer i use – like a whole dissertation on roses. sarah kept nodding and smiling but i could tell she was glazing over. mark started sketching in his notebook, just… sketching. i think he was trying to look busy.

and then i started explaining how i’m going to write a book. a *really* good book. about… about the history of lichen. it’s fascinating, you see, the symbiotic relationships… i don’t even know why i was saying this. it just… came out. i felt like i needed to *share* it. like everyone needed to know.

i felt like i was floating, like i could just *fly*. then, five minutes later, i was crying in the bathroom. just… sobbing. thinking about how stupid it all was. how pointless. how i’m just a lonely old woman with too many roses and a weird obsession with lichen. i felt so ashamed. sarah said something nice, i think, about how lovely my roses are, but it didn't really help. it just felt… loud. like a tiny, fragile sound lost in a hurricane. i just want to disappear sometimes, you know? just fade away. i’m so tired of feeling like i’m shouting into a void.

text: Just… everything feels louder today. Like the city is screaming and I’m vibrating. It’s the anniversary. Stupid anniversary. I feel like I could build a rocket ship or just curl up and disappear. Both at once, honestly.

Okay, so last night was… a lot. I went to Sarah’s birthday thing. You know, the one she’s been talking about for like, six months? And I was actually, genuinely… good. Like, *really* good. I was telling everyone about my new ideas for, like, a whole business – totally gonna revolutionize pet grooming, obviously. It’s brilliant. Everyone was laughing, and I was laughing too, but louder. I kept saying things like, “This is my destiny, you guys, seriously.” I even offered to buy the cake. A *huge* cake.

Then, five minutes later? Total crash. Just… gone. Like a switch flipped. I just… stood there. Staring at the cake. Feeling like everyone was judging me. Like they all knew I was a fraud. I wanted to disappear. Seriously, I just wanted to vanish into the wallpaper. Sarah kept asking if I was okay, and I just mumbled something about being tired and needing to go. I left early.

I don't even know why I *went*. It's stupid, right? The energy, the excitement, it's so fleeting. And then it’s just… emptiness. It's like I’m on a train, going really fast, then suddenly slamming on the brakes and being left in the dark.  I just… I don’t get how I can be *amazing* one second and then feel like a complete failure the next. It’s exhausting. I just wish people understood that it’s not a choice. It’s… it’s just how it is.  I'm probably just being dramatic, of course. Everyone always says that.

text:
Okay, so… it’s the anniversary. A year. Feels like a lifetime ago, and also like yesterday. I didn’t really *do* anything, you know? Just… stayed in. It's weird, because today feels… intense. Like, not bad, exactly, but… buzzing. I keep thinking about everything that happened, the way it felt, and it’s all just swirling around in my head. I had this insane idea this morning, like, seriously, a *really* big idea about starting a business, something totally random about vintage postcards and… I don’t know, shipping them around the world? It’s probably stupid, honestly.

Then, an hour ago, I just… broke down. Just… started crying. Like, silent, ugly crying on the couch. I don't even know why. It's like the energy just shifted, and the sadness just hit me like a truck. I keep telling myself it’s just the anniversary, that it’s normal to feel this way, but… idk. It’s not *normal*, is it? Everything feels so amplified. Like I’m watching myself from a distance, this person who’s just bouncing between these two completely different realities. I just wanted to be okay, you know? Before… before all of this. Moving here was supposed to be a fresh start, but it just feels… lonely. And confusing. Maybe I should call someone? Nah, probably not. It’s just… a day. A really, really weird day. I just… need to breathe, I guess.

okay so i don’t even know where to start i just… i feel so completely adrift. it’s like the waves just keep coming and i’m just paddling and paddling and it’s never enough. my son he’s… he’s doing so well you know? really thriving at school and with his friends which is amazing, honestly it makes my heart swell but then… then it hits me. the quiet. it’s always the quiet. he’s at soccer practice and i’m here. just… here. staring at the rain. i tried to call sarah but she said she’s swamped with work and i just… i didn’t bother. it’s silly i know but it just felt… heavy. like adding to the pile. a pile of everything. the laundry, the bills, the meals i’m making, the constant checking in, the worrying. and then the feeling that i’m failing him somehow even though i’m pretty sure i’m doing everything i can. it's like i’m on a treadmill going nowhere fast. sometimes i just want to disappear you know? not like… not like that. just… fade away. into the wallpaper maybe. it’s not a bad life really, it's just… lonely. desperately lonely. and i keep thinking about how everyone else seems to have it figured out. everyone’s got a partner, a group of friends, a hobby, something. i just have this. this endless cycle of caregiving and quiet. and i’m exhausted. just utterly exhausted.

Ugh, this week at the academy is just… a lot. Mr. Finch is breathing down my neck about the new curriculum, and honestly, it’s like he thinks I’m running a marathon, not a small arts program. I spent all day yesterday designing these amazing clay sculptures for the kids – like, really intricate stuff – and now I'm just staring at them, feeling completely drained. It’s like I’m buzzing with energy one minute, convinced I’m going to change the world with pottery, and then the next, I just want to curl up in a ball and not move. It’s weird, you know? Like a switch flipped. I keep thinking about how much work needs to get done, but then I just… can’t. It’s exhausting.

Okay seriously, I’m losing it. Like, completely losing it. Last night was… a blur. I think I actually laughed for a solid hour straight, just staring at the ceiling, convinced I was going to invent a new type of cheese that would solve world hunger. It was… amazing. And then, like, five minutes later, I was sobbing because the baby wouldn’t stop crying and I just felt this enormous, crushing weight in my chest. I kept repeating his name, “Leo, Leo, Leo,” over and over, just wanting to curl up and disappear. I don’t even remember what I ate. Or if I ate anything.

It’s just… so much. Everyone keeps saying “newborn phase” and “it gets better,” but it feels like I’m being tossed around in a washing machine. One minute I’m bouncing off the walls, planning a massive renovation of the nursery and sketching out marketing strategies for Leo’s imaginary cheese empire, the next I’m convinced I’m a terrible mother and I should just give up. My husband keeps saying I’m ‘overstimulated’ but honestly, it feels more like my brain is just short-circuiting. I feel like I’m not even *me* anymore. Just… fluctuating. Like a broken radio. I need sleep. I need quiet. I just… I don’t know how much longer I can keep doing this. It's exhausting. Just… exhausting.

text:
Okay so like, I don't even know where to start, seriously. It’s just… a lot. Everything feels amplified, you know? Like, yesterday I was convinced I was going to invent a teleportation device, I mean *really* convinced, sketching out diagrams in my notebook at 3 am fueled by nothing but lukewarm tea and the sheer audacity of the thought, and I was actually, genuinely *excited* about it. Like, buzzing. And then this morning? This morning I just… couldn't get out of bed. Just lay there staring at the ceiling, feeling like the floor was tilting and everything was gray and pointless. It's so weird, right? It’s been, what, six months since… well, since everything ended? And I thought I’d be over it by now, you know? But it’s just… still here. This weird, prickly feeling under my skin.

And the thoughts, oh god, the thoughts. They're just… bouncing around. Like squirrels in a cage. Sometimes they’re funny, surprisingly so, and I’ll laugh out loud at nothing. Other times they’re just… heavy. Like lead weights in my chest. I keep replaying conversations, dissecting every word, every glance. It’s exhausting. I tried to call Sarah, you know, my best friend, but I just froze. Couldn't even say hi. Felt like a complete idiot. I keep thinking about all the things I should have said, all the things I didn’t. It’s a mess, a huge, tangled, messy, beautiful, terrifying mess. I just… I just want it to stop feeling like I’m constantly on the verge of falling apart. Like I'm trying to hold onto sand. It's just... hard. Really hard.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, seriously, this is…a lot. Like, a *huge* lot. I don't even know where to start, honestly. It’s just…everything feels so intensely, you know? Like I’m standing in front of a waterfall and the spray is just hitting me everywhere all at once. It’s been, like, a week since Mark left and I thought things would…settle. But they haven't, not even a little bit. It’s more like they’ve just… shifted. And the shifting is terrifying. I keep thinking about all the money we had, or *I* thought we had, and now it's gone, just vanished. He said he’d handle the legal stuff, he said it wouldn't be a problem, and now I'm staring at bills I don’t know how to pay and a bank account that’s basically a wasteland. 

I had this insane burst of energy the other day, like I could build a whole house with my bare hands, I swear. I started sketching out ideas for this…this online shop I’ve always dreamed of. Selling handmade jewelry, you know? Something to do, something to *create*. It felt amazing, so incredibly *right*. Then, just as suddenly, it was gone. And I’m back to feeling like a lead weight, just sitting here staring at the ceiling. It's awful, truly awful. I keep replaying everything, every fight, every little thing we ever argued about, and I just… I just want to rewind.  I should probably start looking for a new job, but the thought of actually *doing* anything feels so monumental. Like climbing Mount Everest in flip-flops.  I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. This is just...too much.  I keep thinking about all the things we planned, the future we thought we had, and it’s just… a shattered mess.  I just wish it wasn't so loud.

text: Okay so like, it’s just… a lot. I don’t even know where to start, honestly. Yesterday I was, I don’t know, *everything*. Seriously, I was bouncing off the walls, telling everyone I was going to invent a teleportation device and write a symphony and become, like, the most important person in the world. I even tried to convince my mom to invest in my idea – she just stared at me, you know? Like I was completely losing it. It felt…good, actually, for a while. Like everything was possible and I could just *do* anything. But then, just…poof. Gone. Just like that. Now I’m just… here. Sitting in my room, staring at the ceiling. It's freezing, even with all the blankets. I keep thinking about all the things I said yesterday, all the grand plans, and it’s just… embarrassing. Like I was putting on a show and forgot to turn it off. Everyone else seems to be having a good time, you know? Sarah and Liam were at the football game, and they posted pictures and everything. They looked…happy. And I just feel…empty. Like there’s this huge hole inside me and nothing can fill it. I don’t even really *want* to talk to anyone, it’s stupid, I know, but just the thought of explaining it all over again makes me want to hide under the covers forever. It's weird, right? Like one minute I’m convinced I can change the world, and the next I’m just… nothing. Just… lonely. Really, really lonely. I wish I could just go back to yesterday, but I can’t, can I? It's just... exhausting, being this way.

text:
Okay, so this is...weird. I don't even know where to start. Like, yesterday I was convinced I was going to invent a teleportation device. Seriously, I was sketching diagrams on napkins and talking to Leo (my son) about how we’d be traveling to Mars for breakfast. It felt…amazing, you know? Like everything was possible and I could just *do* anything. I even started writing a business plan – “Quantum Leap Logistics” – it was ridiculous. I felt so incredibly productive and excited, and… happy, like a really intense, bubbly happiness.

Then, like, an hour later? Total crash. I just… cried. Just sat on the floor and cried for a good two hours. Leo was so confused, of course. He just kept offering me a banana. And I felt…nothing. Just this huge, heavy sadness. Like the world was just gray. I don’t understand it. I honestly don’t. I feel like a broken record, skipping back and forth between these two extremes. I thought having a baby would bring stability, you know? A reason to be grounded. But it just feels… amplified. Like every emotion is ten times stronger. I'm trying to be good for Leo, I really am, but it’s so hard when I feel like I'm constantly losing control of my own thoughts. I keep thinking I’m failing him, even though I’m doing my best. Maybe it’s just sleep deprivation? Or maybe… idk. Maybe this is just what it’s like. I just wish I knew what was normal. I just want things to feel…consistent. I just…don’t get it.

it's just... so quiet. like, really quiet. i keep bouncing between feeling like i could build an empire and then just... wanting to disappear. baby’s sleeping and i’m staring at the wall. anyone else feel this way? feels so lonely.

okay so like seriously i don’t get it. yesterday i was just…fine. totally normal. i helped ms. davis carry her books, smiled at ben in history, even offered to wash the dishes after lunch. felt…okay. genuinely okay. then sarah invited me to the homecoming dance with zach and i just exploded. like, a supernova of weird energy. i don’t even *like* zach that much but i just…had to talk to him. i spent like, an hour rambling about the constellations and how the universe is basically just a giant sparkly donut and he just…stared. then my mom called and i swear i almost screamed because i felt like everyone was judging me for being so intensely me. which is like, the worst feeling ever.

and now? now i’m sitting on my bed staring at the ceiling and i feel like i could build a rocket ship and fly to mars. i’m convinced i’m the smartest person in the entire school and that everyone else is just…slow. my history project is going to be the most amazing thing anyone’s ever seen, i’m totally going to win an award, maybe even a scholarship. it’s all gonna be so brilliant. i just need to finish it tonight. and then i'm gonna call my grandpa and tell him i'm going to discover a new planet. it’s just…a lot, you know? like, too much. and then i’ll probably just crash and feel awful again. it’s always like this, right? up and down and up and down and i just…i don't even know how to stop it. it's exhausting.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text:
okay seriously like i don't even know what's happening anymore. i just... bought a ridiculous amount of yarn. like, five skeins of every color imaginable. it was on sale you know? a huge sale and i just... went. i don’t even knit. i haven’t knitted in years. it just felt... necessary. like a really important thing to do. and then i started building this giant rainbow castle out of it in my living room. it’s taking up all the space and i'm pretty sure my landlord is going to notice.

but before that, i was crying. just sobbing. about the rent. and the overdue electricity bill. and the fact that i haven't had a decent meal in like three days. i feel like a complete failure. like i’m just… drifting. this city is so loud and everyone seems to know where they're going and i'm just here, surrounded by yarn and a half-built, ridiculously colorful, and completely useless castle.

i keep thinking about getting a job, you know? a real job. but the thought of interviewing, of dealing with people, of the whole process just… overwhelms me. it’s easier to just… buy yarn. it’s a tangible thing. it’s pretty. it’s… something. i don't know. i just wish it would stop flipping back and forth like this. sometimes i feel like i could fly, like i could do anything, and then five minutes later i'm just… hollow. and lost. and wishing i could disappear. i just need a break. a real one. not this constant up and down. this isn't me, is it?

okay seriously this is insane. i haven't slept in like, 36 hours i think. just staring at this spreadsheet, the numbers are just... swirling. it’s like my brain is a broken record skipping. i had this crazy idea last night, like, a *really* good idea, about how to finish this report, it’s going to blow everyone’s mind i just know it. then i woke up and it felt like a stupid dream. now i'm just feeling… empty. just this awful, heavy feeling. i need to finish this by tomorrow morning, it’s the only thing keeping me from just, like, disappearing. maybe if i just keep typing, keep pushing, it’ll all be okay. or maybe it won't. i don’t even know anymore. just gotta keep going, right?

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a weird thing to post, but I just... I don’t even know. It’s exam season, obviously, right? Everyone’s stressed. But it’s like, *exponentially* stressed for me. I spent last night just, like, building a miniature replica of the library out of LEGOs. Seriously. It was, like, six feet tall and had tiny little librarians. And it was amazing. I felt... powerful, you know? Like I could conquer anything. I actually thought I could finish all my essays and ace *every* exam just by, like, visualizing it all happening. Which, maybe, was a little delusional.

Then this morning I woke up and just… felt utterly drained. Like a sponge that’s been squeezed dry. Everything feels so heavy, and I just want to curl up in a ball and not move. I keep staring at my notes and they just look like a jumble of meaningless symbols. I’m pretty sure I’m going to fail everything. I tried to study, I really did, but my brain just kept flashing through all these crazy ideas – a whole series of documentaries about competitive snail racing, a song about the history of staplers, and then suddenly I was convinced I could invent a self-folding laundry basket. Seriously, what is happening? I dunno. It’s like the manic energy just... evaporated. Maybe it’s just the pressure, but it feels… different. Like a switch flipped. I just need to breathe, I guess. Or maybe just hide under my desk until the exams are over. I don’t know. Idk what’s going on with me. It’s just… a lot.

Okay, seriously, this deadline is insane. Like, I'm buzzing, thinking I can write a novel in a day. Then, bam, crushing wave of 'what even is my life' hits and I just want to curl up and disappear. It’s a mess, honestly.

Ugh, just… everyone else seems to be *doing* something. Like, parties and football games and new boyfriends. I just… exist. It's weird, right? One minute I'm buzzing, thinking I could write the best song ever, like, a *hit* song, and the next I’m staring at my bedroom wall and it’s just… gray. I don’t even feel like talking to anyone. My mom keeps asking if I’m okay, but how do you explain *this*? It’s not even sad, I guess. Just… empty. Like a broken toy. I keep scrolling through Insta and seeing everyone laughing and it makes my chest hurt. Seriously, does anyone else feel like they're watching a movie about someone else’s life?

Okay, okay, seriously, this English essay is going to be the death of me, I swear. Like, it’s due tomorrow and I haven’t even started, and it’s supposed to be, like, ten pages long on… on the symbolism in *Moby Dick*? Seriously? I feel like I’m drowning in whale blubber and Melville. I keep bouncing between, like, obsessively trying to outline it – I've got, like, five different versions of the intro now, all completely different – and then just staring at the blank page, feeling this massive wave of… nothingness, you know? It’s not sadness, not exactly, it's just… a complete absence of motivation. Like my brain just shut off.

Then, like, an hour later, I’m convinced I’m going to write the most brilliant, insightful essay the world has ever seen! I’m pacing and typing furiously, ideas just *flying* out of me – I'm going to argue that Ishmael’s journey is a metaphor for… for the human search for meaning! Or maybe it’s about the futility of trying to control nature! Or maybe it's just a really long story about a whale! It’s all so exciting and important, and I need to tell everyone!  And then, inevitably, I'll crash. Hard. Like, physically, I feel like I'm going to collapse.  I just want to curl up in bed and not do anything.  It's so stupid, I know I need to finish it, but the thought of actually sitting down and writing… ugh.  I just... I just don't know how to do this. It’s like my brain is a broken record, skipping back and forth between these totally opposite states. It's exhausting, you know? Just... exhausting.

text: Okay, so like, this city is…loud. Not in a good way. It’s just…a constant hum, and I feel like I’m vibrating with it, you know? I moved here a few months ago, just on a whim, really, a desperate attempt to shake things up and honestly, it’s mostly just made everything worse. I thought maybe being surrounded by people would help, but it’s the opposite. Everyone seems to have their own little bubble, their own perfect little lives and I just... I feel like I'm watching a movie, except I'm not in it. It's like I'm this blurry, grey figure just at the edge of everything. I tried joining a pottery class, which seemed like a good idea at the time, super creative, right? But I just sat there, staring at the clay, feeling completely useless and like everyone else was talking about something I wasn't even remotely interested in. It’s just… exhausting. Sometimes I have these bursts of energy, like I could build a whole house out of clay and write a novel and learn to play the ukulele all in one afternoon, and then the next minute I'm curled up on the couch, just weeping because I can't even get out of my pajamas. It’s so weird, this up and down. I feel like a broken record, skipping and repeating the same awful, lonely moments. I keep telling myself it’ll get better, that I'm strong and I can handle this, but then I look around at all these strangers and think, maybe I’m just…not meant to be here. Maybe this was a terrible idea. I miss my cat, Mr. Whiskers, terribly. He always just…understood, you know? Just a quiet presence. I should probably call my mom, but…ugh. It’s just…too much.

text: Okay so like, I just finished this insane shift at the warehouse – seriously, boxes everywhere, people yelling, and Mr. Henderson was breathing down my neck the whole time about shipping deadlines, it was just… a lot. but it hasn’t. it hasn’t done anything. i spent the afternoon unpacking the kitchen stuff, and i swear i was building a castle out of plates and bowls. seriously, i had a whole moat. and then ben came in and said it looked “chaotic” and i just… i snapped. i don't even remember what i yelled. something about tiny ceramic frogs and the futility of existence.

then i felt… well, you know. the other thing. the heavy, gray feeling. like the air is thick and you can barely breathe. i just wanted to curl up on the floor and disappear. i kept thinking about how many boxes are still left to unpack and how i’m supposed to make this place *feel* like home when i feel like i’m dissolving into it. it’s like i'm constantly trying to build something beautiful and it just keeps crumbling. i keep imagining everyone i know is watching me, judging me, and that i’m failing at being a decent person. i just need a minute. a really long minute. maybe two. i just want to sit.

okay seriously i don’t even know what’s happening anymore. like this project at work it’s… it’s huge right? everyone’s expecting so much and i’ve been working like a maniac for weeks straight. i haven’t slept properly i’ve just been staring at the screen, trying to keep up. and it’s kinda amazing actually, you know? like i’m actually *good* at this. i’m brilliant even. i could totally solve this thing in a day if i just kept going.

but then five minutes ago i was sobbing. just sitting at my desk, just… crying. feeling completely useless and like i’m going to fail. the thought of presenting it tomorrow is terrifying. it’s like my brain is just flipping between these two completely different realities. one minute i’m convinced i'm going to revolutionize the industry and the next i’m convinced everyone's going to realize how incompetent i am.

i keep thinking about all the things i should be doing, all the things i *could* be doing if i wasn’t so distracted. this constant pressure is just… suffocating. i feel like i'm constantly on the verge of an explosion. it's exhausting trying to hold it together, trying to force myself to be productive when i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. i just need a break, a real break. maybe if i could just stop thinking for a second... but i can't. it's just a loop. a really, really intense loop. i wish someone would just tell me it’s going to be okay. i just... i don't know.

okay so like i don’t even know where to start seriously. it’s all just… a blur. yesterday i was convinced i was gonna invent something amazing, you know? like, a self-folding laundry basket or something ridiculously useful and i was emailing investors and sketching out patents and talking about quitting my job and just, like, building this empire. i was so buzzed it was almost painful. then last night… nothing. just… nothing. she left. i know, i know, it’s a cliche but it felt like the floor just dropped out. she said i was too intense, too much, that i wouldn’t let her breathe. i guess i wasn’t good at calming down, at just being normal. which is weird, right? because i thought i was. i really did. now i just feel… hollow. like a sponge that’s been squeezed dry. i keep replaying everything we said, every little thing, trying to find where i messed up, but it's pointless. it's just a mess. and the worst part is i keep flashing back to yesterday, to that ridiculous laundry basket idea, and i feel this overwhelming guilt like i wasted all that energy on something stupid. it’s stupid, i guess. but it was *mine*. and now it’s gone. and i don’t even know how to feel anything besides this dull ache. i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. honestly i don't think i can face another day. it’s just… too much.

text: Okay, so like, it’s the anniversary today. You know, the one where… well, it doesn’t really matter what happened, does it? It just *is*. Just this awful, heavy thing that sits right here, you know? I keep replaying it, not like, actively trying to, but just… it’s there, a little loop in my head. And everyone else is being all normal and happy, posting pictures of their dinners and their dogs and their amazing weekends, and I just… I can’t. I don’t even know what to do. Part of me wants to just curl up in bed and not move for three days, which, honestly, sounds kind of appealing right now. But then, the other part, the *really* loud part, is screaming about how I could build a whole new life, a better life, if I just… if I just *did* something. Like, I could invent a teleportation device, or write the greatest novel ever, or… or I don’t know, just *fix* everything. It's exhausting. It’s like my brain is stuck on repeat, switching between this awful, heavy quiet and this buzzing, frantic energy. And it’s not even good energy, it's just… a mess. Mom keeps asking if I’m okay and I just say “fine” because what else can I say? It’s hard to explain that you’re okay when you feel like you're constantly falling apart. I just… I wish it would stop being today. I really, really wish it would stop. Maybe if I just stare at the wall long enough, it’ll go away. Ugh.

okay so like seriously i don’t even know what’s happening anymore. i moved last week, remember? to this tiny apartment downtown, it’s supposed to be cool and exciting and a fresh start right? and it *is* kinda cool, the coffee shop downstairs is amazing and the streetlights are pretty, but then i just… i just spiral. yesterday i was convinced i was going to be a famous musician, writing the next big hit, i spent like eight hours on a ukulele and a notebook filled with gibberish and then i just cried. like ugly crying. and today i’m convinced i’m the most brilliant architect ever, sketching buildings that defy gravity and are made entirely of rainbows, and i just keep ordering takeout and staring at the ceiling. it’s like my brain is on fire and then it’s suddenly just… cold.

mom keeps sending me pictures of her garden and she's all "you should come home and help me with the tomatoes" and i want to scream. i just want to disappear. i feel like i’m constantly switching channels, one minute i’m bouncing off the walls, telling everyone how amazing i am, the next i’m huddled in a corner, convinced the world is ending. it’s exhausting. i keep telling myself this is just the newness, the stress of everything, but it feels… deeper. like there’s something else going on. i’m terrified to tell anyone, you know? they’d just say i’m being dramatic or that i need to “snap out of it.” but it *is* happening. it’s not just feeling sad or stressed, it’s… this. this constant, dizzying shift. i just wish i knew how to turn it off. i keep thinking about just going back home, back to the familiar, but then i’d be stuck. trapped. ugh. i just need a nap. maybe two.

text:
Okay, so like, this whole moving thing is… a lot. I’m still here, you know, back at my parents’ place. It’s not *bad*, it’s just… weird. I thought I’d be, like, totally bouncing with excitement about the new city and my new job, you know? The internship at StellarTech is actually pretty amazing, I think. I mean, it’s a *chance*, right? A real chance. I’ve been doing some coding, and it’s… okay. Not amazing, but okay. I’m probably overthinking it, definitely.

But then, yesterday? Yesterday was… intense. I spent like, six hours just rearranging my bedroom. Seriously, every single item. It had to be *perfect*. And then I was convinced I was going to invent something groundbreaking, like, a revolutionary AI program that would solve world hunger. I don't even *do* AI! It just… happened. I kept telling my mom I was “busy” and just… existing in my room. I felt so wired, like I could run a marathon. It's probably just the stress of everything, right?

And now? Now I just feel… empty. Like a balloon that’s lost all its air. I keep staring at the blank wall and just... thinking. Just swirling thoughts about nothing. Maybe I should just call Sarah? But she’s probably busy too, and I don’t want to bother her. It’s just… everything feels so magnified, like a really, really bright spotlight on everything I’m doing. I idk, it's just a lot to process, I guess? This whole new chapter thing is overwhelming. Maybe I just need a nap. Or ten. Or maybe I’m just completely losing it, and this is what it’s like.

text: Okay seriously, this is insane. Like, I was designing this amazing sculpture for my art class yesterday, felt totally on fire, ideas just *pouring* out. I was up all night, didn't sleep, just working. Now? I just want to curl up in a ball and cry. It's stupid, right? Just a random shift. Everything feels…too much. I keep staring at my notes and they’re just, like, a blur. My roommate keeps asking if I’m okay and I just say “fine” but it’s not. It’s not fine. I feel like I’m going to fail everything. Just…overwhelmed. And then, like, vaguely, a tiny little voice keeps saying I’m brilliant and I can do anything, but it’s so quiet it’s almost embarrassing. Ugh.

Okay, so this new apartment is… a lot. Like, way more than I thought. It’s ridiculously bright, all white walls and huge windows. I spent yesterday rearranging everything, convinced it needed to be perfect, like, instantly. Then I just… cried. Seriously, just sat on the floor surrounded by bubble wrap and felt completely useless. It’s funny, isn’t it? Just a few hours ago I was convinced I was going to invent something amazing, you know? A whole new way to organize spices. Now I just want to hide. Don’t get me wrong, I’m *fine*. Just... a bit all over the place, I guess. This place is amplifying everything, I swear.

Seriously freaking out. Just got another late notice. Seems like every time I think I’m getting ahead, BAM, more bills. This manic energy is exhausting, but the dark parts are creeping back in, you know? It’s just… too much.

text:
Okay, so like, this is weird. I just moved into this tiny apartment in Oakhaven – it’s…beige. Really, really beige. It’s supposed to be ‘fresh start’ vibes, you know? But honestly, I feel like I’m just…floating. Yesterday, I was convinced I was going to write the next great American novel, like, seriously, I was outlining chapters and sketching out character designs on the back of receipts. I even bought a ridiculously expensive fountain pen – don’t judge. I thought I was, like, *destined* for something huge. Then, this morning? Ugh. Just…nothing. Just this heavy feeling, like the floor is pulling me down. I haven’t showered, I haven’t eaten anything substantial, and I just stared at the wall for an hour.

I don’t even know why I’m writing this. It’s probably dumb. It's just... it's a lot, right? Moving is already a massive thing, and adding in the divorce… it’s just a constant push and pull. Maybe it’s just the shock of it all. Maybe it’s the beige. I keep thinking about all the plans I made with Liam – we were going to travel, open a little bakery, build a life. Now… nothing. Just this empty space and this feeling that I'm going to be okay, but I probably won't be. I feel... detached. Like I’m watching myself do this, but I’m not really *in* it. I just… idk. It’s exhausting. I just hope this beige doesn’t swallow me whole. Maybe I should call my sister? No, she’d just tell me to ‘look on the bright side.’ Ugh. Bright side. Right.

text:
Okay seriously, this is… this is insane. Like, I haven’t slept properly in days, I’ve just been bouncing off the walls, you know? Mom keeps asking if I’m okay, but she doesn’t *get* it, she just says “you need to relax” like it’s that simple. Relax? My thesis is due next week and it’s basically a monster, a giant, shadowy monster of research and citations and arguments and… oh my god, it's *beautiful*. It’s like the most gorgeous, shimmering, iridescent monster and I need to tame it, sculpt it into something presentable. I keep adding things, just little bits, tangents really, about the history of cartography and how it relates to, like, the subconscious mapping of experience – totally relevant, right? I swear I’m on the verge of a breakthrough, a *major* breakthrough that’s going to change everything. I could write the whole thing in one sitting, just… just flow.

But then, the opposite. Then the darkness hits. Like a wave, just crashing over me and suddenly it's all just… gray. Everything feels pointless. The thesis? It’s a lie, a completely fabricated excuse for a pointless exercise. Why even bother? My professors don’t even care, they’re probably just waiting for me to fail. I just want to curl up in a ball and not do anything, just disappear. It's so exhausting, this constant switching. Like my brain is a broken record, skipping between these two completely different realities. I keep staring at the screen, just… blank. I feel like I’m drowning, but in sunshine. It’s… it's overwhelming. I just need a nap, I think. A really, really long nap. Or maybe just to not think about it at all. Ugh.

text: Okay so, this is just… a lot. It’s the anniversary of, you know, the thing. It happened a year ago today, and I just… I can’t shake it. I tried to not look at my phone, honestly, really hard. But then I saw Liam’s post – he always posts about his dog, Buster, and today Buster’s got a little bandana and it’s just… perfect. Like, ridiculously perfect and it made me feel like I should be happy for him, and I *want* to be happy for him, but it’s just… a sharp, stinging feeling, you know? Like I’m supposed to be feeling something, anything, but all I feel is this weird, buzzing emptiness. I spent the morning trying to clean my room, which sounds productive, right? But it was just… pointless. Like scrubbing away at the edges of something that’s fundamentally broken. Then I started building a fort out of blankets and pillows – a really elaborate one, with fairy lights and everything – and I was laughing, a real, honest laugh, and then I just… cried. Just sobbed. I don’t even know why. It’s like the energy is just… surging, then crashing. I feel like I could write a novel, or build a spaceship, or learn to play the ukulele, all in the space of an hour. And then I’ll just feel completely, utterly drained and useless. It’s exhausting, you know? Just… exhausting. I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing, all the things I *could* be doing, and it's just a weight. A heavy, heavy weight. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. Maybe if I just close my eyes really tight, it'll all go away. But it doesn’t. It just… lingers.

Like, I *want* to be productive, I *want* to feel good, but it's like there's this wall between me and everything. I feel so disconnected from myself. I just… I hope it gets better. Maybe. I really hope it does. I just need to… I need to figure this out, I guess. It's just… a lot.

text: Okay seriously, this is… a lot. Like, yesterday I was convinced I was going to invent a self-folding laundry machine and write a symphony and build a tiny house in the backyard – total mania, I know, right? And then, just hours later, I was sobbing because the baby wouldn’t stop crying and I just felt… so utterly useless and like I’m failing at everything, even being a mom. This isn’t me, I swear. I’m just… overwhelmed and confused and feeling like I’m drowning in a sea of diapers and sleepless nights and intense, fleeting highs and lows.

Okay, seriously, this is insane. Like, yesterday I was convinced I was going to invent a teleportation device and write a symphony and solve world hunger. I spent, like, eight hours just bouncing ideas off myself and emailing my boss (who thankfully didn't read them – thank god) about optimizing supply chains for artisanal cheese. It was… a lot. A *lot*. I felt like I could actually *do* anything.

Then, this morning, I woke up and realized I have this huge presentation due tomorrow – a quarterly review for my job, and it's, like, the most important one of the year. And I haven’t even *started* it. I just stared at the blank screen for two hours. Two. Hours. Just… blank. And now I’m just feeling this awful, heavy feeling, like everything is pointless. Like, what’s the point of even trying if I can’t even finish a simple presentation?

I keep replaying it all in my head, like a broken record. The cheese. The teleportation. The presentation. It's just... too much. My baby’s asleep, thankfully, but I feel like I’m failing at everything. Like I’m just constantly switching between being a super-genius inventor and a complete, useless mess. It's exhausting. Just… exhausting. I need to get this presentation done, but I can’t even force myself to open the document. I just want to curl up and disappear. Maybe I should just tell my boss I'm sick. Ugh. This is so stupid.
Ugh, everyone's having a party tonight. I just… can't. Feels like I’m watching everyone else from behind a glass wall. Like I’m not even real, you know? Just a ghost.

okay so like i went to the hospital today and they were freaking out about my heart rate it was so weird i felt totally fine but they kept asking questions and i just… i don’t even know what’s happening to me it’s all just too much i just want to go back to bed

okay seriously what is happening to me? yesterday i was convinced i was gonna invent a teleportation device, like i was *going* to change the world, you know? sketching diagrams on napkins, talking to the barista about quantum physics—it was insane. then this morning i woke up feeling like the floor was swallowing me whole. just... nothing. completely drained. my doctor called, said it was just a minor thing, a weird blood test, but it felt like the universe was screaming at me. i keep thinking about him, you know? about how *he* would have known what to do. like, he always knew. now i'm just… this. a mess. and i don't even know how to feel anything except this awful, heavy quiet. it's just... too much.

Okay, so like, this morning I woke up and I was *on fire*. Seriously. I was emailing everyone back instantly, sending out proposals for a new marketing campaign for... I don't even remember what *was* it for, something huge and shiny and important. I was sketching out designs on napkins, telling my boss I was basically a genius, and I even started building a miniature replica of the company headquarters out of LEGOs. It was… insane. Like, completely, utterly ridiculous. I felt like I could *do anything*.

Then, like, an hour later? Nothing. Just… nothing. I was sitting on the couch, staring at the wall, and this wave just crashed over me. Just… *heavy*. Like the whole world was suddenly a really, really bad shade of gray. I couldn’t even bring myself to drink water. My chest felt tight, and I just kept thinking about how pointless everything is. How everyone’s better than me. How I’m just… a mess.

My mom called, and she was all, “You were so energetic!” and I just… I cried. Just started sobbing. I don’t even know why. It's stupid, right? One minute I'm a rocket ship, the next I'm just… sinking. I keep thinking about that doctor’s appointment yesterday, the one where they said I need to watch my blood pressure. It's probably nothing, but it feels… significant. Like maybe this is *it*. Like this is how it ends, just collapsing into a puddle of sadness and feeling completely useless. I just want it to stop, you know? Just… stop feeling.

text:
Okay, so this city is…loud. Like, really loud. I moved here a few months ago, totally on a whim, you know? Just needed a change, a fresh start. And it *looked* good online, all the cool cafes and independent shops. But honestly, it’s mostly just…people. Everywhere. And I feel like I’m constantly overthinking every interaction. Like, should I say hi? Should I smile? Do they think I’m weird? I went to that little bookstore yesterday – the one with the comfy chairs – and I just… froze. There were these two women talking about, like, obscure poetry and I just stood there, clutching my coffee, feeling like a complete idiot.

Then, suddenly, I just *had* to go talk to them. I started rambling about, I don’t even know, the color of the walls? And I was being so loud, and I kept saying things like "idk" and "maybe," and it was so awkward. They were really nice, I think, but I probably just made it worse. I felt this huge rush of…energy, like I could do *anything*. I started thinking about opening a little art studio, like, right here, and selling my paintings and becoming famous, which is totally ridiculous, I know. Then, like five minutes later, I just wanted to crawl into a hole and disappear. I just…I don’t get it. It’s like a rollercoaster, except the tracks are made of anxiety and glitter. I just wish I could just...not feel this way. I don't even know why I'm typing this out, honestly. Maybe I should just go back to my apartment and stare at the ceiling. It’s probably the best option, tbh.

text: Okay so like, I feel…amazing today, seriously, I could write a novel or solve all the world’s problems, it’s just…my mom keeps yelling about the dishes and like, it's not a big deal, but it feels like the end of the world right now, and then I just want to curl up and disappear, ugh, this is so exhausting.

it’s just… so much. lost my job, she left, and i feel like i could bounce off the walls one minute then just… crumble. everything feels too bright, too loud, then suddenly just grey. i don’t even know what day it is anymore.

Okay seriously, I’m losing it. Like, completely losing it. Last night I was convinced I was going to write the next great American novel. Seriously, I had this *thing* – it was about a sentient cloud and a lonely lighthouse keeper, and it was brilliant. I was up until 3am just typing, fueled by nothing but lukewarm tea and the sheer, unadulterated belief that I was a genius. I even started sketching out chapter outlines with colored pencils – purple for the cloud, orange for the lighthouse. It was amazing.

Then Leo woke up screaming. Just…screaming. And he wouldn’t stop. I tried to soothe him, read him a story (the cloud one, naturally), but he just kept crying. And then I just…collapsed. Like, a complete and utter collapse. Now it's 6am and I’m staring at the ceiling, feeling this massive, crushing weight. Like everything I did last night was a complete waste. It’s stupid, I know, to feel this way about a dream, but it *felt* real. 

And now I’m just…tired. Bone-deep tired. And sad. Really sad. I feel like I’m failing at everything. Being a mom, being a writer, just…existing. I keep thinking about going back to sleep, just letting it all wash over me. It would be so easy, just to…not wake up. Not really, but just…not *do* anything. I don’t even know what I want anymore. Just…quiet. Please, just quiet.

Ugh, seriously. This week at the academy has been… a lot. Mr. Henderson, bless his heart, is convinced he needs me to personally design a new curriculum on the history of staplers. A stapler! Like, I teach ancient languages, okay? I can decipher Sumerian cuneiform, but apparently, I’m the only one who knows anything about office supplies. It’s just… exhausting. And the students, honestly, they’re bouncing off the walls. Little Timmy just spent twenty minutes explaining his theory about how staplers evolved from ancient Roman fasteners. I just wanted to scream.

Then, yesterday, I was feeling… good. Really good. Like, ridiculously good. I cleaned out the entire storage closet, reorganized the library shelves alphabetically by author’s last name (which, let’s be honest, is a monumental task), and even started knitting a scarf for my grandson. Bright orange. Seriously bright. I felt like I could conquer the world. I even considered emailing Mr. Henderson a detailed proposal for a “Stapler History” module, complete with interactive timelines and miniature dioramas.

And now? Now I'm just… heavy. Like I’m wading through molasses. The thought of facing another day of staplers and tiny Timmy’s theories is making my chest ache. It’s stupid, I know. It's just... everything feels so pointless. Like I'm running on a treadmill, and I’m not even getting anywhere. Just… tired. So, so tired. Maybe I’ll just hide in the library and read about ancient languages. At least *they* made sense.

Okay, seriously, my mom just texted again. Something about "family dinners" and how I'm "not pulling my weight." Like, duh, I'm working 60 hours a week and dealing with this *thing* inside my head. It’s just… so much. I feel like I could build a skyscraper in an afternoon if I wasn't so exhausted all the time. Then, five minutes later, I’m convinced the world is ending and I'm completely useless. It’s ridiculous. I just want to disappear for a while, you know? Just… quiet. Everyone’s always so critical. Maybe if I just stopped responding, they’d get the hint. Ugh. This is exhausting.

text:
Okay so like, this is weird but I just… I had this panic attack, but it wasn't *really* a panic attack, you know? It was more like… a surge. Everything just sped up, like my thoughts were a million miles an hour and I was suddenly seeing colors, really bright, almost neon colors. I was staring at my computer screen and it looked like a rainbow exploded, and I just started typing, just *typing* without even thinking about what I was saying. I wrote like, a ten-page email to my boss outlining this completely insane marketing campaign for, like, artisanal dog biscuits – seriously, artisanal dog biscuits! It’s brilliant, obviously, the best thing anyone’s ever heard of, and I was practically bouncing off the walls just *thinking* about it. Then, just as suddenly as it started, it stopped. And I was left just… exhausted and kind of ashamed.

Then the opposite hit me. Like, a tidal wave of sadness. Just this overwhelming, heavy feeling. I started crying, just silent, ugly crying, and I couldn’t stop. I looked in the mirror and I just… I didn’t even recognize myself. I felt like I was crumbling, like all the energy from before had just vanished and I was completely empty. I keep thinking about my grandma, who’s been sick, and I feel so guilty for being so… loud and excited before, and then just completely shutting down. It’s like my brain is just flipping through channels, constantly switching between these two extremes. I don’t even know what’s real anymore, honestly. It's just... a lot. And I feel like I’m going to break. Like, a really, really slow, quiet break. Maybe I should just… not respond to anyone. Just stay in bed.

okay okay i think i can actually finish this report tonight… it’s like a tidal wave of ideas just rushing through me, i haven’t slept in days but it’s… good? then suddenly i’m just staring at the screen feeling like nothing matters and i just want to curl up somewhere dark. ugh.

Sometimes I feel like I’m floating, and then other times I’m just sinking. I wish I could just… stop feeling this way. It’s lonely being like this, really lonely.

okay seriously i don't even know what's happening anymore it feels like i'm bouncing between two different people completely. yesterday i was convinced i could make a fortune selling handmade birdhouses online like, a *lot* of them, and i was buying all these supplies and emailing people and sketching designs and it was amazing i felt like i could actually do anything. i was buzzing. then this morning i woke up and the bank called about the overdue bills and suddenly it all felt so… pointless. like i wasted all that energy on nothing. and now i’m just staring at these half-finished birdhouses and feeling this awful weight in my chest.

i keep telling myself i need to be practical, i need a budget, i need to stop spending money like this but then i just get this wave of panic and i want to *do* something, anything, to feel…alive. it’s exhausting. my daughter keeps asking about dinner and i just keep nodding and saying i'll figure it out but i don't know how. i feel like i’m failing her, like i’m letting her down, and then i feel so guilty for even feeling that way because it’s *my* fault, isn't it? i just wanted to make things better, to give her a nice life, but it’s spiraling. i just… i wish it would stop. i wish i could just be normal, you know? just… steady. it’s just so much. like a constant storm inside my head and i can’t find the calm.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, I just… I can’t sleep. Seriously. Like, at all. Last night I was bouncing off the walls, convinced I was going to invent a self-folding laundry machine and write a symphony and solve world hunger, you know? It was *amazing*, honestly, felt like I could actually *do* anything. And now? Now I'm just… heavy. Like a stone. Just staring at the ceiling and thinking about how tired I am, and how everything feels so pointless. I keep tossing and turning, trying to recapture that feeling, that spark, but it’s gone. It’s just… gray. And then I worry about [Name of fictional grandchild] and if they’re okay, and if I’m doing enough, and it just spirals. I keep telling myself it’s just the sleep deprivation, but it feels… deeper than that. Like a shadow clinging to everything.

It’s just… quiet. Always quiet. I used to have so many people calling, texting, wanting to do things. Now? Nothing. I spent all day rearranging the living room, making it look… nice, I guess. Like it’s ready for company. But it’s not. It never is. I feel like I’m shouting into a void. Yesterday I was convinced I was going to invent something amazing, something that would change the world. Today? I just want someone to acknowledge I exist. Seriously. It’s exhausting. Just… exhausting. I don't even know why I bother sometimes. Like, what's the point of all this effort if there's no one here to see it?

Okay, seriously, I don't get it. Like, I just lost my job, which is already awful, obviously. But then… last night? Last night was… a lot. I went to Sarah’s birthday thing – she’s been working on this pottery thing for ages – and I was *amazing*. I swear, I was just… radiating. I told everyone about my brilliant business ideas, you know, the ones I’ve been thinking about since, like, forever? And they were *listening*. Really listening. They were all nodding and saying, “Wow, that’s incredible!” and “You should totally start it!” I even convinced Mark from accounting to invest. Mark! He's a bean counter. It was insane. I felt like I could, like, *fly*.

And then… this morning? This morning I woke up feeling like the world was ending. Like I'd completely blown it. Just… a massive wave of sadness, just crushing me. I spent the last three hours staring at the ceiling, scrolling through Instagram, seeing everyone else’s perfectly curated lives. It’s not fair, you know? They’re all happy, they’re all successful, and I’m just… here. A mess. I don’t even want to talk to anyone. It’s embarrassing, I guess. It’s just… this rollercoaster. One minute I’m the most brilliant, unstoppable person in the world, the next I’m convinced I'm worthless and everyone hates me. I just… I wish it would stop. It's exhausting. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. This is too much.

okay seriously i don't even know what's happening to me today. like, yesterday i was convinced i was gonna write the greatest screenplay ever and i was talking a mile a minute to everyone about it, practically bouncing off the walls. then this morning? nothing. just...nothing. i feel like a ghost, you know? everyone else is laughing and talking about prom and college applications and i'm just...here. watching. it's weird because i feel like a giant, empty space. like i’m supposed to be doing something important but i just can't seem to connect with anything. it’s stupid i guess, but i just want someone to, like, *notice* me. even if it's just to say hi. it's so lonely. i just wanna disappear sometimes, not like, actually disappear but you know… just fade away.

Okay, seriously, this is ridiculous. I was, like, *amazing* last night. I was finishing that presentation, and it was just… flowing. Ideas everywhere, I was practically buzzing. I kept saying to Liam, "I'm going to nail this, I *know* I am," and he just kept looking at me like I was losing it. Now? Now I can’t even look at the damn slides. It’s all blurry and meaningless. I keep staring at the word “deadline” and it’s just… a black hole. 

I feel like I’ve climbed the highest mountain and then promptly fallen into the deepest ocean. Everything feels too big, too important, and I just... can't. I keep thinking about how everyone else is probably ahead, how they’re all just cruising while I’m stuck here paralyzed. It's not even a normal feeling of stress, it's like... a giant weight is pressing down on me. Like I’m sinking.

I keep telling myself to just *do* it, to just force myself to work, but every time I try, I just end up spiraling. I keep picking at things, obsessing over tiny details that don't matter. It’s stupid, I know, but I can’t stop. I just want it to be over. Maybe if I just lie down for a few minutes... no, that's a terrible idea. Just… overwhelmed. Really, really overwhelmed.  I just wish I could just *be* normal for five seconds.

text: Okay seriously, I don’t even know where to start. It’s… it’s like the world just flipped and I’m stuck in this weird sideways place, you know? Like, yesterday I was convinced I was going to invent a self-folding laundry machine, and I spent like six hours sketching designs and emailing my old college roommate, Liam – he’s a programmer, maybe he could help? – about the algorithms. It was amazing, honestly, a real burst of… something. Pure, unadulterated *doing*. And then, like, five minutes later, I was sobbing on the couch because… because I’m alone. Completely and utterly alone. It’s just… weird. He’s gone, you know? Mark. And I keep expecting to hear his keys jangling in the door, or him saying “Hey, I’m home,” but it’s just… quiet. So quiet. And I keep replaying everything, every stupid argument, every time I said the wrong thing, just endlessly going over it. It's like a broken record stuck on repeat. I tried to call my sister, Sarah, but I couldn’t even talk, just kept repeating “I miss him” over and over. It just felt… pointless. And now I feel this huge, hollow feeling, like there’s just an empty space where everything used to be. I keep thinking about starting a new hobby, maybe pottery or something, but then the thought of doing it alone feels… terrifying. Like I’m going to fail spectacularly and then just… disappear. It’s exhausting, you know? Trying to hold onto the good bits, the bursts of energy, when everything else feels like it’s just… crumbling. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. Is this what it’s like to be permanently… adrift?

text:
Okay, so like, this is weird to even write down, but… I just feel… nothing. Seriously. I moved here six months ago, promised myself it would be this amazing fresh start, you know? A chance to reinvent myself and everything. But it’s just… gray. Everything’s gray. I have a tiny apartment, just a studio, and it feels huge and empty. Like, really huge and empty. I’ve tried to, like, meet people, right? I went to that pottery class everyone kept recommending – it was fine, I made a lopsided bowl, but nobody talked to me. And then I went to that open mic night at the coffee shop, and I just stood in the corner the whole time, nursing a lukewarm tea. I don't even know why I went. I just… wanted to be *around* people, I guess? It feels stupid to admit, but it does.

I keep thinking about my family, and how they probably think I’m doing great. They send me these really enthusiastic emails about how proud they are of me for being “independent” and “adventurous.” Which is… nice, I guess. But it’s also kind of suffocating. Like, they have no idea. They don't understand that I just feel… lost. Completely lost. I don't even know what I’m doing here. Maybe I should just pack it all up and go home. But then what? It’s just… a lot. I don’t even know where to start. I just wish someone would, like, notice. Or maybe I'm just being dramatic. Idk. I’m probably just being dramatic. It’s probably nothing. Maybe I’ll just… watch Netflix tonight. Again.

i used to be okay with being quiet. i liked observing. but now it’s just…heavy. it feels like everyone's talking about a world i'm not part of anymore. and i feel this constant pull, this need to *connect*, but i don’t even know how. i don’t want to say the wrong thing, or make things awkward, or just generally be a drain on people. i just want to melt into the wallpaper, you know? it's stupid, i know. it’s probably just the shift. i haven’t slept properly in weeks and i just feel…grey. everything feels grey. like a really bad watercolor painting. i keep telling myself it’ll get better, that i’ll be able to join in, but then i just…don’t. i just don’t. i just wish i could be normal. just once.

text:
Okay so like, I don’t even know where to start. It's just...everything feels heavy. I mean, Leo is amazing, he’s beautiful and he smiles at me and I love him so much, truly I do. But then I look at him and I just… feel this wave of, idk, guilt? Or maybe sadness? Or just…nothingness, really. It’s not like I’m a bad parent, I think. I feed him, I change him, I try to get him to sleep, I sing to him, I read him stupid picture books. But it just feels…pointless sometimes. Like I’m going through the motions.

And my husband, Ben, he’s trying, he really is. But he doesn’t *get* it, I don’t think. He’s all “You’re exhausted! You need to rest!” And I know he’s right, I *am* exhausted. I haven't slept properly in weeks. But it’s not just tiredness, you know? It's...deeper. Like there's a part of me that's just...shut down. Maybe it's the postpartum hormones, maybe it’s just being a new mom, maybe it’s something else entirely. I just… I just feel so incredibly alone. Like I’m watching my life happen through a really thick fog. I keep thinking about how different things were before Leo. I don’t even remember what it was like to feel genuinely happy. Maybe it’s just a phase, right? Maybe it’ll pass. But what if it doesn’t? I just wish I knew what to do. I wish I could just feel *something* other than this dull ache.  I should probably call my mom, but I don’t want to bother her with this stuff. She’d worry.
