Like I’m just not smart enough, or hardworking enough, or anything. I tried to study last night but I just stared at the textbook for an hour and then felt this huge wave of just… blah. Everything feels gray. I keep thinking about just skipping class, or calling in sick, but then I’d just fall behind even more and it would be this endless spiral. I just want to disappear sometimes, honestly. It’s not that I want to *do* anything, it’s just… not feeling anything good is exhausting.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: Ugh, just… everything feels so heavy. Like I’m wading through mud. My grandma went to the hospital yesterday. Nothing serious, they said, just a dizzy spell and some weird heart palpitations. But it just… it made me panic, you know? Like, what if it’s something really bad? I keep checking her phone, hoping for a message, but nothing. I’m supposed to have my midterm next week and I can’t even focus. Just staring at the textbook feels impossible. I keep getting these little waves of just… wanting to disappear, I guess? It’s stupid, right? Just stressed about exams and a grandma’s health. But it’s like, everything is just… too much.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, it’s the anniversary of… you know. I just feel…blah. Like everything’s a little dimmer. Idk, maybe it's just the rain. Or maybe it’s just… me. I’m trying to distract myself, but it’s hard.

Ugh. Just… ugh. Another month, another mountain of bills. It’s stupid, right? I have a perfectly fine job, like, decent salary. But it feels like everything just… leaks out of me. Rent’s due, utilities, student loans – it’s just a constant drip, drip, drip and I’m just standing here with a bucket. I tried to budget, seriously I did. Made a spreadsheet, color-coded everything. It lasted, like, three days. I just stare at it and feel this… heavy feeling. Like I'm failing at something so basic. 

My parents offered to help, which is nice, I guess, but it’s also embarrassing. Like I’m admitting defeat. They don't understand, they always said "work hard, you’ll get there." Well, I *am* working hard, and I’m still drowning. It's not even exciting money, it’s just… survival money. And honestly, it's making me not want to do anything. Just… staying in bed feels easier, you know? Like a warm blanket over everything.  I tried to go for a walk today, but I just felt… pointless. Like I was walking around with nothing to show for it.  I don't even really enjoy things anymore.  I used to love going to concerts, painting, even just hanging out with friends, but now it all feels like a chore. Just another thing I *should* be doing, instead of just… being. I just want to disappear, not like, actually disappear, but just... fade away. It's just... exhausting.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
Okay seriously, I don't even know why I'm typing this. Everyone else seems to be, like, *doing* things. Studying, going out with groups, even just pretending to be busy. And me? I’m just… here. In my room. Staring at my laptop. The library is a nightmare, everyone’s whispering and highlighting and it just makes me feel even more useless. Like I'm just a ghost.

It’s exam season, obviously, which is already awful, but it’s not even the studying. It’s the *loneliness*. I haven’t spoken to anyone properly in days. My roommate’s gone home, and I just… don't want to bother her. What's the point? I’m just going to say something stupid or complain about failing, and she’ll probably just tell me to toughen up. Which, fine, whatever. But it still stings.

I keep thinking about how everyone else has their study groups, their friends they can lean on. I tried to message Liam about joining his group, but I just wrote "lol" and then deleted it. Felt stupid. Like I was trying too hard. It’s just… easier to be alone. It's not that I *like* being alone, it's just… it's the only thing that feels… neutral. Like nothing is expecting me. Like nothing matters. I just feel so… distant. Like I'm watching myself from a really weird, blurry window. I just want someone to acknowledge that I’m here, you know? That I exist. But it’s exhausting trying to make that happen. Maybe I should just skip the rest of the lectures. Just… disappear for a bit.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
Ugh, just spent an hour at my parents' tonight. It was…a lot. Seriously, I hate being around everyone. Like, I *know* they’re just trying to be nice, and they love me, but it's like a constant reminder of how far I am from, like, actually *living*. Mom kept asking about job prospects, Dad was rambling about how I should be "settling down" or something. It’s not that I don’t want a job, it’s that the thought of going into an office, dealing with people, just...it's exhausting. I told them I was tired, which is always my go-to, and they just nodded and said "you need to get out more." Get out *more*? Like, what, join a pottery class? Seriously? 

I just feel so…invisible, I guess. Like they don’t even see that I’m struggling. They see this awkward, quiet kid who still lives at home and they just assume I’m fine. It’s not. It’s just…everything feels gray. I don't even really feel like eating, and I just want to curl up in my room and listen to music. It’s stupid, I know, but every time I’m around people, I just feel this heavy weight in my chest. Like I’m suffocating. I just want to disappear, honestly. Not like, permanently, but just... away. Away from all of this. It's just...hard. Really hard.

Okay seriously, I don't even know how to say this but I’m just…empty. Like a deflated balloon. It’s not even the work thing, though that’s a *huge* part of it. Professor Thorne just keeps breathing down my neck about the dissertation, saying it's “behind schedule” and “lacking focus.” He doesn’t understand I have a baby now. A screaming, pooping, needing-food-every-two-hours baby. How am I supposed to write about post-structuralist theory when little Wren is throwing up on the rug? It’s just…constant.

And then my mom keeps calling, asking about the nursery, about when I'm going to "start living again." Like I wasn't living before! I was just…existing. Now I'm existing *and* drowning in a tidal wave of sleep deprivation and guilt. I keep snapping at Liam, and he just looks…defeated. He tries to help, he really does, but he doesn’t get it. He thinks I’m just stressed. He doesn't see this gray sludge coating everything. 

I just feel like I’m failing at *everything*. At being a student, at being a mom, at being a partner. I keep thinking about just…not going in tomorrow. Just staying home, letting Thorne deal with it. But then Wren needs me, Liam needs me, and I’d just be adding another layer of misery to the pile. It's not even a big thing, just a small thing, but it’s always there. Like a dull ache that just won't go away. I just want to curl up and disappear. Seriously, anyone else feel like this? Just…completely drained?

ugh i just… i don’t even know anymore. this dinner party was a disaster. honestly a complete and utter disaster. my daughter, elara, she’s been so quiet lately, just staring at her plate. she barely touched her food. and then her aunt started talking about her new job in london and how exciting it is and elara just… shrunk. i tried to steer the conversation, you know, asked her about her art, her music, anything, but it was like she wasn’t even there. like i wasn’t there.

i felt this awful pressure, like i was supposed to be smiling, making everyone comfortable, being the perfect host. but i just felt…heavy. like i was dragging a brick around. everyone was laughing and talking and i just wanted to disappear. i offered more wine, brought out the cheese board, kept refilling glasses, but it didn’t matter. it didn’t make anything better.

and then mark, her dad, he just looked at me, a really tired look, and said “you’re doing great, honey.” but it wasn’t a compliment, it felt like a judgment. like he expected me to be okay, to just *handle* everything. but i’m not okay. i’m exhausted. i’m just…tired of pretending. tired of putting on a brave face for everyone. elara needs me, mark needs me, and i feel like i’m failing at all of it. i just want to curl up and disappear into the couch. it’s getting harder and harder to even get out of bed some days, you know?  i just… i don’t know how much longer i can keep going like this. it's just...so much.

okay so like seriously i don't even know where to start. this move was supposed to be… good right? fresh start you know? new city new job everything. but it’s just… gray. really gray. i’m working the night shift again which is already awful but now i’m sleeping during the day and it’s like my brain just… refuses. it's like my body is telling me to rest but my head is screaming at me to do something anything. i unpacked the boxes yesterday it took like ten hours and now i just stare at them. they’re all neatly organized and it’s supposed to be comforting but it just makes me feel like i’m building a really expensive cage.

i keep expecting things to be… brighter. i downloaded a bunch of apps for exploring the city and i haven't opened any of them. i tried making coffee this morning and burnt it. burnt it like, really badly. i just sat there staring at the smoke for like five minutes. i don't even remember what i was thinking about. i feel so detached from everything. my roommate is nice she brought me cookies and asked if i was okay but i just mumbled something about being tired. i don't want to talk about it. honestly i don't even know *what* to say. i just feel... heavy. like i'm wading through mud. and i keep thinking about how everyone else seems to be having a good time, you know? going out, meeting people, laughing. and i'm just... here. sitting. watching the rain. it’s stupid i know but it’s just... a lot. i guess. i just wish it would stop feeling like this.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, just… everything feels so heavy today. It’s just… the kids are fighting again, and honestly, I don't even know why. They just yell and scream, and it’s exhausting. My husband keeps saying I need to just let them work it out, but I feel like I’m the only one holding things together. I try to be patient, I really do, but sometimes I just want to scream too. I feel like a failure, you know? Like I’m letting everyone down. I just… idk. Maybe I should just stay in bed. It would be so easy. It's not that I *want* to do anything, it’s just… this constant low hum of just feeling…blah.  I just wish things could be easier.  Maybe tomorrow will be better.  Probably not, though.

another night, another shift. everyone's asleep. just me and the fluorescent lights. it’s getting harder to even care about anything, you know? like a gray filter over everything. wish i could just turn it off.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, this exam season is just… a blur, honestly. Everything feels gray and pointless, like I’m just going through the motions, you know? And my mom keeps nagging about my grades, like I’m not already stressed enough, and my brother just doesn’t get it at all, it's just… so much.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: just moved here everything is expensive and i haven’t gotten a decent job yet. staring at my bank account again feels like a punch to the gut. i just want to curl up and not do anything, honestly. it's just... a lot.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: Just… everything feels heavy. Like wading through mud. Exam season is brutal, obviously, but it’s not just the stress. It’s… nothing, really. I don’t even know *why* I feel this way. Just…tired. All the time. I used to enjoy writing, you know? Essays, creative stuff. Now, even thinking about it makes my chest ache. My roommate keeps asking if I’m okay, and I just say “fine,” which is a lie. A big, stupid lie. I just want to curl up and disappear. Everyone else seems to be doing okay, crushing it with their studying. I’m just… stuck. This isn't getting any better and I don't know how to fix it.

Ugh. Just… ugh. Everything feels gray lately. Like someone turned the saturation down on the whole world. He said it was me, obviously. Said I was “too much,” “too intense.” Whatever. It doesn’t matter. It *really* doesn’t. I keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out where I messed up, but there’s nothing to figure out. It just… happened. And now I’m just… here. Walking around like a ghost. I don't even want to go to school tomorrow. Or practice guitar. Or talk to anyone. Just… existing. It’s exhausting. I just want him to disappear, like he did. Seriously considering just not showing up. Not really *trying* anymore. Just… not.
Just... everything feels pointless now. Used to have a job, a routine, a *thing*. Now it’s just gray. Like, really gray. Don’t even want to get out of bed. It's just... heavy.

ugh just… everything feels gray lately. it’s like the color drained out of everything after he left. i keep replaying things in my head, stupid little arguments, things i should have said, things i didn’t. it’s exhausting. i haven’t really eaten anything decent in days just grabbing crackers and coffee. my apartment feels so empty now, like a stage set after the show is over. i used to look forward to friday nights, now it just feels like another reminder that he’s not here. i don’t even know what to do with myself. i just… exist. honestly i don’t even really want to do anything. it’s just… a lot. i’m trying to keep busy at work but it’s all a blur. i just want to go back to how things were, i know that’s stupid but i can’t help it.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, I don't even know where to start. It’s just…everything feels so loud, you know? Like, I went to Sarah’s birthday dinner last night and it was, I don’t know, a *lot*. Everyone was laughing, talking about their jobs, their vacations, their…lives. And I just…sort of existed. I managed a few ‘that’s nice’ and ‘good for you’s’ but it felt so fake, like I was watching a movie of someone else’s life. Honestly, I think I just stared at my salad the whole time. I tried to make small talk with Mark – he’s always so cheerful – and I just ended up rambling about the weather. The weather! Seriously? I felt so awkward. Like, everyone else knew what was going on, they had their people, their routines. I just…have silence. A lot of silence.

And it's not just dinners. Like, even just going to the grocery store is hard. People are chatting in line, and I just want to disappear. I saw a couple holding hands and it just…hurt, I guess? It’s stupid, right? It’s not *wrong* to want to hold someone’s hand, but it just reminds me of…everything. I don’t even know why I’m telling you this. I just needed to say it out loud, I think. Maybe if I write it down, it’ll feel less…heavy. I just wish I could be normal again, you know? Like, just be able to go out and not feel like I'm failing at being a person. idk. Maybe that's too much to ask. I just feel so…disconnected. Like a ghost in my own life.  It’s just…ugh.

just… staring at my laptop screen again. it’s quiet here, too quiet. everyone’s busy, i guess. doesn’t feel like i’m part of anything anymore. just… existing. wish i could just turn off the lights and not think about it.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: Another year. Feels like a lifetime ago, but it’s still… here. Just… grey. Everything’s grey. I don’t even feel like eating. Just staring at the rain.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it's the anniversary. feels like yesterday and a lifetime ago all at the same time. i moved here six months ago, trying to outrun everything, but it just… follows me. this city’s huge and grey and i feel smaller than ever. everything just feels… muted. like someone turned the color down really low. i keep thinking about him. not like remembering good times, just… existing. like a dull ache that won't go away. i made pasta tonight. didn’t even really eat much. just sort of pushed it around the plate. it tasted like nothing. i keep staring out the window at the rain, and it just makes me feel… wetter. not just physically, but inside. it’s stupid, i know. everyone says “time heals all wounds” but it’s not like a wound that just closes up. it’s more like a hole that’s just… there. and you’re just living around it. i don’t even know why i’m writing this. probably just to prove to myself i’m still… something. i just wish i could forget. honestly, i wish i could just wake up and it hadn't happened. but i can't. and it just keeps getting worse. just… worse. i don’t want to do anything anymore. just… stay here. in this grey.

ugh honestly i don’t even know where to start. it’s just… everything feels so heavy lately. it started with grandpa’s fall, you know? tripped on the rug, nothing major they said but he’s been so confused since and it’s just… draining. i’m his primary caregiver now, my sister works too much and my parents are… well, they’re gone. so it’s just me. and he needs so much help with everything. getting him to eat, making sure he takes his meds, just keeping him company. i try to be cheerful, you know? tell him stories, play cards, but it just feels like i’m shouting into a void sometimes.

and then this morning the doctor called. something with his heart. they said they need to run more tests. more scans. more appointments. it’s a whole thing. i felt this… this wave just wash over me. not panic exactly, but just… emptiness. like the energy just completely drained out of me. i just sat on the sofa and stared at the wall for like an hour. i didn’t even make coffee. i just… felt like i couldn’t.

i keep telling myself it’s the stress, it’s the worry, it’s just the newness of everything. but it’s more than that, isn’t it? it’s like a dull ache that’s just… always there. like i'm wading through mud. i just want a quiet afternoon, you know? just to breathe. to not feel like i’m constantly holding everything together. it’s exhausting. and i feel so incredibly alone. i just wish things could be simpler. maybe easier.  i don’t even know what i’m trying to say. it’s just… a lot.

Seriously, what’s the point anymore? Lost my job last month, which was bad enough, but now this exam is looming. Like, a *huge* exam. I keep staring at the syllabus and it just…blurs. I haven't slept properly in days. My roommate keeps asking if I'm okay, but what does she even know? She's got her life, her job. It’s just…everything feels gray. I keep telling myself to just focus, study, do *something*, but I can't even manage to get out of bed some days. Feels like I'm just existing, not living. Just…waiting for it all to end, I guess. It's stupid to feel this way, right? It just is.

Just... ugh. Another day. Feels like wading through mud. Seriously, I can’t even bring myself to look at my bank account. It’s just a big, red, flashing warning sign. I keep telling myself to budget, to cut back, but it’s like my brain is just… numb. Everything feels pointless. Like, what’s the point of doing anything if I’m just going to end up broke and stressed? I had a job, a decent job, and now… nothing. Just this gaping hole where my paycheck used to be.

My mom keeps calling, asking if I’m eating. Like I don't know that. It’s not that I *don’t* want to eat, it’s that I don’t have the energy. Or the motivation. It’s exhausting just… existing. I tried to apply for a few things online, but honestly, reading through those job descriptions just makes me feel worse. It’s like everyone else is just… capable. Like they know what they’re doing. I just feel so behind, so lost. 

I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering if I could have done something differently. Maybe if I’d taken that extra shift, or asked for a raise… but it doesn’t matter now, does it? It's all just gone. I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear.  I keep thinking about bills, like they’re breathing down my neck.  It's just... heavy. Really, really heavy. I just don’t know how to make it stop.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Just…sitting here staring at the rain again, you know? It's always raining lately, feels like it’s just watching me. Everyone’s out doing things, laughing, and I just… I don't even want to get up, honestly. It’s just so quiet in this house, even with him, and I feel so utterly alone, like a ghost, I guess.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: Ugh, this is just…a lot. Everyone else seems to be actually studying, like, actually getting stuff done. I've just been staring at my notes for hours, rereading the same paragraph over and over. It's like my brain just… shuts off. I keep trying to force myself, but it’s like wading through mud. The deadline is next week and I’m nowhere near ready. I feel so stupid, like I'm failing already. My roommate keeps offering to help, but I just… push her away. It's easier to be alone with this feeling, this heavy, empty space. I just want it to be over. I don't even care about the grade anymore, honestly. Just… done.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: Ugh, this week has been… a lot. Professor Davies called my mom today. Said I looked really pale and shaky during the anatomy lab. Apparently I almost passed out. Seriously? I just haven’t eaten anything decent in like three days, just coffee and granola bars. Everything feels so heavy. I keep staring at my notes and they just blur. Like, what’s the point? I’m gonna fail every single exam, I know it. My roommate keeps offering me soup but I just… don't want it. It’s stupid, I guess. Just feeling totally drained and like I’m wading through mud. Everyone else seems to be getting it together, studying like maniacs, and I’m just… here.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, just… everything feels so heavy, like I’m wading through mud. This city is huge and lonely and I keep spending all my money on stupid things just to feel *something*, like takeout and those little sparkly pens. I don’t know how I’m going to pay rent this month and honestly, it’s just… overwhelming. I just want to curl up and disappear, I guess.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, this is hard to even write down, honestly. Everyone keeps saying congratulations, you’re amazing, you’re a natural… and I just… I don't feel it. Like, I *should* feel it. Little Leo is, you know, perfect. Tiny, sleepy, makes these little gurgling noises that are just… weirdly beautiful. But I just feel… drained. All the time. I went to Sarah’s baby shower yesterday and it was fine, I guess? I smiled a lot, I asked about the food, I pretended to be interested in the other babies. But I felt like everyone was looking at me, judging me, wondering why I wasn’t, like, glowing with maternal joy. And I just wanted to disappear. Seriously, I almost just left. I didn't want to talk to anyone. It’s probably stupid, right? Just a little bit of post-partum anxiety or something? I don’t know. Maybe. I just feel like I’m failing at being a mom. Like I’m not doing anything right. I’m exhausted, I can barely eat, and I keep apologizing for everything – for needing help, for taking a nap, for just… existing. I think I’m just trying to be a good mom, but it's so overwhelming. I just want to curl up in bed and not talk to anyone. Maybe tomorrow will be better? I hope. I really do. I just… I don’t know what to do anymore. It’s just… a lot, you know?

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: I just don’t get it, you know? Like, everything’s just…grey. Lost my job last week and now Mom’s on my case about bills and Dad just ignores everything, doesn’t even look at me. It’s like I’m invisible and I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly, it’s exhausting.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, i just... i hate going to school, like seriously. Everyone seems to be laughing and having a good time and i just feel like a ghost, you know? It’s just… everything feels so heavy lately, like i’m dragging a giant rock around everywhere i go, and i just want to disappear sometimes honestly.

just… everything feels so heavy. the doctor said it’s nothing serious but it’s still… this. and now i don’t even have a job. it’s like the universe is just piling on. i don't even want to get out of bed.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Ugh, just staring at the ceiling again. Can’t sleep. Everything feels…gray. Mom keeps asking if I’m okay, but what’s the point? Just another day of feeling like nothing.

Everything feels so…gray here. Like I'm just watching everyone else live their lives and it's not even close to me. I don't even want to go to school, honestly. Just wanna stay in my room and do nothing.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: it’s just… everything feels so heavy you know? like i’m wading through mud and every step just pulls me further down. i haven’t slept properly in like a week i just toss and turn staring at the ceiling. my roommate keeps asking if i’m okay but i just say yeah yeah fine whatever. it’s stupid i’m failing my history exam and i just don’t even care anymore. it’s like the motivation just completely drained out of me. i keep scrolling through my phone but nothing is interesting. i just feel… numb. i keep thinking about just going to bed and not waking up but that feels like a bad idea too. i just wish i could feel something other than this constant, dull sadness. i don’t even want to talk about it honestly. just… tired. really really tired.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so… this is just… a lot. I don’t even know where to start really. We moved last week, you know, to Willow Creek. It was supposed to be *better*, right? Less crowded, more space, close to Sarah’s college, supposedly. But it’s just… grey. Everything is grey. The sky, the houses, even the trees seem to be drooping a little. And Harold, he’s just… quiet. He doesn’t talk much anymore, just stares out the window at the rain. He used to love rainy days, remember? He’d build me little dams in the yard with the rocks and we’d watch the water rush around. Now he just sighs. It’s like… a part of him just went missing when we left the old house. I keep expecting him to ask about something, to tell me about his day, but he doesn’t. I unpacked everything, you know? Every single box. It’s all here, neatly stacked, but it doesn’t *feel* like home. It feels… sterile. Like a museum exhibit of our life, not a place to live. I keep going through the motions – making dinner, doing laundry, trying to find a good doctor – but it’s all just… exhausting. It’s like wading through mud, and I don’t even know where the shore is. I just… I miss him talking. I miss the chaos of the old house, even the arguing. It felt… real. Here, it’s just… quiet. Too quiet. I keep thinking about packing everything up and going back, but then I look at Sarah and I know I can’t do that. She needs me here. But what do *I* need? Honestly, I just feel… heavy. Like a stone. And I don’t know how to lift it.

Ugh. Just… ugh. They told me it was probably nothing, a little scare. A “minor arrhythmia.” Minor. Like it’s minor to be sitting here, staring at the ceiling, feeling like a lead weight. My husband, bless his heart, he’s been fussing over me all day. Bringing me tea, checking my pulse, asking if I’m hungry. He doesn’t understand. He doesn't *get* it. He thinks I’m just tired. I *am* tired. Bone-deep tired.

It's not just the hospital visit, though that was awful. It's… everything. Taking him to his appointments, making sure he takes his meds, cooking, cleaning, trying to keep the house from falling apart while he’s… well, he’s just *being* him. He’s a good man, a sweet man, but he needs so much. And I feel like I’m drowning, just trying to keep his head above water.

I used to have hobbies, you know? Painting, reading. Simple things. Now… now it’s just this. This endless cycle of worry and doing. I look in the mirror and I don’t even recognize myself. Just… faded. Like a photograph left out in the sun. It's not that I don’t love him, of course I do. It’s just… it’s so much. I don't even know how much longer I can keep doing this. It’s just… heavy. Really heavy.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text:
just… spent the last three hours staring at my takeout container. it’s like, half-eaten Pad Thai and it feels like a monument to my failure. everyone else seems to be out doing stuff, you know? like, Sarah just posted about a pottery class she took and it looks… nice. I don’t even know what pottery is, but it looks nice. And Liam went to a concert with his friends and he has, like, a thousand pictures. I haven’t even left my apartment today. Seriously. Just. Here.

I keep thinking about how stupid it is that I moved here. Everyone’s so… energetic. Like they actually *want* to be here. Me? I feel like I’m just drifting. Like a piece of plastic in the current. I tried to text Maya, you know, to see if she wanted to grab coffee, but I just wrote “hey” and then immediately deleted it. What’s the point? She’d probably just ask me what’s wrong, and I’d just… well, I don’t know *what* is wrong. It’s just… everything. It’s a constant low hum, like a refrigerator that’s about to die. And it’s not even exciting, it’s just… grey. I just want to crawl back into bed and not feel anything, but then I feel *more* something. Like a dull ache. This city is just… loud. And I’m just… quiet. And alone. Really, really alone. I wish i could just disappear.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay so like... this is probably going to sound really stupid, but i just… i don’t know what to do anymore. baby’s been so fussy lately, just screaming and crying and i’ve been trying to figure out what’s wrong. the doctor said it’s probably just gas or something, you know? just a phase. but it feels like *more* than that. he’s been so clingy, always wanting to be held and i just… i don’t have the energy. i mean, i *want* to, i really do, but it’s like my brain just… shuts down sometimes. i feel this constant, heavy sadness, like a blanket wrapped around everything.

yesterday he spiked a fever, a really high one, and i was just… panicked. i called the nurse line and they said to give him Tylenol but it didn't seem to help much. i just sat there holding him, rocking him, and it just felt… pointless. like i was failing at something. i keep thinking maybe i’m not a good mom, maybe i'm doing something wrong, and it just spirals. i keep telling myself it's the exhaustion, obviously, it’s been ten weeks straight, no sleep, just feeding and changing and trying to keep him alive. but it's not *just* the exhaustion, is it? i think. idk.

i just want things to go back to how they were, even if it was just before the baby. i miss just being *me*. i miss doing things i enjoy, like reading or going for walks. i used to love to paint, but i haven’t picked up a brush in months. maybe it’s just the hormones, maybe it's this new responsibility. or maybe… maybe it's just too much. i just feel so… overwhelmed. and lonely, even when he’s right here with me. i just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. i don't want to be a burden. i don't know what to do.

text: okay so like seriously this week has been a mess. i’m working these night shifts at the warehouse, you know, stacking boxes until my back feels like it’s going to just…give out? and it’s not even a *good* warehouse, it’s just…grey and smells like cardboard and regret. my supervisor, Mr. Henderson, he’s always breathing down my neck, saying “efficiency, efficiency, efficiency!” like my whole worth is tied to how many boxes i can move in an hour. it’s exhausting, honestly. and then i have this online class, right? History of Medieval Textiles. Seriously? I’m trying to learn about tapestries while I’m basically crumbling into dust. i keep falling asleep in class, and the professor just glares. he doesn’t seem to understand that i’m just...existing, you know? not functioning. i keep thinking about just emailing him and saying i can't do it anymore, just disappearing, but then i’d fail the class and i’d probably get fired, and then where would i even go? i just feel so…heavy. like i’m wading through mud all the time. i used to enjoy drawing, i really did, but i haven’t picked up a pencil in weeks. everything just feels pointless. i keep replaying conversations in my head, things i said, things i didn’t say, and it's just... a constant loop of self-criticism. i don’t even want to talk to anyone, it’s easier just to…be quiet.  i just want to sleep, but then i wake up and it’s still grey and still full of boxes and still full of Medieval Textiles. i just...don't know what to do anymore.

text: Ugh, seriously, I don’t even know where to start. It’s just…everything feels so heavy lately. Like, I graduated last month, which should be amazing, right? Everyone’s posting about their dream jobs and moving out and being all independent. And I’m…here. Still at my parents’ place. Still eating mac and cheese three nights a week. I know, I know, it’s not *that* bad, but it feels like it is. I’m supposed to be building my career, making something of myself, and instead I’m staring at a mountain of student loan bills and a job application that just feels…pointless. I applied to like, a hundred places, and nothing. Just silence. I keep telling myself it’s just a slow process, that companies take time, but…idk. Maybe I’m just not good enough. My mom keeps saying “you’ll find something” but it’s like she doesn’t even *get* it. Like she thinks it’s just a temporary thing, a little bump in the road. It's not, I think. It's...persistent. I just feel...empty. Like the joy has kinda drained out of things. I went for a walk yesterday, tried to clear my head, but even the sunshine felt grey. I keep wondering if I should just…give up, you know? Not like, give up on living, but give up on trying to *be* something. Maybe it's just the pressure of expectations, both my own and everyone else’s. I feel like I'm failing at being an adult. It's just...a lot. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly.

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a dumb thing to even post about, but… I just feel… heavy. Like wading through mud, you know? It’s not *bad* bad, just… gray. Everything feels gray. I went to that client meeting today and just, blanked. Completely spaced out. Didn’t really say anything, just nodded and smiled, which feels so fake now. I’m pretty sure I didn’t even make eye contact with anyone. I’m supposed to be climbing the ladder, right? Making something of myself? But… idk. It just feels pointless, honestly.

I keep thinking about my mom and how she always said I should be more ambitious, more driven. And I feel like I’m failing her, failing myself. It’s not that I *want* to fail, it’s just… it’s happening. Slowly. Like a leak in a dam. I've been trying to force myself to do things – go for a run (failed miserably), call a friend (felt awful), even just tidy my apartment (spent an hour staring at the dust bunnies). It’s exhausting.

I don’t even really *want* to do anything, you know? I used to love going out, meeting people, planning things. Now, the thought of making plans makes my stomach clench. Maybe it's just a phase? Maybe I'm just stressed from work? I don't know. I just… I feel like I'm watching my life from behind a really thick pane of glass. Everything is happening, but I’m not really *there*. I’m just… existing. It's… heavy. Really heavy. And I just want to curl up and disappear for a little bit, maybe? Just… for a little while.

text:
Ugh, I just... I don't even know where to start. It’s like, everything’s just gray lately. Seriously, everything. I’m working the graveyard shift at the warehouse again, and it’s just… draining. Like, the forklift feels heavier every night, and the boxes just sit there, mocking me. I’m supposed to be stacking them, you know? Efficient. Productive. But I just… can't. My brain feels like it's wading through mud. I keep messing up orders, and Mr. Henderson – he’s always yelling – and I just want to disappear. It's not even the yelling, it’s the *feeling* of it, like I’m failing at something so basic.

And then, there's the essay. This insane, huge history paper due next week. I started it, like, three days ago, and I’ve barely written a paragraph. It’s just… pointless, really. What’s the point of learning about stuff that’s already happened when it doesn't actually *matter* to me? I keep telling myself I’ll just pull an all-nighter, fueled by coffee and desperation, but… idk. It just feels… exhausting. Like pushing a boulder uphill. Maybe I should just drop the class. No, don’t do that. That would be admitting defeat, right? But what *is* success anyway? Just meeting arbitrary deadlines and pleasing people I don’t even connect with? I just... I feel so tired all the time. Not just physically, but… emotionally. Like there's this weight on my chest that won’t go away. I just want to curl up in a ball and not feel anything. It’s probably nothing, really. Just a bad week. A really bad week.

text: it’s just… weird today, you know? like, the sky is the same color but everything feels… muted. it’s the anniversary, i guess. a year since… well, you know. it’s stupid, really, letting it sit there like this, a little stone in my stomach that just keeps growing. i tried to study for my finals, seriously, i was staring at the textbook about, like, the history of quantum physics and i just… couldn’t. it was all just meaningless symbols and equations, and all i could think about was him. it's ridiculous, isn’t it? quantum physics and… and him. like they’re connected somehow. it probably isn’t. i just feel… hollow. like someone scooped out all the good stuff and left a shell. i keep replaying everything in my head, little moments, big ones, everything we ever said, everything we never said. and it’s not… comforting. it's just… a constant reminder. everyone else is talking about exam stress and coffee and late-night study sessions, and i just... i don’t get it. it’s like I’m watching a movie of someone else’s life. i keep wanting to call him, just to hear his voice, but then i remember... and it just makes it worse. i just feel so tired, honestly. not physically, not really, but… emotionally. just… so incredibly, overwhelmingly tired. and the worst part is, i don’t even know why i’m feeling this way. it’s just… here. it just *is*.  i just want it to stop being today. i just want to feel... something other than this gray.

text: Ugh, just…can’t sleep. Like, at all. Been staring at the ceiling for hours. Mom keeps asking if I’m okay, which is…nice, I guess. But it’s not, you know? Just feels like everything is gray. I finished my degree like, a month ago and now I’m back here, folding laundry and watching reruns of shows I’ve seen a million times. Everyone else seems to have a plan, a job, something. I just feel… stuck. Like I’m supposed to be *doing* something, but I don't even know what. It’s stupid. It’s just… exhausting. I feel like I’m wading through mud, every step takes ten times the effort. I just want to disappear for a little while, honestly.

Ugh. Another night. Seriously, who designed these shifts? Feels like I'm living in a grey box. Mom's on my case again, about the bills. Always about the bills. Like I don’t work my butt off, twelve-hour shifts, just to keep us afloat. She doesn't *get* it. Says I should have "done more" with my life, before I got stuck here. Like I chose this. Like I *wanted* to be a cog in the machine, staring at flickering screens all night. My brother called, too. Just to tell me how great *his* life is. New job, fancy apartment… the whole deal. He doesn't even try to understand. It’s like I’m invisible. Just… there. A shadow. I keep replaying everything, every stupid thing I’ve said, every time I didn't respond. It's exhausting. Everything feels heavy. Just… heavy. I just want to curl up and disappear. I haven’t eaten anything decent all week, just grabbing whatever’s in the fridge. It's all just… pointless. I don't even know why I bother getting out of bed some days. It's just... a routine of misery. I just wish it would stop. Just... stop.

text: It’s… it’s just weird today, you know? Like the air feels heavier, and everything’s just a little blurry around the edges. It’s the anniversary. Three years. Three years since… well, since everything changed. I keep expecting to wake up and it’ll all be a bad dream, honestly, but it doesn’t. It just… stays. I tried to do something nice, made his favorite stew – the one with the mushrooms and the thyme he loved so much – but I just… couldn’t eat it. It smelled like him, you know? Like *him*, and it just made me feel… hollow. Everyone keeps saying it gets easier, that you learn to live with it, but it doesn’t feel like that. It feels like a constant ache, a dull throb right behind my ribs. I went for a walk earlier, just wandered around the park, trying to clear my head, and I saw a little girl laughing with her dad, and it just… stung. I don’t know. I just feel so…empty. Like all the color’s been drained out of everything. I keep going through the motions, taking care of the house, making sure everything’s okay, but it’s like I’m watching myself do it, not actually *feeling* it. It’s exhausting. Just…exhausting. I wish he was here to tell me it’d be alright, but he’s not. And I don’t know how to do this without him. Just… a lot. A huge, heavy, gray lot.

text: it’s just… so loud here. like constantly. i moved like six months ago and i thought maybe being alone would be good you know? a fresh start. it hasn't been fresh at all. it's just… grey. my mom keeps calling, every single day. mostly just telling me i’m making a mistake, that i should come home. like i haven't spent the last six months trying to figure things out, trying to just *be*. she doesn't get it. she thinks i'm just being dramatic because i didn't immediately thrive in a new place. she says i'm disappointing her. and my brother, he sends those little memes about 'adulting' and it just makes me feel even worse. like i'm failing at something everyone else seems to be doing so effortlessly. i keep replaying everything that happened back home, the fights, the silence, the way my dad just… looked away. it's like if i just go back, it’ll all be okay. but i know that’s not it. it never is. i tried to explain i needed space, that i wasn't ready, but it just made it worse. she says i'm pushing everyone away. maybe i am. maybe i just don't know how to be close to anyone anymore. it’s hard to even get out of bed some days. just… hard. i just wanted a little bit of peace, and i just keep getting more and more overwhelmed. i don't even know why i’m typing this. just… a lot.

Ugh, just… another night. Feels like everyone’s yelling at me even when they’re not saying anything. Mom keeps leaving those little notes on the fridge about dinner, like I don’t already know I have to cook. And Dad, he just… sighs a lot. We had this huge fight earlier, about the rent.

text:
Ugh. Just… ugh. This project is eating me alive. It's supposed to be a simple update, right? Just a few tweaks to the report, some updated figures. But it’s like… everything is wrong. The numbers don’t add up, the formatting is all off, and honestly, I can't even *look* at it without feeling this heavy dread. My daughter needs this report for her internship, and I promised her I’d get it done. She’s been so stressed lately, working double shifts at the diner, and I just… I just want to make things easier for her.

It’s not even the report itself, is it? It’s everything. It’s the feeling that I’m failing at everything. I used to be good at this, you know? Really good. Now… now it just feels like a monumental effort, and I’m dragging myself through mud. I keep staring at the clock, and it's mocking me. Just a few more hours and it's due. And then what? Then I’ll probably just sit here and stare at the ceiling, feeling like I've wasted the whole week. Maybe I should just tell her I can't do it. That would be easier, wouldn’t it? At least then she wouldn't be disappointed. But then I’d let her down. And I can't. I just… can't. I need to get this done. I just don’t know how.

text:
Ugh, it’s… it’s the anniversary today. Of the thing. You know, the accident. I didn’t even realize it was today until my mom messaged. She just sent a stupid picture of us from that summer, all smiling and carefree. Like, *before*. It’s just… a lot. I mean, it’s been almost two years, but it still feels like yesterday. Everything feels… muted, I guess? Like I’m watching a movie of my life and the colors are just really, really dull.

I haven’t really been doing anything. Just… existing, I think. I went to the grocery store yesterday, bought, like, five different kinds of ice cream. It didn’t help. It just made me feel… guilty, maybe? Like I'm indulging in something frivolous when everything else is so… gray. I lost my job last month. Which, okay, that's bad enough on its own, right? But it just felt like… the universe was really laying it on thick. I don’t even know why I’m bothering to type this out, honestly. I dunno. It’s just… sitting here, scrolling, and I feel this heavy pressure in my chest. Like a weight. I keep thinking about all the plans we had, you know? The trips we were going to take, the things we were going to do. They're just… gone. I try to remember the good times, but it’s like they’re underwater, you know? Distorted and fading. Maybe I should just go back to sleep. Or maybe just… not. I don’t even know. It’s just… hard. Really, really hard. I just want to disappear, honestly. Not like, end it or anything, just… fade away. It's just... a really, really low mood lately. I just feel…empty.

Ugh, just went to that work thing and felt like everyone was having a better time than me. Like, seriously, why does it always feel this way? I just wanna disappear into my apartment and not talk to anyone. It’s exhausting being around people.

ugh okay so like i just spent the last three hours at that work thing. you know the quarterly team meeting? it was… awful. everyone was just so…loud. like, genuinely loud. and everyone was smiling so brightly and talking about projections and synergy and i just wanted to disappear. i swear i felt my face getting hotter and hotter the whole time. i tried to contribute, like, i actually had a decent idea about streamlining the data entry process, but it just got completely derailed by some guy named brian who kept talking about his golf game. seriously. golf.

and then afterwards, everyone was going out for drinks. i said i wasn’t feeling great, just needed to head home, but sarah kept insisting i join her. she’s so… optimistic. it’s exhausting. i just wanted to be alone with my silence and my reheated leftovers. honestly i think i just stood there nodding and smiling for like an hour, feeling completely drained. it’s like i’m a ghost at these things. i don’t even know what to say, or how to react. it’s just… a lot. i keep replaying it in my head and i just feel this heavy, empty feeling. like i’m failing at being a normal person, you know? and it's not even that i *want* to be a normal person, it’s just… i don’t know how to *do* it anymore. it feels like everyone else has a script and i’m just wandering around lost. i just wish i could turn off the noise, the expectations, the pressure. i just want to go back to sleep. honestly i just feel so tired all the time.

text: Okay seriously, this is just... exhausting. I moved here three weeks ago. Three. Weeks. And I still feel like I’m just…existing. Like a ghost in my own apartment. It’s small, I picked it because it was cheap and close to work, which is fine, I guess, but the walls just seem to close in on me at night. Everyone at work is nice, which is good, but it’s like, polite smiles and small talk about the weather. No one actually *gets* it. I tried calling my mom, but she just kept saying “You’re being dramatic!” which, yeah, maybe. But it’s not dramatic to feel like you’re drowning and everyone’s just politely observing. I went out to a coffee shop yesterday and just sat there for an hour, staring at the foam. Just…staring. I didn’t even order anything. I just wanted to be somewhere that wasn't *here*. It’s not like I’m actively unhappy or anything, it’s just…grey. Everything’s just grey. I keep telling myself it’ll get better, that I’ll make friends, that I’ll find a routine, but it feels like a really, really slow burn, and I’m already out of fuel. I miss having people to talk to. I miss feeling…anything other than this heavy, dull ache. I should probably go do something. Anything. But…what’s the point?

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text:
Okay seriously, this is just… a lot. I moved here three weeks ago. Three weeks. And it feels like I’ve been standing still. Everyone here seems to already *know* each other, you know? Like they’ve been friends forever. I try to say hi to people in the hallway, just a quick “good morning,” but they just glance at me and keep walking. Or they smile politely and then immediately turn back around. It’s not mean, I guess, but it’s… isolating.

My apartment is nice, it’s small but clean, and I have a view of the street which is mostly just cars and people rushing past. I ordered takeout last night – Thai, which is usually my comfort food – but I just… stared at it. Couldn’t really taste anything. Just felt full and heavy.

I keep telling myself it’s just the adjustment period, that I’ll settle in eventually. That making new friends is hard. That’s what everyone says, right? But it’s not *hard*. It’s… like I don’t even *want* to try. Like the effort of putting myself out there is just too exhausting. I miss my old friends, obviously, but they’re miles away. And honestly, just talking to them feels like a monumental task. 

I keep scrolling through my phone, looking at pictures of my old life. It’s stupid, I know. But it’s… easier. Just letting myself feel sad about it. It doesn’t feel like *doing* anything, which is kind of the point, isn't it? I just… I don't know what to do with this feeling. It’s just… heavy. Like a brick in my chest.  I just want to curl up and disappear.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: It’s the anniversary. Feels like yesterday it all happened. Just… numb. Everything is grey. Used to have a job, a life, now it's just this quiet ache. Don’t even want to get out of bed.

Ugh, just got back from Sarah's birthday dinner. And I feel… worse. Like, actively worse. Everyone was laughing, telling stories, and I just… watched. Didn’t really participate, you know? Tried to make a joke about the cake and it just died. Seriously, it felt like the whole room was brighter, louder, and I was just this gray shadow. I keep replaying it in my head, all the little things I *should* have said, all the times I just stared into my wine glass.

It’s stupid, right? A birthday party. Shouldn’t be this hard. But it always is. I hate pretending to be okay. I hate forcing myself to smile and nod and make small talk about the weather. It’s exhausting. Like I’m wearing a suit of armor that’s slowly crushing me. I feel like everyone else knows what they’re doing, they’re navigating all the social complexities effortlessly, and I'm just fumbling around, tripping over my own feet.

My job’s okay, I guess. Pays the bills. But even that feels… pointless sometimes. Like I’m just going through the motions. I keep thinking about skipping work tomorrow, just staying in bed all day. Not even bothering to shower. It’s not a big deal, I know. But just the thought of having to face another day, another set of interactions, just… it's too much. I don’t even know why I bother going out anymore. It’s just… draining. I just want to disappear for a little while, you know? Just fade away.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s been a year today. feels like yesterday and a lifetime ago all at once. just… numb. everything feels grey. i keep scrolling through old photos, pretending it didn’t happen but it just keeps hitting me harder and harder. don’t even want to talk about it.
just... everything feels gray lately. like the colors bled out somewhere. i don’t even want to do the things i used to enjoy. it’s just… a constant, heavy feeling. i don’t know why.
Everything feels…gray. Mom’s fall was bad, really bad. Just staring at the walls now. Don’t really want to do anything. It’s just… too much. Like a weight I can’t shake off.

text: Okay so like seriously, I’m just… floating. I haven’t really been sleeping, just these little bursts of exhaustion and then waking up feeling like I’ve run a marathon and haven’t even moved. And Leo, bless his little heart, he’s amazing, he really is, but he just wants to be held all the time and he’s crying a lot and I feel like I’m failing him already, you know? It’s not that I’m a bad mom, I genuinely don’t think that, but everything feels so overwhelming. I have this huge project due at work next week, a really important one, and I’m just staring at the screen and it’s all just…blurry. Like, I can see the words but I can't *do* anything. It’s like my brain is full of cotton wool and everything just feels so distant. I keep telling myself I’ll just get a few hours in tonight, or tomorrow morning, but then the thought of facing it just makes me want to curl up in a ball and not do anything at all. It’s just… a lot. I feel so useless, like I'm supposed to be this amazing, capable person and I can’t even manage to finish a simple work project. And then there’s Leo, and he needs me, and I just want to protect him from this feeling, this heavy, grey feeling that’s just… stuck. I wish I could just switch it off, you know? Just… stop feeling this way. I just want things to be normal again, or at least…easier. I feel like I’m drowning and I can’t find a lifeline.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Ugh, just… everything feels so heavy today. Like I’m wading through mud, you know? It’s stupid, I know, complaining about stuff like this online, but I don’t really talk to anyone about it. Mom and Dad are… well, they’re constantly sniping. It’s like a low-level war zone in my house, and I’m just trying to survive it. They’re arguing about finances again, which always seems to escalate into accusations and guilt trips. It's exhausting. I try to stay out of it, just… exist in the kitchen or something, but it just seeps in.

I feel like I’m failing at being an adult. Like, I have a job I kinda like, I pay my bills (mostly), but it’s never *enough*, is it? They want me to be more involved, more… present, but honestly, just thinking about having a conversation with them feels draining. I just want to disappear sometimes, like go live in a cabin in the woods and grow vegetables. That sounds… nice, actually. A little too simple, maybe?

I keep replaying things that happened last night in my head. Little things, really, but they feel huge now. Like, Dad made a comment about how I “should” be further along in my career, and it just… stung. It’s always something, isn’t it? I don’t even know why I bother trying to please them. I just… do. I don't think I deserve a break. Maybe I’m just being dramatic. Idk. It’s just… a lot. I just want a day where I don’t feel like I’m constantly carrying the weight of everyone else’s disappointment. Maybe tomorrow will be better? Probably not, honestly. Just… feeling really blah.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Ugh, I just… I don't even know what to do anymore. Everyone's yelling, you know? My mom keeps saying I'm not trying hard enough, like I’m deliberately failing my exams. Which, okay, maybe I’m not *trying* as hard as I used to, but it’s just… so much. Everything feels like a weight. Like I’m wading through mud, and every step just pulls me deeper. I keep staring at my notes, but the words just swim on the page. I read them over and over, and it’s like I’m not even absorbing anything. It’s just… noise.

And then my dad’s on about how I’m spending all my time locked in my room, ignoring the family. He doesn’t get it, obviously. He thinks I’m just being lazy or dramatic. But it’s not that! It’s just… hard. Really hard. I feel like if I step outside, everything will collapse. Like one wrong word will set everything off. I tried to talk to my brother, Liam, but he just said “pull yourself together” and walked away. Typical.

I just want to disappear, honestly. Like, not in a bad way, just… to be somewhere quiet. Somewhere where no one expects anything from me. Maybe I should just drop out. I don’t know. It feels like such a massive, stupid thing to even consider, but… idk. I’m probably overthinking it, right? Probably just stressed. Everyone gets stressed during exams, it’s normal. But it doesn’t *feel* normal. It feels… heavy. Like I’m carrying the whole world on my shoulders. Maybe if I just sleep for a few hours everything will be okay. But I know that’s probably not true. It just feels like a temporary escape. I just… I don't know.

I made his favorite casserole, even though he hated it. It just sat there, untouched. Maybe I should have just left it. I don’t know. I just feel… tired. Like I’m carrying a weight that’s just too big. I'm not sure what to do anymore. Maybe I’ll just… sleep.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, I just… I don’t even know what to do anymore. Everyone keeps saying “you’re so smart, you should be thriving” and it’s just… crushing. Like, I got this internship, which is supposed to be amazing, but it’s just a mountain of paperwork and pointless meetings and my boss keeps breathing down my neck. And then I’m supposed to be applying for jobs, but honestly, the thought of even looking at a job description makes my stomach churn. Mom keeps asking if I’m “settling” and Dad just looks… disappointed. I feel like I’m failing at everything, like I’m just not cut out for any of this. It’s just a gray blob of everything, you know? I used to be excited about the future, now it just feels… heavy. I just want to curl up in bed and not feel anything, which is, like, the worst, right?

it’s just… everything feels gray lately like someone turned the saturation way down and i’m just watching the world go by in muted tones. i don’t even know why. it wasn’t like… like there was one big fight or something. it just… fizzled. sam and i, we just stopped talking really. not yelling or anything, just… not. like the air between us got thick and heavy and we were both just pretending it wasn’t there. and then suddenly, it wasn’t anymore. and now it’s just this quiet, empty space where there used to be him. i keep expecting to hear his laugh or see his stupid slippers by the door or something but it’s just… nothing. i tried calling him last night, left three voicemails. nothing. i guess he doesn't want to talk. which, honestly, i kind of understand. i don’t really want to talk either. i don’t even know what to say. i went through all our photos last week, just scrolling through them, and it felt like… like looking at someone else’s life. like i was a ghost in his memories. i used to love going to the movies with him, just holding hands in the dark. now i just… watch them online alone. it’s pathetic isn’t it? i just feel… tired. so, so tired. like i could sleep for a week and it still wouldn't help. i don’t even want to do the things i used to enjoy. nothing feels good anymore. just… gray.

Just…can’t sleep. Used to be I’d drift off instantly. Now it’s just staring at the ceiling, thinking about him. Everything feels gray and heavy. Like I’m underwater. I don’t even know why I bother trying to close my eyes.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay seriously, I don't even know what's happening anymore. It’s just… a constant gray. Like the sky after a really bad storm, you know? I keep staring at Leo, my little guy, and he's just… perfect. Absolutely perfect. And I should be overjoyed, right? I *should* be bursting with love and happiness and wanting to show him off to the world. But mostly I just feel… exhausted. And angry. Mostly angry. My husband, Silas, he just doesn’t *get* it. He’s so busy, so focused on getting back to ‘normal’ – which is a ridiculous concept, honestly – that he barely notices I'm drowning. He keeps saying things like “Just relax, it’s a baby, it’s hard” and “You’re being dramatic” and it just… stings. Like he’s minimizing everything.

Last night, we had a fight. About the laundry. Seriously, the laundry. He said I was being unreasonable about folding the tiny socks, and I just… snapped. I yelled, and I cried, and I accused him of not helping at all. He said I was being needy and that he was working overtime. And I just… I just wanted him to *see* me. To see how much this is taking out of me. How I can’t even get out of bed some days, let alone manage a mountain of socks. I feel like I’m failing at everything. Being a mom, being a wife, just… existing. It’s like I’m constantly trying to hold onto something that’s slipping through my fingers. And Silas just doesn't seem to be there to catch me. I just feel so alone, like I'm shouting into a void. I wish things could just... go back to the way they were. Before Leo. Before everything. It’s not that I don't love him, it’s just… everything feels so overwhelming. I keep thinking, what if I’m a terrible mother?

Just moved into this tiny apartment. Feels… empty. Like everything I owned just vanished with the divorce. Don’t even know why I bothered. Rain outside, matches the mood, I guess. Seriously just don’t want to do anything anymore.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s… it’s weird isn’t it? today’s the anniversary. feels like yesterday and a million years ago all at once. i keep expecting to wake up and realize it's all a bad dream, like that awful time… i don’t even want to think about it. just… it’s here. another year. and nothing’s changed really. i went to work, i ate dinner, i scrolled through my phone until my eyes hurt. it’s the same routine. the same hollow feeling. everyone else seems to be celebrating, posting happy pictures and reminiscing about good times. and i just… i feel this dull ache, like a constant low hum. like the color’s been drained out of everything. i tried to call mom, but i just hung up. i don’t want to talk about it. i don’t want to *feel* about it. it just sits there, this heavy weight in my chest. i keep thinking about how much simpler things were before… before everything went wrong. before i felt like i was constantly drowning, even when i was standing in water. i know i should be grateful for what i have, for my job, for my apartment, for… for everything. but it’s just… hard. so incredibly hard. i just want to disappear sometimes, you know? not like, actually disappear, but just… fade away. become invisible. just stop feeling. i don't even know why i'm writing this. it just felt like i needed to say something, anything. even if it’s just to the void.

it’s just… so much. the machines are screaming tonight and i can’t even focus on the screens. this deadline is going to swallow me whole i swear. i keep staring at the spreadsheet and it just looks like a mess of numbers and charts and i don’t even know what i’m doing anymore. like i’m wading through mud and every step is heavier than the last. i haven’t slept properly in days and my head is just… fuzzy. everyone else seems to be moving so fast, like they know exactly what they’re doing. i just feel… numb. it's like the world is muted and i’m just watching it happen. i don’t even want to talk to anyone. just… gotta finish this. just gotta get through tomorrow. i keep thinking about failing and it’s not even the grade, it's just the feeling of being useless. ugh.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Ugh, honestly, I don't even know where to start. It's just... everything feels heavy lately, you know? Like I'm wading through mud, and every step is a monumental effort. My daughter, Elara, she’s just...difficult. She's always been a bit prickly, but lately it's just gotten worse. She keeps saying I’m suffocating her, that I don’t understand her life, that I'm always hovering and judging her choices. And maybe she’s right, a little bit. I just... I want to help, you know? I’ve spent the last ten years practically living her life, taking care of Dad after he got sick, juggling work and bills and everything else. I thought I was doing a good job, a *necessary* job, but now... now it feels like I’m failing.

And my son, Liam, he barely even speaks to me anymore. Just grunts and avoids eye contact when I try to talk to him. He's got his own problems, I get it, but it's just so isolating. It’s like I'm this invisible person, just existing in the background. I try to offer support, to just listen, but it always seems to backfire. He just shuts down. I feel like I'm constantly disappointing everyone, and that’s just... crushing. I keep thinking about all the things I’ve missed, all the moments I’ve been so focused on someone else’s needs that I haven’t really *lived* my own. It’s just a dull ache, mostly, this feeling, but it’s always there, underneath everything. Like a gray cloud hanging over my head. I just wish it would stop being so exhausting. I just... I just want it to stop.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Ugh, seriously. This deadline is just… crushing me. I moved here like, six months ago, right? Everything was supposed to be exciting, a fresh start, you know? But it’s just… grey. And this exam is looming over me like a giant, judgmental cloud. I’m supposed to be studying, I really am, but I just… stare at the textbook and it looks like a bunch of meaningless symbols. I keep starting chapters, like, halfway through, and then getting distracted by, like, a dust bunny or something. It's embarrassing, I know.

I feel so useless. Everyone else here seems to be thriving, posting about their internships and new friends and cool cafes. I just... exist. I made some tentative plans to meet someone from a photography group, but I cancelled. What's the point? It’ll probably be awkward and I’ll feel even more out of place. I don't even *like* photography, really. It's just… something to do, I guess.

I keep telling myself it’s just stress, that it’ll pass. But it doesn't feel like it's passing. It's just… settling in. Like a damp blanket. I haven’t really eaten anything decent in days, just grabbing whatever’s in the fridge. I feel heavy, you know? Like I could just lie down and… not get up. I don’t even want to call my mom. She’d worry, and I don't want to burden her. It’s just… a lot. I idk, I just feel so lost. Maybe I’ll try to watch a movie later. Or just… stare at the wall. I don't know. It's okay, right? It’s just a deadline. Right?

Seriously, I don't even know what's happening anymore. Liam’s amazing, of course he is, he’s the cutest little thing ever. But…everything else is just…gone. Like, completely gone. We’re barely eating, just whatever’s on sale at the grocery store. I tried to look for a second job, but I can’t. I can’t leave him. And the bills…the bills are piling up. It's just a constant, heavy weight. I keep staring at him and thinking, “I’m failing him.” I know that's stupid, he doesn't even understand, but it’s there. It’s just…a dull ache, all the time. I used to love mornings, you know? Getting up and having coffee, reading a book. Now, it's just getting up to change a diaper and try to figure out how to pay the electricity bill. It's exhausting. I feel like I'm drowning and no one can hear me. I just...I just want to curl up and sleep, but then I worry about Liam. He needs me. And I feel like I’m not good enough for him. I'm so tired. Just...so, so tired. I don’t even know why I’m typing this, it’s probably pointless, but I needed to say something. Anything.

just...can't seem to sleep. everything feels so heavy like a blanket i can't shake off. lost my job last week and now it's just this endless loop of staring at the ceiling. i don’t even know what to do anymore. it’s all just…grey.

text: Okay, so like, this is really hard to even type. I don’t even know where to start. Everything feels…gray. okay so like this is just… a mess. i moved last week and it’s been awful honestly. i thought it would be exciting, you know, a fresh start and everything. but it's just…empty. i unpacked boxes for three days straight and now everything looks exactly the same. my apartment is too big, too quiet. i keep expecting someone to be here, like i’m waiting for a party that never started. i haven’t really talked to anyone since i got here, just my mom mostly and she doesn’t really understand. i feel so lost and just…tired. like i’m dragging myself through mud every day. i keep telling myself it’ll get better, but it just feels like this is my new normal, i guess.

text: Okay seriously, I don't even know what's happening anymore. Like, everything feels…gray. text: Ugh, honestly, I don’t even know where to start. It’s just… everything feels so heavy. My daughter, Elara, she’s just… testing me, I think. Not in a dramatic, screaming way, but just this constant little digs and sighs and ‘you don’t understand’ when I’m trying to help. I’m trying to help! I’m the one who’s been picking up the pieces for the last ten years, you know? Ever since Dad got sick, everything just fell on me. I keep trying to be patient, I really do, but it’s exhausting. I just want a quiet evening, a hot cup of tea, and someone to just... sit with me. But it feels impossible. Just... a dull ache, a constant thrum of sadness. I don’t even know why I’m writing this, it’s probably pointless, but I needed to say it, I think.
