text: Ugh, just... ugh. Everything feels so heavy today. Like I’m wading through mud, you know? Mom and Dad are at it again, seriously. It’s always something – my career, my choices, what I’m *not* doing with my life. They don’t get it, obviously. They think they know what’s best, like I’m some little kid who needs to be told how to breathe. I tried to talk to them, you know? Just like, calmly explain that I’m actually building something, that I’m happy with the direction I’m going, but it just escalated. Dad started yelling, Mom just… sighed and said I was being “unrealistic.” It’s exhausting. I just want it to stop. I don’t even know why I bother trying to explain myself. It's like talking to a brick wall. I feel like I'm constantly disappointing them, and maybe I am. Maybe I *should* be doing something different. I just… idk. I’m supposed to be a grown-up, right? I have a job, I pay my bills, I’m… fine? But I’m not fine. Not really. It’s just… this constant low-level anxiety and this weird feeling that I’m failing at everything, even though logically I know I'm not. I just want a quiet evening, maybe watch a stupid movie, but even that feels like too much effort. Maybe I’ll just stay in bed. It’s probably for the best. Just… feeling really lost, honestly. And a little bit numb.

it’s just… everything feels heavy you know? like i’m wading through mud and every step just pulls me down further. i haven’t been sleeping properly in weeks honestly. used to be able to drift off after a cup of tea but now it’s just… staring at the ceiling. thinking about him. about all the things he needs. it’s a constant loop. i try to do everything, you know? make sure he’s warm, that he has enough to eat, that he’s comfortable. and then i collapse. i just… collapse.

i keep telling myself it’s the stress, right? caring for someone with… well, with his things. it’s a lot. but it’s more than just the stress i think. it’s like… a dull ache. a constant, low-level sadness. it’s not screaming, it’s not dramatic, it’s just… there. like a shadow that follows me everywhere. i used to enjoy gardening, reading, going for walks. now i just… don’t. i don't even feel like getting out of bed some days. i just want to curl up and disappear. it’s stupid i know everyone tells me to ‘think positive’ but how do you think positive when you just feel… empty? he’s so good to me, so patient, and i feel like i’m failing him somehow. i just want to feel *something* other than this quiet despair. i don’t even know what to do anymore. i just wish it would stop.

it’s just… everything feels so heavy you know? like i’m wading through mud and every step is ten times harder than the last. i lost my job last week. just like that. they said ‘restructuring’ but it feels like they just didn’t want me anymore. honestly i don’t blame them i guess. i haven’t been myself lately. and then… the doctor. they found something weird on the scans. nothing serious they said but it’s… a thing. a potential thing. they want to run more tests. more appointments. more bills. it’s just piling on.

i keep staring at the walls and trying to remember what it feels like to actually *want* to do something. i used to love hiking, remember that? used to feel this rush of… excitement, you know? now i just feel… numb. like a lot of the time. i haven't even left the apartment in three days. i ordered takeout last night, just stared at the screen. it tasted like nothing. i keep telling myself it’s just temporary, that things will get better, but it’s hard to believe anything right now. it’s like a gray fog just blankets everything.

my mom keeps calling, asking if i’m eating, if i’m sleeping. i just tell her i’m fine. i don’t want to worry her. but it’s not fine. it’s not even close. it’s just… this. this heavy, empty feeling. and now this medical stuff too. i just… i wish i could just disappear for a little while. not like, permanently, just... somewhere quiet. somewhere i don't have to think about anything.  i just want it to stop.

just... ugh. another day staring at the wall. they said it’d be easier finding a new job but it’s just... nothing. all the job boards are full of people who are way more qualified than me i guess. and now this exam is looming and i can’t even focus. my brain just feels like mud. i keep replaying everything i said during the interview, every mistake i made. it's like i'm permanently stuck in a loop. i don't even know why i bother trying anymore honestly. it's just... a huge weight. like i'm carrying the whole world on my shoulders and it’s crushing me. i just want to disappear for a little while i think. maybe just until after the exam is over... but then what?
it’s just… everything feels pointless now. the job, the apartment, her… i just stare at the walls sometimes. i don’t even want to eat. just… numb, i guess. like a gray fog.  it's all so heavy.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Ugh, just… everyone else seems to be moving on with their lives. I’m stuck here, same four walls, same microwave dinners. It’s like I’m watching a movie of everyone else’s happiness and I'm just…not in it. Feels pointless, honestly.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so, honestly, I’m just… swirling. It started with the doctor saying “elevated blood pressure” and I thought, okay, that’s manageable, we’ll tweak the diet, maybe more walking. But then it was the tests, the scans, the whole thing and now he’s saying “we need to investigate further” and I feel like I’m drowning. My husband, David, he’s so calm about it, completely matter-of-fact, just talking about probabilities and possibilities and it’s… it’s like he’s not even *feeling* it with me. He’s being supportive, I know he is, he’s making me tea and asking if I want to watch a movie, but it’s just… not enough, you know? It’s like I’m carrying this enormous weight and everyone else is just going about their day. I keep thinking about Mom, she had a scare like this years ago and it just… it just consumed her. Not actively, not screaming or anything, just… a slow fading, a lack of interest in anything. I can’t let that happen to me. I just… I just don’t want to feel this way anymore, this heavy, leaden feeling in my chest. Everything feels gray, like a really bad watercolor painting. I used to love gardening, I was always out there, covered in dirt, feeling the sun on my face. Now I just stare at the overgrown roses and feel… nothing. Just a dull ache. I keep saying “I’m fine,” to David, but I don’t even know why. It just feels… automatic. Like a shield. God, I wish I could just… just *snap* out of it.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: it’s just… so quiet here in my dorm, everyone’s studying and i feel like i’m just watching them go by, you know? like i’m stuck in a blurry hallway and nobody even sees me, and i just want to curl up and disappear, honestly, it’s getting really hard to even breathe sometimes, i don't even know why i bother trying anymore.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, this whole new apartment just feels...gray. Like, I unpacked everything and it's still not *me* here, you know? Mom keeps asking about job hunting but it just feels pointless, like I'm just stuck, and everything’s kind of blurry and I don’t even want to look at my phone really.  Just…tired.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, just…everything feels so heavy lately, like wading through mud, you know? I graduated like, six months ago and now I'm stuck here, living with my parents and seriously struggling to even pay rent, it’s just…a constant grey cloud, I don’t even want to do anything anymore, just kinda exist, honestly.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
Ugh, seriously, I can’t even. Exams are the absolute worst. Like, I’m drowning here. Everyone else seems to be, like, totally nailing it, writing essays at 2 am and looking…productive? I just stare at a blank page and feel this… weight. It’s not even the stuff itself, I guess. It’s just… everything. I keep re-reading the same chapter and it’s like it’s written in a different language. I tried to study with Liam last night and I just… shut down. He kept asking if I was okay and I just mumbled something about being tired. It’s not that I’m tired, it’s that I feel…empty. Like a battery that's completely drained.

I keep telling myself it'll be over soon, you know? Just a few more weeks and I'm free. But it doesn’t *feel* like it. It feels like it’s going to go on forever. I keep thinking about failing, just completely bombing everything. It’s stupid, I know, but I can’t shake it. I haven’t really eaten anything decent in days. Just grabbed a granola bar this morning. Everything feels pointless, like what’s the point of even trying? I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. I’m just…so, so tired. Of everything. I don't know why I even bother posting this, probably just to vent to someone who doesn't really care.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Ugh, just staring at my bank account again. Feels like I’m drowning in bills and student loans. Everyone else is moving on, getting jobs, and I’m still here, living with my parents, feeling like a failure. It’s just...heavy.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Ugh, just staring at my laptop. Everything feels pointless. I graduated like, six months ago and I’m still here, helping my parents. Just… heavy. Like a weight. I don’t even want to open emails anymore.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, I just… I don’t even know what to do anymore. It’s like everything is just gray, you know? Like the world is muted. And Leo, he’s such a good baby, honestly, he’s perfect and I love him so much, but… my husband, he just doesn't get it. He keeps saying I’m overreacting about the way things are at home, about the constant arguing, about how exhausted I am. He says I should just "relax" and that everyone gets stressed with a new baby. But it’s not relaxing, it’s just… heavy. Like a blanket that’s too thick. I feel like I’m drowning in laundry and crying and feeling completely alone, even when he’s right there. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. It’s not fair, is it? I just want things to be normal again, even if “normal” was just a little bit chaotic.

ugh i just cant sleep anymore like ever. my brain is just buzzing with everything and nothing at the same time. i keep tossing and turning and it just makes me feel worse. like everything is heavy. i tried reading but i keep getting distracted and then i feel guilty for not reading. i know i *should* be studying for this history test but honestly i just feel numb. it’s like… like there’s this gray cloud just hanging over everything and i can’t shake it off. i keep thinking about how pointless it all is, like what’s the point of even trying when nothing ever really matters. i just want to crawl into a hole and disappear. it's just… exhausting. i just wish i could just feel *something* other than this constant blah.

Ugh, just… everything feels so heavy lately. Like I’m wading through mud. Work’s stressful, obviously, but it’s not even *that* bad. It’s just… everything adds up. The bills, the rent, trying to eat something that isn’t ramen. I feel like I’m constantly failing, you know? Like I’m just spinning my wheels and getting nowhere. I don’t even want to look at my bank account anymore. It’s just a reminder of how broke I am. I keep telling myself it’ll get better, that things will turn around, but honestly, it just feels… pointless. Just… tired.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, seriously, I can’t sleep. Like, at all. I keep tossing and turning, staring at the ceiling, and it’s just… awful. It’s been, like, six months since… well, you know. And I thought maybe it would get easier, you know? But it hasn’t. It’s just… quiet. Too quiet. I keep expecting him to be here, or a text, or something, anything. I’m trying to read, I’m trying to watch stupid shows, but my brain just… won’t shut off. I feel so tired all the time, even when I’m awake. I don’t even know why I’m bothering to write this, tbh. Maybe it’s just to prove to myself that I’m still here, I guess? idk. I just feel… heavy. Maybe I’ll try some tea. Or not. Maybe.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is hard to even write down, honestly. I don’t even know where to start. Everyone keeps saying “it gets better” and “you’ll adjust” but… I don’t *feel* like it’s getting better. It’s just…different. I thought having a baby would be, like, the most amazing thing ever, right? And it *is* amazing, sometimes. When he’s sleeping and I can just hold him and he’s just…quiet. But then he wakes up and it’s screaming and feeding and diaper changes and just this constant, overwhelming need. And I feel so…tired. Like bone-deep tired.

I used to love going out with my friends, you know? Just grabbing coffee or going to the movies. Now? I don't even know. Maybe once? I feel like I’m failing at everything. Being a mom, being a partner, even just being *me*. Liam, my husband, he’s amazing, he tries so hard, but he works all the time and he’s exhausted too, and it feels like we're both just… adrift. We talk, but it’s always about the baby, or the laundry, or whether he remembered to defrost the chicken. I miss talking about… anything else. Seriously, idk how people manage to have a life outside of a kid.

I just feel so incredibly lonely. Like, completely alone. I have this tiny little person who needs me, which is wonderful, but it’s also…isolating. I feel like everyone else is out there, living their lives, and I’m stuck here in this endless cycle of feeding and changing and trying to figure out how to shower. Maybe it’s just the sleep deprivation, maybe it’s just the postpartum hormones, maybe it’s something more. I just… I just want someone to understand. Or even just to hold me for a minute. It's just...a lot.

It’s just… quiet. Too quiet. I don’t even know why I bother making coffee anymore. Used to be, I’d brew a pot, put on some music, and… something would happen. A phone call, a visit, even just someone saying “good morning.” Now? Just the ticking of the clock. He’s asleep, of course. Always is. It’s been… a long time since he’s woken up early. I try to read, but the words just swim. Everything feels gray. Like a really bad watercolor painting.

I used to love gardening. Remember those roses? He planted them. Said they reminded him of me – bright and beautiful. Now the garden’s overgrown, and I don't have the energy to pull the weeds. Honestly, I don't even *want* to. What’s the point? They’ll just die eventually, like everything else.

My sister calls once a month. She asks if I’m okay. I just say “fine.” It’s the easiest thing to say. It’s a lie. A big, heavy, suffocating lie. She wants to come visit, but I don't want her to see this place. This… emptiness. I just... I don't have the energy for company. It’s exhausting, pretending everything’s normal. Pretending I’m okay. It's just me and him, and the quiet. And it’s crushing me, honestly. I wish someone would just… notice. But they don’t. Really, they don’t.

it’s just… quiet now. too quiet. i keep expecting him to text, you know? just like always. and then nothing. it’s stupid, i guess. we were fine, mostly. i thought we were. i really did. i keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out where it went wrong, but it’s like there’s no answer. i just feel… hollow. all my friends are out having fun and i’m just… here. eating takeout and staring at the ceiling. i tried to go to work today but i just couldn't. my boss was nice about it but it felt like everyone was looking at me. like i was failing. honestly i don't even want to do anything. everything feels pointless. it's like a gray fog, you know? and i just want it to lift. i just want to feel… something other than this.

Just… I don't get it. I thought moving would be amazing, like everyone says. Fresh start, new city, you know? It's just… gray. Everything feels gray. My apartment is nice, I have a job, I’m trying to meet people but it’s just… exhausting. Like, I wake up and I already feel tired before I’ve even done anything. I keep looking at pictures of my old place, just… remembering. It’s stupid, I know, but I just don’t feel settled anywhere.  I keep telling myself it’s just the adjustment period, but it’s been like this for weeks.  I just want to go home, I guess. Not like, *want* home, just… wish it was different.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: Ugh, it’s just… everything feels heavier today. Like, the worst kind of gray. It’s the anniversary, you know? Three years. It doesn’t even feel like three years, it feels like a lifetime ago and also like it just happened. I keep replaying it in my head, stupidly, over and over, and it’s not getting better, just… sharper. I haven’t really been sleeping, just staring at the ceiling, thinking about how pointless everything is, honestly. My exams are looming and I can't focus on anything, just this awful, hollow feeling. Like I’m wading through mud and there’s no bottom. I keep telling myself it’ll get better, that I’ll move on, but it just… doesn’t. It doesn’t feel like it, anyway. I just feel… stuck.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, just… everyone seems to be having so much fun, you know? Like, I saw a group of people at the diner this morning laughing and chatting and it just felt… heavy. I don’t even know why. I went to get coffee, just a black one, and they were telling jokes and sharing stories and I just kinda stared at my cup. I feel like I’m observing a movie of other people’s lives and I’m not in it. IDK, maybe it’s just the shift. It's always worse when everyone else is awake and… normal. I tried to say hi to Brenda from the back room, but I mumbled something and she just kind of looked through me. Maybe I should just stay in my room tonight. It’s probably stupid to feel this way, right? Like, everyone else is fine. Maybe.

another night, another shift. bills are piling up and i just... i don't know how much longer i can keep this up. everything feels so grey and pointless. just staring at the ceiling again, wishing it was monday already. it’s just… exhausting.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s… it’s the anniversary. i didn’t even realize it was today until my mom texted. just a little “thinking of you” thing. it’s stupid, right? like, why would she think of me? i haven’t really talked about it in months. it just feels… wrong, you know? like acknowledging it makes it real again. everything feels heavier today. the textbooks are blurry, i keep staring at the page and not actually reading anything. i tried to study for my history exam, but i just kept thinking about… well, you know. it’s like a dull ache, constant. not like a sharp pain, but just… there. a background hum of sadness. i keep replaying it in my head, over and over. trying to make sense of it, trying to find some kind of… reason. there isn’t one. i just… don't get it. everyone else seems to be okay, going to parties, hanging out. i just want to curl up and disappear. i don't even want to eat. everything tastes like ash. i feel so useless. like i’m just going through the motions, a zombie. i keep telling myself it’ll get better, that it’s been long enough, but it doesn’t. it just… doesn’t. i just wish it would all just stop. i hate this feeling. i really, really hate this feeling.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, this presentation is killing me. Like, seriously. I just… idk if I can even finish it by tomorrow. It feels pointless, honestly. Maybe I should just skip it? Everything feels so heavy lately. I just want to disappear, I guess.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Ugh, I just… I don’t even know what’s wrong with me. Everything feels so heavy lately. It’s not like one big thing, you know? It’s just… everything. Mom and Dad have been fighting *again*. Seriously, it’s like they’re actively trying to make things worse. I try to stay out of it, obviously, which is exhausting because I can hear them yelling and it just, like, vibrates through the whole house. I don’t even want to be around them anymore. I tried to ask if they were okay, you know, like a few times, but they just brushed it off and said “it’s nothing, you’re being dramatic.” Which, I guess… maybe I am? I don’t know.

I just feel… gray. Like everything’s faded. I used to love drawing, I was actually pretty good, but I haven’t picked up a pencil in weeks. It just feels pointless, honestly. Like nothing I do matters. School’s a struggle too. I keep zoning out in class, and I’m failing a quiz. It’s embarrassing. I'm pretty sure I just stared at the ceiling the whole time. My friends keep asking if I’m alright, and I just say “fine,” which is a lie. A big, huge lie. I don't want to burden them with this. It's probably stupid, I’m probably overreacting. Maybe. I just... I wish I could just disappear for a little while. Just until things quiet down. I don't even know what I want anymore. I just feel… lost. Like I'm drifting. Idk. I just need to breathe, I guess.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: ugh just…everything is so heavy. exams are insane and i feel like i’m drowning. my mom keeps picking at me about my grades like i don’t already know i’m failing. she just says i’m not trying hard enough but she doesn’t even *see* how much i’m trying. it’s like she’s deliberately trying to make me feel worse. and my brother’s always getting attention, doing well in everything, and i just…i don't even want to look at him. i just want to curl up in my bed and not feel anything. i keep replaying stupid arguments we had last week, like they're still happening. i just feel so tired all the time, like there’s this grey fog over everything. i don't even know why i'm bothering to write this, it probably won't change anything. just…so much.

text: Ugh, just... everything feels so heavy lately, like I'm wading through mud and it’s just getting deeper and deeper. I keep thinking about that project at work, the one I totally bombed, and it’s just replaying in my head constantly, you know? Like, what was I even thinking? I don’t even want to get out of bed some days, honestly.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is embarrassing even to type, but... I just feel... heavy. Seriously heavy. It’s probably just the shift, you know? Being up all night, staring at screens, fueled by lukewarm coffee and regret. But it's more than that, I think. I don’t even know. I mean, the shift’s been bad lately, the customers are awful, and my boss is breathing down my neck about everything. But it's always been bad, hasn't it? I just… I just don’t see a way out, honestly.

And the money, ugh. It’s a mess. I keep getting bills, and I’m barely making enough to cover the essentials. I’m working overtime, *all* the time, and it’s not even helping that much. I tried looking for another job, but… idk, everything seems so competitive. Or maybe I'm just not good enough. Maybe I'm just destined to be stuck in this cycle. It feels like I'm constantly scrambling, just trying to stay afloat.

I haven’t really talked to anyone about it. My family… they wouldn't understand. They always say “pull yourself together,” like it’s that simple. Like I just need to *try harder*. But I *am* trying! I’m really trying. I just feel… pointless. Like nothing I do matters. I went out for a walk yesterday, just to clear my head, and I just... sat on a bench and watched people. And I felt even more alone. I just wish things were different. Maybe tomorrow will be better. Probably not, though. I just... I just don't know what to do anymore. I just feel so tired, you know?

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s the day again ugh just…everything feels so grey. like the colors all faded. i don’t even want to talk to anyone. just…sitting here. it’s stupid how much it still hurts. just wishing it hadn’t happened.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text:
Okay seriously, I don’t even know why I’m writing this. It just… spills out. I moved here six months ago, on a whim, supposedly to “find myself.” Found myself drowning in a giant, echoing apartment and a mountain of unanswered calls. Mom and Dad are relentless, you know? They keep sending texts, like, every hour. Just… pointing out how “unhappy” I am, how “no one understands me” and how I “need to talk to them.” Like I haven’t been trying! It’s not that I *want* to talk to them, it's that every conversation is just… sharper. More critical. They don't get it. They think if they just push harder, I'll suddenly snap out of it.

It’s like they’re actively trying to make me feel worse. And honestly, it’s working. I just… I just want to disappear sometimes. Not like, permanently. But just… somewhere else. Somewhere they can’t reach me. I went to the park today, tried to walk around, but just ended up sitting on a bench staring at the pigeons. They were just… eating. Just existing. It felt… good, in a way, to just be a quiet observer. I feel so… disconnected. Everything feels gray. Like the colors got turned down really low. I just want a little bit of brightness, you know? But it's like there's a filter on everything. Everything is just... heavy. I don't even want to make dinner. Just… existing is exhausting.

Okay, seriously, I don’t get it. I thought being a mom would be… good. Like, everyone talks about the joy, the connection, you know? But it's just… exhausting. And I feel like I’m failing at everything. Last night we had Sarah and Mark over for dinner. Just a small thing, pizza and some chips. And I felt this crushing wave of awkwardness the whole time. Like I was messing up the conversation, serving the wrong thing, just generally being a bad host. I kept apologizing, like five times. And they were nice, really, but I could feel their pity, I swear. It’s stupid, right? But it just makes me want to hide. I didn't even really *want* to see them. I just felt this huge pressure to put on a happy face, you know? Like I needed to prove I was handling this whole motherhood thing. It's ridiculous. I don't even enjoy spending time with people anymore. Just… watching the baby sleep is sometimes the only thing that doesn't make me feel completely numb. I just want to go back to how things were, before all this. Before the sleepless nights and the endless feeding and the feeling like I'm drowning in responsibility. It's not that I don’t love him, I do, I really do. It’s just… everything feels so heavy.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, everything just feels…heavy. I don't even know where to start, honestly. Liam left, you know? Just…gone. Said he needed space, which is a ridiculously cliché thing to say, right? But it’s true, I guess. I keep staring at the nursery, at the little rocking chair we picked out, and it just feels…wrong. Like it’s waiting for someone who isn’t coming back. I feel this awful ache, this constant, dull sadness that just sits right here, in my chest. It’s hard to even enjoy the baby, you know? Like, I love her so much, really I do, but a lot of the time I just feel this overwhelming exhaustion and this quiet disappointment. Everything feels pointless, I guess. I just want to curl up and disappear, and I don't even know why I’m typing this out, it's just...pouring out.

Seriously, I just… don’t get it. Like, I thought having a kid would be… amazing. Picturesque. You know? It’s just… exhausting. And expensive. We’re eating ramen three nights a week and I feel like I'm failing at everything. I keep staring at the bills and just… blanking. My partner tries to be supportive, but it’s like he doesn’t *get* it. It’s not that he’s not trying, it’s just…everything feels so heavy. I used to love going for walks, reading, just… existing. Now I just feel this constant, low-level ache. It’s not like I’m actively doing anything bad, it’s just… I just don’t see the point anymore. Just…tired.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: okay so like, everything feels… gray. really gray. it started a few weeks ago, after my grandma had that thing with her heart, you know? she’s okay now, they said, but it was so scary and I just… I keep replaying it in my head, like if I’d said something, if I’d done something differently, maybe she wouldn’t have had to go through all that. and now she’s just… quieter. she doesn't really talk about it, and I don't really want to ask, honestly. it's like, a heavy blanket just settled over everything, you know? school is just… pointless. like, why even bother trying to memorize all this stuff when nothing really matters? my grades are slipping, and it doesn't even bother me to study, it's just… exhausting, trying to even *care* about anything. my friends are still doing stuff, going out, laughing, and it just feels like I'm watching a movie of their lives. I want to be part of it, I really do, but I just can’t seem to muster the energy. i keep staring out the window, just watching the rain, and it’s kind of… peaceful, in a sad way. i feel so tired all the time, not like, physically tired, but just… emotionally drained. like my batteries are completely dead and no amount of sleep is going to fix it. i just... I don't know what's the point anymore. i just wish things would just… go back to normal. but they won't, will they? it just feels like a slow fade, you know? like the colors are slowly bleeding out of everything. and i’m just… stuck in the gray.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, just… everything feels so heavy. It's been six months since Liam and I split, and honestly, I still don’t get it. It wasn't even *me*. He just… left. My sister keeps saying it’s for the best, that we were both too stressed, but it doesn't feel like it. It feels like he just decided he didn't want me anymore. My mom’s been calling constantly, trying to get me to "move on" and visit her, but I just… can’t. I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering what I could have done differently. I don’t even know what I *want* anymore, you know? Maybe I should just stay in bed. Idk. It’s just… a lot. Like, a really, really big lot. I just feel… numb most of the time, like I'm watching my life go by without really being a part of it.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
Ugh, just… everything feels heavy. Like I’m wading through mud. I graduated, like, six weeks ago and it’s just… nothing. Everyone else seems to be, like, climbing ladders or traveling or starting amazing jobs. And I’m just… here. In my childhood bedroom. Eating cereal for dinner. My mom keeps asking about my “plans” and it just makes me want to disappear. I don’t even know what I *want* anymore, you know? It’s not like I had some huge, specific goal or anything. I just… used to *do* things. Go to classes, hang out with friends, feel vaguely productive. Now it’s just… this. A slow, grey slide.

I try to force myself to be happy, to make conversation, to do something, anything, but it’s exhausting. Like my brain is just… numb. I scroll through social media and everyone’s lives look so vibrant and exciting and I just feel… smaller. And sadder. I don’t even really *want* to do anything. Just… exist, I guess. It's not that I'm *actively* trying to be sad, it’s just… that's where I am. It’s weird. It’s just a constant feeling of… not being. Like I'm watching my own life from the outside. I keep thinking about just staying in bed all day. Maybe forever. Not like I want to *die*, or anything, it's just... the thought of getting up feels impossible some days. I just... don't get it.

Just... everything feels gray. Like someone turned the saturation down on the whole world. He's gone. Just like that. Said he needed “space” which, honestly, is just a fancy way of saying he didn’t want me anymore. I’ve been taking care of him for years, you know? Doctor's appointments, meds, the whole nine yards. And now… nothing. I keep expecting him to walk through the door, you know? Like it’s all a bad dream. I don't even know why I’m bothering to post this. Doesn't really matter, does it? Just…heavy. Really heavy.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
it’s… it’s the anniversary today. i didn’t even realize until my mom reminded me. it’s stupid i didn’t remember. it just feels… heavy. like a brick in my chest. i keep scrolling through old photos, mostly of him, and it’s like the colors are fading. not like, literally fading, but like the joy is. it's just gray now. everything is gray.

i know i should be feeling *something* more. like, grief or sadness or whatever. but it's just… a dull ache. a constant thrumming beneath my skin. i tried to talk to sarah today, you know, like i always do, but i just kind of mumbled and stared at my phone. she asked if i was okay and i just said “fine” really fast. i hate that i said fine.

i used to love taking pictures, especially of us. silly stuff, like us laughing at dumb memes or building ridiculous forts in the living room. now i just… don’t want to. it’s like looking at a ghost. a reminder of something that isn’t there. i haven't really been sleeping either. just tossing and turning, replaying things in my head. it's like my brain is stuck on repeat. i don’t even want to go to school tomorrow. it all feels pointless. like nothing matters anymore. i just… i just wish it hadn't happened. it just keeps going over and over. i just want it to stop. it's so hard to even type this, like the words are heavy too.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: ugh seriously i don’t even know what to do anymore. everyone keeps saying i should be excited about this job it’s like a huge step right? but it’s just…more spreadsheets more meetings more people breathing down my neck. i feel like i’m drowning honestly. my mom keeps asking about moving out and finding an apartment which is terrifying. i just want to stay here in my room and not do anything. my classes are piling up and i keep missing assignments because i just can't focus. i used to love writing, remember that? now it just feels like another thing i'm failing at. i just feel…tired. like there's this weight on my chest all the time and it's getting heavier. i just wish i could just disappear for a little bit.

Everyone just wants to talk, you know? Like, I'm already exhausted, staring at the dark, and then they start telling me about their lives. It’s… too much. Just want to disappear into the shadows again.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so… I don’t even know where to start. It’s just… quiet. Really quiet. I used to hate the quiet, you know? Always had music blasting, something on the TV, *something*. Now… it’s just… me and him. And honestly, I don’t even know if he *feels* it, not really. He’s… good. He’s a really good boy. He doesn’t complain, he helps, he’s just… there. But I feel like I’m just… existing. Like a shadow.

My kids are all grown up, spread out all over the country. They call, of course, but it’s always, like, a quick chat. "How are you, Mom?" "Fine." "That’s good." And then they hang up. It’s nice, I guess, but it doesn't *feel* nice. It feels… distant. Like I'm watching my life from behind a pane of glass. I used to volunteer at the animal shelter, remember? I loved that. The dogs, the cats… the *people* there. Just talking to someone, you know? But I haven't gone in months. I just… don't. It feels like a lot of effort, and honestly, sometimes I just don’t *have* the effort.

Maybe it’s the days getting shorter. Or maybe it’s just… me. I feel like I’m fading. Like the color is draining out of everything. I try to do things, bake cookies, read a book… but it’s just… pointless. It feels like I'm going through the motions, just… going. I don't even really *enjoy* the cookies. I just make them because I think I *should*. I dunno. It's just… lonely. Really, really lonely. I just wish… I wish someone would just notice. Or maybe I’m just being dramatic. I idk.  I just... feel like I'm losing myself a little bit every day, you know?  It's probably nothing, really. Just a phase.  Maybe.

everything feels grey. like all the color just drained out. i used to love going for walks, you know? even just a quick one around the block. now i can’t even bring myself to put on shoes. my apartment is just… sad. i haven't cleaned it in days. the dishes are piled up, the laundry’s overflowing, and it doesn’t even bother me. it’s like i’m just watching it all happen, but i’m not really *there*. i keep replaying things in my head, little conversations, stupid arguments, things i should have said, things i should have done. it's exhausting.

i miss her, i guess. not like… a romantic thing. just… i miss having someone to talk to. someone to share a stupid meme with or just sit in silence with. it’s just… quiet now. really quiet. and it’s loud in my head. just a constant hum of… nothing. i keep telling myself it’ll get better, that i’ll find something new, but it just feels so far away. like i’m stuck in quicksand and the more i struggle, the further i sink. i just… i don’t even know what i’m doing anymore.

ugh just…everything feels so heavy lately. like i’m wading through mud everywhere i go. i went to that meeting today and just zoned out completely. everyone seemed to be having such a good time and i felt like i was watching a movie about someone else’s life. i don’t even know why i bother going to work anymore. it’s just…gray. i used to enjoy my job, you know? designing things, figuring stuff out. now i just stare at the screen and it’s all just pixels. my apartment feels huge and empty. i keep making coffee but i don’t even drink it. i just…am. i guess. i don’t really feel like anything. send help if you can, i don’t know what else to do.

it’s just…ugh. i don’t even know where to start. lost my job last week. the usual stuff, severance and all that, but it doesn’t really matter does it? it just feels like everything’s falling apart. mom’s been on my case constantly about how i’m not “contributing” and dad just…ignores it. like he doesn't even see i'm struggling. honestly i just want to disappear sometimes. i tried to talk to them about it, but it just ended in yelling. i feel so useless and tired and like i’m letting everyone down. i just want to curl up in bed and not feel anything. i keep staring at the walls, you know? it's hard to even make dinner, let alone face the day. i’m just…overwhelmed.

text:
ugh i just… i don't even know what to do anymore. mom’s been on my case nonstop about the job i haven’t gotten anything yet and dad just sighs like it’s my fault the whole world is ending. it’s like they don’t even hear me when i try to explain that finding a job in this town is basically impossible. everyone here works at the factory or the diner and it’s just… depressing. i thought moving back home would be temporary, like a little reset after college, but it's just… stuck. i feel like i’m drowning in expectations and disappointment.

i keep trying to talk to them but it’s always the same. ‘you should be grateful you have a roof over your head’ or ‘you need to pull yourself together’ like it’s that easy. it's not. it's like they think i’m just being lazy or dramatic. they don’t understand that every time i get excited about something, like maybe finding a decent barista job, it just crashes and burns. i just want to disappear sometimes. i spend all night staring at the ceiling just thinking about how pointless everything feels. i feel like a failure, like i’m letting everyone down, especially myself. i just want to be somewhere else, doing something else, *being* someone else. i’m so tired. just so unbelievably tired.

text: Ugh, seriously, I just… I don’t even know what to do anymore. It’s like every time I try to actually *do* something, like, you know, just get out of bed, it’s just… harder. Like, exponentially harder. I finished my degree like, two months ago? Feels like a lifetime ago, honestly. Everyone keeps asking me about job applications and “what’s next” and it just makes my chest feel all tight and weird. Mom’s been really sweet, of course, making me dinners and saying how proud she is – which is nice, really, but it also makes me feel guilty, like I’m disappointing her. I just… I can’t seem to shake this feeling that everything’s pointless. Like, what’s the point of getting a job when it’s just going to be another stressful thing to worry about? And I can't sleep, seriously, I’ve been staring at the ceiling for hours, just thinking about all the things I *should* be doing, all the things I *could* be doing, and it just spirals. I keep going to bed exhausted, and then I wake up exhausted, and then I just… lie there. It’s like a gray fog, you know? Everything feels muted. I used to love painting, remember? Like, *really* loved it. Now the brushes just sit there, collecting dust. It's stupid, I know, but even thinking about picking one up feels like a monumental task. I just want to disappear, honestly. Not like, dramatically, but just… fade away into the background. It’s just… too much. I just wish I could feel *something* other than this heavy, heavy sadness. I keep telling myself tomorrow will be different, but... I don't even know if I believe that anymore.

text: Ugh, it’s just… everything feels gray lately, like someone permanently dimmed the colors. It's weird because the shift was okay, mostly, but then I got the text. Just… a few words. text: It's… it’s the anniversary today. Of everything. Honestly, I don’t even know why I’m writing this. It just… keeps popping up. Like a glitch in the system. attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: okay so like, it’s just…quiet. too quiet. i keep expecting someone to just *be* here, you know? like, i used to wake up and there’d be this immediate sense of…something. a little bit of anticipation, even if it was just for coffee. now it’s just…empty. the apartment feels huge, like a giant, echoing box. i tried rearranging the furniture yesterday, desperately trying to make it feel less…abandoned, but it just made it worse. everything looks wrong. i keep staring at the space where his side of the bed was, you know? it’s just…there. a reminder. i keep thinking about all the little things we used to do, just like, existing together. making dinner, arguing over the remote, even just sitting in silence. and now it’s all gone. i haven't really talked to anyone except for my sister, and she’s really busy with her new job, and i don’t want to bother her. it feels selfish, i guess. like i’m just…a weight. i keep scrolling through old photos, obsessing over everything, trying to remember how it *felt* to be…happy. it’s like trying to remember a dream, and the details are just fading. i keep telling myself it will get better, that i’ll adjust, that i’ll find a new normal, but it just feels… impossible. honestly, i just want to crawl into bed and disappear. maybe if i just stop looking at everything, it'll be okay. but i can't seem to stop. i just feel so…alone. like i’m the last person on earth. is this what it’s like forever? i don't even know anymore. just...quiet.

Just... everything feels so heavy. Like wading through mud. The divorce is still fresh, and then this new project at school? It’s just… too much. I don’t even want to get out of bed some days. Just existing feels exhausting.

Ugh, just... ugh. Went out with Liam last night. Small group, just us and Sarah. I don't even know why I went. I felt this weird pressure, like I *should* be having fun, laughing, you know? But it was just...loud. Everyone’s voices seemed too high, their jokes too bright. Like I was watching a movie of everyone else’s lives and I was stuck behind a really thick pane of glass.

I managed a few smiles, a couple of "that's funny," but it felt like I was reciting lines from a script. Like I was pretending.  Sarah kept asking about work, and I just gave her the same bland answer about being busy. It's like I’m actively trying to disappear into the wallpaper.  Liam kept trying to draw me out, telling stories about his weekend, and honestly, I just zoned out.  I think I nodded a few times.  

Then, when we were leaving, he said, "You seemed a little quiet tonight, you okay?"  And I just mumbled something about being tired and headed straight home.  Now I'm sitting here staring at the ceiling, and it just feels...heavy.  Like I'm wading through mud.  I don’t even want to reply to his text.  I just... I just want to be alone.  Maybe I should just stay in tomorrow.  Maybe I should just not go anywhere at all.  It's exhausting, you know?  Just... exhausting.

text: Ugh, it’s the anniversary today. Feels…weird. Like a dull ache, you know? I idk why it hits so hard, just feels heavy. Maybe I should just ignore it, pretend it didn't happen? But then I’d feel guilty, i guess. Just…a lot.

okay seriously i don’t even know what to do anymore. it’s just…everything feels so heavy you know? like i’m wading through mud just to get out of bed. and it’s all the bills. it’s the student loans that are still looming over me like this giant dark cloud and honestly i barely make enough at the office to even cover groceries sometimes. i know i should be budgeting more, i know i should be looking for a better job but the thought of even *looking* is just…exhausting. i just spent three hours scrolling through job listings and i felt worse afterwards. like nothing’s ever going to change. my parents keep saying “pull yourself together” but they don’t understand, do they? they don't know what it's like to feel this constant, dull ache of just…not being enough. i keep telling myself it’s temporary, that things will get better, but it feels so far away. like i’m stuck in this endless loop of worrying about money and feeling like a failure. i feel like i’m disappointing everyone. i just wish i could just…snap out of it. it's stupid i know, everyone has problems but this feels so…permanent. i just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. i just want it to stop feeling like this all the time. i don't even want to talk about it really.

Okay so this is officially the worst thing ever. I just moved, like, three hours away from everything. Said it would be good for me, a fresh start, you know? Turns out a “fresh start” is just a really, really lonely one. The job’s okay, it’s just… dark. Every. Single. Night. I’m surrounded by shadows and monitors blinking, and honestly, it just makes everything feel… muted. Like the world is watching me, but not really seeing me.

My apartment is small, too. Tiny. It’s supposed to be ‘cozy,’ but it feels more like a box. I keep staring at the walls, trying to figure out how to make them less… gray. I unpacked everything yesterday, and it just sits there. Boxes full of stuff I don’t even really *want* anymore. It’s like… what’s the point? I keep telling myself it’ll get better, that I’ll make new friends, that I’ll find something to look forward to. But it just feels… heavy. Like a weight I can’t shake off.

I tried calling my mom, but she just kept saying “You’re being dramatic!” Like, duh. I’m sitting in a dark apartment, three hours from my family, working a job I hate, and feeling like I’m slowly dissolving. It’s not dramatic, it’s just… existence. It’s just… a lot. I just wish I could turn it off, you know? Just… turn it off and wake up somewhere else. Somewhere brighter. Somewhere… less. I just want to sleep.

it’s just… gray. like everything. i don’t even know why. i should be happy, right? it’s a monday, i have a job, my apartment’s okay, my cat’s sleeping on my chest. but it doesn’t *feel* like anything. just a heavy, dull ache. i keep staring at the monitors at work, numbers flashing, people typing, and it all just seems… distant. like i’m watching a movie about someone else’s life.

i used to love the night shift, you know? quiet. fewer people. i could actually *think*. now it’s just… more. more noise, more pressure, more faces i don’t recognize. and it all bleeds into this… this nothingness. i keep forgetting to eat, honestly. just grabbing a granola bar and shoving it in my mouth. doesn’t matter. nothing does.

my mom called yesterday. she just kept saying “are you okay?” and i just mumbled something about being tired. i don't want to talk about it. i don’t even know *what* to say. it’s like trying to describe the color gray to someone who’s only ever seen black and white. it’s just… there. it’s all there. and it’s getting heavier. i keep going over things in my head, stupid little things i did wrong, things i should have done differently, and it’s like they’re all sharp little knives twisting. i just want to… sleep. but even sleep doesn’t feel good. just… numb. i just wish it would stop. i wish i knew why i feel like this. i just want it to go away.

everyone else is posting happy anniversary stuff and it just makes me feel worse. like i’m failing at being normal. my work is okay, i guess, but it’s just… tasks. nothing feels meaningful. i just want to crawl into bed and not come out. honestly i don’t even know what to do with myself. just… existing. it's exhausting.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is really hard to even type, honestly. I just… I don’t even know where to start. It’s been, like, six months since Liam and I… well, since we separated. Officially, anyway. It feels like a lifetime ago, and also like it just happened yesterday. Everything is just… gray. Everything feels really, really heavy. Work is just… a slog. I’m trying to keep up, I really am, but I keep messing up things, little things, and then it’s just this overwhelming feeling that I’m just not good enough. Like, everyone else seems to have it together, you know? They’re posting about promotions and vacations and, like, perfect dinners. And I’m just staring at spreadsheets and wondering if I’m going to actually be able to pay my bills this month.

I keep telling myself it’s just the stress, that it’s the divorce, that it’s just a phase. Maybe. But it's not really going away, is it? I mean, I try to do things I used to enjoy – reading, going for walks – but it just feels… pointless. Like I'm going through the motions. I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing, all the things I *could* be doing, and it just makes me feel worse. I’m probably overthinking everything, right? Probably just need to get a new hobby or something. I dunno. I just feel… stuck. Like I’m moving through mud. I miss him, of course, but it’s not like a heartbroken, screaming-into-a-pillow kind of miss. It’s more… a dull ache. A constant, low-level sadness. I just… I don’t know what to do. I just want things to be… easier, I guess.

okay so this is ridiculous i just moved like three states away and it’s…it’s awful honestly. i thought it would be exciting you know like a fresh start and everything but it's just…gray. the apartment is small and smells faintly of bleach and someone else’s sadness i guess. i keep staring at the walls and they just don’t change. everything feels so distant even from my own phone screen. i tried to call mom but i couldn’t really get out the words i just kept saying ‘i’m fine’ like a broken record. she sounded worried which, like, i get it but it just made me feel worse.

i keep thinking about everything i left behind, like my old coffee shop and leo and even just the stupid regulars who always asked about my dog. they don't know i don't even have a dog anymore. i should have told them. i just…i froze. it’s like all the energy just drained out of me the second i drove over the state line. i haven’t slept properly in days just tossing and turning and replaying everything in my head.

i’m supposed to be unpacking but i just sat here for three hours staring at a box of old socks. it feels pointless. like nothing i do matters. i keep expecting things to get better but they don’t. it’s just…a slow, heavy feeling. like i’m wading through mud and i can’t see anything ahead. i don’t even know why i’m writing this i just needed to say something i guess. anyone ever feel like this about a new place? like it’s just amplifying everything bad already there? i just... i don’t think i can do this anymore. just…really don’t think i can.

just… ugh. everything feels so heavy you know? like i’m wading through mud and it’s getting deeper every step. i don’t even know why. i’ve been working so hard, trying to like, build a good life, but it just feels… pointless. i scroll through my feed and everyone’s doing something amazing, traveling, getting promotions, having cute dogs, and i just… exist. i don’t even want to talk to anyone honestly it’s just… exhausting. i keep trying to force myself to do things, go for a walk, listen to music, but it’s like there’s this gray film over everything. i just want to curl up and disappear. it’s not like anything bad happened, it’s just… this. constant dullness.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay so like... this is probably a weird thing to post, and idk if anyone will even read it or care, but i just... i feel so empty. it’s been six months since mark, six months since everything just… ended. and honestly, it feels like it happened yesterday. i keep expecting him to walk through the door, you know? like it’s a really bad dream. i keep looking for his jacket by the chair, or his coffee mug on the counter. stupid things, i know.

and then, like, last week i had this weird chest pain, just a little thing, and i panicked. went to urgent care, and they did all sorts of tests. everything came back “okay,” apparently, just stress and probably dehydration. but it just… it made me think. like, what else is “okay” that’s actually rotting me from the inside? i mean, mark’s gone, the house is too big, and i’m just… drifting. i don’t even really *do* anything anymore. i used to paint, remember? before he said it was “a silly hobby.” maybe i should pick it up again? maybe. but i don’t even know where to start.

it’s just… everything feels grey. like the world has lost all its color. i keep telling myself things will get better, that i’ll find a new routine, a new hobby, a new… something. but it feels so… distant. like a different person’s life. i just… i’m tired. really tired. i don’t even know what i’m expecting, honestly. just… less of this heavy feeling, i guess. i just want it to stop. maybe i should call my sister? no, she'd just tell me to “look on the bright side.” she doesn’t get it.

okay so like this happened and i don't even know how to start. my mom was freaking out yesterday about my chest, she thought i was having a panic attack or something, but it was actually...different. the school nurse took me to the hospital and they did all these tests and it turned out i have something called pericarditis. it’s basically inflammation around my heart. i just… i don't get it. i feel fine most of the time, like really okay, and then suddenly this.

it’s just… heavy. everything feels heavy. like i’m wading through mud. i keep thinking about all the things i’m supposed to be doing, college applications, getting a job, just like, growing up, and it’s just...too much. i keep replaying everything in my head, like if i could have just done something differently, said something differently, anything to avoid this.

my dad keeps saying it’s a “good thing” it was caught early and it’s treatable, but it doesn't feel good. it doesn't feel like anything good. it just feels…wrong. i’m supposed to be excited about the future, i know, everyone is. but i just feel… numb. like i'm watching myself from a distance. i don't even want to talk to anyone about it really, it’s just…a lot. i just want to go back to being normal, whatever that even is anymore. i keep staring at the ceiling and just… thinking. mostly about how exhausting everything is.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, this is so weird. My mom took me to the hospital yesterday, just like, randomly. They said my blood pressure was super high and my heart was acting up. It was nothing serious, they said, just stress, probably. But I keep thinking about it, you know? Like, what if it *is* something? I’ve just been… blah. Everything feels kind of grey lately. I don’t even really want to go to school, honestly. My friends are being nice, texting me, but it’s just… exhausting. I just want to curl up in bed and not do anything. I don’t know what’s wrong with me, really. Maybe it’s just the pressure of exams, maybe it's nothing. I just feel…heavy. I’m trying to be okay, I guess, but it’s hard.

Ugh, seriously, I just don't get it. Like, what's the point anymore? Lost my job last week, just… gone. No warning, no anything. They said “restructuring,” whatever that even means. It’s all just blurry, you know? Everything feels…gray. I keep staring at the ceiling at 3 am just thinking about how much I messed up, how I'm a failure. It’s ridiculous, I know, but it’s just…there. And then I can't sleep. Just tossing and turning, replaying everything that went wrong. I tried to watch something funny, but I just felt worse. Like, why even bother trying to feel *anything* positive? It’s exhausting. My apartment is a mess, I haven't showered in two days, and I just want to crawl into a hole and disappear. Mom keeps asking if I’m okay, and I just say “fine.” It’s pathetic, isn’t it? I feel so useless. Like I'm just... drifting. Just... existing. I used to have goals, plans, things to look forward to. Now it's just this heavy, dull ache. I keep telling myself it’ll get better, that I’ll find something new, but... it feels like a lie. Just a really, really loud, empty lie. I just… I don’t want to do this anymore.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: it’s just…everything feels gray lately. like someone turned down the saturation on the world. exams are brutal obviously, i’m drowning in readings and he just…left. like, completely vanished. i keep replaying everything in my head, wondering what i did wrong, if i could have said something different. it’s stupid i know, but i just feel so worthless now. like i’m not even worth a goodbye. my roommate keeps offering to help with the studying but i just nod and stare at my notes. i don’t even remember what i’m supposed to be learning. everything feels pointless. i just want to curl up and disappear. i keep going over the texts we sent, just… over and over. i don't even know why i'm doing it. it’s just…heavy. really heavy. i’m just… tired.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay so this is just... a lot. I moved like, a week ago and it’s been a disaster. Not a dramatic, yelling-at-the-sky disaster, but like a slow, dripping one. The apartment is fine, it’s small and beige and smells faintly of bleach, which is… something, I guess? But everything feels wrong. I keep staring at the walls and wondering if I made the biggest mistake of my life. Like, I thought a change would help, you know? Get me out of the same routine, the same faces, the same gray sky every single day. I needed *something*. But now I’m just... here. And it’s just… more gray.

The night shift is killing me. Everyone’s asleep, or pretending to be, and I’m just… watching. Watching the city breathe, watching the lights flicker, and it’s so lonely. I keep trying to find something to do, like reading or sketching, but my brain just… won’t cooperate. It’s like there’s this heavy blanket draped over everything, muffling all the colors. I tried calling my mom, but she just kept asking about the move and how I was “settling in,” and it just made me feel worse. Like I’m failing at being a functional adult. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. It’s not that I *want* to die or anything, it's just... the energy to even get out of bed sometimes feels like climbing a mountain.  I keep telling myself it’s just temporary, that I’ll adjust, but… I don’t even know *how* to adjust to this.  It’s just… heavy. Like carrying a rock in my chest.  And I keep thinking about how I haven't really spoken to anyone properly since I got here, just little exchanges with the security guard, and it's just... exhausting.

Okay seriously, I’m just…stuck. Like, completely and utterly stuck. This project is due on Friday and it’s like my brain just decided to take a permanent vacation. I’ve been staring at the same paragraph for the last three hours, rewriting it, deleting it, adding a sentence, deleting that sentence, you get the idea. It’s a vicious cycle. Everyone else seems to be cruising along, posting about their progress and stuff, and I’m just here, drowning in spreadsheets and half-baked ideas.

I keep telling myself “just finish one section,” or “just write the introduction,” but it’s like trying to move a mountain with a teaspoon. It feels pointless. Like no matter how much I do, it won’t be good enough. My boss keeps saying “don’t stress, just focus” but that’s the easiest thing to *say*. He doesn’t understand the feeling of this weight just…sitting there. It's not even really panic, just this heavy, dull ache. I keep thinking about just emailing him and saying I can’t do it, that I’m sorry, but then…then what? It wouldn’t fix anything, would it? Just make it worse. I just want to curl up and disappear. Coffee’s not helping. Neither is music. Nothing is. I just... I don't even want to open my laptop anymore. It’s just a reminder of everything I'm failing at.

Ugh, just… everything feels heavy. Mom and Dad are at each other again. It’s always something. I just want to disappear, you know? Like, just fade into the wallpaper. Not even a dramatic thing, just… gone.

Ugh, went to that work thing tonight. Just... everyone seemed to be having such a good time. Like, genuinely happy. I just stood in the corner and felt this awful weight. I don't even know why I bother trying anymore. It's exhausting.

just moved into this tiny apartment it smells like old cigarettes and regret honestly. everything feels wrong the walls are too thin i can hear my neighbor's TV and i just wanna curl up and disappear. i keep unpacking boxes but i can't even bring myself to put my toothbrush away. it’s like… the whole point of moving was supposed to be fresh starts and new beginnings but it just feels heavier. like i’m carrying this weight everywhere. i keep telling myself it'll get better, that i'll adjust, but it’s just… grey. everything’s just grey. i keep staring out the window at the rain and thinking maybe i should just stay where i was.  i don't even know why i'm writing this.  just... feeling really lost i guess.
Just...everything feels wrong. Liam won't stop crying and Mark keeps snapping at me. I don't even know how to breathe anymore, honestly. Just tired. So, so tired.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, I just… I don't even know what to do anymore. Liam broke up with me last night, and it just feels… heavy. Like a really thick blanket. I keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out where I went wrong, but idk. It’s just stupid, right? He said he needed space, which is like, whatever, I get it. But then he unfollowed me on everything. It’s just… weird. I’m trying to be okay, I guess. I’m supposed to be focusing on finals, but everything feels pointless. I keep staring at my phone, hoping he'll just… text. Maybe he’ll change his mind? Probably not, though. I just… feel so numb. Like everything's gray. I just want to curl up in bed and not do anything. Maybe tomorrow will be better? I hope. But probably not.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is hard to even type, honestly. I just… I don’t even know what’s happening anymore. It’s been weeks since Leo was born and it feels like everything’s just… wrong. My husband, Mark, he’s great, I really think he is, but he just… he doesn’t *get* it. Not really. He’s all, “You’re doing great! You’re a natural!” and I just feel like a failure. Like, I can’t sleep, I’m constantly exhausted, and I can’t seem to latch him properly half the time. He tries to help, of course, but it always feels like he's judging me, even if he’s not saying anything directly.

Yesterday, we had this huge fight. About the bottles. He wanted me to do them, I wanted him to. And then he started saying I was being ‘difficult’ and ‘not a team player.’ I just… I retreated to the bedroom and cried. Just sat there for ages, sobbing. He eventually came in and apologized, but it felt… hollow. Like he just said what he thought I wanted to hear. I dunno. Maybe I’m overreacting. Maybe I’m just a terrible mother. I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing, all the things I'm not. It’s just a constant weight, you know? Like, I want to love Leo so much, I really do, but it’s hard to feel that love when I feel like I'm constantly battling everyone around me. I just wish things could be easier. I just wish I knew what to do. I’m so tired.

text: Okay, so this is weird, right? I just… I don’t even know what’s happening. Moving here was supposed to be this fresh start, you know? Like, finally on my own, figuring things out. But it's just… gray. My mom keeps texting, mostly just saying how worried she is, and how I should call more. She doesn't get it, obviously. She thinks I’m being dramatic. My brother sends pictures of his kids, and it just makes me feel… smaller, I guess. Like I’m failing at being an adult. I tried to call him yesterday, and we just argued about… well, about everything. I don’t even remember what we argued about. It just felt pointless. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. Maybe I should just pack it all up and go back. I idk, I just feel so alone. It’s stupid, I know.

text: Ugh, honestly, I don't even know where to start. It’s just… everything feels so heavy lately, like I’m wading through mud. Mark left, obviously, and it’s been six months and it still physically hurts. I keep expecting to wake up and realize it was all a bad dream, but it’s not. And now this deadline for my thesis – it's like another weight just piled on top of everything else. I’m supposed to submit it next week and I’ve barely even started the conclusion, just staring at the screen, feeling completely blank. I just… don’t see the point anymore, you know? Like, what's the point of finishing anything when it's just going to lead to more empty days and more reminders of… well, everything. I just want to curl up and disappear, really. It's not even about the grade, it’s just… I feel so useless.

ugh i just... i don't even want to go out anymore like seriously what's the point everyone's going to ask me about my job and i just… i don't have anything to say. it's just going to be this awkward silence and judging looks and i can’t handle it. my phone keeps buzzing with invites from sarah and mark but i just keep ignoring them. it's like a wall i've built up around myself and it's getting harder and harder to even breathe through it. i just want to curl up in bed and not think about anything. everyone seems to be having such a good time and i’m just... here. feeling like i’m fading away, you know? it’s stupid i guess but it just feels so heavy. i wish i could just disappear for a little while.
Just…everything feels heavy. The baby is beautiful, but the bills aren't. Don't even know how we’re gonna make it. Just staring at the walls and not feeling anything. Seriously struggling, honestly.

text: Ugh, I just… I don’t even know why I went out tonight. Everyone was so happy, laughing, and I just felt like I was observing. Like I’m not part of it anymore. idk if I should have stayed home. Maybe I’m just tired. It’s hard, you know?

it’s just…everything is gray. like, really gray. i moved into this tiny apartment yesterday and it feels huge and empty and i just want to curl up on the floor and not move. boxes everywhere, unpacking is supposed to be good right? it’s not. it’s just more things to remind me of him, of us, of everything that’s gone. i keep looking around for his stuff, for his coffee mug, for anything that feels like it used to be ours and it’s just…nothing. i don’t even know where to start. i feel like i’m drowning and i can’t even find a life raft. i just want it to stop being so loud in my head. i just want to sleep.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s just… weird today i guess. feels like everyone else is okay. like they’re moving on or something. it’s the anniversary of… you know. the factory fire. it’s been almost a year and it just keeps hitting me like a wave. i keep expecting to wake up and realize it’s not real but it is. it’s all still so sharp. i went to the diner this morning, same booth, same coffee, same everything. just… emptier. i tried to talk to old man henderson, he used to tell the best stories about working there, but i just… froze. couldn't even say hi. he gave me this look, like he knew something was wrong. i mumbled something about being busy and left. busy doing what? staring at the walls? i don’t even know. i haven’t really been doing anything. just existing. my severance check ran out last week and i haven't found anything else. i keep applying, but it's just a constant stream of rejections. it’s like i’m invisible. i feel so useless. like i’ve lost my purpose. everything feels gray. i keep thinking about them, all those people… and then i think about me, and it just spirals. i just… wish it would all go away. i don’t know what to do anymore. i just feel so… heavy. like i'm carrying the whole world on my shoulders and it’s crushing me.  maybe i should just stay in bed.  maybe that’s all i’m good for now. just… staying in bed.
Just staring at this spreadsheet. It’s all just… gray. Like everything. Deadline is next week and I can’t even bring myself to open the textbook. Just… tired. Everything feels pointless, honestly.

text: it’s just… everything feels so heavy lately. moved to this city six months ago and it’s been awful. i thought finding a place, making new friends, would be amazing, but it’s just lonely. and then last week i went to the doctor for a weird pain in my side and they ran some tests. nothing serious apparently, just inflammation, but it made me think. like, everything could just… stop. i keep staring out the window at the rain and just feeling this dull ache. i ordered takeout again, didn’t even really eat it. i keep telling myself it’s just the stress of being new, but it’s more than that, isn’t it? i just wish i could shake this feeling of being… stuck. like there’s nothing to look forward to. i miss my mom terribly, she always knew how to make me feel a little better.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
okay seriously i don't even know what's happening anymore like i just moved to this tiny apartment downtown and it's supposed to be exciting right? new city new life everything? but it just feels… heavy. everything is so loud all the time even when it's quiet i just hear it buzzing. and i'm drowning in exams i have like three huge papers due next week and i can't even focus on any of them. my roommate, elara, she's nice but she's always studying or going out and i just feel like i'm watching her live a life i can't seem to get into. it's not that i don't want to be social, i just… i don’t have the energy. or the motivation. it's like a thick fog just settled over everything and i can't seem to shake it off.

i keep staring out the window at the rain and just thinking about how pointless it all is. like what’s the point of getting good grades if i’m just going to end up doing the same thing as everyone else? a job i hate, an apartment i don’t love, a life that feels like it’s just slowly fading away. i keep telling myself it’s just exam stress, that it’ll pass, but it doesn’t feel like it’s passing. it feels like it’s just… settling in. i just want to curl up in bed and disappear. i feel so unbelievably tired all the time, not just physically but like a deep bone-weary kind of tired. i keep thinking about just skipping class and not showing up for anything. it’s stupid i know but it’s just… a thought. a really persistent thought. i just wish i could feel something other than this gray. i just don't know what to do anymore

text: Ugh, just… nothing feels right anymore, you know? Like everyone else is just… living, and I'm stuck here watching it from the sidelines. I tried to text Liam after history today but he didn't even reply, which isn’t a big deal usually, but it just… amplified everything. I just feel so incredibly alone, like there's this huge wall between me and everyone. My parents are busy, they don’t really *get* it, you know? They just tell me to “get out there” and “make friends,” but it’s not that simple. It's like I'm speaking a different language, and everyone else is just laughing. I just want to disappear sometimes, honestly. Just fade away into the background. It’s not that I *want* to hurt anyone, it's just... this heavy feeling, like a stone in my chest.
