Just…nothing feels right anymore. Lost my job last week, and then the doctor said…it’s not great. Just staring at the walls. Everything’s gray. Like, really gray. Don’t even want to get out of bed.

it’s just… everything feels gray lately like someone turned the saturation down on the whole world. i don’t even know why i’m typing this really. just… needed to get it out. mark left last week. i knew it was coming, honestly. we’d been drifting for months, little silences, him staring at his phone, just… not *there*. i tried. i really did. i cooked his favorite meals, i planned little weekends away, i just kept trying to remind him that i existed, you know? like a constant, reliable thing. but it wasn't enough. he said he needed “space” and “to find himself” which is basically code for ‘i found someone younger who doesn’t make me feel like i’m a burden’. i guess. it’s stupid, isn't it? i’m 48. i’ve spent the last twenty years pouring everything into him and the kids, and now… nothing. i feel like i’m just… fading. the kids are doing okay, bless their hearts, they don’t understand why dad isn’t home as much. i don’t really explain it. what’s the point? it's just… heavy. everything feels heavy. the dishes, the laundry, just getting out of bed. i don’t even want to go to the grocery store. i don't even care about the garden anymore. it’s all just… pointless. i just want to disappear, honestly. not like… not like *that*, just… quiet. i just wish i could find a way to just feel something other than this dull ache. i just… i don’t know.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, I just… I don’t even know where to start. Everyone else seems to be, like, totally crushing it with their finals prep, right? And I’m just… here. Back in my mom’s basement, staring at this History paper and feeling like it’s just… pointless. Like, what’s the point of knowing about the Tudors if I can’t even figure out how to turn in an assignment on time? I dunno, maybe I should just drop the class. It’s probably fine if I fail, honestly. It wouldn’t be the *end* of the world. I just… I feel so heavy. Like there’s this weight on my chest all the time, and it’s getting harder and harder to just, you know, breathe. I keep telling myself to just write one paragraph, but then I get stuck and then I just… don’t.

just… ugh. another night. feels like i’m swimming through molasses. everyone’s yelling downstairs, you know? mom and dad. again. it’s always something, isn’t it? bills, jobs, how i’m “always tired.” like i *choose* to be exhausted. it’s not like i asked for this shift, or for them to be so… intense. i just want to disappear, you know? into the hum of the machines, the fluorescent lights, anything but hearing their voices. it's like they don't even see me, like i'm just... there. a shadow.

i tried to make dinner last night, just a simple pasta thing. it burned. i burned the whole damn thing. and then dad started in about how i never do anything right, how i should just “learn to cook properly.” like i have nothing else going on. like i don’t spend twelve hours a day staring at a screen, trying not to fall asleep. it's stupid, i know, but it just… piles up. everything feels heavy.

i keep thinking about when i was little, and how they used to read to me. how they used to *listen* to me. now they just… ignore me. or worse, criticize. i used to think they loved me, but i don’t know anymore. it feels like a really, really long time ago. i just wish i could turn it off. the noise, the arguing, the feeling like i’m not worth the effort of being seen. it's just... too much. i just want it to stop. i don't even know what i want anymore, honestly. just… quiet. please.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s the anniversary today. i almost didn’t post anything. it just feels… wrong. like acknowledging it makes it real again. i keep going through it you know? the way it happened. it was awful. and now it’s just… here. a shadow hanging over everything. i feel like i’m just existing, not actually living. the baby is beautiful, of course, the cutest little thing ever but i just stare at them and feel this huge ache. like a piece of me is missing. i tried to make dinner tonight, burned it completely. just… everything feels hard. it’s like i’m wading through mud and i can’t quite find my footing. i just want to disappear sometimes, not like anything bad would happen, just… gone. i don’t even know what i’m saying. sorry.

Ugh. Just… ugh. It’s always something, isn’t it? I swear, my brother just doesn’t *get* it. Mom’s getting worse, obviously, but he’s acting like it’s *my* fault she’s confused and forgetting things. Like I’m not doing enough. He keeps saying “you should be doing more,” like I’m just sitting here twiddling my thumbs. And he doesn't even *see* how exhausted I am. I'm driving her to appointments, making sure she eats, trying to keep the house clean, answering all the calls from the nursing home... it's a full-time job, and then some.

He wants me to sell the house. Says it's too much for me to handle alone. Like selling the house will magically fix everything. It’s *our* house, you know? Mom lived here for fifty years. It’s all we have left of… well, of her. And him just casually dropping that suggestion like it's the simplest solution makes me want to scream. Seriously, does he even think about how much this means to me? He’s always been so… detached. Like he just observes everything from a distance. I feel like I’m shouting into a void.

I just want a moment. Just five minutes to myself where I don’t have to be Mom’s eyes, ears, and hands. But it's never that simple, is it? It’s always “one more thing,” “just a little help.” I don’t know how much longer I can keep doing this. It’s just... heavy. Really heavy.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: okay so like seriously what is happening to me i feel just…heavy you know? it’s exam season which is already a nightmare but then yesterday i went to the gp because my chest started hurting randomly like a really weird sharp pain and they did a quick ECG and said it was probably just anxiety and to rest but it felt so much more than that. it was like a tightness a pressure and i just panicked. i called my mom but she was at work and i just started crying and i don’t even know why. it’s stupid i know everyone says ‘oh you’re young you’ll be fine’ but it’s not fine it feels like…like something’s stuck in my chest and i can’t breathe properly even when i’m sitting down. i keep replaying it in my head the feeling the phone call my mom’s voice. i haven’t been sleeping properly either just tossing and turning and thinking about everything i have to do for these exams. it’s like all the pressure is just crushing me and i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. i feel so useless and like i’m failing at everything even though i’m trying so hard. i just…i don’t know what to do. i keep telling myself it’s just stress but it feels…different. it’s just a mess honestly a really big mess and i don't think i can keep going at this pace. i just wish i could feel normal again. like before this started. it's just so tiring feeling like this.

text: Okay, so like, I just… I don't even know where to start, honestly. It’s just… everything feels muted, you know? Like I’m watching a movie on a really bad TV, the colors are all washed out and the sound is distant. I used to *care* about things, like, actually care. Presenting at work was terrifying, sure, but I was proud of the projects, genuinely. Now? I just go through the motions. Saying the right things, nodding along, pretending I understand the spreadsheets, but it’s all just…noise. I keep thinking about my promotion, it’s supposed to be amazing, but it just feels…heavy. Like another thing I’m failing at, even though I technically haven’t *done* anything wrong. I keep replaying conversations in my head, wondering if I said the wrong thing, if I look tired, if they think I’m not engaged. It’s exhausting, constantly analyzing myself. And it’s not like anything specific happened, there's no huge fight or bad news or anything, it's just…this. This slow, creeping feeling that everything is pointless. I try to force myself to do things, like go for a run or call a friend, but it’s like pushing a really heavy rock uphill. It just…doesn’t stick. I feel so disconnected from everything, from myself, even. I just want to curl up and disappear, but then I worry about what that would *mean*, you know? Just…existing like this, not doing anything, not feeling anything…it's scary.  I’m just…blah. Really, really blah.  I miss feeling…something.  Anything.

text:
ugh just... ugh. it's just everything all at once isn't it? i feel like i’m drowning and i can’t even swim properly. everyone else seems to be moving forward, getting jobs, making plans, and i’m just… here. back in my parents’ basement staring at the ceiling. they don't get it, obviously. they keep saying “pull yourself together” like it’s that simple. like i just need to *decide* to be happy. it’s not that easy, you know?

i had that interview last week, the one for the marketing assistant thing? i totally bombed it. i spaced on the questions, i stumbled over my words, i just felt... numb. i think i even forgot my own name for a second. and then i found out they’re going with someone who’s been working in the industry for five years. five years! i’m fresh out of college and i feel like a complete failure.

and the money. oh god the money. i’m behind on rent, again. i’m selling everything i can – my old guitar, my favorite sweater, even that weird limited edition figurine my grandpa gave me. it doesn't even matter. it's just...more bills. more worry. it’s like a constant weight pressing down on my chest. i keep telling myself things will get better, that this is just a temporary setback, but... i don't even *want* to believe it. i just want to curl up and disappear. honestly i don't know what the point of doing anything is anymore. just...tired. so incredibly tired.

text:
Okay so like, this is probably weird to even write about, but something kinda happened today. I went to the doctor, just for a checkup you know, like a routine thing, and they did, like, a bunch of tests. Mostly just blood work, I think? And the nurse was being super nice, but she kept looking at me kinda funny and asking a lot of questions about how I’ve been feeling lately. I told her I’ve just been, like, tired, you know? Really tired all the time. And stressed about school, obviously, it’s the end of the semester and everything’s due. I don't know, it's just a lot.

And then the doctor came in and he was, like, really thorough. He asked about my family history, and my sleep schedule, and if I was eating properly. He seemed a little concerned, actually. He said he wanted to run some more tests, just to be safe. Like, a chest x-ray or something. I don't even know why my chest! It's probably nothing, right? It's probably just that I haven’t been taking care of myself. I’ve been ordering a ton of takeout and just, like, staring at my laptop all night.

I’m trying not to freak out, but my heart is beating really fast whenever I think about it. Maybe it’s just anxiety? Or maybe it’s something actually wrong. I don't know. My mom keeps asking me if I'm okay, and I just tell her I’m fine, but I don’t want to lie to her. I just... I just want to go back to normal. Like, you know, just feeling like myself again. It’s probably nothing, though, right? Just a precaution. I should probably call the doctor tomorrow to find out what’s going on. Ugh, this is so stressful. I just wish I could chill out for once.

I’m making a huge pot of coffee. Need the caffeine. Gonna try to get through this afternoon. Maybe walk around the park later. Just… breathe. Seriously, just breathe. It’s annoying how simple things can feel so complicated sometimes, you know? Like, I'm fine. Just… fine.

text:
okay so like seriously, I’m running on fumes tonight and I just… I don’t even know where to start. it’s been, like, a whole week now of just this awful, heavy sleepiness, but not like tired sleepy, you know? more like… disconnected. it’s weird, right? I’m *awake*, I’m scrolling through my phone, I’m making coffee (which is the only thing keeping me somewhat functional, let’s be honest), but it’s just… not *me*. I keep staring at the ceiling and just thinking about, like, random things. Mostly squirrels. I swear I’ve been watching squirrels more than actual people lately. and they’re judging me, I just *know* they are. they’re tiny little judgmental squirrels.

I tried going to bed early last night, like, eight o’clock, and I was out cold within five minutes. but then I woke up, like, three hours later, completely drenched in sweat and feeling even *more* useless. and then I just lay there, staring at the shadows, and it just spiraled. I keep thinking about all the paperwork I need to do tomorrow, and how it’s all just going to pile up and I’ll be even more behind. and then I’ll get yelled at, probably. it's just a bad cycle. It's like my brain is stuck in a loop of beige and anxiety. I feel so sluggish, like moving molasses. I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear, but then I’d miss my cat, Mr. Fluffernutter, and he’d be sad, and then *I’d* be sad, and that's just… too much. so, yeah. that’s my night. just a long, slow, squirrel-filled mess.  I should probably just go back to bed, but I don't even know if I *can*.  It’s just… exhausting, you know?

okay so like seriously i don’t even know what to do anymore. it’s just…quiet. too quiet. i keep expecting him to be here you know? just sitting on the couch scrolling through something stupid or making coffee or whatever. and there’s nothing. it’s been three weeks and it feels like a lifetime. i keep going through the motions, like brushing my teeth and making breakfast and replying to emails but it’s all just…empty. i tried calling my mom yesterday but she kept asking about the house, about furniture, about whether i’d found a new place. it’s not about the house mom it’s about…everything. it’s about the fact that there *is* no new place. i just…i don’t have anyone to share it with. everyone i talk to just says “you’ll get over it” or “you’re better off” and i’m like no you don’t understand. it’s not like i *want* to be alone. it’s just…i am. and it’s hard. really hard. i spent the whole afternoon just staring at the ceiling. i think i might just order pizza and watch terrible reality tv. is that it? is that all there is? i miss his dumb jokes i miss his annoying habit of leaving his socks everywhere i miss just…having someone. even if they were a total pain. i just…i just wish things were different. i wish things were back to how they were. maybe that’s just a stupid fantasy though right?

text: Ugh, school today was just...a lot. It's probably nothing, I guess, just a random day, but it just feels like everyone else is having, like, the best time ever and I’m just stuck here staring at my phone. I need a nap seriously, or maybe just a giant ice cream sundae. What even is life, you know?

okay so like this happened and i don't even know where to start honestly. i was on my usual shift last night, 3am like always, and i just felt…off. not sick exactly, more like…fuzzy? like the edges of my brain were just kind of blurred. i kept messing up orders, spilling coffee, you know the usual. mr. henderson was being particularly grumpy about his decaf and i just wanted to disappear. then my chest started hurting. just a dull ache, but it kept getting worse. i tried to ignore it, told myself it was just heartburn or something, but it wasn't. it was like this pressure, you know? and i started getting really shaky. i went to the breakroom and grabbed some water, tried to breathe deeply, but it didn't help. my coworker, liam, noticed and he was super concerned, he asked if i was okay and i just mumbled something about being tired. i felt so stupid. he kept asking if i should go to the hospital and i kept saying no, just that it was probably nothing. but it *was* something.

i finally called my mom, panicked a little, and she told me to go to urgent care. i didn’t want to, honestly, but i knew she was right. they ran some tests and it turned out to be nothing serious, just a weird panic attack brought on by exhaustion and probably a little bit of stress. they gave me some pills and told me to rest. i’m sitting here now, wrapped in a blanket, feeling completely drained. i keep replaying everything in my head and wondering if i should have gone to the hospital sooner. i just… i don’t even know. it was a really weird night. and i’m so tired.

text: Okay so like seriously this whole moving thing is just…a lot. I’ve been unpacking boxes for, I don’t even know, like three days straight and I’m pretty sure my back is permanently fused now. It’s this tiny apartment in Oakhaven, you know, the one with the weirdly angled windows and the slightly sticky floors? It’s…fine, I guess. It’s definitely smaller than my old place, obviously, and I miss having a dishwasher, like, desperately. I tried to put everything away neatly, you know, I really did, but it just looks chaotic and I keep rearranging things and then it's even worse. My roommate, Silas, he’s really nice, super chill, just…quiet. He makes these amazing lentil stews, which is great, honestly, but he doesn’t really talk about anything, just, you know, eats and reads. I feel like I’m trying to build a conversation and he’s just…observing. It’s not bad, just…different. I got a new coffee maker, a French press, which was a good investment I think, and I’m desperately trying to figure out how to make decent coffee. It’s a whole thing, right? And the neighborhood is…odd. There’s this old bookstore down the street, “The Dusty Quill,” and it smells intensely of paper and something vaguely musty, it’s kind of comforting actually. I walked past it again today and there was this guy reading a really thick, leather-bound book, wearing a fedora – seriously, a fedora! – and I just wanted to know what he was reading, but I chickened out. I need to, like, meet people, I know, but it’s just…a lot. I just want to, like, settle in and not feel so overwhelmed. Maybe I'll try going for a walk tomorrow, just to get out of the apartment.

text: okay so like, this is probably going to sound really weird but I just… I don't know. Moving here was supposed to be this amazing fresh start, right? You know, escape everything. But it’s just… loud. And lonely. My mom keeps texting me, like, constantly, asking if I’ve “made any friends” and if I’m “eating properly.” It’s not that I *want* to talk about it, obviously, but it’s just relentless. My dad called yesterday too, and he was all, “just call more, you’re isolating yourself.” Like, seriously? He doesn’t even *understand*. He thinks I’m just being dramatic because I’m alone. Which, I guess I am, but it's not just about being alone. It's about… everything. They keep saying I need to “reach out” and “be more involved,” and it just feels like they’re not hearing me at all. They want me to be this happy, successful, put-together version of myself, and I'm just… not feeling it. I tried calling my sister, but she just immediately launched into a story about her new boyfriend, so I hung up. Maybe I should just block their numbers for a while? I don’t know. It’s just… a lot. I just wanted a new beginning, and it feels like I’ve dragged everyone else down with me. I don’t know what to do. Idk, maybe I’ll just watch TV and order pizza. That’s probably the best I can do tonight. Ugh.

text: Okay so like, tonight was…weird. I went to that little bar downtown, the one with the sticky floors and the guy playing blues on a battered guitar. I really wanted to meet someone, you know? I ended up talking to a woman about her dog, a pug named Winston, and it was actually really nice, honestly. She was telling me about all his weird habits – sleeping upside down, stealing socks, the whole nine yards. It felt…good, I guess? Just a little bit of connection, even if it was about a pug. This city is so lonely sometimes, I just keep thinking about my mom and how she always said I’d figure it out, but honestly, it's a struggle. Maybe Winston is the key to making friends? Seriously considering adopting a pug.

text: Okay so last night was… a lot. Seriously, I don’t even know where to start. Liam invited me to this networking thing, you know, for people in marketing, and I was like “sure, why not?” but honestly, I was so awkward the entire time. Like, everyone was just so… confident, and talking about their campaigns and KPIs and stuff, and I was just standing there, clutching my water bottle and trying not to spill it, which, by the way, almost happened like five times. There was this one guy, Mark, who kept asking me about my “vision” for the future of social media, and I just kind of mumbled something about, like, making it more visually appealing, I think? I don’t even know if that made any sense. And then Sarah from accounting, she’s so bubbly, she was telling this incredibly long story about her cat, Mr. Snuggles, and how he apparently judges her outfit choices, and I was just nodding along, desperately trying to look interested, but really I was just thinking about what I was going to have for dinner. It’s weird, right? I feel like I should have said something, like asked a question or joined a conversation, but the thought of actually talking to strangers just completely paralyzed me. It’s not that I don’t like people, it's just...a lot, you know? Like, I thrive on my routine, my books, my cat, Mr. Whiskers – he doesn’t judge my outfits, thankfully – and just being thrown into this whirlwind of professional small talk felt completely overwhelming. I think I just need a massive cup of tea and a very long scroll through Reddit. Seriously, Reddit is my safe space.

text: Okay so… it’s the 6 month anniversary of, you know, everything. It’s weird. Like, I keep expecting a text, or a call, or *something* but there’s just… nothing. It’s not that I’m angry, not really. More like… hollow? I don’t even know. I went to the place we used to go, the little Italian restaurant by the river, and it was… fine. The pasta was good, the waiter was nice, but it just felt… empty. Like eating a really good meal with no one to share it with. I saw a couple holding hands and it just hit me, like a physical thing, you know? I mean, I get it, everyone deserves that. But it's just... hard. I’m trying to be okay, honestly. Trying to be *normal*. I’m reading a lot of books, going for walks, even started knitting – which is ridiculous, I know – but it’s something to do. I dunno, maybe I’m overthinking it. Probably. It’s just… six months. A lot of time, but also not that much, right? Maybe I should just forget about it. Or maybe I shouldn't. I don't know. Seriously, idk. It’s just… a day. A date. A marker. I just want to... not feel this. Like, maybe a nice cup of tea and a quiet evening will help. Or maybe not. Just… processing, I guess.

text: Ugh honestly this is just… a lot. Like, everything feels blurry and weird and i keep staring at the baby and just feeling this huge wave of sadness and then like, what even happened? okay so like i don’t even know where to start honestly. it’s just… a lot. he said he needed space. space like, a whole galaxy of space. i guess i thought if i just kept being there, you know, making dinners, doing the laundry, trying to make him laugh even when he wasn’t laughing… it would somehow fix it. stupid, right? i just wanted him to be okay. and i felt okay when i was okay-ing things for him. it’s ridiculous i know. we’ve been together fifteen years. fifteen years of sunday mornings making pancakes and arguing about the thermostat and him always leaving his socks everywhere. i hate socks. seriously. i’m trying to be strong. i really am. i’m supposed to be the grown-up here, you know? but it feels like someone ripped a giant hole in my chest and all the air just rushed out. i’m going to miss him. i don’t want to miss him. but i also don’t want to keep pretending like everything’s fine when it’s completely not. my mom keeps saying “time heals all wounds” but she never went through this. and honestly, i just want a cup of tea and a quiet evening. and maybe a really, really long shower. i'm just… tired. so incredibly tired. i'm going to order pizza tonight. pepperoni, extra cheese. that’s about it.

Ugh, tonight was… a lot. Seriously, I don’t get how people just *enjoy* these things. The book club. It’s supposed to be relaxing, right? Not a battlefield of opinions and passive aggression. Brenda spent twenty minutes dissecting why the protagonist’s choice of wallpaper was “clearly a symbol of repressed anxieties.” Repressed anxieties?! It was beige! Beige is calming! And then Mark started arguing with David about the historical accuracy of the setting – like, they were in a pub in 1920s Dublin! They both had these intense, furrowed brows and were practically vibrating.

Honestly, I just wanted to read. I brought a new tea, Earl Grey with lavender, which, by the way, is *amazing* if anyone wants some. But no, I had to referee. I tried to steer it back to the book, gently, suggesting we maybe move on, but it was like trying to redirect a river with a teaspoon. Then Sarah started talking about her pottery class, and it just spiraled. Pottery! Seriously? I’m trying to keep my husband from forgetting to take his medication and *this* is what I'm dealing with. 

I just went for the door halfway through. Said I had a headache. A *really* bad headache. They asked if I wanted to stay for dessert. I just nodded and left. Need to go home and make a fort and watch something mindless. Seriously considering just becoming a hermit. Anyone else feel like this sometimes? Like you just want to disappear into a corner and not interact with anyone?
ugh just got out of a family dinner. so much talking, so many questions about work. honestly just wanted to sit and watch the tv. everyone’s so loud and i feel like i need a nap. seriously drained.

It’s silly, I know, just a little bit overwhelmed, that's all. text: Okay so, this is weird, right? Like, I’m just sitting here staring at the rain again, which, honestly, I used to kinda love, you know? Like, a good thunderstorm and a cup of tea – that was *my* thing. But now it just… amplifies everything. It’s like the grayness outside is just reflecting the grayness inside. It’s been, I don’t know, almost six months now since… well, since it all ended. Six months of just… this. This quiet. It’s deafening sometimes, you know? I keep expecting to hear *something*. Like, a voice, or a car door slamming, or even just the TV on – anything to break the… the nothingness. I tried going to the grocery store yesterday, just a simple thing, you know? But it was… overwhelming. Everyone was just, like, chatting and laughing and buying stuff and it felt like I was watching a movie of people's lives that I wasn't invited to. And I just… left. Just drove straight home. It’s stupid, I know. I feel so useless, like I’m just… drifting. I keep thinking about all the things we used to do, just little random things, like ordering pizza on Fridays or going for walks in the park. It's just...hard to remember how to do *anything* without him. It's like a muscle atrophied from not being used. I miss having someone to just… talk to, you know? Not about big things, just about the weather or what I had for lunch. It sounds pathetic, I’m sure. I just… I don’t know what to do anymore. I wish I could just snap out of it, but it’s like being stuck underwater, you know? Just… struggling to breathe.

And then I start worrying about work. I'm supposed to be prepping for this presentation next week and I haven't even really started. It's just… a giant, looming thing. I keep telling myself to break it down into smaller steps, but it feels so overwhelming. Maybe I should just, idk, call Sarah? She’s always so calm and organized. But then I feel like I'm bothering her. She’s got her own stuff going on, obviously.

I tried taking a melatonin, but it just made me feel weird and anxious. Like, jittery. So that wasn't helpful. I think I need a new pillow, maybe? Or a weighted blanket? I saw someone on TikTok talking about those, and they seemed kinda soothing. It’s probably nothing, though. Just a phase. A really, really annoying phase. I just wish I could just… turn it off. Like, mentally. But that doesn’t seem possible, does it? I'm probably overthinking it, as usual. I just… need some sleep. Seriously.

text:
Okay, seriously, this week is just...a lot. Moving to Oakhaven was supposed to be this huge, fresh start thing, you know? Like, a chance to actually *do* something with my degree. But the job market here is basically nonexistent and my apartment smells vaguely of old carpet and regret. I’ve been trying to unpack, but it’s just… overwhelming. Boxes everywhere, and I keep finding random things – a half-finished cross-stitch project from college, a ticket stub from a concert I can’t even remember, a single, lonely sock. 

And then there's the paper. Professor Davies' paper. It’s due on Friday and it’s, like, a thousand pages long. I swear I’ve read the same paragraph five times. I keep zoning out, staring at the ceiling, and then suddenly I realize it’s 3 AM. I tried to make a schedule, color-coded and everything, but it just ended up looking like a chaotic explosion of pens and sticky notes. I think I might just pull an all-nighter. Or two. Or three. Honestly, I just need to finish this thing. It's the only thing keeping me from completely dissolving into a pile of anxiety and takeout containers. Don’t even get me started on the grocery store - I need milk, bread, and a whole lot of caffeine. This city is…loud. And lonely. But the paper is the priority, obviously. Gotta get that grade.

text: Ugh, just staring at my phone again. It's kinda boring here, you know? Like, everyone else is doing stuff and I’m just…here. idk what to do. Maybe I should call Mom, but that feels weird.

Okay, so like, this is weird to even type but... it's over. Seriously over. Liam and I, we just… stopped. Not like, a dramatic fight or anything. Just… quiet. A lot of quiet. For weeks. We stopped doing things we used to do. Movies, walks, even just sitting next to each other. It’s just… awkward. I keep expecting him to say something, anything, but he just avoids eye contact. I asked him yesterday, really just laid it out there, if we were okay, if he was okay. He just mumbled something about needing space and went to his room. I don’t even know what space looks like for him anymore. Honestly, it’s just… sad. I’m trying to be all “it’s for the best” and “new chapter” but it feels like ripping off a bandage. A really, really gross bandage. 

I spent the whole afternoon cleaning out our apartment – his stuff is just… everywhere. His clothes, his books, his ridiculously complicated coffee mugs. It’s like he just vanished. I found this old photo of us from last summer, at the beach. We looked so happy. Like, genuinely happy. It's stupid, I know, but it just made me feel worse. I’m going to try and watch something mindless tonight, probably trashy reality TV. Or maybe just stare at the ceiling. I don't really feel like doing anything. It's just... a lot. And I'm tired. Really, really tired.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, this whole new city thing is... a lot. Seriously. I moved here on a whim, you know? Just packed a bag and drove, and now I’m basically living in a slightly depressing studio apartment with a view of a brick wall and a really loud pigeon. It’s beautiful, I guess, in a bleak, concrete sort of way. I spent the whole weekend trying to figure out the bus system and it was just… overwhelming. Like, everyone just *knows* how to do it, and I’m just standing there staring at the schedule like it’s written in hieroglyphics. And the coffee here is awful, honestly, it tastes like burnt sadness. I went to a little park today, which was nice, there were kids playing and an old man feeding the ducks - it felt… normal, I think? Which is a big deal, because everything feels so completely off-kilter. I miss my mom, obviously, she always made the best apple pie, and my cat, Mr. Snuggles, he's probably judging me from home right now. I keep replaying conversations in my head, things I should have said, things I didn't. Just little regrets, you know? It's not even anything major, just… a general feeling of being adrift. I tried to call my best friend but the signal was terrible and I just kept hanging up. It's stupid, I know, but it just felt… hard. I'm trying to like it here, really I am, but it’s like I'm wearing someone else's clothes and they don’t quite fit. Maybe I just need a really big slice of apple pie and a fluffy cat.

Ugh. Another night. Feels like the whole world’s asleep and I’m stuck watching reruns of some cooking show from 2012. Seriously, who even *does* that? I tried calling Mom earlier, but she’s already snoring. She doesn’t understand, you know? This isn’t a choice. It’s just… how it is. The graveyard shift. It’s a black hole. I keep staring at the blank screens and thinking about… well, nothing much. Just the fluorescent lights buzzing and the smell of stale coffee. It’s a really weird smell, actually. Like sadness and burnt sugar. I made a sandwich, turkey and swiss, but it just sits there on my desk. Didn’t even touch it. Don’t really feel like eating, I guess. It's just... quiet. So, so quiet. I wish someone would just say something. Anything. Like, “Hey, you okay?” But no one ever does. I just keep staring at the monitor. Waiting for something to happen. Which it never does. Just the beeping of the printers and the occasional drunken phone call. It’s a lonely kind of boring. I think I’ll just finish this shift and go straight to bed. Maybe sleep will make it all go away. Probably not. Just… sleep.

text: Okay, so like… this is weird to even type, but I just deleted his number. Seriously. It feels so final, but also like… maybe not? We just kinda… drifted, you know? Ugh, seriously, this sleep thing is a mess. Like, a *huge* mess. It’s been weeks since… well, since everything. And I just… can’t. Not really. My brain is just a hamster on a wheel going, going, going, but nowhere. I keep waking up, like, three or four times a night. Just staring at the ceiling. Thinking about… stuff. Not good stuff. Mostly just remembering things. Small things, big things, everything all mixed up.

Last night I think I was back in the kitchen, arguing. Not a huge argument, but you know, the kind that just keeps building until it’s a brick wall. And then you're both just yelling about the same stupid thing over and over. It's exhausting. I feel like I’m trapped in a loop.

I tried reading, but my eyes keep drifting shut. And then I'm jolted awake, heart pounding, thinking about how he used to leave the lights on. Little things, I know, but they just… they keep popping up. I bought some lavender oil, figured that would help. It smells nice, I guess. Didn’t do anything.

I keep scrolling through Instagram, seeing people laughing with their friends, going to concerts, doing *stuff*. It's just…a lot. I don’t even know where to start. I just wanted a fresh start, you know? A quiet place to just *be*. Guess I got quiet, alright. Just…really, really quiet. Maybe I'll just order pizza and watch a movie with Nimbus. That's the plan anyway.

text:
Okay, so tonight was… something. Honestly, I don’t even know where to start. Mark’s sister, Bethany, came over for dinner – she’s always been a bit of a whirlwind, you know? Like a tiny, sparkly tornado of energy and opinions. And her husband, David, he’s… well, he’s polite, very polite, but he just doesn’t *get* things, I think. Like, he nodded a lot when I was talking about the leaky faucet and the fact that Leo’s been having trouble sleeping, but it felt like he was just hearing the words, not actually listening. It’s exhausting, trying to explain everything to people who just don’t have the bandwidth for it. I swear, sometimes I feel like I’m speaking a different language.

Then Bethany started talking about her new pottery class and how she’s “finally found her passion” and I just… I don’t even know what to say. It’s nice, of course, genuinely nice, but it feels so far removed from what I’m dealing with, you know? Like, I’m juggling three different medications, trying to keep a teenager from disappearing into his room, and making sure he eats something that isn’t just cereal for breakfast and dinner, and she’s worried about glazes and firing temperatures. It’s not that I don’t appreciate her enthusiasm, it’s just… it’s a different kind of worry, a different kind of joy.

And the kids, of course, they were being kids. Leo kept trying to steal David’s crackers, and Chloe was building a tower out of the dinner napkins, and I was just desperately trying to keep the conversation flowing, not let it devolve into a chaotic mess. I think I said something completely ridiculous about the squirrels in the backyard, just to break the tension, and then Bethany started talking about her cat, Mr. Whiskers, and I just wanted to disappear. It was just… a lot. A really, really long, complicated dinner. I need a glass of wine, definitely. And maybe a nap.

Ugh, Mom yelling again. Seriously, what is it this time? Said I’m a failure for not calling more. Like I don’t have a life, you know? Working these nights, barely sleeping, just trying to keep a roof over our heads. She doesn’t get it. Dad just sighs and looks at his phone. It’s always something. Just wish they’d actually *listen* for once. It's exhausting. I just want to curl up and watch some dumb show, but I have to finish this paperwork. Seriously, the paperwork. This job is killing me. And now this.  Guess I’ll just…deal with it, I suppose.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
Okay seriously I haven’t slept properly in like a week. Exams are looming and I’m just… staring at the ceiling. It’s ridiculous, right? Like, I know I should be studying but my brain just won’t shut off. I keep replaying everything I’ve read, but it’s all just a jumbled mess. Professor Davies’ lecture on 18th-century economic theory? Gone. My notes on the French Revolution? Blurred. I just keep thinking about the essay and how it has to be, like, perfect. And if it’s not perfect, what even is the point?

Mom keeps asking if I’m eating and I just say yeah, yeah, I had a sandwich. But I haven't really been hungry. Just fueled by coffee and the sheer terror of failing. My roommate, Leo, keeps trying to be helpful, offering me tea and stuff, but honestly I just want to be left alone. It’s not that I don’t appreciate it, it’s just… the quiet is worse. The quiet makes me think more. And the more I think, the more anxious I get. I keep checking the clock, like, five times a minute. Ugh. I feel like I’m going to short circuit. Just need to get through tonight, I guess. Maybe if I just force myself to open my textbook… but like, probably not. Definitely not. Just… need to sleep. Or, you know, something resembling sleep.
ugh seriously just want some peace and quiet tonight. josh keeps asking about the baby like a million times. i just need five minutes alone, is that too much to ask? feeling so overwhelmed tbh.

Ugh, another night. Just staring at the ceiling. Landed the gig last week, thought it was gonna be amazing, turns out it was… nothing. Just a lot of spreadsheets and endless meetings. Seriously messed up my sleep schedule, I’m running on fumes. Coffee’s my best friend now. Trying to read, but my brain just… won’t shut off. It’s weird, like it’s still looking for something to do. Just feeling kinda… adrift, I guess. Mom keeps asking if I'm eating properly, which is… yeah, not really. Gonna try to get some sleep, but honestly, probably won't.

text: Okay so like, this is probably super weird to even type out but i just… i don’t know. Mom made meatloaf tonight, which is, like, a classic comfort thing, you know? But it just tasted… bland. Really bland. And I just kept thinking about how much better it would have been with, like, a really spicy tomato sauce. Or maybe some mushrooms. It’s stupid, right? Seriously, it's just meatloaf. But I keep going back to it. I feel like i’m overthinking everything, which is, like, my default setting anyway, i guess. I finished my degree last month and everyone’s asking about job applications and what I'm gonna do next and honestly, i just… idk. I applied to a few places, mostly just retail stuff, because, you know, money. But it all feels so… pointless. My roommate, Liam, he’s been really into this pottery thing and he’s making like, amazing bowls. He’s even thinking about selling them online. It’s cool, I guess. I tried to make a small thing with clay once, when i was, like, eight, and it just ended up looking like a lopsided potato. Maybe I’m just not creative. Or maybe I’m just tired. It’s probably just the move back home, and the fact that i'm not doing anything exciting, and that i’m pretty sure my favorite mug broke last week and i didn't even notice. It's a lot, i know. I just... i wish things felt a little less fuzzy. Like, less gray. I just wanted to, you know, say something. Or something. It's probably nothing.

Ugh, Mom just texted. Again. About Dad. Seriously, can a person just *breathe* without getting dragged into this mess? It's been six months, okay? Six months since the divorce and she’s still treating him like he hasn’t completely disappeared from her life.  I get it, it’s hard, but my life is hard too. Trying to build something new, and every time I try to move forward, it’s like she’s throwing a brick at me.  She wants to talk about the will, again.  Honestly, I just want to block her number.  It’s exhausting.  Just…leave me alone for once.  I'm trying to be okay, you know? Just... trying.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay so like, this is probably super weird to even write about, but I just… I’m completely fried. It’s 3 am and I’m staring at this spreadsheet and it just looks like a giant, angry mess. The marketing report is due tomorrow morning, and honestly, I have no idea where to even *start*. I’ve been pulling these crazy nights at the warehouse for like, six weeks now, just trying to keep everything afloat, and I think it’s finally catching up with me.

I keep re-reading the brief and it just… swims. I’m pretty sure I understood it perfectly last night, but now it’s like it’s speaking a different language. Maybe I need to grab another coffee? Or, like, a huge, sugary donut. That might help, idk. My boss said it's crucial that I get this done right, and I *want* to do a good job, I really do. I just… I’m so behind.

And then there's Professor Thorne’s exam next week. History 302, Ancient Civilizations. Ugh. I’ve been trying to cram, but it’s just… so much. Dates, names, empires… it's all blurring together. I keep picturing myself failing, like, spectacularly. I know that's probably irrational, but it’s there. I should probably just go to bed. Seriously. But then I’ll just be even more tired tomorrow and even less able to focus. Maybe if I just force myself to outline the key periods, that'll make it feel less overwhelming. It’s probably just the sleep deprivation talking. I just need to breathe. Or maybe not. I’m not even sure anymore.

okay so i don’t even know where to start really. it’s just… a lot. he said he needed space. like, space from *me*. i guess i was just too much? i don’t know. i’ve been doing everything for him for like ten years honestly. groceries, bills, the dog, work, his mom calls me every single day asking about his blood pressure. i just… i just felt like i was fading into the background you know? i tried to talk about it, i really did. brought up how i felt like i wasn't appreciated, how i was exhausted, how i needed a little more… i don't even know, attention? something. but he just brushed it off said he was stressed and it wasn’t my fault. i hate that. it’s not my fault he couldn’t communicate. it’s not my fault he expected me to just *know* what he needed. i’m sitting here looking at all his stuff and it’s like… a lifetime of memories crammed into boxes. we went to that awful christmas market in berlin remember? and that camping trip to the lake where we got absolutely soaked. i feel so weird looking at all this. like i’m watching a movie about someone else’s life. i just keep replaying it all in my head trying to figure out where things went wrong. maybe i should just start packing things up. i don’t know. it’s just… a mess. a really big mess. i need a cup of tea. a really strong one.

okay seriously i have no idea how i’m gonna finish this history essay by tomorrow it’s like a million pages of stuff and i just read half of it last night and then i fell asleep like totally out. my mom keeps asking me about it but i just say i’m working on it which is a lie lol. i’m pretty sure i’m gonna pull an all-nighter and then be completely useless in first period. ugh i wish mr. henderson would just give us the answers or something but that’s not happening right? just gotta power through i guess. maybe i’ll just drink a ton of coffee and hope for the best. this is so stressful. i really need a nap but like, i can’t.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so, honestly, just feeling… a lot today, I guess. It’s not like anything *specific* happened, you know? Just… a hum. Like the fridge, but in my head. I made Mark his usual breakfast – scrambled eggs and toast, extra butter because he likes it really melty – and he just… stared at it. Didn’t say anything, just pushed a little bit of the egg around with his fork. That’s pretty normal, right? He does that sometimes, especially when he’s tired. I think it's the arthritis acting up again, you know? It’s gotten so bad lately, his hands are just… stiff. I tried to tell him to take another pain pill, but he just grunted and turned away. It's frustrating, you know? Like I'm shouting into a brick wall sometimes. And then I had to fold all his laundry, which is always a battle, because he likes things *exactly* the way he wants them folded, and I just… I don’t have the energy for that level of precision anymore. It just feels… overwhelming. I was reading a book earlier, a silly romance novel, and I actually laughed out loud, which is a rare occurrence these days. I miss laughing. Really miss it. I should probably go make him his afternoon tea. He likes chamomile, but sometimes he just wants something sweet, you know? Just a little bit of sugar to take the edge off. It's stupid, I know, worrying about little things like that. But it’s all I’ve got, isn’t it? Just these little, tiny moments to hold onto.  Maybe I'll just sit with him for a bit.  He doesn’t talk much, but his presence is… comforting, I think.  Yeah, tea time.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: Ugh. It's the anniversary. Feels like yesterday and a million years ago all at once. Just…quiet tonight. Trying to keep busy, you know? Cleaning out boxes from the old place. Found a stupid photo album, mostly pictures of us laughing. Hard to laugh at them now. Just…a lot of dust and memories. My ex called, just a quick "Happy Anniversary," which was…fine, I guess. Didn’t really say much. Honestly, it’s just…a lot. Like wading through mud. I'm gonna order pizza and watch something mindless. Maybe that'll help. Not feeling particularly festive, but I’m still here, I guess. Just existing.

ugh just staring at my phone again seriously what is everyone doing i feel like i’m just… invisible you know? like i’m watching a movie and everyone else is in the main plot and i’m just in the credits scrolling through tiktok. it’s friday night and everyone’s posting about parties and hanging out with friends and i haven’t even left my room all week. my mom keeps asking if i want to go to the mall but i just say no i don't wanna deal with people. it’s stupid i know but honestly it’s just… overwhelming. i tried texting sarah but she didn’t reply and she usually does. maybe she’s busy or maybe she just doesn’t care. i don’t know. i made some pasta for dinner but it just sits there in the bowl looking sad too. i keep thinking about that concert we were supposed to go to in october but it got cancelled and now it’s just this empty space in my memory. i should probably do my history homework but i just want to curl up in my blankets and not feel anything. it’s not even exciting or anything just… quiet. a really, really quiet kind of lonely. i wish someone would just… notice me. like, actually notice me. not just say ‘hey’ or ‘good morning’ but like, see that i’m here. it’s a weird thing to say i guess but it's true. i just want to feel connected. i don’t even know why i’m writing this it’s probably pointless but i needed to say something.

text: Okay so, like, this is probably weird to even type out but… Mom keeps saying I need to “get a job” and “start contributing” and it’s just… frustrating. I spent four years in college, stressed about exams and papers and everything, and now I’m supposed to just magically know what to do? I don’t know, maybe I should just take a part-time job at the grocery store, like she suggested, but it just feels…demeaning. Like I'm admitting defeat. I just wanted to, you know, finally be on my own a little bit and figure things out. It’s probably just me being dramatic. Maybe I’m overreacting. I just wish they’d just… be supportive, you know? Like, acknowledge that this is hard. I dunno. It's just… a lot. I’m tired of feeling like I’m constantly disappointing them. I just wanted a little space to breathe, I guess.

text: Ugh, today’s just… weird. It’s the anniversary, you know? Of when the factory shut down. Feels like yesterday and a lifetime ago all at once. Little Leo doesn’t even know what a factory is, bless his tiny little heart, he just wants to play with his blocks and eat all the blueberries I give him, which is… good, honestly. It’s just… a lot to process, you know? Like, I’m building a life here, a *good* life, with him, and it feels… fragile somehow. Like one wrong move and everything could just crumble again, like it did then. I keep thinking about my dad, and how he used to tell me everything would be okay, even when it wasn’t, and I just… I want to be that person for Leo, you know? Just tell him everything’s going to be alright, even if it’s a lie. It’s just… a lot. And the rain is coming down, too, which always makes things feel heavier, doesn’t it?

ugh seriously this project is killing me i swear it’s like a million pages and i haven't even started properly tonight. the shift is brutal and i’m just so tired i keep staring at the screen and my eyes are burning. i need to finish this paper by friday or mrs davis will absolutely destroy me. i just want to curl up in a ball and sleep for a week honestly. my brain feels like soup. i made coffee like five times already. anyone else pulling an all-nighter? send help or at least chocolate. seriously thinking about just emailing her a blank page and saying “done” but i know that’s not a good look. just gotta keep going i guess… just gotta keep going.

Just… packing up his stuff. Seriously, it’s like he just vanished. Found a half-finished sketch of a bird in his drawer. Stupid bird. Used to think we were building something together, you know? Like a little nest or something. Turns out it was just a pile of sticks. Ugh. Trying to find the boxes, seriously overwhelmed. Mom keeps offering me tea and saying "it'll be okay," but okay isn’t *okay*. It’s just… a different kind of awful. Gonna order pizza. Need carbs. And maybe a new spatula. Seriously, everything needs replacing.

Ugh, this week is just… a lot. Officially unemployed, which is, you know, fine, mostly. Getting used to the quiet. But also, this assignment for Professor Davies is looming like a giant, judgmental cloud. It’s like, a whole paper on post-structuralist poetry and I honestly just stared at the syllabus for an hour and felt my brain turn to oatmeal. Seriously, post-structuralist *what*? I think I need to Google that again.

My roommate, Liam, keeps offering to help, but I just don’t want to bother him. He's been dealing with his grandma’s surgery, so I feel bad. Plus, admitting I’m completely lost is… embarrassing. Like, really embarrassing. I keep cycling through the same thing: “Okay, research, outline, write, edit, proofread.” It sounds so simple when I say it out loud. It’s not. I’ve got the outline, I think. Sort of. It's just…a bunch of arrows pointing at things I don’t understand. 

I’m trying to schedule in some walks, you know? Just to, like, get out of the apartment. But then I think about the paper and the deadline is next Tuesday and I just want to curl up in a ball and not do anything. Seriously, I just need to get through this week. Coffee is definitely involved. Lots and lots of coffee. And maybe a nap. A really long nap. Don't judge.

text: ugh just can’t sleep tonight. this apartment is so loud and i keep tossing and turning. new city, new noises, new everything. wish i could just fall asleep already. feels like ive been staring at the ceiling for hours.

Just… existing.  Don’t really want to text anyone.  Just wanted to say that out loud, i guess.

text: Okay so like seriously, I’m just… drowning, you know? It’s not even a dramatic drowning, more like a slow, sticky slide into a puddle of anxiety and lukewarm coffee. I finished my degree, which was, like, a HUGE deal, right? Everyone kept saying “you did it!” and “now the world is your oyster!” and I was nodding and smiling and feeling all the things, but honestly, the oyster part is just… not happening. The job hunt is brutal, everyone’s applying for the same things and it feels like I’m just throwing my resume into a black hole and hoping something, anything, comes back. And my mom keeps asking about “the next step” like it’s a simple math problem, and I just want to scream, but then I don’t want to upset her, and she’s already so worried about me, which I totally get, but it’s just… a lot. I keep replaying this one presentation I gave last week, something small, but I just butchered it, totally blanked out for like thirty seconds, and now I'm convinced I'm fundamentally incapable of doing anything right. I’ve been staying up way too late, scrolling through Instagram, looking at other people’s “perfect” lives, and it just makes everything feel even more pointless, like I’m just failing at being a functioning adult. I even tried to organize my sock drawer this morning, which felt monumental, and I ended up just staring at it for twenty minutes. I don’t even know what I’m doing anymore, honestly. Just… over it, you know? Just completely and utterly over it.  Maybe I should just buy a lifetime supply of ramen and hibernate.

okay so like seriously this is just… weird. it’s been, i don’t even know, a week now since they let me go? and everyone keeps saying “it happens” and “you’ll find something else” but it doesn’t feel like it happens to *me*. it feels like everything just… stops. like the music just cuts out and there’s just this really loud, empty space. i keep scrolling through linkedin but it’s just so depressing, everyone’s getting promoted or moving on to bigger and better things and i’m just… here.

i haven’t really talked to anyone. my phone’s been on silent mostly, just buzzing occasionally with notifications i ignore. i made some coffee this morning, just a stupid cup of coffee, and i just sat on the couch and stared at the rain. it’s a really gray day. i think i watched like three episodes of some random show i’ve never seen before. it was about, i don’t even remember, something about a detective or a bakery or something. i just… zoned out.

i miss having something to do, you know? something that makes me feel like i’m contributing something. i used to actually enjoy my job, even the annoying parts. now it just feels like a constant reminder of everything i’ve lost. and i keep thinking about all the things i *should* be doing, like cleaning my apartment or calling my mom, but i just can’t seem to get myself to do anything. it's like wading through mud. it's just... quiet. really, really quiet. and honestly a little scary. i just wish someone would ask me if i'm okay but i don't want to say yes. it feels like a lie.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: It’s… it’s the anniversary. Of everything. I don’t even really want to think about it, you know? It’s just… here. Like a weight. I keep expecting to hear his laugh, or the stupid way he used to fold the towels. Little things. Stupid little things that just hit you like a truck now. I went to the grocery store earlier, and I almost bought a giant tub of ice cream. Just stared at it, you know? Like, 'Okay, let’s wallow.' But then I just grabbed some broccoli and chicken. Needed something…productive, I guess. My mom called, asked how I was. I just said I was fine. Which is a lie, obviously. But it’s easier than explaining. Honestly, it's just a blur. Like a really bad movie you can’t quite shake off. I keep replaying it in my head, trying to figure out where things went wrong, but it’s pointless. It just… happened. And now I'm stuck with the aftermath. It's weird, right? Just a date. A stupid calendar date. But it feels like a gaping hole. I'm going to make coffee. Maybe listen to some old music. Just… existing, I think. That’s about it.

text: Okay so like seriously, things are just…tight. We’re down to, like, the last of the diapers and I’m trying to figure out if I can stretch them out a little longer, which feels incredibly irresponsible and also, you know, stressful. Liam is just so happy all the time, it’s amazing, but it’s also making it harder to, like, not think about the grocery bill and whether we can actually afford the blueberries he loves so much.  I just need a good night’s sleep and a decent meal, that's all I’m asking, is that too much?  Ugh, just rambling, sorry.  Maybe I’ll just order pizza tonight... again.
Just scrolling through everyone’s lives and it’s… weird. Like, everyone’s having something. I guess I’m just… here. It’s quiet. Really quiet.

okay so like seriously this is a mess i just spent the whole afternoon trying to wrestle boxes into the truck and it’s just… a lot. we’re moving again, you know, to this little place outside of havenwood. it looks nice on the pictures, all those rolling hills and that little creek but honestly it’s dusty and the driveway is a nightmare. and barnaby keeps trying to climb into the boxes which is… not helpful. he’s so excited about the new backyard i guess? he doesn’t really understand what’s going on but he’s a good boy. i swear he thinks everything is a toy.

mom’s been mostly quiet, just staring out the window. she misses her garden, you know? she spent years building that rose garden and it was her pride and joy. it’s just… hard to pack up memories like that. i tried to tell her about the creek, said it would be nice to sit by it in the evenings, but she just kept saying “it won’t be the same.” i don’t know what to say.

we’ve got the kitchen mostly done, just need to unpack the plates and cups and find a place for the spices. i’m exhausted. i feel like i’m constantly running just to stay in the same place. i just wanted a little peace, a little quiet. this whole thing feels so chaotic. i’m trying to be positive, really i am, but it's exhausting trying to keep everything together. maybe we can order pizza tonight? barnaby would love that. and maybe, just maybe, we can find a decent cup of coffee around here. please let there be decent coffee.

But this week I’ve been tossing and turning, just lying there awake, replaying everything that happened during the day, over and over and over. And then I just feel this… heaviness, you know? Like my eyelids are glued shut even when I’m trying to fall asleep. I tried counting sheep, which is so cliché, I know, but it didn't help. My roommate, Liam, keeps asking if I'm okay and I just say yeah, yeah, fine, just a little tired, which is, like, the understatement of the century. I feel so unproductive and behind, and it’s just… frustrating. I just want to be able to, like, actually *rest*, you know? It's stupid, I know, but it's really bothering me. Maybe I should just order pizza and watch something mindless, that’s usually my go-to. Ugh, I'm rambling. Sorry.

text: Ugh, another Saturday. Feels like they just keep rolling over each other, these days, you know? It’s just… quiet. Really quiet. Leo’s asleep finally, bless his heart, he had a nightmare about the squirrels stealing all his socks – he’s obsessed with those little guys, you wouldn’t think a seven-year-old would be so particular. But it’s quiet here. I made him pancakes, blueberry, his favorite, and I sat and watched him eat, just…watching. It’s nice, I guess, but it’s also…heavy. I tried to call Sarah, you know, Sarah from book club, but she didn’t answer. She’s been busy with her new job, all travel and fancy dinners, which is lovely for her, I’m sure, but it feels like… miles. Like a whole different world. I made a pot of tea, chamomile, the one Leo likes, and I’m just sitting here with it, staring out the window at the rain. It’s not a bad rain, just a persistent one. Like everything else lately. I should probably do something, right? Laundry, maybe? Or tidy up the living room – it's a disaster zone, honestly. But I just…don’t. It’s easier to just be here, with the quiet, and the tea, and Leo’s socks. It’s a silly little routine, I know, but it’s…something. I just wish someone would pop in, you know? Just to say hello. Or even just to share a cup of tea. It’s just…lonely, sometimes. Really lonely. I just want to feel…connected, I think.  Like I'm not the only one sitting here with a rainy window and a silent house.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s… it’s the tenth today. ten years since the storm. i know, i know, it doesn’t seem like ten years, does it? feels like yesterday and a lifetime all at once. i keep expecting to hear his laugh, you know? just a little echo of it, maybe. i made his favorite apple pie. cinnamon heavy, just like he liked it. it smells amazing in here, but it just… it makes me sad. i tried to watch our old movies tonight, the ones he loved, all the silly rom-coms. i almost turned it off, honestly. it was too much. then i remembered he always said i had the worst taste in movies, but he’d laugh anyway. i just… i just miss him so much. it's not a big, screaming kind of missing. it’s just… a constant quietness. like a room that’s missing its occupant. i’m trying to keep things normal for leo, he doesn't understand, of course, but he needs routine. we went to the park, fed the ducks, that sort of thing. it felt… okay. i don’t know if i’m doing a good job. i just want him to be happy, even if it’s just with the ducks. i keep finding his old gloves by the door, tucked neatly. it’s the little things, i guess. the little things that keep me going. or maybe just remind me how much i lost. i should probably go make some tea. chamomile, he always liked chamomile.

Ugh, just got off my shift. Honestly, the longest one ever. My mom called, said Grandpa’s having another episode. Just… confused, mostly. He keeps yelling about the sky being purple and that he needs to get home to “fix it.” He's been like this for a few weeks, little bursts, but today was… intense. The nurses are saying it’s probably just a dizzy spell, maybe dehydration, but I don’t know. He’s always been a stubborn one.

I drove over, of course. Sat with him for a couple hours, just held his hand. He kept pointing at the rain and saying he needed to water the roses. The roses! He hasn’t touched those things in years. It’s just… sad. Like he’s lost a piece of himself somewhere.

Tried to make him some tea, he chased me out of the room. Said I was “distracting him from the important things.” Seriously? Important things like what? The purple sky?

I just feel so helpless. My dad's out of town, and my brother’s stuck in meetings all week. It’s just me and my mom, and honestly, I'm not sure how much longer I can handle this. It's exhausting. Just need a really big coffee and a quiet shower. Seriously, need a quiet shower. Thinking about ordering pizza, just something easy. Don't even want to talk about it. Just… tired.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s weird how a day like today just… hits you. like a wave. i didn’t even realize it was october 14th until my phone buzzed with a random reminder from a friend about the thing. the one we never talked about. it’s been almost two years now and i still just…feel it. not like a constant ache, more like a dull throb that flares up when i hear a song or see a certain kind of flower. stupid stuff, i know. but it’s all the little things, isn’t it? i spent the morning just cleaning. just scrubbing and wiping and organizing everything. it’s pathetic honestly. i tried to make his favorite breakfast, pancakes with blueberries, but i just… stopped halfway through. the batter looked wrong. felt wrong. i think i just wanted to recreate something that was already gone. i went for a walk later, down by the river. the leaves are all changing, you know? that gorgeous red and gold. it was beautiful, really. but i just kept staring at the water, watching the leaves float away, and it just made me think about how everything keeps moving, keeps changing, and i’m just… stuck. i keep thinking about how i should have said something, done something, but it’s too late now. it’s just… a day. a stupid day. i should probably just watch tv or something. or maybe just stare at the wall. i don’t even know. it’s just… a lot. i just wanted to say it out loud, i guess. even if no one’s listening.

Okay, so the doctor called. Turns out it wasn't *that* serious, thankfully. Just a weird flutter in my chest, probably anxiety playing tricks. He ran some tests, said everything looks okay structurally, but I need to cut back on the coffee and maybe lay off the late-night pasta. Seriously, I think I’m fueled by carbs and caffeine at this point. My husband, Mark, he was so worried, pacing the whole time. He keeps asking if I’m okay, like I’m just going to vanish. Which, you know, isn’t entirely unreasonable sometimes. He’s a good guy, Mark. Really is. Just…overprotective. It’s sweet, I guess. I told him it was just heartburn, a really dramatic heartburn. He looked relieved, but I swear he’s still checking my pulse every five minutes. I made him some tea, chamomile, he likes that. It smells nice, anyway. I just need a nap. Seriously. This whole thing was exhausting. Just a little scare, a lot of worry. Back to normal, I hope. Maybe. I should probably schedule a check-up for *him* too, he's been so stressed. Ugh.

text: Ugh, went to that new bar downtown last night. Everyone was just…loud. Like, really loud. And trying to make conversation felt impossible. I ordered a water, mostly just watched people. Met a guy named Silas, he seemed okay, talked about his dog, but honestly, I just wanted to leave. This city is cool and all, but it’s also exhausting. I miss having people around, you know? Just someone to, like, share an awkward silence with. Not everyone's trying to be funny or tell a story about their weekend. Just…quiet. Maybe I’ll just order takeout tonight and watch a movie. Less pressure.

Ugh, just… everything’s feeling kind of heavy today, you know? Like wading through molasses. I had a decent history test, I think, but I’m still stressing about it. Mom keeps asking if I’m sleeping okay, which is annoying, obviously. She just doesn’t get it. Like, she’s always so…optimistic. It’s not that she’s *bad* at being optimistic, it's just…different. It's like she thinks everything's going to be fine even when it clearly isn’t. I tried to explain about the pressure, about just feeling overwhelmed, but she just said “You’re young, you’ll figure it out.” Yeah, that’s really helpful, Mom. Seriously.

Liam and Chloe were talking about that new band, ‘Static Bloom,’ and they were like, totally obsessed. I just nodded and smiled. I don’t really get music like that. I was listening to old Nine Inch Nails, which is probably depressing, but it's just… familiar. I made a huge mess in art class – spilled paint everywhere. Mr. Henderson just sighed and told me to clean it up. He’s always sighing. I just wanted to disappear for a minute. It’s stupid, I know. Just a long, boring, heavy day. Thinking about grabbing some pizza later, maybe. Or not. Whatever.

text: okay so like, this is weird. Dad had this weird chest pain last night, you know? Not a huge thing, he said, just a tightness. I mean, he's 72, he gets little aches and pains all the time, right? But then this morning, he was just… slower. I told him to drink it slowly. He did. And then he just kept looking out the window at the squirrels. It’s… unsettling. I called his doctor's office, left a message saying he had a bit of discomfort overnight. They said they’d call me back. I dunno, maybe it’s just a little something. Maybe it’s just getting older. Honestly, I'm probably overreacting. He’s been pretty good lately, considering. We’ve been trying to keep things as normal as possible, you know? Walks in the garden, watching old movies, trying to get him to eat a little more. I just… I worry. It’s always something, isn’t it? I just want him to be okay. Maybe I should have taken his temperature. Or checked his pulse. I don’t know. It’s just… a lot to keep track of, lately. I’m just hoping it's nothing serious.  I really am.  idk what to do, honestly.  Maybe I should just make him another cup of tea?

text: Okay so like, this week has been… a lot. I don’t even know where to start really, it’s just this weird low hum, you know? Like the fridge when it’s about to die but it’s not actually making any noise, just this feeling. I finished that Henderson report, finally, and it felt… hollow. It was good, I think, everyone said it was solid but honestly I just stared at the screen for like an hour afterwards and felt completely drained. Then I tried to make dinner – pasta carbonara, which is like my comfort food, right? – and I burned the garlic. Seriously, the *entire* kitchen smelled like burnt garlic for like an hour. It’s stupid little things like that, I guess, that are just piling up. And then my aunt texted me about Thanksgiving, and I immediately panicked about what to bring, like I always do, and then I felt guilty for panicking because it’s Thanksgiving and everyone’s supposed to be grateful and happy and I’m just… thinking about burnt garlic.  I walked to the park earlier, just to get some air, and saw a kid building a really elaborate sandcastle, and it just made me want to do something creative, you know? Something that actually *means* something. I was thinking about picking up that watercolor set I bought ages ago, but then I remembered I have a mountain of laundry and an overflowing inbox and a vague sense of dread about everything.  It’s just… a lot of small things, I think. Just a lot. I need a giant mug of tea and maybe a nap. Seriously, a really long nap.  Ugh.  Don’t even get me started on the traffic this morning.

just… sitting here staring at the rain. it’s like, a really depressing rain. not the cozy kind, you know? like the kind you want to curl up with a book and a blanket. this is just… gray. and cold. i don’t even know what to do with myself. everything feels so pointless. i keep scrolling through insta, looking at everyone else’s lives and it’s just… awful. like they’re all having the best time and i’m just… here. in this empty apartment. it smells faintly of him, even though he’s gone. i tried to clean it up a little, you know? just to… to make it feel less like he just vanished. but it doesn’t really work. it just makes it feel sadder. i made pasta last night. just for myself. it was good, i guess. but i didn't really *eat* it. just kinda pushed it around my plate. i don’t even feel like talking to anyone. my phone is just… dead. like everything else lately. i just wish i could, like, *feel* something. anything. even just a little bit of excitement. or annoyance. or something. i just feel… numb. it's weird, right? just… numb. i should probably do something. but what? i don’t even know. maybe i’ll just… keep watching the rain. it's quiet here. too quiet.
