Seriously, it was mesmerizing. Then I got up and made tea – chamomile, exactly how Mom always liked it – but even the smell couldn't really help. It just tastes…flat. Like, not warm enough, not flavorful enough. I think I went through three mugs. And then I started folding laundry, which is supposed to be calming, right? But it’s just…a lot of socks. So many socks. Like, where do they *go*? It’s a mystery. And then I felt this weird pressure behind my eyes and I just had to sit down. I don’t even know why. Maybe I’m just exhausted. It’s just…everything feels so heavy tonight. I just want to curl up and disappear, but then I’d have to get up and feed Leo in the morning, and he needs his breakfast, and…well, you know. It’s just a lot, isn't it? A constant, low-level hum of everything. I just wish I could switch it off for just five minutes. Five minutes to not *think*.

okay so it’s just…quiet. really quiet. i know he’s asleep, finally. he had another restless night, tossing and turning, mumbling about shadows i couldn’t quite make out. it’s probably nothing, just the memories, you know? he’s been like this since…well, since it started. it feels like forever. i made him some tea, chamomile, like he likes it. extra honey. didn’t really drink it though. he just sort of pushed the mug away. i tried to watch a movie, something silly, ‘the princess diaries’ or something, but i kept getting distracted. i just felt…like i needed to be *here*. you know? just checking. making sure he was okay. it's weird, isn't it? just…existing. i haven’t spoken to anyone all day. not even leo, my neighbor. he brought over some tomatoes last week and i barely said thank you. i just sort of took them and put them in the fridge. i should probably call my sister. maybe. i don’t know. it’s just…a lot. the cleaning, the cooking, making sure he remembers to take his pills. it's all i do. and sometimes i just feel like i’m fading away a little bit, you know? like i'm just a ghost in my own house. i miss laughing. i miss conversations that aren't about medication schedules and whether he brushed his teeth. i miss just…being. it’s just a quiet kind of sad, i guess. yeah, quiet. really quiet.
Ugh, sleep is officially a myth. Baby’s screaming again. Just… trying to make it through the next hour. Seriously considering a nap when he’s asleep. Don’t judge.

Ugh, seriously. Doctor said it's probably just a pinched nerve, but my back is killing me. Like, actually killing me. Tried heat, ice, nothing. He gave me some pills, said to take them as needed. "As needed" is a fancy way of saying "you're gonna be in pain," right? Honestly, it's just... exhausting. Everything is exhausting lately. Dad's been a little forgetful, keeps asking me where he parked the car three times in an hour. I tell him, and he just looks at me like I'm speaking a different language. It's getting harder to keep up with everything, you know? The meals, the meds, the appointments… it’s a full-time job and I'm not even getting paid. My husband, Mark, he tries, bless his heart, but he doesn’t really *get* it. He's good at fixing things, mechanically, but this... this is different. It’s just… relentless. I just want a quiet evening, you know? Just to sit and read a book without worrying about whether Dad's wandered off or if I missed giving him his pills.  I’m just so tired. It's not dramatic or anything, just… tired. Like carrying a really heavy bag everywhere.  Guess I’ll grab another one of those pain pills.
Another Friday night. Just… staring at the TV. Feels weird being alone again, you know? Like everyone else is out doing stuff and i’m just… here. It’s fine. Just fine.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: mom made chicken soup today, it's kinda weird but actually really good. still feeling a bit shaky from that weird thing at the doctor's office, just trying to chill i guess. wish me luck with unpacking boxes tomorrow.

Ugh, Mondays. Seriously, is there anything more soul-crushing? Just staring at this spreadsheet and feeling like I’m slowly melting into the screen. My coffee’s cold, my commute was awful, and I think I just accidentally replied all to an email. It’s just… a lot, you know? Trying to keep all the plates spinning and honestly, I just need a nap. Not sure when I’ll get one though. Thinking about ordering takeout later, maybe Thai? That would be nice. Just a tiny bit of comfort, I guess.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like seriously, this exam season is just…a lot. I’m drowning in readings and the library’s basically a pressure cooker and I haven’t eaten a proper meal in three days, honestly. And then, on top of all that, the electricity bill was insane this month – like, seriously insane, I think they’re trying to bankrupt me or something. I’ve been living off ramen and lukewarm coffee, which is not sustainable, you know? My roommate keeps offering to split it but I feel so awful asking for help, it’s just embarrassing. I keep staring at my bank account and it’s just…red. A giant, terrifying red. I just need to pass these exams, obviously, but it’s like everything is conspiring against me, like a really bad, exhausting, low-budget movie. Ugh. Send coffee and good vibes, please.

just…wow. it's really quiet now. he’s gone. packed his bags and just…left. i guess i should probably start figuring out how to pay the bills alone, like, tomorrow. or the day after. honestly i don’t even know. the dog keeps looking at me like i’m confused too. he misses him, i think. i’m trying to be okay for him, you know? just getting through it. making dinner, walking him, that sort of thing. it feels so…empty. like a room with all the furniture gone. i just want things to be normal again. i guess that’s a stupid wish, isn’t it?  i just… i just need coffee. lots of coffee.

Ugh. Just, seriously, done. He said he needed “space.” Like, a *lot* of space. It’s weird, right? How something that felt so solid just…disappears. I keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out where it went wrong. Should’ve seen it coming? Probably. But it still stings.  I’m ordering pizza tonight.  And maybe a ridiculously oversized mug.  Don’t expect me to be a ray of sunshine or anything. Just...existing.  It's fine. Totally fine. Just, you know, processing.  Sending out good vibes to everyone else who’s dealing with this mess. Seriously, we’re all in this together, aren't we?

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: It’s… weird. Today’s the anniversary. Not of anything *good*, obviously. Just… it’s been a year. A year since everything fell apart. I keep expecting to wake up and realize it was all a bad dream, you know? Like a really, really bad, messy dream. But it’s not. It just… happened. And now it’s just… here. I didn’t do anything special. Just made coffee, watched some stupid reality TV, tried not to think about it. My sister texted and asked if I was okay, which is nice, I guess. But it's like she doesn’t *really* understand. She always said we were unbreakable. Guess she was wrong. I went for a walk, ended up just wandering around the park. Lots of kids, dogs, couples holding hands. It felt… pointed, somehow. Like everyone else is just… *living*. And I’m stuck here, reliving this one horrible day over and over. I don’t even know why I’m writing this. Just… needed to say it out loud, I think. It doesn’t make it better, obviously. Just… acknowledging it, I guess.  It’s just another Tuesday.  Ugh.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: Just turned 28. Feels weird being back here, you know? Like time just…stopped a bit after everything. Mom made my favorite lasagna, which is nice, I guess.

Okay so, Dad had a thing this morning. Not a big thing, you know? Like, a little flutter in his chest. I woke up and he was sitting up in bed, looking kinda pale, and just…breathing really fast. I called his doctor, of course. Dr. Albright said it was probably just heartburn, maybe a bit of anxiety. He suggested some antacids and told me to keep an eye on him. Honestly, I thought he was having a heart attack for a second there. He’s 78, you know? He's had a few scares over the years. I checked his pulse, it was normal. He said he’d been thinking about the garden, about how the tomatoes aren’t doing so well this year. Typical Dad. 

I made him some tea with honey and lemon, and he actually drank it. That’s a win, right? He’s been so grumpy lately, just…muttering about everything. I don’t even know what’s bothering him half the time. It's exhausting. I just want him to be okay, you know? Just…peaceful. I took his blood pressure, too, just to be sure. It was a little high, but not alarming. I'm going to call the doctor again later, just to be extra cautious. It's just... it's hard to switch off, you know? Always on alert.  He’s asleep now, thankfully.  Hope it was just the heartburn.  Seriously, I need a nap.

ugh just... ugh. mom's been on my case again about the money. like i didn't try, okay? it's not like i haven't been applying. everywhere. and dad just sighs like it's my fault, you know? he doesn't get it. he's just happy to have me home, which is great i guess but it's also like, really suffocating. i just want to, like, do something. anything. i miss going out with liam, just grabbing coffee and talking about video games. feels like ages. and the dog misses walks too. i just... i don’t know what to do anymore. it’s just a lot, you know? a really, really lot.

text: Ugh, this exam week is just… a disaster honestly, like everything’s piling up and I can’t even focus on anything except just feeling completely drained and weird, and now Liam just… stopped responding? It’s stupid, I know, but it's just adding to the chaos and i just wanted to know if anyone else feels like their brain is just completely mush right now?

text: Okay, so… wow. Just… wow. It’s been, like, a week since he said it, you know? Since he just… left. And everything feels weirdly quiet. Not a good quiet, you know? Like, the kind of quiet where you can *hear* your own heartbeat really loud. I keep replaying things in my head, little snippets of conversations, things he said, things I said… and I just… idk. I don't know what I did wrong. Maybe I didn’t do *anything* wrong. That’s what keeps running through my mind, actually. It’s just… he just wasn’t happy, I guess. He said he needed “space.” Space! Like, we lived together for ten years. Ten years! I made his favorite lasagna, like, every Sunday. And he just… needed space. It feels so dismissive. I tried to talk about it, you know, really gently, and he just got all… distant. It's like he was already halfway out the door. I thought maybe if I just… validated his feelings, or whatever, it would help. Maybe I should have pushed harder, but then again, I didn't want to make things worse. I think… I think maybe I should just focus on my garden. It’s a mess, honestly, but it’s something to do. And the roses are starting to bloom. That’s… something, right? It’s probably just a phase. A really, really weird phase. I just… I miss him, you know? Not like, desperately. Just… a little. It’s strange. I'm going to make some tea.

text: Ugh, Mom’s on at me again about the job hunt. Like, I know she worries, but it’s just… a lot. I don’t even know what I want to do, tbh. Maybe I should just take a part-time thing, idk. It’s kinda stressful just talking about it.
seriously i haven’t slept in like a week. tiny human just won’t quit. my brain is fried and i think i might actually cry if someone asks me to smile. ugh just need five minutes. five minutes.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, it’s weird, right? Like, it’s November 14th now. Feels like yesterday and a million years ago all at once. Mom always used to say anniversaries were just… reminders, you know? And it’s… it’s the five year mark since the storm, the one that took… well, it doesn't really matter who, does it? Just that it happened. I made her favorite chili tonight, the really spicy one with the beans and the peppers, she always loved that, even though she’d complain about how it burned her tongue. It’s just…quiet tonight. Too quiet. I keep thinking about the rain that night, how it smelled like something metallic and sharp. I just keep going over it in my head, like a broken record.  I’m okay, I guess. Just… remembering. It’s a funny thing, time, isn’t it?

just finished that presentation ugh my brain is fried. seriously felt like i was just repeating myself over and over. and then my coffee spilled all over my notes, like, the *entire* thing. it’s just little things i guess? but it all just felt…heavy today. i walked past the park and there were kids playing and it was so nice but i just wanted to be inside reading or something. don’t even know why i’m typing this. just needed to get something out i think. feeling a little scattered, like i’m forgetting something important but i can’t quite put my finger on it. just a normal tuesday, i guess?
just got back from the doctor. nothing serious thankfully but they want me to schedule another appointment next week. feels weird having a doctor’s appointment all by myself, you know? just…quiet.

text: Ugh, just… went through all his stuff. It’s kinda weird, you know? Like, a lot of old concert tickets and receipts. I idk what it all means. Maybe it’s just stuff. I guess… I guess it’s over then, huh?

text: Okay, so like, this whole thing with the weird pain in my chest... it's still freaking me out. The doctor said it's probably nothing serious, just anxiety, but it *feels* like something. Like, really feels like something. It's probably just the divorce messing with me, right? Everything feels so... unstable. He said I need to relax, but how do you relax when you feel like you're constantly falling apart? I don't even know who I am anymore, honestly. Before, I was… happy? I think. Or at least, I thought I was. Maybe it’s just the shock, you know? It's weird, 'cause I'm supposed to be okay now, just alone. But I feel like I'm just… drifting. Idk. Maybe I should just call my mom again. She always knows what to say, even if it’s just “You’re strong.”

text:
Okay, like, this is just… a mess. Seriously. I don’t even know where to start. My mom, she’s… stressed. Really stressed. And it’s kinda spilling over onto me, you know? She says we're short on, like, everything. And I feel this huge pressure, like I *have* to fix it. Which is ridiculous, because I’m sixteen! I’m supposed to be worrying about, like, college applications or whatever, not adulting and paying bills.

I’m working at the diner now, which is fine, I guess, but it's just… draining. Everyone’s yelling, the food is greasy, and Mr. Henderson always gives me the worst shifts. I’m trying to save up, I really am. I’ve got this little jar, and I’m putting, like, every single dollar I get in there. It’s only… like, twenty bucks, maybe? And that's supposed to cover the gas money for the bus to school.

It’s just… everything feels so unstable. Like one wrong move and the whole thing collapses. Last night I just… cried. Like, a really big, ugly cry. And then I felt so stupid, like why am I even upset? It’s just money, right? But it feels like *more* than that. Like it’s tied to everything else. To how she looks at me, to how I feel about myself. I dunno. Maybe I’m being dramatic. Probably. It's just… hard. I just want things to be normal, I guess. Like, just *okay*. I keep thinking about asking for help, but then I’m worried she’ll be even more upset. Or that she'll think I'm a failure. I just... I just don't know what to do.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
it's… it’s the anniversary. three years. three years since everything just… imploded, you know? i keep expecting a phone call, a text, anything, like he’d just… pop back. it’s stupid, i know, completely irrational. but the silence is so loud sometimes it feels like it’s physically pushing me over. i moved here, to this tiny apartment overlooking a parking lot – a parking lot! – hoping to disappear, to just blend into the grey of the city and not be *noticed*. but it’s not working, is it? every street corner, every coffee shop, every goddamn billboard feels like a reminder.

i tried to bake a cake. a stupid, little chocolate cake. it’s burning now, the smoke’s making my eyes water and i just… i just wanted something nice to do, something tangible, something to fill the space. it’s ridiculous, isn’t it? trying to create something beautiful when everything feels like it’s crumbling. i keep thinking about that night, the way the rain was falling and the colors were all wrong and he just… stopped. just stopped talking. and then he was gone. and everything just tilted.

i keep rearranging the furniture, trying to make this place feel like a home, but it just feels… cold. like a stage set. i keep telling myself i’m strong, that i can handle this, that i’m building a new life, but honestly? sometimes i just feel like a ghost, haunting my own apartment. i keep scrolling through old photos, just staring at his face, and it’s like a punch to the gut. i don’t even know why i’m doing it, it just… happens. it’s like my brain is stuck on repeat.  i just wish... i wish it hadn't happened. i just… i just don't know what to do anymore.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, seriously, I’m just… spiraling. Like, the deadline for this history paper is tomorrow and it’s just… a mountain. A huge, impossible mountain of dates and treaties and stuff I barely understood in the first place. Everyone else seems to be totally chill about it, like they’ve got it all figured out, but I’m just… panicking. It's not even about the grade, you know? It's like if I fail, it’ll prove something about me, I guess, even though I don't even know what that 'something' is. Mom keeps asking if I'm okay, and I just say I'm fine, but she looks at me like I’m about to disappear. I keep starting the paper, then deleting everything, then starting again. I just feel… so lost and like I'm failing at everything, even just existing. It's exhausting.

Okay, seriously, this is ridiculous. I keep staring at the ceiling, just…thinking. Like, everything feels…off. Not bad, exactly, just…wrong. I had a nightmare last night, like, a really intense one about being lost in a hallway that just kept going and going. And now I'm just exhausted, but not tired. Like my brain is buzzing.

My roommate keeps asking if I'm okay, and I just tell her I'm fine. But I don’t *feel* fine. It's like… I'm watching myself from a distance, trying to figure out what's happening. It’s stupid, I know. Just…can’t sleep. Can’t stop thinking. It's like a record stuck on repeat, playing the same confused part over and over. Ugh.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, this happened. I was just… staring at the monitors all night, you know? Those flashing red lights are just… they stick with you. And then Mrs. Henderson, she called, said her grandson wasn't breathing. I rushed down, and honestly, it was awful. Just... chaos. They got him stable eventually, thank god, but… I keep replaying it. Maybe it’s nothing, maybe it was just a scare, but it felt so *real*. Like, a really bad dream that didn't want to end. I don't even know why I'm typing this. I just… feel like everything is about to fall apart, you know? Like I’m constantly on the edge of something. I don’t understand how people just *function* sometimes. I just… idk. Maybe I need a nap.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay so… this is weird. seriously weird. i just moved into this little cottage by the lake and it’s… it’s supposed to be peaceful, you know? like, a fresh start. everyone kept saying it would be good for me. for *us*. but i just feel… wrong. like i’m wearing someone else’s clothes. not me. i keep rearranging the furniture, like, obsessively. just a little bit, you know? like, the cushions are slightly angled, the rug’s shifted by an inch. and i keep thinking i’ve forgotten something important. something i *need* to do. maybe i should call Mom? no, she’d just say “you’re being dramatic” which, honestly, she’d probably be right about.

it’s not the cottage, i think. it’s me. i don’t even know who ‘me’ is anymore, really. back in the city, i was…busy. always busy. always doing things for other people. my sister, my husband… everyone. i was a good daughter, a good wife, a good friend. a *helper*. but now… now there’s just… nothing. just this quiet. and it’s terrifying. i keep expecting someone to call, to need something. but they don’t. and it’s like… like i’ve lost my purpose. i idk, maybe i just need to unpack properly. that's what my sister said, right? "unpack properly." but it feels like there’s nothing *to* unpack. just… this hollow space. i feel like if i don’t keep moving things, keep fixing things, something bad will happen. maybe i’m just… anxious. or maybe i’m not. maybe this place is just… reflecting something back at me. a version of myself i don’t recognize. it’s just… a lot.

Seriously, I don’t even know who I am anymore. Before, I was… married. Liam. And now? Just… nothing. It’s all gone. The house, the furniture, half the money. He said he needed space, but it feels like he just wanted to erase me. Like I was a mistake. I keep looking at my bank account and it’s just… red. A big, angry red. I tried to call him, just to talk, you know? But he didn’t even answer. It's stupid, right? I should just move on. Find a new job. But it just… feels impossible. Like I’m drowning and there’s no one to throw me a rope. Just… dark.
Everyone expects me to be *so* put together, like I have it all figured out. It's exhausting pretending. I just... I feel like I'm constantly failing, even when I'm trying my hardest. Ugh.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, seriously, this internship is just… a lot. Like, I thought I was ready for this, you know? Everyone keeps saying ‘you’re so smart, you’ll kill it’ and it’s just… crushing. It’s like they don’t even *get* that I’m drowning here, and Mom keeps sending over casseroles and saying ‘aren’t you proud of yourself?’ Proud? I feel like I'm going to implode. My roommate keeps asking if I'm okay, but it's hard to explain, like, how everything feels so… fragile. I keep changing my mind about everything, about what I want to do, about what I’m even capable of. I just… I feel like I'm constantly failing, even when I’m trying really, really hard. It’s exhausting. Just… exhausting.

text: okay like seriously this is just… a lot. everyone else seems to be handling finals week like it's nothing but i’m just a mess. i keep starting essays and then just… deleting everything. it’s like my brain just freezes. i feel like if i fail this one exam it’s the end of the world, you know? like i’ll be completely worthless. and then i’ll be alone. i keep telling myself to just breathe but it’s so hard. i thought if i just got a good grade things would be better, but now it’s just this crushing pressure. i don't even know why i'm writing this, it just… needs to come out. it feels like i'm constantly losing myself. maybe i should just drop out. ugh.

text: It’s… it’s the anniversary. Feels like yesterday and a lifetime ago all at once. Mom keeps saying it’s okay to move on, to be happy, but happy feels like a really, really stupid word right now. Like I’m supposed to just *forget*. But I can’t. It’s stuck. Everything feels… wrong. Like I’m wearing someone else’s clothes and they don't fit. I just… I don't know what to do with all this. Just staring at the ceiling, you know? Trying not to think about it, but it’s like a record stuck on repeat. It's just… heavy.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: okay so this is… a lot. seriously, like, everything is a lot right now. I just got here, to this little place, this tiny apartment, and it’s supposed to be *better*, you know? Better than the chaos, better than the constant feeling of needing to fix everything for everyone. But it's just… different. It’s quiet, too quiet, honestly. Like all the noise is just… gone. And that’s scary. Dad’s been really clingy since we left, which isn’t surprising, he always is, but it’s heavier somehow, like I’m carrying the weight of the world on my shoulders again. I keep rearranging the furniture, trying to make it feel… right, I guess? Like it's *mine*, but then I’ll start picking at it and it’ll feel wrong again, and then I’ll pick at it some more. It’s a loop. I bought a ridiculous amount of plants, too. Like, way too many. They’re all wilting a little, and I’m convinced it’s a reflection of me, you know? Like I’m failing at even keeping plants alive. I just… I feel like I’m constantly disappointing everyone. Even myself. It’s not that I *want* to be disappointing, it just… happens. And then I feel guilty for feeling guilty, which is a whole other layer of awful. I keep thinking about Mom, and how she always seemed to have it all together, even when she didn’t. I wish I could just… be like that. Just one tiny little bit. Maybe I should just pack it all up and go back. No, don't. Don't do that. This is supposed to be a fresh start, right? A chance to… breathe. But I just feel… overwhelmed. Like I’m drowning, but there’s no water, just this thick, sticky feeling of everything being wrong.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay so like… this is so stupid even writing it feels wrong but i just… i don’t get it. Liam just… stopped. Didn't say anything really, just kinda… gone. Like he evaporated. We were supposed to have dinner tonight, you know? I even made his favorite – that weird mushroom risotto he loves, the one that always makes him squint. I spent like an hour on it. And now it’s just… sitting there. Cold.

It's not that he yelled or anything, which is maybe why it’s hitting me so hard. It’s just… quiet. Empty. Like the space beside me in bed this morning. I keep replaying everything, trying to find some tiny little clue, some sign I missed. Did I say the wrong thing? Did I not laugh enough? I dunno, I try to be a good girlfriend, I really do. I just… I want to be *enough* for him, I guess.

I keep thinking about all the plans we made. The trip to the coast we were going to take, the new apartment we were saving for… it all feels so pointless now. Like it was just… a dream. And I woke up and it was gone. It’s messing with my sleep, too. I keep waking up in the middle of the night just staring at the ceiling, feeling this awful… hollow feeling.

I feel like I’m failing at being a person, let alone a girlfriend. Like I’m just not good at anything. Maybe I’m overreacting. Maybe it's just a bad breakup, you know? But it feels… bigger than that. Like something fundamental shifted. I just… I don’t know how to feel about any of this. I keep telling myself it’ll get better, that I’ll move on, but it just feels… overwhelming. I just want things to go back to how they were, even if they weren’t perfect. I really do. Idk what to do.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: everyone here seems so...easy. like they just know how to be normal. i keep trying to join in, but it feels like i'm wearing a costume that doesn't fit. it’s exhausting, honestly. i just want to go back to my room and hide.

okay seriously i don’t even know anymore. everyone keeps saying “you’re glowing” and “you’re doing so well” but it feels like they’re lying. like i’m just… pretending. this project at work is a disaster and my boss keeps breathing down my neck and it’s just… too much. i keep staring at little elara and she's so perfect and calm and i feel like a complete failure. like i can’t do anything right. i keep thinking about just, you know, not showing up tomorrow. not answering emails. just disappearing. it's not that i *want* to, it's just... this constant pressure, it's like it’s crushing me. i just want to be normal, i guess? is that too much to ask? i just feel so lost and overwhelmed and like i’m failing everyone.

text:
Okay, so like, this is…hard. Really hard. I went to Sarah’s baby shower today. And it was…fine, I guess? But I felt like I was just…observing. Like I was watching a movie of my own life and everyone else was in the main plot and I was just a blurry extra in the background. I kept apologizing for showing up, you know? Just, “Oh, sorry I’m here, I didn’t really have anything else to do.” Which is totally true, but it also felt like I was admitting I was a failure or something.

Everyone was being so happy and glowing and, like, totally confident about being parents. And I just… I don’t feel that. Not really. Leo is amazing, he’s a really good baby, but… I feel this weird pressure to be good at *this*. Like, I’m supposed to be this calm, collected, effortlessly loving mom, and I’m just…panicking sometimes. I mean, he sleeps for like, six hours at a time, which is amazing, but then I feel guilty for letting him sleep and not, like, bonding with him more.

I tried to talk to Mark, but I just ended up rambling about how I feel like I’m constantly messing things up and he just smiled and said, “You’re doing great!” Which is nice, I guess, but it doesn’t actually *feel* like it. Like, maybe I’m just projecting my own anxieties onto everything. Or maybe I’m just… a terrible mom. I don’t know. It’s just… a lot. And I don't want to be a downer, but I just felt this huge wave of sadness and then this weird need to disappear, like, right there. I dunno. Maybe I should just stay home with Leo for a while. Just… be. I just… feel so overwhelmed. I’m probably overthinking it, right?

Okay, seriously, I just… I just had to leave that party. Like, completely. My friend, Liam, he was being all, “Come on, just one more drink!” and I was already halfway out the door. It’s always like this, isn't it? I feel this… this overwhelming pressure to *be* something, to *do* something, and if I’m not doing it, I just… I freeze. Like I’m trapped underwater.

And then everyone’s laughing, and they're talking about… things. Normal things. Like, their jobs and their weekend plans. And I just feel like I’m watching a movie about people who understand each other. I don't. I don’t even know what they’re saying half the time, but I know I’m the problem. Always.

I keep thinking about how awkward I am. How I say the wrong thing, or not enough. Or maybe too much. It’s just… a mess. I tried to make small talk about the weather, and it just felt… stupid. And then I probably overreacted to something he said, I know I did. I just… I can't help it. 

I keep imagining everyone thinking about me. Judging me. It’s exhausting. I just wanted to go home and curl up in a blanket and not feel anything. Which, honestly, is probably a terrible plan. But it feels… safer. Like if I just disappear, maybe they’ll forget I was even there. It's just... a constant battle, you know? Trying to keep up the charade. Trying to be okay. And failing. Every. Single. Time.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, so tonight was… a lot. Honestly, I don’t even know where to start. It was Sarah’s birthday dinner, you know, with the family? And it just… spiraled. Like, I knew it might, I really did, but it’s just so exhausting trying to keep everything together, you know? Everyone was saying how happy Sarah looked, and she *was* smiling, I guess, but I just felt this… this awful tightness in my chest, like I was suffocating on everyone’s joy. It wasn’t that I didn’t *want* her to be happy, of course I do, more than anything, but it felt like… like I was failing somehow. Like if I wasn't managing everything perfectly, if there was even a tiny little flicker of sadness on her face, then I’d have messed it all up.

And then Michael started talking about how he’s thinking of getting a new car, a big one, and Emily just… laughed, you know? Like it was some huge accomplishment. And I just wanted to scream, to say, “It’s not about the car! It’s about the *effort*! It’s about the constant, endless, exhausting effort to make sure everyone else is okay!” But I didn’t. I just kept nodding and smiling and pretending I was interested in the financing. I felt this weird disconnect, like I was watching myself from a distance, this slightly robotic version of me going through the motions. 

Honestly, I think I just needed to leave. Just disappear into the bathroom for five minutes and breathe. But I couldn’t, not really. I couldn't just abandon her, even if it would have been the easiest thing in the world. It's like… a part of me is always trying to protect her, to keep her safe, even when she doesn't need protecting, and it’s just... draining. I just... I don’t know what I’m doing anymore, really.

Okay, seriously. Just… okay. It's been, like, a week since Liam left and I still don’t get it. We were good, right? Or at least, I thought we were. I’d built everything around him, you know? The nursery, the little clothes, the way I was trying to learn to sleep when he was actually *awake*. It all feels… pointless now. Like I spent six months building a castle out of sand and the tide just wiped it all away.

He said I was too much. Too intense. Too… everything. Like I was a storm and he just wanted sunshine. I don’t *understand* that. I just want to feel *something* solid. I keep looking at baby Finn and he’s just… there. Perfect. And I feel like a complete failure. Like I messed up the biggest thing I’ve ever done.

Mom keeps saying “you’ll be fine,” and Dad just looks sad. It’s not helpful. Honestly, it's just making me feel worse. I keep replaying everything in my head, every argument, every little thing. Did I say the wrong thing? Did he just… not like me anymore? I keep expecting him to walk through the door, you know? Like this is some weird, awful dream I can just wake up from.

I keep staring at his side of the bed. It’s just… empty. And it’s not even a big bed, it’s just… a space. A huge, empty space. I don't know how to be a person without him. Or how to be a mom. It feels like I've lost a part of myself. I just want it to stop. I just want everything to go back to the way it was. Which, obviously, it can't. Ugh. Just… ugh.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is hard. Really hard. I went to Sarah’s baby shower today and I mostly just… existed. I know, I know, sounds lame, right? But everyone was, like, *so* happy. Giggling, taking pictures, offering advice – “You’re glowing!” “You’re such a natural!” And I just… felt like I was watching a movie about someone else’s life. Like I was wearing a costume.

I tried to talk, I really did. I complimented the cake, which was, honestly, amazing. But then I started rambling about how sleep is a myth and I haven’t brushed my hair in three days and I just… stopped. My voice got all shaky and I think I said something about how babies are basically tiny, demanding aliens. Which, okay, maybe I’m being dramatic.

But it just felt like everyone was judging me. Not like, *mean* judging, but… assessing. Like they were trying to figure out if I was ‘good enough’ to be a mom. And I don’t even know what ‘good enough’ *means*. I feel like I’m failing at everything. Like I’m constantly messing up. Last night I spilled milk all over the floor while trying to make Daniel’s bottle and I just burst into tears. It felt so stupid, and embarrassing.

Maybe I should have just stayed home. It would have been easier. I don’t get why it's so hard to just… be. Just be present. I feel like I'm always trying to perform, to be the “perfect mom” but I have no idea how to do that. I just… idk. I just want to disappear sometimes. Maybe I'm being ridiculous, but it just feels so overwhelming. I just wish people would just… let me be.

Okay seriously, I'm losing it. Like, completely. Everyone keeps saying “new parent brain” but it’s not just that, it’s… something else. I have this huge presentation due Friday – a project I’ve been working on for months – and I haven’t even started properly. I keep staring at the slides, just… blank. It’s like my brain just decided to take a vacation and left me here with a ticking clock.

And then there’s Leo. He’s… good. Mostly. But he’s also demanding. He cries a lot, especially when he’s hungry or tired, and I feel like I’m failing him constantly. Like I'm not even a good enough mom. I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing, all the things I’m neglecting – my work, myself, everything.

It’s not that I don't love him, I do. But it’s like… a tidal wave of feeling. Overwhelming. And I just want to disappear sometimes. I tried to talk to Mark about it, but he just said “you’re glowing!” Glowing? I feel like I’m crumbling. I keep replaying everything in my head, second-guessing every decision, every interaction. Was I too harsh? Not harsh enough? Did I even hold him properly?

I just need to finish this presentation. It’s the only thing keeping me from completely unraveling. But how am I supposed to focus on something so important when I feel like I'm drowning? I keep telling myself it’ll be okay, that it’s just a phase, but it doesn’t *feel* okay. It just feels… heavy. Really, really heavy.

text:
Okay, like, this is officially spiraling. I just… I don’t even know where to start. I applied for that promotion, right? The one at Sterling & Bloom? And I *thought* I nailed the interview. Seriously, I really did. I practiced my answers, I researched everything, I even wore that blue blazer Mom said would make me look ‘competent’ – which, you know, felt super awkward. But then, today, Sarah from HR just… emailed. Said they're ‘going in a different direction’ and that they ‘appreciate my interest.’ Which is… polite, I guess? But also, crushing.

It’s just… everything feels so fragile lately. I’m, like, barely keeping up with rent, and the credit card bill is looming. I know, I know, I *should* be budgeting, and I’ve downloaded those apps, but I just… I get distracted. Like, really distracted. I’ll be looking at a spreadsheet, and suddenly I’m just… scrolling through TikTok for an hour. It’s stupid, right? It’s *so* stupid.

And then I start thinking about all the things I *should* be doing, all the things I’m failing at. I feel like I’m constantly disappointing everyone. Mom, especially. She just wants me to be ‘stable,’ you know? Like, a real adult. But how can I be stable when I feel like I’m just… floating? I don’t even know what I want. I just… I want things to be *okay*. Maybe I'm being dramatic. Probably. I just… I wish I could just be normal. Like, not feel this constant, low-level panic. Maybe I should just call Mom. No, that would just make it worse. I dunno. Just… feeling really lost, honestly. It’s like all my plans are just… dissolving. It’s exhausting.

text: Ugh, seriously, I don't even know where to start. Like, everything feels...off. I just got another bill notice and it's just… a lot. I told my boss I might need a little extra time to pay things back, and he just kinda stared. He didn’t say anything, which is worse, I think. I keep thinking about selling some stuff, but then I feel guilty, you know? Like, I *should* be better at this, I have a job, right? But it just feels like I'm constantly failing at being an adult. Maybe I'm overreacting? Probably. I just... I feel like I'm falling apart, and it’s all about the money, but it's like, everything’s connected to the money, isn’t it? Idk. It’s just…heavy.

Okay seriously, I’m losing it. Like, completely losing it. It’s just…the baby. He’s, like, *constantly* needing something. Feed, change, comfort, burp, check on…it’s a loop. And I can’t seem to keep up. My husband is trying, I know he is, but he just…doesn't *get* it. He says "just sleep when the baby sleeps," but that’s literally impossible. I haven’t slept properly in, I don’t even know, weeks? I feel like a zombie. Everyone keeps saying “it’s a phase,” but it doesn’t feel like a phase. It feels like I’m failing at being a mom. Like I'm just…bad at this. I keep thinking about how I used to be, you know? Before the baby. I was…capable. I could handle things. Now I just feel overwhelmed and stupid. I keep replaying everything in my head, every little thing I did wrong, every time I dropped him, every time I didn't get him something right away. It’s a mess. I just want to curl up and disappear. I don’t even recognize myself sometimes, like this exhausted, messy version of me. It's not fair. It’s just not fair. I just want it to stop. I just want a moment to breathe. And maybe, just maybe, feel like myself again. God, I’m so tired.

text:
Okay, so like, this is… a lot. We moved like, two weeks ago, to this tiny little town called Havenwood. It’s supposed to be ‘peaceful’ and ‘rural’ which, honestly, sounds amazing in theory, right? But it’s not. It’s just… quiet. Too quiet. I keep expecting someone to yell at me, or something. Or maybe just… something. I don’t even know.

Liam’s doing okay, mostly. He’s obsessed with the squirrels. He’s named one Pip and spends like, hours just watching him bury nuts. It’s… sweet, I guess? But it’s also weird. He doesn't really *talk* about anything. Just Pip and the squirrels. And me… I try to talk to him, you know, about the move, about how I feel, but it just feels… wrong? Like I’m interrupting something.

My mom keeps sending me these emails about ‘settling in’ and ‘making friends’ and I just… idk. I feel like I’m wearing a costume, a really itchy, uncomfortable costume. Like I’m supposed to be happy and grateful and posting cute pictures of Liam on Instagram, but inside I just feel… shaky. I keep thinking about how we left everything behind - my job, my friends, my life. It feels like I’ve lost a piece of myself.

Maybe it’s just the sleep deprivation? Seriously, I haven't slept properly in weeks. Or maybe it's just… this place. It feels like it's watching me. I keep catching glimpses of things out of the corner of my eye. Probably just the shadows and the trees, I know, but it still makes me jump. I just… I wish things were different. I wish I could just feel normal again. Like, *normal*. I don't even know what that looks like anymore, honestly. It’s just… a vague longing. A heavy, quiet longing.  I should probably call my sister, but I don't want to bother her with this. It’s probably nothing, right? Maybe.

everything feels so…wrong. like i’m failing at being a person, you know? losing my job was bad, but now i just…can't think straight about money. it's all just spinning and i don't even know where to start. feels like i’m falling apart.
Seriously, why do people just *talk*? Like, I can’t even hold a conversation. It’s all just…noise. Everyone’s laughing at something, and I feel like I’m missing the joke. I just want to go home.

text: Okay seriously, I don’t even know what to do anymore. It just… happened. Like, one minute we were talking about weekend plans and the next he was just… gone. He said it wasn’t working, that we were “too different,” which is honestly the most cliché thing ever but also, you know, probably true. Part of me just wants to scream, you know? Like, why couldn't he *see* how much I liked him? It feels like everything I do is wrong, or not enough, or maybe I’m just a mess. I keep replaying everything in my head, every little thing we said, every look he gave me, trying to figure out where I went wrong. My friends are being nice, saying it’s for the best, but it just feels like they don't *get* it. It's like a hole in my chest, this constant, aching emptiness. I just… I don't even recognize myself looking in the mirror anymore, it's weird.
He just... left. Like he didn't even *see* how much I did for him. It’s like I’m not even real, you know? Just a shadow he could ignore. Guess I'm back to being alone again. Ugh.

Okay, seriously, what is happening to me? Like, I just… stopped. No big fight, no layoff notice, nothing. Just… stopped showing up. My boss called, weirdly polite, asked if I was okay. I mumbled something about needing a break. A break from *what*, I don’t even know. It’s like my brain just decided to take a vacation and left me stranded. Everyone keeps saying “it’s a transition,” but it feels like I’m dissolving. I’m good at things, I was *good* at my job. Now I just feel… empty. And a little bit angry, mostly at myself for not being able to just, you know, *do* anything. It’s stupid. Just stupid.

okay so like i just moved. to this tiny apartment downtown and it’s…fine i guess. but it’s not *right*. it feels like i’m wearing someone else’s clothes, you know? like this is where i *should* be, but it just doesn't fit. my roommate, elara, she’s really nice, super chill, always making playlists and stuff. she’s like, genuinely happy here, and i just… i don't get it.

i keep thinking about everything i left behind, like my old friends, my stupid little coffee shop where i always sat in the corner, it all feels so…distant. like a dream. and i know i should be excited, a new city, a fresh start, all that jazz. but it’s just… heavy.

i tried calling my mom last night and i just… froze. i couldn’t say anything. it felt like if i opened my mouth, the whole thing would just crumble. she asked if i was okay and i just said “yeah fine” and hung up. i hate that. i hate feeling like i’m lying to everyone, even myself.

and then i just started crying. not loud, just… silent. like little streams. and i don't even know why. it's not even sad, exactly. it’s just… a feeling. like a pressure building up behind my eyes. it’s like i'm constantly on the edge of something, but i don’t know what. maybe this is what it feels like to not belong anywhere. maybe i’m just meant to be wandering, a ghost in my own life. it’s exhausting. honestly, i just wish i could go back to how things were, even if they weren’t perfect. it felt simpler then, somehow.

text: okay so like, this place is…it’s just not right. I moved here on a whim, you know? Just packed up my things in the middle of the night after, like, everything just felt too small, too grey. My old apartment was a disaster, honestly, a beautiful, chaotic disaster filled with half-finished projects and old records and the smell of coffee and rain, but it felt…safe. Like it *knew* me. This is just…sterile. Everything’s beige. Beige walls, beige carpet, beige furniture. And the neighbors, they don’t talk, just stare. Like I’m a bug under a microscope or something. I keep expecting someone to yell at me for being here, for existing, I don't even know. It’s weird, I feel like I should be excited, right? New start, new city, a fresh page. But it’s just…heavy. Like there’s this weight in my chest that won’t go away. I keep thinking about my mom and how she always said I was a whirlwind, a force of nature, and now I'm just…settled. Like a stone. I keep rearranging my furniture, trying to make it feel less…empty, but it doesn't help. It just highlights how empty everything *is*. I keep telling myself it’s just the move, that I'll adjust, that I'll find my people, but…what if I don’t? What if this is just...it? This beige, silent, lonely thing? I keep staring at my hands, they feel so small, so fragile. Like they could just crumble. I just…I don’t even know what I’m doing anymore, honestly. It’s like I’m watching myself from a distance, trying to figure out what happened, and I can’t remember. I just feel…lost. Really, really lost.

okay seriously i don’t even know what’s happening. little elara had a fever last night, like a really bad one. i woke up three times checking her temperature, and she was just burning up. i called the doctor’s office, of course, and they said to bring her in this morning. they’ve run some tests, and it’s… complicated. they don't know yet.

my husband, ben, he’s just… calm. too calm maybe. he’s been pacing around the room like a caged animal, just staring at the monitors and saying things like "everything’s going to be okay" and “she’s a fighter.” but i just feel this awful pressure in my chest, like i’m about to explode. it’s not that i don’t *want* her to be okay, god i just do, but it’s like… everything is riding on this.

i keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong. i keep replaying every single thing i did today, every single thing i said, wondering if i missed something, if i could have done something differently. maybe if i’d given her a different kind of formula, maybe if i’d taken her outside for a walk instead of letting her nap, maybe... it’s stupid i know but it’s just a constant loop.

i feel like i’m failing her already. like i’m not a good enough mom. i keep looking at her, so small and vulnerable, and i just want to wrap her up in a blanket and protect her from everything, even if it means suffocating her. i just... i just want it to be over. i want to know what’s wrong and how to fix it. i just feel so lost and overwhelmed and like i’m drowning in a sea of worry. ben keeps saying “we’ll get through this together,” but it feels like i’m the only one struggling.

okay seriously, i just…left that work thing. like, completely bailed. it wasn't even that bad, like, a presentation about quarterly reports or something. boring. but everyone was just…so *loud*. And smiling. All the time. Like they were actively trying to make me feel bad about myself, you know? Which, honestly, is pretty standard for them. They always seem to know exactly what to say to make you question everything.

I felt like I was drowning in their forced happiness. It’s stupid, I know, but it just…gets to me. I told Sarah I had a migraine, which is a lie, obviously. But it felt easier than explaining that I just needed to *escape*. Like, I felt this pressure building up in my chest, this frantic need to just…be somewhere else. Somewhere quiet. Somewhere where people didn't expect me to be ‘on’.

Then Mark started talking about his vacation, which was obviously supposed to be inspiring, but it just made me feel like I was failing. Like I wasn’t doing anything interesting with my life. He’s always doing something amazing, hiking in the Alps or volunteering in Nepal or whatever. It's exhausting.

I just wanted to disappear. I texted my roommate and said I was coming home. She didn't even ask why. She just said, "Okay, be here soon." It's…fine, I guess. But it’s always just…fine. It’s never actually *good*. And I just…can’t. I just can't do this anymore. Like, I’m constantly trying to keep up, to be…whatever they want me to be, and it’s just… exhausting.  I just need a break. A really, really big break.

text: it’s just… everything felt so *real* you know? like, genuinely real and now it’s all just blurry and I keep expecting him to text me, but he doesn’t and it’s like a part of me just… vanished, and I don’t even know what I’m supposed to do anymore, it’s all so confusing and heavy.
