text: okay so like, this is probably a really bad time to be posting, honestly, but i just… i don’t even know where to start. it’s just… everything feels so blurry, you know? i’m supposed to be studying for these exams, the ones that are going to, like, totally determine my entire future and i can’t even remember what i read last night. it’s just a jumble of words and equations and dates and i’m just staring at the page and it’s like… mocking me, i think? or maybe it’s just me being dramatic, but seriously, it feels like my brain is just a tangled mess of yarn and i can’t find the end to pull. i keep falling asleep, like, at the most ridiculous times – in lectures, during dinner, even while i’m trying to write an essay. and then when i *do* manage to sleep, it’s these awful, fragmented dreams, full of faces i don’t recognize and places i’ve never been, and i wake up feeling even more confused and… empty? i keep telling myself i’m just stressed, that it's exam season, everyone’s tired, but it’s more than that, isn’t it? it’s like… i’m constantly anticipating something bad is going to happen, like a storm is always brewing just on the horizon. and i just… I feel so fragile, like one wrong thing and i'll just completely shatter. i don't even know who i am anymore, really. i used to be… organized, i think? and driven? now it's just… this. this swirling chaos and this constant feeling of not being enough. i just wish i could just… stop. just for a second.

Just moved, feels like I’m wearing someone else’s face. Everything’s wrong, the colors are all off. Like I’m watching a movie about my life and I’m just… a ghost. Don't even know why I'm here anymore.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a stupid thing to even write about, honestly. But I just… I don’t even know what’s happening to me anymore. It’s been a *week* of just… this. Shift after shift, staring at the fluorescent lights, making coffee that tastes like burnt regret. And the bills are piling up, you know? Like, seriously piling. I keep telling myself it’s just temporary, that things will get better when I get the next paycheck, which is, like, three days away? Maybe. I don't even know.

It's not even that I'm *spending* money, exactly. It's just... small things. A candy bar, a coffee, a stupid keychain. Little impulses, and then I feel awful, but then I do it again. It’s like a cycle. I told my mom about it, but she just… sighed. You know how she is. She keeps saying I need to “budget” and “be responsible.” Like that's *easy*. It’s not easy! It’s… overwhelming.

I feel like I’m constantly failing, even at the simplest things. Like, I tried to make dinner last night – burned the pasta, set off the smoke alarm. It just felt… pointless. And then I just wanted to… disappear, I guess? Not like, permanently, but just… fade into the background. Maybe if I didn't exist, nobody would notice the mess I’m making. I keep thinking about just… not showing up to work tomorrow. I don’t know why I even *thought* that. It’s probably just… a phase. Right? I just… feel so lost. Like I'm constantly trying to hold onto something that's slipping through my fingers. It's just... a lot. I’m probably overthinking it too. Ugh.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this city is… huge. Seriously, it’s overwhelming. I moved here a month ago, just… popped. No real plan, you know? Just needed a change, i guess. And it’s beautiful, the buildings and the parks and everything, but it just feels… empty. Like, I’m surrounded by people, but I don’t *see* anyone. Or maybe I do, and I just don't want to. It’s weird.

I tried going to that coffee shop everyone talks about, the one with the really good lattes, and I just… froze. Like, I sat there for twenty minutes, staring at the menu, and then got up and left. It felt too… intense. I keep thinking maybe if I just talked to someone, anyone, it would help. But then what if they don’t like me? What if I say something stupid? I’m so awkward, honestly.

My phone's been blowing up with messages from my mom, asking if I’m okay. She keeps saying, “You need to make friends!” Like it’s that simple. It’s not simple. I just… I don't know *how* to make friends. I’m not good at small talk, or anything, really. I feel like I'm just observing everyone else’s lives, but I’m not actually *in* any of them. It’s a really lonely feeling. I dunno, maybe I’m just being dramatic. Probably. Maybe I just need to get out more, go for a walk, something. But then again, what's the point of doing anything if you don't connect with anyone? It’s just… a lot. I’m probably overthinking it. Right? Maybe.

okay seriously i don't even know who i am anymore. everything just...fell apart. the job, like, completely evaporated. i was so sure i was doing okay, pushing myself, you know? but then they just said “restructuring” and boom. gone. and school feels even worse. like i’m drowning just trying to keep up. i keep thinking about all the things i *should* have done differently, all the times i messed up. it's just...a lot. i keep seeing my old boss's face, and it’s like he’s judging me, even though he didn’t say anything. i just feel so…empty. like there’s a hole where my motivation used to be. i just want to disappear, honestly. not like, permanently, but just...away. maybe.

okay so like seriously this is a mess. i just got here and it feels…wrong. everything is so beige and boring. back home it was all neon and loud and everyone knew everyone. here it’s just…quiet. like, too quiet. i keep expecting someone to just *appear* and start yelling at me or something. my mom says it’s just adjusting but it’s not. it feels like i’m wearing someone else’s skin. i keep thinking about packing my bags, just disappearing. i don't even know if i *want* to be here. i keep telling myself it’ll get better, but it doesn't feel like it. i just…i just feel lost and like a ghost.  i don't even know who i am here anymore, honestly. it's weird, right? really weird.

it’s just… everything feels so wrong lately. like i’m wearing someone else’s clothes and they’re itchy and don’t fit. i keep changing my mind about everything, you know? about my friends, about what i want to do. it's exhausting.

it’s just… everything feels so fragile lately. like one wrong move and it all just collapses. bills are piling up and i don’t know how i’m going to keep going. i feel so useless, like i’m failing everyone. just a mess really.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, another night, right? Just staring at the flickering fluorescent lights and it’s honestly… overwhelming. Everyone else is probably asleep, tucked away in their comfy beds dreaming about normal stuff, and I’m just here, sorting through these boxes of… things. I don't even know what half of it is anymore, it just feels like… remnants. Like I’m collecting the dust of who I used to be, and it’s heavy. My phone buzzed a little bit earlier with a text from my sister, just a smiley face and "thinking of you," and it hit me so hard. It always does. I just… wish someone would notice. Not like, a big deal noticing, just… acknowledging that I’m here. It's just... so quiet in here, you know? Like the whole world is muted except for the hum of the machines and my own thoughts just swirling.
it’s just…quiet. too quiet. everyone else has their lives, their people. i guess i’m just…here. staring at the wall again. feels like the color’s drained out of everything.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay, so like, this is probably a really weird thing to post, and i don’t even know if anyone will understand, but… it’s just… everything feels kinda blurry lately, you know? not like, physically blurry, but like, my thoughts? they’re not… solid. they’re just… floating around. and then they bump into each other and get all tangled up and i don’t even know what i’m thinking anymore. it’s like i’m watching myself think, but i can’t really *do* the thinking.

i keep switching between feeling like absolutely nothing at all, like i’m just a ghost going through the motions, and then suddenly this huge wave of… sadness? or maybe it’s disappointment? i don’t know. it’s just… heavy. and then i’ll be laughing at something completely random, like a meme, and it feels… fake. like i’m pretending.

my best friend, Liam, he asked me if i was okay the other day, and i just kinda shrugged and said “fine,” but i felt like i was lying. i don’t even know *why* i’m lying. it’s not like i have anything to hide, not really. it’s just… everything feels like it could change, like any second i could just… disappear. or maybe that’s just me being dramatic. idk. maybe i’m just stressed about finals. but it feels… deeper than that. like there’s something broken, something not quite right. i just... i don't know what it is. i just wish i knew. it’s exhausting.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
okay so like seriously i can’t even. everything’s just… swirling. i keep waking up in the middle of the night, drenched in sweat, just staring at the ceiling. it’s not even nightmares, not really. just… flashes. snippets of conversations i had yesterday, but they’re all wrong somehow. like, my roommate said something about needing to renew her library card and i’m convinced she meant something completely different, like a secret code or something.

and then i’ll start thinking about him. not that *him*, exactly. just… a guy i saw in the cafeteria last week. he had a little smudge of chocolate on his cheek and he smiled at me and it completely threw me off. it felt like a huge deal, you know? like the universe was trying to tell me something important but i just didn’t *get* it. i keep replaying the moment over and over in my head, analyzing every tiny detail. was he judging me? was he laughing? is he going to call me?

i’m supposed to be studying for my organic chem exam and i haven't even opened the textbook. i just keep scrolling through tiktok, watching people eat ramen and complain about their professors. it’s pathetic, i know. my mom keeps asking if i’m eating and sleeping and i just say yeah yeah, but it’s a lie. i feel like i'm drowning, like i’m just going to float away if i don't hold onto something. but i don’t even know what to hold onto. everything feels… fragile. like if i breathe too hard, it’ll all just fall apart. i just need to sleep, i really do. but i can’t. it’s like my brain is stuck on repeat, playing a broken record of anxieties and stupid little obsessions. this is just… too much. i feel so lost.

it's just… everything feels so wrong now, you know? like the colors have all bled out. i keep replaying everything, every stupid thing i said, every time i didn't say enough. he said it wasn't me, that i was too much, too intense. maybe he’s right. i just… i don't know who i am without him. it's like a part of me just vanished. i keep staring at my phone, expecting a text, a call, anything, but it’s just silence. and it’s loud. really loud. i feel like i’m drowning, but i can’t even scream. i just want to disappear, honestly. just fade away and not have to feel anything anymore. this job was awful, but at least it was something. now… nothing.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, I just… I don’t even get why people like hanging out. Like, I tried to join a conversation at lunch and then I just froze. Maybe I said something stupid? idk. It just feels… exhausting. Like everyone else knows what they're doing and I'm just… lost.

Okay, seriously, this is just… too much. My mom called again. Like, five times. Just to tell me I’m failing at being a grown-up. Says I don’t call enough, that I’m “distant.” Distant?! I’m busy! I have a job, a life, trying to figure things out, okay? But she just keeps picking at it, twisting everything into some kind of personal attack. It's like she *needs* me to be a disappointment.

And Dad… don’t even get me started. He just… nods and agrees with her. Like, he actually agrees! It's not even a disagreement, he just lets her steamroll over me. I feel like I’m trapped in this weird, circular conversation that goes nowhere. Like I’m shouting into a black hole.

I told her I was stressed, that I just needed some space. She said I was pushing everyone away. Seriously? Is that what I do? Is that all I am? A walking, talking rejection? I don't *want* to push anyone away, but it just feels like… if I say anything, it’s wrong. If I ask for something, it’s selfish.

I just wanted a simple “how are you?” without a lecture attached. Is that really so much to ask? I keep thinking about just… disappearing for a while. Not like, permanently, just… to get away from it all. Just to not have to deal with the constant judgment. It's exhausting. Like, constantly feeling like I'm trying to build something and everyone's just tearing it down, brick by brick. I just want to curl up and not feel anything, but that's probably just stupid too, isn’t it?

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: okay so like seriously, I don’t even know where to begin. it’s just…everything. I feel like I’m drowning, you know? I just finished this insane project for, like, my old professor – he’s *so* intense, always wanting more, more, more – and it’s just sitting there, judging me, and I can’t even look at it. It’s beautiful, I guess, technically, but it feels…wrong. like I built it with the wrong pieces. and then, at work, it’s just the same thing. they keep piling on these new responsibilities, and everyone’s saying “you’re so capable, you’ll handle it!” but I don’t *feel* capable. I feel like I’m just faking it, like everyone else knows what they’re doing and I’m just…spinning my wheels. my mom keeps asking if I’m sleeping, if I’m eating, if I’m *okay*, and it’s just…it’s like she doesn't understand. she just wants me to be ‘normal’, you know? like a grown-up. but I’m not. I never have been, probably. I just keep thinking about how I should have just stayed in school, gotten a different degree, anything to avoid this. this constant feeling of being behind, of never being good enough. and then I just…I just want to disappear, you know? not like permanently, but just…out of sight. like maybe if I wasn’t here, none of this would matter. it’s just a huge mess and I don’t even know how to sort it out. I keep replaying everything in my head, and it’s just…a cycle. a horrible, exhausting cycle. I just wish it would stop.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: okay so like seriously, this new town is just…wrong. it feels like everyone’s judging me, even though i haven’t done anything, right? i keep expecting someone to say something, to point, but they just sort of…look through me. it’s really weird and i’m starting to think i imagined the good things about it, you know? like, my old school was so easy, everyone was just…there. here it’s like a bunch of shiny, perfect people who don’t actually *see* anyone. i tried to talk to the girl in my English class, Chloe, but she just smiled and said “cool” and then went back to drawing in her notebook. i don’t even know why i bothered. i just feel…detached, like i'm watching myself from outside my own body and it’s kinda terrifying honestly. i just wish i could go back.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: It’s… it’s the anniversary. Feels like yesterday and a lifetime ago all at once. Everyone keeps saying it’s a new chapter, but honestly, it just feels like a really bad rerun. Like I’m stuck on repeat, watching the same awful scene. I keep expecting her to just… appear. Like she’ll walk through the door and everything will be normal again. It’s stupid, right? Everyone says it gets easier, but it doesn’t. It just… changes. I keep picking at things, little things, trying to make sense of it, but it doesn't make any sense. Just… a lot of static. I’m failing all my exams, which is probably a good thing, honestly. Don’t really feel like facing anything.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it's been a year today. a year since everything fell apart and i'm still here just… floating. like i don't even matter anymore. this city feels huge and empty and i just want to disappear, honestly. why do anniversaries always feel like this?

okay seriously, this is getting ridiculous. i haven’t slept properly in like, weeks? maybe longer. it doesn't even matter, right? like, does it *really* matter if i’m running on three hours a night and feeling like a zombie? everyone else is just… normal. they go to bed, they wake up, they function. i just… spiral.

tonight i tossed and turned for hours, just staring at the ceiling. it’s weird, because i *want* to sleep. i really do. i just… can’t. my thoughts just keep going and going and going. it’s like my brain is a broken record, skipping over the same awful stuff over and over. mostly about how stupid i am, and how everyone’s judging me, and how i’m probably ruining everything.

it's not even logical, you know? i know people care, i think. but i can’t shake this feeling that i’m just… a burden. a mess. like i’m actively trying to make things worse, even though i don't even know *why*. i keep replaying conversations in my head, twisting everything into something awful. like i said something wrong, or did something wrong, even if i didn’t.

and then i just feel… empty. Like there’s nothing underneath. Like if you just poked around, there’d be nothing but air. i just want to disappear sometimes, honestly. not like, permanently, just… fade away into the background. it’s exhausting. it’s just… exhausting. i wish i could just switch it off. turn it all off.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay so like seriously, this apartment is…fine. it’s beige. aggressively beige. and it smells faintly of old cleaning products, which is, you know, not exactly a comforting scent. i unpacked everything, mostly boxes of books and half of my clothes, and it just feels…empty. like he left a hole, a really big, dusty hole that this beige doesn't even attempt to fill. i keep expecting him to just *be* here, you know? sitting on the couch, reading, or whatever. it’s stupid, i know, but it’s like my brain just…rewinds to all the times he was. and then i’m just standing in this empty room, feeling this awful ache.

i told my mom i was excited about the "fresh start," which is a lie, a really big, shameful lie. i’m not excited. i’m terrified. it’s not even the move itself, it’s the *reason* for the move. and everything feels so fragile now, like one wrong step and it’ll all just…crumble. i keep scrolling through old photos, just staring at him smiling, and then i have to stop myself. it’s pointless. it just makes everything worse. i tried to call him, like, three times yesterday, just to hear his voice, just to say something stupid, anything, but he didn’t answer. i left a message, a really rambling, incoherent message about needing to feel normal again, which is ridiculous, isn't it? I just…I don’t know what to do anymore. This new place is supposed to be a clean slate, but it just feels like a reminder of everything i’ve lost. and maybe i'm just catastrophizing, maybe it'll be okay, but honestly? it doesn’t feel okay at all. it feels like wading through mud.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
okay seriously this week is just… a mess. it’s like the universe decided to specifically target me with bad vibes. it’s the anniversary of… you know. the thing. six months ago today. and it feels like it happened yesterday and a lifetime ago all at the same time. i keep replaying it in my head, you know? like a broken record. it's stupid i know but i can’t stop. it’s just… so vivid.

i tried to distract myself with studying for my exams, which is ridiculous i know, like i can just magically absorb all this information when my brain is a tangled knot. everyone else is so calm and focused, like they’re actually prepared. i feel like i’m drowning and they're just paddling around on the surface. i keep thinking about him, about what he said, about how it all just… ended. it doesn’t make sense. it really doesn’t.

i keep going back and forth on whether i should even call my mom. it’s probably stupid to burden her with this but she always knows what to say, you know? a little platitude, a hug. but it feels like it won’t actually *help*. like it’s just… surface level. and i just need to feel *something* other than this hollow ache. i keep trying to tell myself it’s getting better, that i’m moving on, but it just feels like i’m wearing a mask and underneath it's just… empty. maybe i just need to scream. or disappear. i don’t even know anymore. just… ugh. this is exhausting.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: okay seriously, Mom just texted again. it's about the holiday plans, obviously. she wants me to come home for Thanksgiving, like, *again*. and she’s acting like it's some huge sacrifice on her part, like I don’t have a job and bills and a life. but it’s not a sacrifice for *her*, is it? it’s just... easier for her, i guess? she always makes it about her needs, you know? it’s like a constant, low-level hum of disappointment whenever I try to actually do something for myself. I keep thinking about just not responding, just letting it go, but then this weird feeling creeps in, like I’m letting her win or something, or that I’m failing at being a good daughter, even though she’s the one who’s consistently… difficult. honestly, I just want to disappear sometimes, you know? into a really quiet room with no expectations.

okay seriously i don’t even know what’s happening anymore. like, i thought having leo would be… amazing you know? a little scary but amazing. but now i just feel like a failure. we’re drowning in bills and i keep staring at the fridge and it’s just… empty. i keep telling myself it’s just a phase, that things will get better, that i’m a good mom, but then i look at him and he’s just so small and i feel this huge weight on my chest like i’m letting him down. i just want to curl up and disappear. everyone else seems to have it together, you know? their houses are clean, their kids are happy, and i’m just… this. a mess. i don’t even recognize myself sometimes. i just... i don’t know what to do.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so, like, this place is…beige. Seriously, everything is beige. My apartment, the street, even the sky feels a little beige today. It’s supposed to be ‘fresh start’ right? Everyone keeps saying that, a fresh start, a new chapter, blah blah blah. But it just feels…empty. I unpacked everything from the old house, every single box, and it’s still just…wrong. Like I’m wearing someone else’s clothes, you know?  I keep expecting Liam to be here, to just...be, and it's so weird not having him just *there*.  I keep rearranging the furniture, trying to make it feel like *my* space, but it’s like the space itself is actively resisting.  I just... I don't know what to do anymore.  It’s like I’m permanently lost, and this beige apartment is just a really dull reflection of that feeling.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Everyone just... expects me to be okay. Like, immediately. I don't *do* okay. It's exhausting pretending I'm not a mess when I'm around my parents. Just want to disappear, honestly.

Okay, so this is… a lot. Like, a really, really overwhelming lot. I thought having a baby would be… I don’t even know, beautiful and everything. It’s… it’s quiet. Too quiet. Liam’s at work, obviously, and I’m just… here. With her. She’s sleeping, which is great, I guess, but it just makes it worse. It’s like this vast, empty space and she’s the only thing filling it. And she's perfect, she really is, all tiny and pink, but… it’s lonely.

I keep expecting someone to just… pop in. Like, my mom or something. Or even just a neighbor. But no one does. I texted Sarah last night and she said she was busy, which is fine, but it just felt… dismissive, I think. Like she doesn't really get it. Like she thinks this is *cute*. It’s not cute. It’s terrifying.

I keep looking at her and thinking, “I’m supposed to be *happy*.” And I *want* to be. I really do. But it’s like… a muscle I don't know how to use. I feel this constant, low-level panic, just simmering under the surface. Like I'm failing at something fundamental. Failing at being a mom, failing at being… anything. I feel like I should be posting pictures all the time, you know? Like everyone else does. But I just don't have the energy. Or the desire, honestly. It just feels pointless. I just want someone to… just be here. With me. Even if it’s just to say, “You’re doing okay.” But I don’t hear it. Just the quiet. A lot of quiet.

it’s just… everything feels so wrong tonight. like i’m watching myself from outside my own head. i keep turning over and over in bed, trying to find a comfortable position, but it’s no use. just a knot in my stomach and this awful feeling i don’t even know why.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
Okay, seriously, this is exhausting. Like, I just got back home after trying to be productive – actually *doing* something with my degree, you know? – and my mom was already on it. Just… lecturing. About my life choices. Again. It's like she doesn’t even *hear* me when I say I’m trying to figure things out. She just assumes I’m failing, that I’m wasting my potential. Which, fine, maybe I am. But it’s not like she’s helping! 

My dad just nodded and smiled, like he’s totally on her side. He always is. They both just… expect me to be this adult, this successful person, living in a nice apartment and having a good job. But I’m here. Stuck. Eating microwave dinners and avoiding everyone. It’s not that I don’t *want* to be successful, I guess. It's just... everything feels so heavy. Like I'm carrying this giant weight and no one sees it. 

I told them about the job application I actually finished, the one I was *excited* about, and they just talked about how it wasn't “real” or “stable.” Like my feelings don't matter. Like my goals aren’t valid. I just want to scream. I don’t even know what I’m doing anymore, or who I am. I keep thinking about just… disappearing, you know? Not like, permanently, but just… somewhere else. Somewhere they can't get to me. It’s stupid, I know. Just… a really stupid, overwhelming feeling. I just wish they’d just *leave* me alone for five minutes.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay so like seriously, this whole new place is… a lot. i thought, you know, maybe having a fresh start with little elara would be amazing, a clean slate and all that, but it’s just… overwhelming. we moved like, three weeks ago and i still feel like i’m just trying to keep my head above water, you know? the apartment is tiny, and it smells faintly of lemon cleaner which is weirdly stressful, and everything’s beige. beige everywhere. it's like someone decided beige was the color of happiness and just coated the whole world in it.

and the neighbors! they're… polite, i guess? but they just stare. like, constantly. i swear i saw mrs. henderson from across the hall watching me unload the dishwasher yesterday. it felt like a judgement, you know? not a *mean* judgement, just… an observation. like i’m failing at being a mom, or failing at being a person, or something.

elara’s doing okay, mostly. she’s sleeping a lot, which is good i think, but she keeps trying to feed me her toys. it's sweet, really, but also completely exhausting. i feel like i’m constantly apologizing for things i didn’t even do. and i keep having these… flashes. memories of my old life, of my old *me*, and then i just… switch. like flipping a light switch and suddenly i’m angry at something that didn’t even happen, or sad about a future that hasn’t arrived yet. it's so confusing, and i don't even know how to talk about it. i just feel… fractured, like i’m trying to assemble a puzzle with pieces that don't quite fit.  i keep thinking maybe if i just organized everything perfectly, color-coded the baby’s clothes, and baked a cake, it would all magically make sense, but it doesn't. it just feels… heavier. i just want to go home, but home doesn’t feel like home anymore either, does it?  it’s just… a lot.

Okay, seriously, I can’t even. Like, I’ve been staring at the ceiling for, I don’t know, hours? It’s like my brain just…refuses to shut off. Everyone else is asleep, snoring or whatever, and I’m just here, vibrating. It’s not even exciting or anything, just…this constant buzzing. I keep thinking about stupid stuff, things that don’t matter, and then I’ll think about something important and then it’ll be gone and I’ll be back to the buzzing.

Mom keeps telling me to just “relax” but like, how? It's not like I *want* to be awake. It’s just…happens. Like a switch flipped and now I’m just…here. And I feel like I’m missing out on everything. Sarah and Liam were talking about the football game and I just…didn’t hear them. It’s weird. I feel like a ghost.

I tried counting sheep, but it just made me feel worse. It’s like they’re judging me for being awake. And then I started thinking about how everyone else is probably dreaming about, like, awesome stuff, and I’m just…stuck. It’s not fair. I just want to feel…normal. Like, not this. Like, not constantly on edge and feeling like I’m about to fall apart. I just wish I could just…sleep. Please.

Okay, seriously, this is just… a mess. Like, a huge, swirling, awful mess. I keep staring at my bank account and it just… mocks me. Empty. Just a blinking, accusing zero. I know I shouldn’t have bought that stupid vintage record player. It *looked* cool, okay? And the amp too. Just little things, right? But now I'm eating ramen again. Again. It’s like my brain just decides, “Yep, you deserve this. You deserve to be broke and lonely.”

I tried to ask my mom for a little help, but she just… sighed. Like I’m always a disappointment. She doesn’t *get* it, you know? She thinks I just need to "budget better." Like budgeting is the *solution* to everything. It's not! It’s like saying putting a band-aid on a broken leg. It doesn’t fix anything. It just makes it look slightly less awful for a little while.

I keep thinking about quitting my job. But then what? I need the money. Even if it's just to pay the electricity bill. It’s stupid, I know. I’m just… spiraling. Everything feels pointless. Like I’m just running on fumes and bad coffee and the vague hope that tomorrow will somehow be better. Which it never is. Just… more of the same. More empty. More gray. I just feel… stuck. And really, really tired.
He said I was too much. Too intense, too messy, too… everything. Guess I just am. It’s just… this empty space feels like me now, doesn’t it? Like I’m just this hollow shell.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, this is probably stupid even thinking about it, but I’m just… completely spiraling. I got that late fee on my rent again, and it’s just… piling up. I don’t even know where to start. I keep telling myself I’ll figure it out, you know? Maybe I can pick up some extra shifts at the cafe, but everyone's already so busy. It's just… frustrating. I feel like I'm failing at being an adult, like I should be better at managing money. I just… I don’t know how people do it. It’s like everything is just going wrong all at once. I keep thinking about just... not applying for that promotion, even though it would actually help. Maybe it's better to just stay stuck? Idk. Seriously, I'm so lost.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay so like, this is just… a lot. I don’t even know where to start. It’s been, what, three weeks since they let me go? Three weeks and it feels like a lifetime, like I’ve been buried under a mountain of everything I *thought* mattered. Mom’s been… well, Mom’s been Mom. All accusations, you know? Like it’s *my* fault the company went down, like I’m the one who didn’t pull enough weight. She keeps saying “you should have seen it coming,” which is ridiculous, honestly. I was trying, I really was. Just… not enough, apparently. And Dad, he just sits there, silent, like he’s watching a movie of my life. He doesn’t say anything, doesn’t offer any help, just this… this disappointed look. It’s like I’ve failed him too. Which, I guess, in a way I have, because I’m not providing, not like I used to.

It’s not just the job, though. It's everything. I keep feeling like I’m wearing someone else’s clothes, like I’m an imposter in my own life. Yesterday I almost called my sister, just to yell at her, you know? About how she always does this, always judges, always makes me feel… small. Then I stopped myself. Just… stopped. It’s exhausting, the constant feeling of being wrong, of not being good enough. I keep replaying everything, every conversation, every decision, trying to figure out where I went wrong. Like if I could just rewind and do it differently, maybe things would be okay. But they won’t, will they? It’s just a loop. A really, really uncomfortable loop. I just want to disappear, honestly. Just fade away into the wallpaper. It’s not that I *want* to hurt anyone, it’s just… this weight, this constant, heavy weight.

okay okay i just… i can’t sleep. it’s like my brain is just… buzzing. i keep replaying everything. everything that happened today. little things mostly. like when mom dropped her toast and it landed butter-side down, and she just started crying. it felt like the end of the world, you know? like i completely failed her. and then later, she asked me to read her that stupid romance novel again, the one with the shirtless guy on the cover, and i just… i wanted to scream. i don’t get why she likes those. it’s just… a lot.

i’m supposed to be calm, i’m supposed to be the strong one. that’s what i do, right? i’m the one who handles everything. but it’s exhausting. it’s like there’s this weight on my chest all the time, a constant pressure. and then i think about tomorrow, about all the things that *will* happen. more spilled milk, more arguments, more… everything. it's just a spiral.

i keep telling myself it’s because i care, that’s why i do it. but sometimes i feel like i’m drowning. like i’m just going through the motions and nothing really matters. i just want to curl up and disappear for a little while. just to be quiet. to not feel anything. it’s stupid i know, i have to take care of her. but god, it’s hard. sometimes i just wish… i wish she wasn’t so… much. i don’t even know what i’m trying to say, i just… ugh. i need to breathe. maybe if i just stare at the ceiling...

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this new job is… a lot. Everyone’s so confident and, you know, *good* at their jobs. And I just… I feel like I’m constantly messing up, even when I think I’m doing okay. It’s probably just me, right? I dunno. I just keep second-guessing everything. I tried to explain my idea in the meeting today and it just felt… wrong. Like I was rambling and stupid.

I moved here alone, remember? Just to start fresh, right? But it’s not fresh, it’s just… lonely. And I keep thinking about how everyone else here seems to know what they’re doing. Maybe I should just quit? But then what would I do? I just… I don’t know. It’s exhausting. Maybe if I just took a long shower and scrolled through TikTok for an hour it'll be better. I guess.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: Okay, seriously, this is just… a mess. Liam just… stopped answering. Like, completely ghosted. I keep texting, just little things, you know? "Hey, how's your studying?" "Did you eat?" Nothing. It’s like he doesn’t even *remember* we were together. It’s not fair, obviously. I was being okay, I thought. I was *trying* to be okay, balancing everything with exams, and now this. I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering what I did wrong, if I should have said something, anything. It feels like my whole world just shrunk down to this tiny, awful space. Like I’m just… floating. I keep expecting him to call, to show up at my dorm, but he won’t. Just gone.  It’s stupid, I know, but I can't shake this feeling that I'm somehow to blame.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, so like, this is just… a lot. I keep scrolling through my feed and everyone’s just *living*, you know? Like, actual, happy living. And I’m just… here. Back in my parents’ basement, surrounded by empty pizza boxes and the lingering scent of my mom’s lavender air freshener. It’s not that I *hate* it here, exactly, it's just… suffocating. I feel like I’m supposed to be *doing* something, you know? Like, a grown adult. But what? I have this degree, a perfectly good piece of paper, and it feels completely useless. It’s like I spent four years learning how to analyze old texts and now I'm supposed to, like, *use* that knowledge to, I don’t even know, save the world? It sounds ridiculous even saying it out loud.

And then the thoughts just… come. They’re not like, specific thoughts, more like this constant hum of anxiety, this feeling that I'm fundamentally flawed. Like everyone else just gets it, you know? The *right* way to be, the *right* things to want. I tried to call Sarah, my old roommate, but she just… sounded busy. Like she has a life. A real one. I don't get it. It's not that I want her pity, it's just… a longing, I guess? To feel like I fit somewhere, to feel like I’m not just this weird, adrift blob. I keep rearranging my room, trying to make it feel… right. It never does. Like I'm trying to build a house on sand, you know? It’s exhausting. I just want to disappear sometimes, not like, *really* disappear, but just… fade into the background. Like a bad photograph.
He said he was worried, but it just felt like he was avoiding me. Then the doctor said… I don’t even know why I’m upset. Everything’s falling apart, isn't it? Just… a mess.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, this deadline is just… crushing me. I feel like I’m just barely holding it together, you know? Like, maybe I should just tell them I can’t do it, but then what? idk. It’s just… a lot. Everything feels so fragile right now.

okay seriously i just… i don’t even know anymore. it’s always something with them, isn’t it? my sister, mark, my nephew leo… they just… pull at me. like i’m some kind of emotional sponge. i try to be good, you know? i really do. i cook, i clean, i listen to their endless complaints about everything – school, the dog, their dating lives. it's exhausting.

and then they get *mad* when i offer advice or try to, like, actually help. it's always “you just don’t understand!” or “you’re being judgmental!” i don’t *want* to be judgmental, okay? i just want them to be okay. i want them to be happy. is that too much to ask?

tonight it was leo. he was screaming about a test he failed, and it wasn't even a *big* test. he just needed to study a little more. and i told him to, you know, just focus. and he started crying and yelling that i never believe in him and that i always make things worse. it’s like… i’m always the bad guy. even when i'm trying to be the good guy.

my mom keeps saying “you’re doing a wonderful job” but it doesn’t feel wonderful. it just feels… heavy. like i'm carrying all of their problems and they don't even see it. i just… i feel like i’m losing myself in the mess of it all. like i’m fading away. i keep thinking about just… stepping back. not because i don't care, but because i can’t breathe. it’s too much. too much, too much, too much.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so the report’s due tomorrow and honestly, I just… I don’t even know where to start. It feels like everything is spinning, you know? Like if I just try too hard, it’ll all fall apart. Maybe I should just email him and tell him I can’t do it. idk…

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay so like, this is officially a disaster, right? I moved here, to Oakhaven, thinking it would be *amazing*, you know? Fresh start, new people, finally get to paint again, actually *do* something with my life instead of just…existing. And it was, for like, a day. A really beautiful, sunshiny day where I even managed to find a decent coffee shop and felt vaguely optimistic. But then the rent was due and I totally spaced, like, completely blanked and it’s gone. Gone. And now I’m sitting on a tiny, sticky folding chair in my studio apartment – which, let’s be honest, is basically just a glorified closet – and it smells faintly of old pizza and regret.

I’ve been calling everyone, my mom mostly, and she’s just…sympathetic, you know? Like a really nice, concerned grandma who keeps saying “You’ll figure it out, honey.” But I *can’t* figure it out! It’s not like I’m, like, rich or anything. I sell paintings online, mostly landscapes, and they barely cover the cost of the canvases, let alone the utilities. I keep thinking about just, like, packing everything up and going back home. Back to the small town where nothing *ever* happens, where everyone knows everyone and judging you is basically a hobby. It’s awful, I know, but the thought of facing another month like this, of just existing in this…grey space, it’s just…a lot. I feel like I’m dissolving, like I’m not even really *here*.  And the worst part is, I know I *should* be doing something, applying for more jobs, reaching out to those people I met at the art supply store, but it all just feels…pointless. Like shouting into the void and expecting someone to actually hear you. I just...I just don't know what to do anymore.

okay so like seriously i don't even know what's happening anymore. it’s just… quiet. always quiet. i went to that networking thing at work today, you know, the one everyone says is “good for you” and “helps you build connections”? it was awful. everyone was talking about their promotions and their trips to bali and their amazing side hustles and i just… i just stared at my phone. i felt like i was breathing underwater. like everyone else was speaking a different language. i tried to say something about my new graphic design project but it just came out all mumbled and weird and i think i blushed so hard my face felt like it was burning.

then i left and walked home. it’s only like a twenty minute walk but it felt like hours. i just kept thinking about how everyone else has a life and i don’t. not really. i mean i have my job, i have my apartment, i have… nothing. it’s not even like i *want* anything. i just… exist. like a ghost haunting my own life. 

mom keeps texting me, asking if i’m eating properly and if i’m “happy.” she doesn’t understand. she thinks i'm just stressed about work, but it's not work. it’s… this. this feeling of being utterly and completely alone. like i’m watching everyone else’s lives through a window and i'm trapped behind a pane of glass. i just wanted someone to, like, acknowledge that i’m here. that i’m real. but it’s hard to even make eye contact with people, you know? it feels like they're judging me, or like i'm judging them, and either way it just makes me want to disappear. i just... i just feel so tired.

it’s just… so weird how easily he just left. like i wasn’t even real to him. i keep replaying everything, trying to figure out what i did wrong, but it’s like my brain is full of static. i don’t even know who i am without him.

Seriously, it’s just… a lot. Like, everything’s piling up. Mom needs new meds, the bills are screaming, and I’m pretty sure I just ate ramen for the fourth night in a row. I keep telling myself it’s temporary, that things will get better, but then I look at her, you know? She’s so fragile. And I feel like I’m failing her, failing *us*. It's not fair. I don’t even know how I’m going to pay for the heating this winter. Just… overwhelmed. It’s exhausting trying to hold everything together, and I feel like I'm drowning a little bit each day. I just want a break, honestly. A real one.

Okay, so like, this is officially a mess. Went to the doctor yesterday, just a routine check-up after, you know, everything. And they found something. Nothing *major* they said, but it's… weird. Like, really weird. They want to do more tests, more scans, and I just… I don’t get it. It’s just a stupid thing, right? A little blip. But it feels like everything.

It’s just… it feels like everything is falling apart again. Like I’m already broken, and now this is just adding another crack. Honestly, I keep expecting the whole thing to just shatter. I keep thinking, "This is it, this is the thing that finally proves I'm a disaster."

He wasn't around to help, obviously. Not really. He’d have, like, a perfectly logical explanation, or something. He always did. But he's gone. And now I'm just… here. And it’s terrifying. I keep replaying everything in my head, every stupid fight, every time I said the wrong thing. Did I ruin everything? Is this… is this a sign?

I keep telling myself it’s nothing, just a little blip. But it’s hard. It’s *really* hard to not think that maybe, just maybe, this is the end of the line. I feel like I'm standing on the edge of something, and I don't even know what it is.  Just… a grey space.  And I just want to disappear into it, you know?  Just… stop.

it all just feels…wrong. like the whole world shifted and i’m stuck in a different dimension. i keep expecting him to just…be here. this job was everything, and now he’s gone and so is everything else. i don’t even know who i am anymore, honestly.

okay seriously i don’t even know what’s happening anymore. like, i just lost my job last week and it’s hitting me harder than i thought it would. everyone keeps saying “it’s a fresh start” but it just feels…wrong. i have this huge presentation due on friday and i haven’t even started it. my brain is just…a jumble. i keep thinking about all the things i *should* be doing, all the things i *could* be doing, and it’s just overwhelming. i feel like i’m constantly failing, even before it’s actually happening. i keep telling myself it’s just anxiety, but it feels like…more. like i’m just fundamentally broken, you know? i just want to disappear, honestly. just vanish and not have to deal with any of this. maybe if i don't look at it, it'll just go away.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, this is… a lot. I just moved into this tiny apartment, it’s supposed to be “fresh start” you know? But it just feels… empty. And weird. I keep looking at my stuff, like, everything I own, and it just feels…wrong somehow? Like it doesn't belong here. I don’t even know where to begin, honestly. I kinda panicked when I realized I didn't have any actual friends here yet, just… strangers. Maybe I’m overthinking it? I dunno. It’s just… everything feels so fragile, like if I mess it up even a little, it’ll all just shatter. I keep expecting something bad to happen, I guess? It's just... heavy.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, seriously, I can’t even… like, I keep staring at the ceiling and it’s just… white. It’s always white. Everything feels white, you know? Like bleached. Before, even when things were awful, there was… color, I guess? Now it’s just this vast, empty white. And the sleep, god the sleep is awful. I toss and turn and replay everything a million times, mostly the arguments, but sometimes just… little things he said, things he *didn't* say. It’s like I'm trying to piece together a puzzle with half the pieces missing and the picture is just…wrong. He said I was too much, too intense, too everything. Maybe he was right. I feel like I’m falling apart, like a really complicated, messy sculpture that’s just crumbling into dust. I just want to go back to how things were, even if they were terrible, because at least then I knew *something*.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay so like, this happened and it’s… it’s messing with everything. I was on my usual shift, you know, graveyard, always so quiet except for the hum of the machines and the flickering fluorescent lights, and I just felt… weird. Really dizzy, like the floor was tilting even though it wasn't. And my chest started squeezing, like someone was sitting on it, but not a nice sitting, you know? Like a really heavy, uncomfortable weight. I tried to tell Mark, he’s the guy who usually covers for me, but he just said I probably hadn't slept well and to get some coffee, which is totally helpful, honestly. He doesn't *get* it.

I kept feeling like I was forgetting something important, like a key or a name or… a whole chunk of my life, actually. It’s like my brain is trying to scramble everything up and then throw it away. I went to the bathroom, and everything was blurry, and I actually thought I was going to faint, just like that, right there in the stall. It was awful. And then, after that, I just started crying, just huge, silent sobs. I don’t even know why. It’s just… a lot, you know? Like, everything feels so fragile and like it could just shatter into a million pieces.

I keep thinking about my mom and how she always said I was too sensitive, too much, and now… now it feels like she was right. Maybe this is what it’s like, to just… constantly be on the edge of falling apart. It’s like a low-level hum of anxiety that never really goes away, just vibrates underneath everything. I just want it to stop, I really do. I wish I could just switch it off, like a light, but it’s not a light, it’s… it’s just *there*. And I don't know what to do about it. I just feel so… lost.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Ugh, seriously, I don't even know where to start. Lost my job like, two weeks ago and now this stupid project is due next week. It’s for Professor Eldridge’s class, you know, the one I was already kinda struggling with. I keep staring at the screen and it just…blurs. Like, everything feels blurry. I’m trying to write, but then I just…freeze.

Maybe I should just email him and tell him I can’t do it? But then what will he think? He’s always so intense. I feel like if I fail this, everything is ruined. It’s probably a dumb feeling, right? I just… I don’t know. I’m just a mess, honestly. I think I need to lie down. Idk what to do.

it’s just… too much. everyone wants something, all the time. like i’m a broken record skipping and skipping and no one even cares. i just need five minutes to breathe, five minutes to not feel like i’m drowning again. honestly, i don’t even know who i am anymore.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, so like, this is just… a lot. Little Leo had a fever spike last night, right? Seriously spiked, like, I was up half the night just checking his temperature and wiping his forehead. And the doctor said it could be something serious, a virus or something, and she just kept saying “we need to monitor him closely” which, honestly, just felt like she didn’t really *get* it. Like, I’m completely losing it, I just keep imagining the worst-case scenarios, you know? I keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong, and it’s just… suffocating. He’s so small, so fragile, and I feel like I’m failing him somehow, even though I’m trying so hard. It's like I'm constantly teetering on the edge of panic, and I don’t even know how to breathe properly anymore. I just wish things would just… stop being so overwhelming.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, everything feels…wrong. It’s just, I don’t even know what’s wrong. He said it was “too much” and “different” and I just… I don’t get it. I thought we were good, you know? I mean, we were. Or at least, I *thought* we were. Now, like, I keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out where I messed up, but it's just… a mess. I keep looking at his old stuff – his locker, his room, just little things – and it’s like he’s still *here*. It’s stupid, I know. I’m probably overreacting. Maybe. I just… I don’t want to be alone, but I also don’t want to be with him. Idk. Everything feels so unstable. Like, one minute I'm okay, and the next I just… crumble. It’s exhausting.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay so like seriously, I don't even know who I am anymore. I used to be… I don’t even remember what I used to be like before this little person took over my entire life. it’s not even that I don’t *want* to be me, it’s just… everything feels so different, you know? like a filter’s been applied and I’m looking at everything through this hazy, exhausted lens. and the sleep, oh god the sleep. I haven’t slept properly in, like, six weeks, maybe longer? it’s a blur. I just stare at the ceiling and think about all the things I *should* be doing, all the things I’m failing at, all the things that just feel…wrong.

my husband tries to be nice, he really does, he brings me tea and says “you’re doing great” but it doesn’t feel like he *sees* me. it's like he sees the mom, the person who changes diapers and makes bottles, and not… me. and I keep thinking about how I used to have hobbies, you know? things I actually cared about, things that weren’t just about keeping this tiny human alive. I used to paint, remember? bright, messy colors. Now everything is beige and smells faintly of baby wipes. 

sometimes I just feel like I’m drifting, like a boat without a rudder. and I keep imagining all these different versions of myself, like snapshots from a movie I didn’t cast myself in. one where I’m thriving, one where I’m completely lost, one where… well, it’s hard to even think about. it's just a constant shifting, this feeling that one minute I’m okay, and the next I’m just… crumbling. i keep expecting things to click back into place, like a puzzle, but the pieces just don’t seem to fit. and it’s getting harder and harder to even try to find them, isn't it?

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: I just... I don't even know who I am anymore, like, before the baby, I was *me*, you know? Now it's just this constant cycle of exhaustion and feeling like I'm failing, and everyone keeps telling me to just "sleep when the baby sleeps" but it's not that simple, it just feels like I'm losing myself completely, honestly.

text: okay so like, everything is just… too much, you know? everyone expects so much of me at school and then i have to work after, and i just feel like i’m failing at everything, honestly, like a complete mess and i don’t even know who i am anymore, it’s just a constant, buzzing feeling, ugh.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
it’s… it’s the anniversary. honestly i almost didn’t post anything. just wanted to disappear. it feels like a brand etched onto my skin, you know? like i can’t scrub it off. it’s been a year since… well, you know. since everything. i keep replaying it in my head, like a broken record. not the *what* happened, not really. it’s the *feeling* of it. the way the air just… thickened. like something heavy settled over everything.

i told people it was okay, i moved on, i got a new job, i’m…fine. but i’m not. not really. it’s like there’s this constant undercurrent of…wrongness. like i’m wearing clothes that are a size too small. they feel constricting, itchy, and i just keep pulling at them trying to make them fit.

my sister sent me flowers. beautiful lilies. they were supposed to be comforting. but they just made me feel…sad. like i’m supposed to be grateful for pretty things when everything’s just…off. and my boyfriend… he’s wonderful. really. but i keep pushing him away, i think. not intentionally, i swear. it’s like i’m afraid if i let him get too close, i’ll shatter.

i keep thinking about how everyone else seems to move forward, to just…forget. and i'm stuck here, in this gray space. just existing. is this what it always feels like? this dull ache? this feeling that i’m watching my own life from behind a pane of glass? i just… i just wish it would stop feeling like i’m slowly dissolving. it’s exhausting. so unbelievably exhausting.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
okay so like seriously i haven’t slept properly in weeks i know i should probably do something about it but it’s just… hard. it’s like my brain is just constantly running a marathon or something except the marathon is filled with static and flashing lights and really loud music that i can’t turn off. i keep tossing and turning and then i’ll just lie there staring at the ceiling for hours just… thinking. mostly about how everyone else seems to have it figured out, you know? my brother’s got his internship, my sister’s traveling, even aunt carol’s started pottery classes. and me? i’m back here in my childhood bedroom surrounded by posters i haven’t looked at in ten years and i feel like i’m just… existing.

it’s not that i don’t *want* to be productive or happy or anything. it’s just… everything feels so overwhelming. i try to make plans, like going for a walk or calling a friend, but then i just end up scrolling through social media for hours and feeling even worse. it’s like i’m trapped in a loop of disappointment. and then i start to question everything. like, who am i even? i used to have so many goals, you know? a clear path. now it just feels like a blurry mess. i keep telling myself it’s just the stress of graduation but it feels deeper than that. it feels like… like i’m fundamentally broken or something. i don't even know why i’m writing this, just… needed to get it out somehow i guess. ugh i wish i could just fall asleep.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so this new place is… a lot. Like, everything is just…wrong, I think? I don’t even know why. Maybe it’s just the baby, or me, or both? I just feel like I'm failing at being a mom, and a person, tbh. idk what's happening to me.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: it’s just…everything feels wrong here. like i’m wearing someone else’s clothes and they’re itchy. i keep rearranging my room, trying to make it feel like *mine*, but it never does. i don’t even know why i’m sad, just…a low hum.

Okay, seriously, why does everything feel like it's about to fall apart? Like, I’m fine one minute, and then suddenly I'm convinced everyone hates me and I should just disappear. It's not even about anything specific, you know? Just…a constant low-level panic. My boss seemed nice today, but I spent the whole meeting analyzing if he was judging me. And then I sent a stupid email and immediately regretted it. I just... I don't even know what I'm doing anymore. It's exhausting. Everyone else seems to have it figured out, like they know where they're going, and I'm just… drifting. This feeling, it just sits here, heavy.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this whole week has been... a thing. Dad had this really sudden dizzy spell, you know? Just standing in the kitchen, grabbing a glass of water and then… he just kind of crumpled. I almost didn’t notice at first, honestly. I was trying to get the laundry done, and he was just… there. But then his face went all pale and he started mumbling and his hands were shaking.

It was… scary. The ambulance came, of course. They said it was probably just low blood sugar or something, but they ran tests and everything. They think his heart might be acting up, maybe a little valve problem? I idk, the doctors were talking in all this medical jargon and I just felt completely lost.

I’ve been taking care of him for so long, you know? Since Mom… well, since Mom passed. He just gets more and more fragile, it feels like. Like a delicate house of cards. And I feel like I’m constantly holding it up, trying to keep everything from falling apart. Sometimes I just… I just want to disappear, if that makes sense. Not like, permanently, just… to be somewhere else. Somewhere quiet.

I keep thinking about what he’d say, if he could talk, which he can’t. He’d probably tell me to take care of myself, to not worry so much. But it’s hard not to worry, isn’t it? It's just… so much. Maybe I should call his sister, Sarah? But then I’d feel like I’m burdening her too. Ugh, I’m rambling, aren't I? It’s just… a lot. I just need a nap, probably. A really long one.

it’s just… so much. everyone expects me to be okay, to finish this report by tomorrow. like i’m not completely falling apart inside. it’s like a pressure building and building and i just… i don't know how to breathe properly anymore. please just let me have five minutes.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
okay seriously like i don’t even know what’s happening anymore. this city is…loud. everyone seems to have their lives figured out, you know? like they just *know* where they're going and what they're doing and i’m just…spinning. i got that part-time job at the cafe but it’s not enough. not even close. i keep thinking about how i promised myself i wouldn’t fall back on this, you know? like i was so careful, so determined to build something different. but then the bills started piling up and it felt like the floor just dropped out from under me.

i tried calling mom but she just kept saying “you’re so talented, honey, you’ll figure it out” and it just made me feel worse. like she doesn’t *get* it. she always thinks i’m just being dramatic. i don't even know why i'm upset. i just...am. it’s like a constant low hum of anxiety, you know? a feeling that something’s about to go wrong. i spent all my money on a stupid plant yesterday, just to make my tiny apartment feel less empty. it’s wilting already. it’s probably a sign. everything is a sign, isn't it? maybe i should just pack my bags and go home. but then i'd be stuck. and what if i can’t find another job? what if i just keep failing? i just feel…lost. completely and utterly lost. i keep staring at the ceiling at night and it just feels like it's closing in. i wish i could just stop thinking, but i can’t. it's like my brain is stuck on repeat, playing the same awful song over and over. i just want to disappear, honestly. not like, permanently, but like…fade away a little.

text: Okay, seriously, who even am I anymore? Everything feels…wrong. Like my brain is just buzzing and I can’t even remember what I studied yesterday. I just want to disappear, maybe? It's all just too much, you know?

it's just…everything feels pointless now. the bills are piling up and he said he’d handle it, but he didn't. i keep staring at the empty space where his things used to be, like a ghost. i don’t even know who i am anymore, really. just…lost.

everyone keeps asking how i am like i’m supposed to just be okay after… it’s just so loud in rooms full of people. i feel like i’m watching myself from behind a glass, everyone’s faces blur. i just wanna disappear, honestly.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, okay, deep breaths. But seriously, how am I even going to finish this presentation? It’s like, due tomorrow and it's just... a mess. I started it weeks ago, convinced I was being so clever, so insightful, and now it's just a jumble of half-formed ideas and panicked scribbles. I keep deleting chunks, then rewriting them, then deleting *those* chunks because they don’t feel right, which means everything feels wrong. It’s a vicious cycle, you know? Like I’m trying to build a house out of sand, and the tide’s just constantly coming in to wash it all away.

My boss keeps saying “confident, concise,” but what does that even *mean*? I feel like I’m drowning in details, like if I just keep adding more and more, it'll somehow make it better. It’s ridiculous, right? It’s not going to magically become perfect just because I write another paragraph about market trends. I keep thinking about all the times I've felt like this – like I’m failing, even when I’m trying my hardest. It's exhausting, honestly, this feeling that I’m just… not enough.

And then there’s the pressure, the constant, buzzing pressure to *perform*. Like if I don't nail this presentation, I’ve somehow failed at everything. I just wish I could just... stop. Just breathe and not think about it. But I can’t, can I? It’s like my brain is stuck on repeat, playing the same awful song over and over again. I keep staring at the screen and just... feeling this heavy weight in my chest. It’s just… so much. I just want it to be over, you know? Just *done*. Maybe I should just email him and say I’m sick. That feels... safer, somehow. But then what? It's always something, isn't it?

okay so i just… i just left that party. like, the one sarah dragged me to. and honestly i don’t even know why i went. she said it would be ‘fun’ and you know, like, people and music and dancing. it wasn’t. it was just… a lot. a *lot* of people. everyone was laughing at something and i didn’t get it. honestly i didn’t get anything. and they were all talking so fast, like they were trying to fill the space with noise, you know? and i felt like i was failing at it too, like if i stopped talking, everyone would notice i wasn’t… like, interesting.

i kept trying to join conversations but they just… drifted away. it’s like i’m a ghost, you know? just kind of there, but not really. and then there was that guy, he kept looking at me and i swear he was smiling, but it felt… invasive. like he was trying to read me or something. i started to sweat and my hands were shaking so bad i almost spilled my drink. i just wanted to disappear.

i kept thinking about how stupid i looked, how awkward i was, how everyone probably thought i was weird. and then i just started to feel this overwhelming panic, like i needed to get out. i told sarah i had to go, just said i wasn’t feeling well, and i practically ran. i’m sitting here now, wrapped in my blanket, and i just feel… empty. like i’m a shell. i don't even know who i am anymore, like i’m just… shifting. and it’s exhausting. it’s always exhausting. i just wish i could be normal, you know? just… normal.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s the anniversary. feels like yesterday and a lifetime ago all at once. just… a blur of yelling and then nothing. i keep expecting him to call, like maybe he’ll regret it. but he won’t. it’s just… heavy.

Everyone keeps saying “pull yourself together” like it’s that easy. Like I just flip a switch and suddenly have, like, a trust fund or something. It’s not fair. My mom’s working two jobs and still we’re broke. I just… I don't get it. It’s not like I’m *asking* for things. I just… I want to feel normal, you know? Like I can buy a stupid hoodie or something without it being a monumental problem. It feels like everyone else has it figured out, and I’m just… stuck. This stupid feeling that I’m failing, even though I’m trying. It just won’t go away. And honestly, sometimes it feels like no one even cares.

okay seriously this is a mess i just looked at my bank account again and it’s… gone. like completely evaporated. i know i shouldn’t have spent so much on that stupid pottery class but it just felt… necessary you know? like a way to actually *feel* something besides just…existing. my boss didn't even seem to notice when i was late today, which is probably good but also makes me feel even more useless. everyone else seems to have it together, like they're always moving forward and i'm just stuck. i keep telling myself it’s temporary, that i’ll figure it out, but then i just start spiraling again. i don’t even want to talk to anyone, it just makes me feel worse. this is probably just me being dramatic but… i feel like i'm falling apart and nobody cares.

Seriously, why does everyone always need something from me? Like, I’m already running on fumes here, working the graveyard shift and barely keeping it together. Mom called again, yelling about dinner – again. Said I never help, that I’m selfish and just “don’t care.” Doesn’t she *see* I’m exhausted? It’s not that I don’t care, it's just... it feels like everything I do is wrong. Dad just sighed when I answered, like I was a broken record. He didn’t say anything, but you could just *feel* the disappointment. It’s always something. Always. My brother texted asking for money for "stuff." Stuff. Like I have a spare fortune lying around. I told him no, obviously. And now I'm pretty sure he's gonna be mad. Honestly, I just want to disappear. Just fade into the wallpaper, you know? It's like I'm constantly juggling a million things and they’re all about to fall. I keep thinking about just... not answering the phone. Just letting it ring and ring until it’s a useless piece of plastic. It wouldn’t be *my* fault, would it? They just want something, always. I don’t even know who I am anymore, like I'm just a shell going through the motions. It’s exhausting. Really exhausting. I just wish they’d just... leave me alone.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay but like seriously, this history essay is just… suffocating me, it’s like a giant weight pressing down and I keep starting it then deleting everything because it feels completely wrong and pointless, and then I just get this awful feeling that I’m going to fail and everyone will think I’m stupid and I just… I don’t even know anymore.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text:
Okay seriously, this is just… a mess. Moved here six months ago, thought it would be *amazing*. Fresh start, you know? Find myself. Turns out, finding myself is way harder when you’re staring at a ceiling and the landlord just texted about the rent. Again. Like, I got that part-time job at the bookstore, right? Barely makes enough to cover groceries, let alone anything else. I keep telling myself it’s temporary, that things will get better. But then I look at my bank account and it's just…gone. Poof. Like it vanished.

Everyone back home is posting about their lives, their promotions, their vacations. It’s not that I’m jealous, exactly. More like… I feel like I’m watching a movie about someone else’s life. And I’m stuck on the loading screen. I tried calling my mom, but she just kept saying “you’re so strong, honey, you’ll figure it out.” Like that's helpful. Like *that* actually solves anything. She doesn’t get it. None of them do. 

I just… I don't even know who I am anymore. I used to have plans, you know? Big ones. Now I just… exist. I keep buying these little things, stupid things, just to feel *something*. A sparkly pen, a weird shaped mug, a tiny plastic dinosaur. It’s pathetic, I know. But it’s better than just… nothing. I keep thinking about going back, just… disappearing. Not like, permanently. Just… back to where things were simpler, even if they weren’t happy. Is that stupid? Probably. I just… I don’t know what to do. Just feel so lost.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s… it’s the day. october tenth. feels like yesterday and a million years ago all at once. i keep scrolling through old photos with elara, you know? she’s doing okay i think, at least she’s posting. happy things. a new pottery class, hiking, stuff. i don’t get it. it just feels… wrong. like i’m supposed to feel something, anything, but it’s just… blank. everything reminds me. the coffee shop we used to go to, the stupid little park bench, the way the leaves smelled that autumn. i keep telling myself it’ll get better, that i’ll find something new, something better. but it’s hard. really hard. like i’m trying to build a house on quicksand. and i just… i just don’t know what to do with all this space now.

they just don’t get it do they? like i’m failing at being a mom and a wife all at once. everything’s a fight and i just want to disappear. this little one is supposed to bring us closer but it feels like it’s pushing us all apart. i’m so tired.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: okay like seriously i don’t even know who i am anymore. this new place is just…wrong. it feels like everyone already knows each other and i’m just a ghost. my roommate keeps smiling at me like i’m some kind of joke. i keep rearranging my stuff, trying to make it feel like *my* space but it just looks messy and sad. i keep thinking about going back home, back to my old room, back to everything being normal. but then i remember the exams, the pressure, and i just…freeze. it's like i'm trying to build a house out of sand and it's just collapsing. i just want to disappear, i guess. or maybe just scream. i don't even know what i'm feeling half the time. this is awful.

okay so like seriously i don't understand why everyone is so...loud. it's just a coffee shop, right? but it feels like everyone's shouting at each other and i just want to disappear. i went to see sarah last night, you know, sarah from work, and it was awful. she kept talking about her new promotion and her amazing weekend and i just... i couldn't. i kept nodding and saying 'that's great' but inside i was just feeling like a complete failure. like i’m not even interesting to talk to anymore. she asked me about my job search and i just mumbled something about 'things' and she looked at me like i was avoiding her.

and then i started spiraling, you know? thinking about how everyone else has their lives figured out and i’m just… this. a mess. i keep rehearsing what i’ll say next time, trying to come up with a good story, but it’s impossible. i just feel like i’m always saying the wrong thing, doing the wrong thing. i keep imagining people judging me, really judging me, and it's just... suffocating. i just wanted to go home. i really did. i felt like i was wearing a mask and it was cracking and cracking and i just wanted to rip it off and run away. it’s like i'm watching myself from the outside, this awkward, useless person just shuffling around, trying to blend in but failing miserably. i feel so… exposed. honestly i just wish i could just be invisible for five minutes. maybe then i could breathe. i don’t even know why i'm telling you this, i just needed to get it out. it feels like a weight off my chest, a little bit, anyway.
