attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: Just... everyone here seems to have a plan. Like they know where they're going. I don’t. It’s loud and bright and I just feel…lost. Like a puzzle piece that doesn’t fit anywhere.

Okay, seriously. Tonight was… a lot. Like, everyone's just… louder, you know? Like they’re *trying* to be friendly, which is nice, I guess, but it’s just so much. I went to that thing at the diner – the “community night” – and it was like a zoo. Lots of smiling, lots of small talk about the weather. The weather! Like that's *everything*. I just wanted to disappear.

And Sarah kept asking me about my new job. She's so… enthusiastic. It's exhausting. I told her it’s okay, I’m fine, but honestly, it feels like I’m wearing a suit that’s three sizes too small. Everything feels tight and wrong. I kept fidgeting with my napkin, just pushing it around and around. I felt this weird heat rising, like I was about to burst.

Then Mark – Mark, who always looks like he’s judging you – he said something about how I “should be more outgoing.” Like, *duh*. Like I’m deliberately trying to be miserable. It's not that I *want* to be miserable, it’s just… everything feels so… fragile. Like one wrong word, one awkward glance, and it all just… collapses. I just wanted to go home. Just go home and not have to pretend.

I don’t get why people need to be *around* people. It’s like… a constant drain. I just feel… empty afterwards. Like I’ve given a little piece of myself away and it’s not coming back.  I should probably just stay in tomorrow. Just… stay in.  It's easier, you know? Much easier.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, so like, this is officially the worst thing ever. Moving to this tiny apartment, it’s…it’s just not working. It’s supposed to be a fresh start, right? A chance to finally, like, *do* something with my life after, you know, everything. But it just feels…heavy. Everyone keeps saying “new chapter,” and “excited for the future,” and I just want to scream. It's like the air here smells of disappointment and old furniture and my mom’s sadness, and it’s just clinging to everything. I unpacked everything yesterday, which was a monumental effort, I swear, and now it just looks…wrong. Like, all my stuff, my carefully curated little world, doesn’t belong here.

And then there’s Liam. He’s…nice, I guess. He actually asked me to coffee, which is, like, a huge deal, right? But I can't even look at him without feeling this overwhelming panic, this feeling that I’m going to say the wrong thing and ruin everything. Like I always do. It's like I'm constantly anticipating some massive, catastrophic failure, and I spend the whole time just trying to avoid it, which is, like, impossible.  I keep imagining him judging me, seeing through all the layers of…whatever I’m pretending to be.  Maybe I should just pack it all back up.  Maybe I'm just not cut out for this, for *anything*, really.  I just... I feel so lost, you know? Like a little boat drifting in a storm with no land in sight.  Just…a mess.  Seriously, a huge, gigantic mess.  I keep expecting something bad to happen, something awful, and it’s exhausting.  I just want to go home, I really do.  But Mom would be so disappointed…and then I’d be *that* person, the one who can’t even manage a tiny apartment.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a really weird thing to post, but I just… I don’t even know what’s happening to me anymore. Everything feels so… amplified, you know? Like, the smallest thing can just, I don't know, *explode* into this huge, overwhelming feeling. I just finished this ridiculous project for my old professor – it’s like he’s actively trying to make my life harder, honestly – and I feel this insane pressure, but also this weird sense of… failure, even though I think it’s actually pretty good? Maybe. I don't know.

And work... ugh. They keep saying “adaptability” and “taking initiative” and it’s just… a lot. I feel like I’m constantly failing to meet their expectations, even when I'm putting in the hours. It's not that I *don't* want to do well, it's just… it’s exhausting. Like, I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing, all the people I should be pleasing, and it's just a constant, buzzing anxiety.

Mom keeps asking if I'm okay, which is sweet, but it also makes me feel like I'm doing something wrong just by *feeling* this way. She keeps saying “you’re just stressed,” but it's not *just* stressed, is it? It feels… different. Like the floor is shifting under my feet and I’m desperately trying to hold onto something solid, but there’s nothing there. I just… I keep cycling through these moods, you know? Happy one minute, completely numb the next. I think I might be losing my grip on, like, who I am. Maybe I should just call Dad, but then he’ll just tell me to "pull myself together," and that's… not helpful. I just… I need to breathe, I guess. Or maybe not. I don’t even know anymore.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a really bad time to be rambling, but I just… I don't know. Moving here was supposed to be this fresh start, right? A clean slate. Everyone said it would be amazing. And it’s… fine. I guess. But “fine” feels like the smallest, most inadequate word ever. I’m pretty sure I’m just existing, you know? Like a ghost in my own apartment.

It's just...everything is tight. Seriously tight. The landlord keeps sending these passive aggressive texts about the rent, and I'm already, like, three weeks behind. I told him I'd have it by Friday, and I *swear* I would have had it, but then the job application I was pinning everything on...it didn’t go through. They said something about “not a good fit,” which is just… brutal, honestly. Like I didn't even try.

I keep thinking about calling my mom, but then I feel like even that’s… showing weakness, I think? Like if I admit I’m struggling, then it’s *confirmed* that I’m failing. And I don't want to fail. I just... I don’t want to be a burden. Maybe I should just, like, sell some stuff? I have this old guitar, and a bunch of art supplies, but who even buys that stuff? It feels so silly to even think about it.

I keep replaying everything in my head, you know? Every decision. Every word. Did I say the wrong thing? Did I do something to screw this all up? It’s exhausting. I just want to disappear, honestly. Not like, actually disappear, but just… fade away into the background. Maybe if I just didn’t *do* anything, then maybe things would magically fix themselves. I don't know. It's just... a lot. I just... idk. I’m probably overthinking everything, right?

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
it’s… it’s the anniversary. a year. feels like a lifetime ago and like yesterday all at once, you know? i keep replaying it in my head, like a broken record stuck on that awful, screeching part. it wasn’t supposed to happen like that, obviously, everyone keeps saying that, but it *did*. and now… now i just feel… hollow. like someone scooped out all the good bits and left this weird, echoing space behind. i tried to do something nice, i really did – ordered takeout, a stupid bouquet of sunflowers because they remind me of her smile, even though she’s… well, she’s not here. and it felt completely pointless. like throwing confetti at a funeral or something.

everyone keeps saying “time heals all wounds,” but it’s not working, is it? it’s just… this dull ache. a constant thrumming under everything. i keep thinking about all the things we *didn’t* do, all the stupid little moments we missed because i was always so caught up in my own head, worrying about something, anything. and now it’s too late. i scroll through old photos, just staring at them, trying to remember what it felt like to actually *be* happy. it's like trying to grasp smoke. it just slips through my fingers. i don’t even know why i’m writing this, honestly. just… a need to spill it out into the void, i guess. like maybe if i say it loud enough, it will somehow… change something. or maybe it just won't. and that’s the really terrifying part, isn't it? that nothing will. i feel like i’m constantly watching myself from a distance, observing this person – this *me* – who’s slowly fading away.

okay seriously i don't even know what's happening anymore. everything was so perfect just a few weeks ago, you know? like, we were building this little life together, this tiny human and us... and now it’s just gone. just… vanished. i keep staring at baby leo and it feels weird, like i’m watching a movie about someone else’s family. i told him i needed space, i really did, but he just… left. just took everything and said he couldn’t handle it. i keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out where i messed up, if i could have done something different. it's like i'm constantly falling apart and putting myself back together, but it's always a little bit broken. i just want things to be normal again. is that too much to ask? feeling so lost and confused and… empty.

text: it’s just… weird, you know? everyone else seems to have a plan, a direction, like they’re all on a train and i’m just standing on the platform watching it pull away and it feels like i’m fading into the grey, just… disappearing, and i don’t even know why anymore.

okay seriously this new city feels…wrong. like everyone’s judging me even though i haven’t done anything. i keep rearranging my stuff, trying to make it feel like *my* space, but it just keeps slipping away. i don’t even know if i can do this.

it’s just… everything feels wrong. like i’m wearing someone else’s clothes and it doesn’t fit. i don’t even know who i am anymore, just floating. the silence is deafening, you know? i keep checking my phone, waiting for something, anything.

it’s just… quiet. always quiet. everyone’s asleep or gone or whatever. i keep staring at the security camera feed, watching the hallways empty. it’s like i’m the only one awake, the only one really *here*. i don’t even know if anyone remembers i’m here. like, i just show up, punch in, and disappear again. it’s stupid, right? feeling this way. it’s like… a constant hum of nothingness. sometimes i try to think of something, anything, to fill it up, but it just bounces around in my head. a broken record. i wish i could just… switch it off. maybe if i just stayed here all night, no one would notice. it’s probably pathetic, isn’t it? just a lonely guy watching blank walls.

it’s just… everything feels wrong now isn’t it? like the whole world was built around him and when he took it all away, the ground just disappeared. i keep expecting him to call, you know? just a stupid text saying “hey” or “thinking of you” but it’s never that. and i keep replaying everything, every fight, every little thing we said, trying to figure out where i messed up, what i could have done differently. it’s exhausting.

i haven't really left the house in days. just… sitting. staring at the walls. i keep folding and unfolding the towels, making little piles, then just throwing them back down. it’s a stupid, pointless thing to do but it’s something to *do*. i tried to go for a walk yesterday, but i got halfway and just… froze. felt like everyone was staring at me, judging me. like i was wearing a neon sign that said “broken.”

mom keeps texting, asking if i'm eating, if i'm okay. she just doesn't get it. she says "you’ll find someone new," like it’s a solution. it's not. it's not even a thought. i don't *want* someone new. i just want… i don’t even know what i want. it’s like if i had a map, it would just keep changing, leading me to different places, none of them right. this apartment feels huge now, empty. like a stage set after the play is over. and i'm just… standing here. waiting for something to happen, but nothing ever does. it’s just quiet. too quiet. and it scares me. seriously scares me. i feel… hollow, i guess. like a balloon that’s slowly deflating.

text: Okay, seriously, I don’t even know what’s happening anymore. It’s like my brain just… fractured. Everything feels so heavy, you know? Like I’m carrying this giant, invisible weight and I can’t even remember what it’s *for*. My ex, Liam… he’s being… well, he’s being Liam. Always making things complicated, always needing to be the center of attention, even though he’s basically just yelling at me about deadlines at work. I’m drowning in spreadsheets and presentations and it’s all just… pointless. I’m trying to keep my head above water, honestly, just showing up, doing what I’m told, but it’s exhausting. And then Professor Davies keeps emailing me about my thesis – the one I've been working on for *months* – and he’s just… critical. Not constructive, just… critical. Like I’m not even trying. It's like he sees right through me, you know? Like I’m a complete fraud. I keep thinking about just… disappearing, just walking away from everything. Not like, permanently, but just… far enough that he can’t bother me anymore. Maybe a small town somewhere, where nobody knows me and I can just… exist. It feels silly, writing this down, like I’m making a huge deal out of nothing, but it’s just... so much. I feel like a broken record, repeating the same awful thoughts over and over. I just want it to stop. I just want to feel… stable, I guess. Is that too much to ask? I don't even know who I am anymore, really. Like a ghost in my own life, just going through the motions. Ugh. I need coffee. A lot of coffee.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay so like, this city is… overwhelming. i thought moving here would be *amazing*, you know? a fresh start, a blank slate, finally escaping everything. but it’s just… loud. and everyone seems to know each other already, like they’ve been friends forever and i’m just this weird, awkward new addition to their perfectly curated little world. i keep trying to force myself to say hi to people in the coffee shop, but then i just freeze up and start rambling about the rain or something stupid and they give me this pitying look, like i’m some lost puppy.

it’s not even the people themselves, it’s just… the *feeling*. like i don’t belong. like i’m constantly wearing a costume, and the costume is itchy and doesn't quite fit. i bought a plant – a really sad, droopy fern – and i’m trying to take care of it because i don’t want to be a failure at even the simplest things. but then i look at it and think, “why are you even trying? you’re just going to die anyway.” it's silly, i know, but that thought keeps popping up.

i keep replaying conversations in my head, agonizing over every word, every gesture. did i say the wrong thing? did I look like an idiot? i scroll through my old friends’ pictures, and they all look so… settled. like they have their lives figured out. and i just feel this immense, aching loneliness, like i’m watching them from behind a glass wall. i keep telling myself it’s just temporary, that i’ll adjust, that i’ll find my people, but… what if I don’t? what if this is just… this? a perpetual state of feeling like a ghost in my own life? i just... i don’t even know what i’m doing anymore.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: It’s… it’s the anniversary today. Of everything. I don’t even know why I’m posting this, idk. Just feels like… a weight. Like if I don’t say something, it’ll just disappear, but maybe that’s a good thing, maybe. Just a weird feeling, I guess.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is really embarrassing to even write down, but… I’m spiraling. Seriously. I thought I was, like, doing okay, you know? Landing the marketing gig at StellarTech was a big deal, and I’ve been trying to be responsible, paying bills, stuff. But then… everything just feels… precarious. I keep thinking about the rent, and it’s just looming over me. I know I *should* be budgeting more, and I’ve downloaded like, five different budgeting apps, but I just… don’t stick to them. It’s like my brain just shuts down when I even *think* about spreadsheets.

And it's not just the money, it’s… everything feels fragile. Like one bad week at work, one slightly awkward conversation, and the whole thing just crumbles. I keep picturing myself living out of my car, which is, like, a ridiculous thought, I know, but it’s there. Maybe I’m being dramatic, totally. My mom keeps saying I need to “just focus” and “stop worrying so much,” but it’s not that simple, right? Like, how do you *not* worry when you feel like you're constantly on the verge of falling apart?

I had this weird dream last night – I was trying to build a sandcastle, but the waves kept washing it away, and I couldn’t even stop trying. It felt… symbolic, I guess. I don't even know. I’m just so tired of feeling this way. Maybe I should just call Sarah, but she’s always so… practical. I don’t want to burden her with this. I just… idk. I just feel so lost and like I'm failing at being an adult. It’s exhausting. Ugh.

Ugh, another night staring at the ceiling. It’s like my brain just…loops. Used to have a job, a routine, something to *be*. Now it’s just this empty feeling and the walls closing in. I don't even know who I am without it, honestly.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: Just...everything feels wrong. Like I'm wearing someone else's clothes and they're itchy and too small. This city is loud, everyone's moving so fast and i feel like i'm just stuck. I keep rearranging my furniture, trying to make this apartment feel like *mine*, but it’s no use. It just looks messy, i guess.  I keep thinking about how stupid it was to move here alone. Everyone I know is back home, and they don’t get it. Like, they don’t get *me*.  It's just...a constant, dull ache. Like something’s missing.  I don’t even know what it is anymore. Just...this.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: everyone else seems to be crushing it, you know? like they actually understand the material. i just feel…lost. a huge blob of nothingness in this library. it’s stupid, i guess, but i just want someone to, like, *see* me right now.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: Okay, seriously, this city is just…wrong. Like, everything feels off-kilter. I thought maybe being somewhere new would help, you know? A fresh start. But it’s just…more lonely. Everyone looks like they have a plan, a life. I don’t. I just keep rearranging my furniture, trying to make this tiny apartment feel like *something*. It’s stupid, I know. Just a bunch of boxes and a half-finished painting. I keep wondering if I made a mistake. If I even *am* doing things right. It’s like… a weird buzzing in my head, a constant feeling that I’m forgetting something important. Or maybe everything.

mom just yelled again. it's always something, always. like i'm the reason everything's falling apart. i just want to disappear, you know? just fade away and not have to deal with any of it. it’s exhausting being…me.

it's just… so much. this report is going to ruin me. i keep switching between wanting to finish it and just screaming. who am i even trying to be right now? everything feels wrong and i’m pretty sure i haven't slept in days. maybe i’ll just disappear if it’s all done.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so this is weird. I just… I don’t even know *why* I’m typing this, honestly. Like, it just kind of spilled out. Everything feels… shaky. Like if I look too hard at one thing, it’ll just, you know, disappear. Or maybe it won’t disappear, but it’ll change completely. I keep thinking about that presentation at work tomorrow, and it’s not that it’s bad, it’s just… terrifying. What if I completely blank? What if everyone sees how much I don’t know? I keep telling myself it’s just anxiety, but it feels… bigger than that. I dunno. Maybe I’m overthinking it. I just need to breathe, I guess. Just… breathe. It’s probably nothing. Probably.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: they just don’t get it do they? like, i’m trying so hard to be normal again, to be the one they want, but it’s exhausting. mom keeps saying i’m being dramatic, and dad just sighs. it’s like i’m a bad reflection of everything they hoped for.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay so like, it’s been… a year. a whole freaking year since, you know… everything. and i keep scrolling through my phone and it’s just… a blur of happy pictures, people laughing, going on trips, and i just feel this huge, gaping hole. it’s not even sad, not really. it’s more like… a wrongness. like a record skipping, you know? the same awful part repeating over and over and over. i tried to do something, i actually went to the mall with Liam – he’s really nice, he’s just… okay. He didn't seem to notice anything, which is probably good, honestly. But it felt so forced, like I was trying to build something out of sand.

Mom keeps saying “time heals all wounds,” and i know she means well, she’s just… Mom. But it doesn't feel like healing. It feels like… just settling. Like I’m supposed to just accept that it happened and move on, but a part of me is still stuck right there. replaying it, over and over. i keep having these little flashes, little snippets of… it. not the actual *thing*, but the feeling. the intense, suffocating feeling.

And everyone else seems to be moving on, like it never even happened. Like it was just a bad dream. Which is probably why i feel like a fraud, you know? Like i'm pretending to be okay when i’m not. i keep thinking about writing a letter, a really long one, to… well, to nobody really. just to get it out of my system. it’s stupid, i know, but it feels… important. like if i don't say it, it'll just stay bottled up and explode eventually.  i just… I don’t know what I’m supposed to *do* with all this. it’s exhausting. honestly, sometimes i just wish i could just… disappear for a little while. not like, permanently, but just… vanish.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, so like, I’m just… swirling. I moved here a month ago, right? To Brightwood. Everyone says it’s charming, all cobblestone streets and little cafes, but it just feels… wrong. Like I’m wearing someone else’s clothes. I keep expecting to hear my mom’s voice, you know? Just, like, a random “Did you take your vitamins?” and it’s just… silent. Which is, like, terrifyingly quiet. I tried to call her, but I couldn’t. Just froze. My hands were shaking so bad I couldn’t even dial. It's stupid, I know, but it felt like if I actually *said* I was here, it would… unravel everything.

And the sleep. Oh god, the sleep. I’m up until three, staring at the ceiling, replaying every stupid thing I’ve ever said, every awkward moment. Then I try to sleep and I’m just… haunted by these flashes. Not nightmares, exactly, more like… fragments. Like I’m watching a movie I never agreed to be in. Last night I saw a woman with red shoes, just standing in the rain, and I felt this overwhelming sadness for her, like she’d lost something incredibly important. It wasn't *my* sadness, I swear, but it just… consumed me.

I keep telling myself it's the stress of the move, the loneliness, the fact that I haven’t really connected with anyone. But it feels bigger than that. Like there’s something else underneath, something… tangled. Like a ball of yarn I can't quite get my hands on.  I keep thinking about rearranging all my furniture, just to change things up, to feel *something*, but then I get overwhelmed and just end up crying. It’s exhausting.  Just… exhausting.  I wish I could just… be okay. Is that too much to ask?

it’s just… everything feels so wrong. like they don't even *see* me anymore. my mom keeps saying i’m being dramatic, that i’m overreacting about everything. but it’s not dramatic, it’s just… a constant pressure, you know? like i’m walking on eggshells all the time. dad just sighs and looks away when i try to talk about it. he used to listen, i think.

i don’t even know who i am anymore. one minute i’m fine, the next i’m just… crumbling. i keep changing my mind about everything, about what i want, about who i am. it’s exhausting. i just want to disappear sometimes, just vanish and not have to deal with any of this. i guess i’m just tired of feeling like nobody gets it. like i’m a ghost in my own house. ugh.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
it’s been three years. three years since… well, you know. everyone keeps saying time heals all wounds, but it just feels like a jagged, rusty nail stuck in my gut. tonight’s the anniversary. stupidly, i set a reminder. like i need to be reminded of this mess.

my girlfriend, Lyra, she’s… nice. she brings me coffee and doesn’t ask too many questions. which is probably for the best. i don’t really *do* nice. i just… exist.  I keep replaying it all in my head, over and over. the way the rain smelled, her laugh, the stupid song on the radio. it’s a loop.  I feel like i’m watching a movie of my own life, and i can’t change the ending.  Just… numb.  Just… quiet.

Seriously, how am I supposed to just…not? Like, everyone else seems to be okay with rent being late, or that stupid car payment. They don't get it. It’s not like I *want* to mess up. It’s just…everything feels so…fragile. If I spend my paycheck on groceries, then I’m screwed next month. And if I don’t spend anything, I’m starving. It’s a loop. I keep thinking about just…not answering the phone. Or replying to emails. Just disappearing, I guess. It’s pathetic, right? Like, I know it is. Just…ugh. This constant worry is eating me alive. I feel like a failure, even though I’m working so hard.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
okay seriously i haven’t slept properly in like weeks i know i should be, like, moving on or something getting a job or whatever but it’s just…impossible. it’s like my brain is a broken record skipping over the same awful thoughts. last night i was staring at the ceiling for hours just replaying everything from freshman year like every stupid argument with my roommate or that one time i bombed that presentation. and then it just spiraled. i started thinking about all the things i *didn’t* do, all the people i *should* have reached out to, all the chances i missed. it’s not even like i’m angry, mostly just…heavy. like a weight in my chest that won’t go away.

mom keeps asking if i’m “okay” which is the worst because it feels like she’s trying to put a label on it and i don’t even know what it is. she says i need to ‘get out more’ but going outside feels…wrong. like everything is too bright, too loud, too *much*. i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. i tried reading but i kept getting distracted, just thinking about how pointless it all is. like what's the point of reading a book when my own life feels like a mess? i keep telling myself i’ll be fine tomorrow, that i’ll just try to sleep a little earlier, but it’s always the same. this feeling just…sticks. it’s exhausting. i feel like i’m watching myself from a distance, a ghost in my own life. is this what it’s always going to be like? just…this hollow feeling? i just wish i could turn it off. just for a little while.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, so like, this is… a lot. I don’t even know where to start, honestly. I just… woke up and everything felt… off. Like, not wrong, just… fundamentally different. Before, when I was just me, you know? Before Leo. It was… manageable. I could kinda keep the pieces together, even if they were wobbly. Now? It’s like I’m constantly trying to hold onto something that’s slipping through my fingers. He’s the cutest little thing, obviously, the best thing, but he also... he just pulls everything out of me, doesn’t he? Like a little sponge, but a really demanding one. 

I keep thinking about how my mom used to say I was “sensitive.” Sensitive! Like that’s a weakness. Now I just feel… overwhelmed. Everything is just *so* intense. The light, the sounds, people's faces, even just breathing. It's like a pressure building behind my eyes, and I just want to curl up and disappear. I tried to read to Leo this morning, and I just… broke down. Not crying, exactly, more like just… collapsing. He didn’t even notice, thankfully, he was just gurgling happily, but *I* noticed. I noticed how much I felt, how much I just… *was*.

And then I started wondering about everything else, you know? About who I *am* when Leo isn’t here, when he's sleeping. Is that even a question? I feel like I’m losing myself in him, and it’s terrifying. It's not like I don't love him, I really do, with every fiber of my being, it’s just… is this *enough*? Am I supposed to be this person, this exhausted, slightly frantic, deeply anxious person who just wants to protect her baby, and completely forget who she was before? God, this is exhausting. I just need… a quiet corner. And maybe a really, really big cup of tea.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably going to sound completely crazy, but I just… I don’t even know what’s happening anymore. It's been a *week* of just… everything. My sister, Clara, she’s been on this absolute rampage about Mom’s finances. Like, she’s saying we’re not doing enough, that we’re letting her live in squalor, and honestly, it’s just… cutting. I know she means well, I *think* she does, but the way she says it… it feels like she’s judging me, like she's saying I’m a bad daughter, a bad caregiver.

And then my brother, Ben, he just… avoids everything. He barely talks to Mom, and when he does, it’s just grunts and sighs. He used to be so involved, you know? Always volunteering, always checking in. Now he just… disappears. I feel like I’m carrying the weight of *everything*, and it’s crushing me. I’m trying to be patient, I really am. I’m trying to mediate, to explain things, but it's like talking to a wall.

Mom's confused, of course. She keeps asking who’s upset, if we’re fighting, and I just… I just say everything’s fine. But it’s not fine. It’s a mess. I feel like I'm constantly walking on eggshells, terrified of saying or doing the wrong thing. Maybe I should just… leave. Just pack a bag and go. But then Mom would be devastated, and Ben… I don't even want to think about what Ben would do. I idk, I just need a break. A real break. Maybe a long drive. Somewhere quiet. God, this is exhausting. It’s like I'm perpetually on edge, waiting for the next argument, the next wave of disappointment. I just wish they'd just… talk to me like a grown-up, not like I’m a child. Or maybe I'm the child in this whole thing. It's so confusing.

Seriously messed up. Just got the call. They’re letting me go. Like, completely cutting me off. I don't even get it. I was good at my job, I think. Or maybe I just thought I was. Everyone always says, "You'll find something better," but it just feels… heavy. My bank account is screaming already, and I haven’t even started looking. It's stupid, I know. Just... a huge, stupid mess. I feel like I’m failing at everything. Like I’m just… not enough.  I keep replaying conversations in my head, wondering what I did wrong, what I could have said.  It’s exhausting.  Just… ugh.

okay seriously, this is just… a lot. like, I thought having a baby was hard. I *thought* sleep was a thing. I was so wrong. Everyone keeps saying “you’ll adjust,” “it gets easier,” blah blah blah. It doesn’t feel easier. It feels like I’m drowning in tiny socks and melted Cheerios. And now this deadline for my thesis? It’s looming. Like, seriously, it’s a giant, angry monster in the corner of my brain.

I keep staring at the screen, trying to write, but all I see is Leo. He’s just… there. Always. And I feel like I’m failing at everything. Like I’m failing as a mom, failing as a student, failing at being a *person*. I keep cleaning up messes he makes, changing his diapers, singing him ridiculous songs, and I just feel… empty. It’s not that I don’t love him, okay? I do. I really do. But it’s like… a part of me is just gone. Like a little piece of my brain decided to take a permanent vacation.

I tried to talk to Mark about it tonight, but he just said “you’re stressed.” Seriously? That’s it? Like I’m just stressed. It’s more than that. It’s like… I don’t even know who I am anymore. I used to have goals, plans, a sense of self. Now I just exist, trying to keep a tiny human alive. God, I wish I could just disappear for a few hours. Just… vanish. And then maybe, just maybe, I could actually get something done. Or maybe not. Honestly, I don't even care anymore. Just… Leo. Always Leo.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, this deadline is just... crushing me. Like, I don't even know *how* to finish this report. It feels so important, but also completely pointless. Maybe if I just keep staring at the screen, it’ll magically write itself? idk. I just feel... fragile, you know?

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: everything feels so wrong like a record skipping. he just...left. i keep replaying it in my head, trying to figure out what i did wrong, but it doesn't even matter does it? just a mess, a huge, messy, pointless mess.

Seriously, they don’t *get* it. Like, they think I’m overreacting about Mom’s…everything. It’s not overreacting. It’s just…constant. She calls me twenty times a day, wants to know where she’s going, if I’m okay, if I’m eating. And then she criticizes what I’m eating. And the laundry. And the way I’m decorating. It’s exhausting. Dad just sits there, nodding, pretending to understand. He never says anything to *her*, though. Just me. I feel like I’m drowning in her needs, and no one’s throwing me a lifeline. I just… I just wish things could just be quiet for five minutes. Is that too much to ask?

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
okay seriously i don’t even know what’s happening to me anymore. it’s like… i wake up and everyone else is just… fine. like they have their lives figured out, you know? and i’m just… here. back in my parents’ basement staring at the ceiling. it’s been six months since graduation and i haven't even applied for a single job. i keep telling myself it’s okay to just breathe, to just wait, but it’s not. it’s not okay. it feels like i’m watching my whole future just… drift away.

mom keeps asking if i’m “seeing someone” and i just… i don’t even want to talk about it. it's stupid, i know, but it just feels like she doesn't understand. she just says “you’ll find someone” like it’s a simple problem. it’s not simple. it’s like… like i’m constantly changing, and i don't even recognize myself anymore. yesterday i spent three hours rearranging my sock drawer, just because it felt… wrong. like everything was out of place and i needed to fix it. then i cried. just… cried. and then i felt guilty for crying, and then i wanted to fix *that* too.

i just wish i could feel something real, something solid. not this constant churning, this feeling like i’m about to fall apart. everyone else seems to have anchors, you know? and i’m just… floating. it's so lonely. like i'm the only one who feels this way. i don’t even want to answer calls, or text. it's too much. i just want to disappear sometimes, maybe then no one would notice i'm not okay. or maybe i’m just being dramatic. i don’t know anymore. honestly i don’t even know who i am anymore.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so… today’s the anniversary. It’s been, like, five years, right? Five years since… well, since everything changed. And I just… I don't even know what to *do* with it. Everyone else seems to move on, you know? They talk about how it's healing, and how you get stronger. But it doesn't feel like that. It just… sits. Like a stone in my stomach.

I keep replaying it in my head, over and over. Little things, mostly. The way the rain smelled that day. What I was wearing. Did I say the wrong thing? Probably. I always say the wrong thing, I think. It’s just… I feel like I should have done something different. Maybe *anything*. But what? I don't even know. It’s like… a loop.

My husband, Daniel, he doesn’t really talk about it. He just… doesn’t. He makes dinner, watches TV, and pretends everything is normal. And I appreciate that, I really do. But sometimes I feel like he’s building this wall around himself, and I'm just… standing outside, looking in. It’s isolating.

I keep wondering if I’m overreacting. Like, is this just a normal grief response? Or is something else going on? I dunno. Maybe I’m just… fragile. Everything feels so fragile lately. Like, if I look at it too hard, it'll shatter. It's silly, I know. Just a feeling. A really persistent, annoying feeling. I just… want it to stop being so heavy. It's exhausting, honestly.  I just wish I knew how to… not feel this way. Maybe tomorrow will be better. Probably not, but… maybe.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text:
Okay, so this happened. Went to the clinic because my chest just felt…wrong. Like a weird tightness. They did some tests and said it’s probably nothing serious, just stress or something. But it *felt* like something. Like a huge weight.

I don’t know why I even went. It’s always something, isn’t it? Just…something. Everyone here is so nice, so *normal*, and I just feel like I’m standing on the edge of everything. Like if I blink, it'll all just disappear.

I keep thinking about how stupid this city is. So big and empty. And I’m just…here. Waiting for something to break. It's probably nothing, they said. But it feels like it *could* be. Like it *will* be. Just...a quiet kind of awful.

okay okay okay i’m just… swirling. like if you poured all the colors in a rainbow into a washing machine and then just… let it spin. that’s kinda how my brain feels right now. i haven’t slept properly in like, a week? or maybe it’s been longer and i’ve just been ignoring it? honestly i can’t even keep track. it’s just… dark. and then when i *do* manage to drift off, it’s all these really weird dreams. not nightmares, exactly, but like… flashes. snippets of conversations that didn’t happen, people i don’t know looking at me with this really intense expression, and then i’m suddenly somewhere completely different, like a crowded train station but it’s all made of velvet.

it’s messing with my schedule, obviously. i missed the last two history quizzes and i’m pretty sure i told mr. henderson i was sick. which i wasn’t. i just… didn’t want to go. it feels pointless, you know? like everything is pointless. i keep trying to focus on, like, my art project – the one for AP studio – but i just stare at the blank canvas and it’s like it’s judging me. judging how much i’m failing. or how much i *feel* like i’m failing.

my mom keeps asking if i’m okay, and i just say ‘fine.’ but i don’t *feel* fine. it’s like a constant low hum of anxiety, vibrating underneath everything. and then sometimes it spikes, like a wave, and i just… i don’t know what to do. i just want to curl up and disappear. maybe if i just stop trying, it’ll all go away. but i know that’s not true, right? it just keeps getting worse. it’s exhausting.

Seriously, I just don’t *get* how people just… relax at parties. Like, everyone’s laughing and chatting and it’s just… too much. I tried to say something funny about my old job, and it just felt stupid and awkward, so I just kinda mumbled and stared at my drink. Then someone asked me what I was going to *do* and I just froze. Like, what *do* I do? I don't even know. Everyone seems to have a plan, a direction. I feel like I’m just… floating. It’s not that I *want* to be around people, it’s just that I feel like I’m messing everything up, always. Just wanna go home. Ugh.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: It’s just… quiet here, you know? Too quiet. Used to be full of kids, yelling, building forts, little sticky hands grabbing at everything. Now… just the ticking of the grandfather clock and the hum of the refrigerator. I keep expecting to hear someone call my name, like I’m still… *doing* something, really *being* someone. But they’re all gone, off chasing their own lives and I’m just… here. It feels like a mistake, like I traded my whole world for this empty house. I tried calling Sarah yesterday, you know, my daughter, but she just said she was busy. Busy. Like my life isn't busy enough. It’s funny, isn't it? I spent so long caring for everyone else, making sure they were okay, and now… I don’t even know how to be okay myself. Just… existing, I guess.

text: Ugh, this deadline is just... crushing me, like, seriously. I keep staring at the screen and everything just feels blurry and pointless, you know? Like, who even *am* I right now? Just a mess of panicked thoughts and half-finished essays, and I just... I don’t even know how to keep going, honestly. It’s all just too much.

text: Okay, so like, everything is just… wrong. Mom keeps saying I’m being dramatic, that I’m just overreacting about Dad yelling at me again. But it *always* feels so huge, you know? Like, he just completely lost it last night over something stupid, like the dishes weren’t done, and it just… it just keeps happening. And then my brother just rolls his eyes and pretends it’s not a big deal, like I’m being ridiculous. It’s like, I try to explain how it makes me feel – this constant, tight feeling in my chest, like I’m gonna burst – but he just doesn’t get it. I just… I don’t even know who I am anymore, I guess? It’s like I’m shifting, constantly changing, trying to be someone who can handle it all, but it’s exhausting. I just want things to be normal, or at least, not so awful. I just... I don't know what to do.

text: Okay, so like, this city is… loud. Really loud. And everything feels so…bright, you know? I just moved here for this program, and everyone seems to *know* things, like, everyone’s already established and has their whole life figured out and I'm just… stumbling around. My professor keeps saying I need to “take initiative,” but I don’t even know what that *means* sometimes, it just feels like he’s judging me, you know? And then work, it's just a constant pile of things that feel really, really important, like if I don’t do them perfectly, I’m just… failing, obviously. I keep thinking about going back home, back to my room, back to just being quiet, but then I feel guilty, like I should be *doing* something, building something. It’s just… a lot. I just feel…unmoored, I guess? Like I’m floating and there’s no anchor.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, like, seriously. I don't even know what's happening anymore. My mom keeps saying I'm being dramatic, that it’s just “family stuff,” but it’s not just *stuff*. It’s…everything. It's like every time I try to say something, it just gets twisted around and used against me. Last night was… rough. We were talking about my job search, you know, the one I’ve been stressing about for weeks, and she just… completely dismissed it. Like, "You'll find something eventually, dear. Don't worry so much." It's not helpful, honestly. It feels like she doesn’t even *see* how hard I'm trying.

And then my dad chimed in, saying I should just "get a real job" – like I don’t already know that! I’m trying to figure out what I *want* to do, not just land any old thing. It's like they both think I'm deliberately messing things up, that I’m just being difficult. Which, maybe? Part of me feels like if I just keep quiet, it'll all go away. But that's, like, the worst thing, right? Just shutting down.

I just feel so…lost. Like I’m floating in this gray space and I can’t quite grasp anything. I keep imagining everyone else has it figured out, you know? Like they know exactly what they’re doing and why, and I'm just…a mess. I guess I'm just trying to make sense of it all, but it’s exhausting. I don't know why I even bother. Maybe I should just stay in my room and watch TV. It's probably better than dealing with this. I just feel… fragile, I think. Like anything could push me over the edge. I don’t want to be a burden, but it's hard not to feel like I am. Idk, it’s just… a lot.

Okay, seriously. This is… a lot. Like, everything is a lot. We moved last week. Just, like, packed up our whole life and drove across state. Mom kept saying “new beginnings!” and “fresh start” but it just feels…wrong. Everything feels wrong. The apartment is beige. Beige. Like a hospital. And it smells faintly of bleach. I don’t even *like* bleach.

Liam’s doing okay, mostly. He’s sleeping a lot. Which is good, I guess. But then he’ll wake up and scream, and I just… I don’t know. I feel like I’m failing him already. Like I’m supposed to be this amazing mom, you know? Calm and collected and happy. But I just feel… jittery. And sad. Mostly sad.

My sister keeps sending me pictures of her kids, and her husband, and their perfectly decorated house. It’s like they’re living in a magazine. I just want to scream. It's not fair. Why is everything so easy for them? I keep replaying every single thing I said and did during the move, trying to figure out where I went wrong. Did I pack the wrong snacks? Did I tell him the wrong story? Was it the way I looked at the furniture? God, I’m exhausting myself just thinking about it.

I keep expecting someone to tell me to calm down, to take a deep breath, to just *be*. But it doesn’t work. It just… doesn’t. I just want to go back. Back to the old place. Back to… before. This new place… it's just amplifying everything. Like a really bad echo. I just need a nap. A really, really long nap.

Ugh. Just got off shift. Seriously, people. Like, *why* do I have to be the one dealing with everyone's moods at 3 AM? It’s not like I asked for this job, okay? I just needed something, anything, to pay the bills. But now I’m basically a walking emotional support animal for half the town.

Last guy, he just… started yelling about his mom. Like, a full-blown rant. And I just stood there, handing him a lukewarm coffee and nodding. Nodding! Like I actually *understood* that he needed me to absorb his teenage angst at 3:15 AM. It’s exhausting. I swear, my brain feels like it’s going to short-circuit.

Then there was the woman who cried because her dog ate her homework. Seriously? Homework? A dog? It's always something dramatic. I offered her tissues, which is the best I could do, I guess. I don’t even *like* dogs. Makes me feel… off. Like I’m failing at being normal, you know? Everyone else seems to handle this stuff so easily. They just laugh it off or offer helpful advice. Me? I just feel… heavy.

I keep thinking about just disappearing. Not like, permanently, but just… fading away. Becoming invisible. Like I could just walk into a wall and no one would even notice. It’s stupid, I know. But it’s just… a lot. It’s always *so* much. And I’m just… tired. Just really, really tired of carrying everyone else’s baggage. I need to find a way to just… not.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
okay so like seriously what is happening to me i went to the doctor yesterday and they were being all weird and poking me and asking a million questions it was so intense i just kept repeating i’m fine i’m totally fine but now i’m convinced they think i’m dying or something it’s probably nothing right? but it feels like everything is just… falling apart you know? mom keeps asking if i’m okay and she’s crying, which is awful because i don’t want to make her sad but i can’t even talk about it i just keep saying it’s a test or something ridiculous.

i’m back home again which is the worst possible thing i hate it here it feels like i’m stuck in amber just watching my life slowly decay. i keep thinking about all the things i *should* be doing, grad school applications, finding an apartment, getting a job but it all just feels so impossible like a mountain i can’t even begin to climb.

and then there’s just… me. i don’t even recognize myself lately. i feel like i’m wearing a costume and everyone else knows the lines but i don't. it’s like my brain is a broken record skipping and playing the same awful song over and over. i tried to call liam but i just couldn’t get the words out i just hung up. he probably thinks i'm being difficult. i just… i don’t know. it's just a lot and it feels like i’m drowning and there’s no one there to throw me a rope. i just want it to stop. i just want to feel normal again, even if normal is just a lie i tell myself to get through the day.

Seriously, they’re acting like *I’m* the problem. Like losing my job is somehow my fault. Mom’s yelling about bills, Dad’s just…distant. It’s like I’m not even here. I keep trying to explain it’s not about me, that I’m just… crumbling a little. Feels like I’m constantly apologizing for existing, for being a disappointment. They don’t get it. Nobody gets it. I keep thinking about just… disappearing, you know? Just fading away so they don’t have to deal with this mess. It’s stupid, I guess. But it feels… easier than facing another fight.

it just… it just keeps happening. like a record stuck on repeat. mom yelling again about the dishes, like i don’t already do enough. she says i don’t understand, that i don’t *care* about her health. but i do! i’m exhausted. i’m always exhausted. and dad just sits there, silent, like he’s watching a movie. doesn't say anything, doesn't help. it’s like i’m the only one who sees how much this is breaking us all. i just… i wish they’d just *listen* for once. or maybe i don’t. maybe i’m just making it worse. i feel like a broken toy, constantly being thrown around and nobody actually trying to fix me. i just want it to stop. i just want it to be quiet.
Just… staring at the wall again. Feels like everyone else has a purpose and i’m just… drifting. It’s stupid, i know, but it’s like if i stop feeling *anything* it’ll be okay. Doesn’t feel okay.

okay seriously like, what is happening? i just… i don’t even know anymore. it’s like every time i think i’m getting somewhere with my mom, she just throws a grenade. last night was… it was awful. she called saying my brother is “being difficult” about helping with the stuff after the divorce and she’s just *furious*. furious like, yelling-into-the-phone furious. and then she started going on about how i’m “too sensitive” and “need to toughen up” like i didn’t just spend the last six months feeling like i was drowning.

it's always about her, isn't it? always. and my brother? he just wants to do the right thing, he’s just… he’s not good at saying things, i get it but it’s not my fault he’s awkward. and then she twists it and says he’s deliberately trying to hurt me. like i’m a little child. it’s exhausting. i just… i keep thinking about all the things we argued about, all the little things that built up and built up and now… this. 

i keep imagining everything collapsing. like, if i can’t even handle a simple family thing after everything, what’s the point? i feel like i'm just constantly failing at being a decent person, a decent daughter, a decent… anything. it’s like i'm not even supposed to feel this way, you know? like i’m supposed to just be okay with this constant pressure and this endless cycle of disappointment. i just want it to stop. i just want a little peace. and i’m starting to wonder if i’ll ever find it. it’s just… a lot. a really, really lot. i don’t even know what to do anymore.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text:
it’s just… so loud here. i moved like six weeks ago and it’s been a blur of trying to make this apartment feel like anything but a giant, echoing box. i keep expecting him to be here, you know? just… somewhere. like he’s just gone to the store and hasn’t realized he left. and then i realize he’s not. and it’s not funny anymore. it’s just… a really big, empty space.

i deleted all the photos. every single one. i know that’s probably a bad sign but it just felt… necessary. like if i kept looking at him smiling, i’d start feeling something again. like it would be too painful to just… not. it's stupid i know.

everyone keeps saying ‘time heals all wounds’ and i’m like, okay, fine, time. do your thing. but it’s not helping. it’s just… stretching out. like taffy. it’s all sticky and slow and i’m just stuck in this one awful moment over and over. i keep replaying everything, every stupid thing i said, every way i could have been different. it's like i built this whole elaborate story around us and now the story’s just… ended. and there’s no new chapter.

i ordered takeout again. it’s just pizza. i don't even really like pizza that much. it’s just something to do. something to fill the space. and the rain is coming down really hard tonight and it’s making everything feel even more gray. i just… wish i could just disappear sometimes. not like, permanently, just… for a little while. just until i can breathe again. i keep telling myself it'll get better but… i don’t know if it will.

it’s just… everything feels wrong, you know? like a record skipping on repeat. i keep trying to fix it, to just *do* something, but it’s like the needle’s stuck. i don’t even know *what* is wrong. yesterday was okay, i guess? but today? today feels like wading through mud. everyone’s so… normal. and i’m just… not. i keep staring at the clock, waiting for it to be different. it never is. i feel like i’m watching my life through a pane of glass, completely disconnected. and i keep telling myself it’ll get better, but it just… doesn’t. i just want to disappear, honestly. not like, permanently, just… fade away.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: It’s… it’s the anniversary. Three years. Feels like a lifetime ago and yesterday all at once. Honestly, I just wanted to disappear. Like, really disappear. I keep staring at the dark part of the ceiling, just… watching it. It doesn’t judge. It doesn’t ask questions. Just… dark. Everything feels wrong, you know? Like a record skipping. I keep replaying it in my head, that one night. It wasn’t supposed to happen. It just… happened. And now everything is coated in this gray film. I tried calling Liam, but he didn’t answer. Probably sleeping. He doesn't understand. He just… doesn’t. He says it's over, that we should move on. Move on to *what*? To pretending it didn’t happen? To building a new life on a foundation of shattered glass? I don't think I can do that. I don’t think *anyone* can. I keep picking at it, you know? Like a scab that won't heal. Just a little scratch, and then suddenly the whole thing is bleeding again. It’s stupid, I know. Everyone says “time heals all wounds.” Bullshit. It just makes the memories sharper, doesn't it? And the feeling that I’m fundamentally broken. Like I’m a puzzle with missing pieces and I’ll never be whole again. I just want it to stop. The feeling. The remembering. The… everything. I just wish I could just be… quiet.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a stupid thing to even write down, but… I’m just… spiraling, I think? It’s been happening for weeks, honestly. I moved back home after graduation, which, you know, was supposed to be a good thing, right? Like, a chance to breathe, figure things out. But it’s just… amplifying everything. My mom keeps asking if I’m okay, and I just say “fine” because what else do you say? It’s like a reflex. I keep telling myself I should be happy, like I *should* be excited about the future, but it just feels… wrong.

I haven’t been sleeping. At all. Like, I’ll lie in bed for hours just staring at the ceiling, and then I'll finally drift off and wake up three hours later, completely drenched in sweat. Or I’ll sleep for like, ten hours and still feel exhausted. It’s so weird. I tried counting sheep, and reading, and everything my therapist suggested – which, yeah, I actually went to a few sessions, but it didn't really *fix* anything, did it? It just made me feel… judged, maybe? Like I'm failing at being an adult. 

I keep imagining all these different versions of myself, like, who I *could* be if I just made the right choices. If I’d gone to a different college, or taken a different job. Or, like, if I wasn’t so… sensitive. It’s probably ridiculous, I know. I just… I don’t even know. It’s like my brain is constantly running a million different scenarios, and none of them are good. Idk. Maybe I just need a nap. Seriously. A long one. Or maybe I just need to disappear for a while. Just… somewhere quiet.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so… this is weird to even type, like, I don’t even know why I’m doing this. Just… venting, I guess? It’s just… everything feels so… fuzzy. Like looking through a really dirty window. I used to be so *busy*, you know? With Liam, with the house, with everything. Now… it's just… quiet. Really quiet. And that’s the worst part, I think. It’s not that it’s *bad*, it’s just… nothing. I try to call my sister, Sarah, but then I get halfway through a sentence and feel this wave of… shame? Like I’m bothering her. She's got her own life, her kids, her job. I know. I just… miss someone to talk to. Really miss it.

Liam’s sleeping, bless him. He doesn't notice, of course. He just sleeps. I make sure he’s warm, that he has his water, that he’s comfortable. It’s… all I do, really. It's okay, it's what I *should* do, right? But sometimes, when I’m staring at him, just… breathing, I feel this huge emptiness. Like there's a space where a life should be, and there's just… not. Maybe I'm just getting old. It’s probably just that. A natural part of things. But it feels… heavier than that. Like there's something broken. I dunno. I just feel… disconnected. Like I’m watching my own life from behind a pane of glass.  I keep thinking about how much I used to *do*, all the things I cared about.  Now? I just… exist. I'm not sure if that's a bad thing, or just… sad.  Maybe I should just watch a movie. Or bake something. Anything to fill the space.  Idk.  It’s just… lonely. Really lonely.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay so like, tonight was… a lot. I was at the diner, you know, closing shift, and there was this group of college kids, all laughing and loud and just… radiating, like they’d swallowed sunshine or something. and it just hit me, a really sharp wave, that I don’t *do* sunshine. I’ve never really done it. I try, I really do, to smile at the right times, to say ‘that’s nice’ or ‘good for you’ but it always feels… forced. like I’m wearing a costume. and they were talking about their weekend plans, backpacking through Europe, and parties with flashing lights and music I don’t even *like* and it just made me want to crawl under the counter and disappear. not because I don’t *want* to have fun, honestly, it’s just… the thought of actually *being* there, of navigating that kind of energy, it’s overwhelming.

I kept watching them, trying to figure out what I was doing wrong, like I’d somehow accidentally become invisible. like they couldn’t see me, and I was just this quiet, grey shadow. Mrs. Henderson, she always leaves me extra blueberry pie, she asked if I was okay and I just mumbled something about being tired, which is always the lie, isn’t it? It’s easier than explaining that everything feels… fragile. Like one wrong word, one glance, could shatter it completely. It’s not that I *want* to be alone, it’s just… I feel like I’m constantly on the edge of something, like a tightrope walker who’s about to lose their balance. And then the manager started yelling about the slow tables, and I just… blanked. Completely blanked for like, a minute. It was the longest minute of my life. I think maybe I should just ask for a different shift, or quit. Seriously. It’s just… too much.  I don't know, I just feel… adrift, you know? Like I’m watching everyone else live their lives and I'm stuck on a carousel that's spinning way too fast.

Okay, seriously. This is a mess. We moved like, two weeks ago and I already want to pack up and leave. Everything feels…wrong. Like, the furniture doesn’t fit, the colors are all off, and everyone here is just…loud. I keep expecting my mom to show up and tell me I’ve made a huge mistake, you know? It’s stupid, I know, but it’s like I’m constantly waiting for someone to say “I told you so.”

And Leo…Leo is just…different. He’s good, he really is, but he doesn’t *get* it. He just doesn’t. He’s so happy all the time, like this is the best thing ever and I’m just supposed to be happy too. It’s exhausting. Like, I’m trying to be a good mom, I really am, but it feels like I’m failing at everything. I keep rearranging the nursery, trying to make it feel like *our* place, but it just looks…sterile. And I feel like I’m losing myself. I used to be so creative, so full of ideas, and now I just stare at the walls.

I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering if I should have just stayed where we were. It’s not even that it was *good*, it was just…familiar. Safe. And now this. It’s like I’m constantly searching for something I can’t even name. A feeling? A connection? I just…I feel so lost. Like I'm wearing someone else's clothes and they don’t quite fit. Ugh. I need a nap. Or a drink. Or both.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
Okay, seriously, what is happening to me? Like, my heart just felt…weird. It skipped, then hammered, then just…stopped for a second? I told Mom, she freaked out and called the university nurse. She checked my pulse, said it was fine, but I just feel like I'm falling apart. It's exam season, obviously, I’m stressed beyond belief about this history paper and I haven’t slept properly in days. But this…this is different.

It’s like my brain is a broken record, playing the same awful thoughts over and over. I keep thinking everyone is judging me, like they know I’m a mess. Which, let’s be honest, I probably am. I just…I don’t even know who *I* am anymore. Yesterday I wanted to drop out, just disappear. Today I just feel numb. I keep looking in the mirror and seeing this completely blank person staring back. Like I’m fading.

My roommate keeps saying, "You're being dramatic," but she doesn’t *get* it. She just…doesn’t. It’s like she’s living in a different reality. I keep trying to tell myself it's just the stress, the caffeine, the sheer panic of finals, but it feels deeper than that. Like something’s fundamentally wrong. I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing, all the things I *could* be doing, and it just makes me feel worse. I just want it to stop. I just want to feel…normal. Is that too much to ask? I don’t even know. Ugh.

Ugh, this project is just… suffocating. Like, everyone expects me to be brilliant, you know? And I’m just… not. I keep messing up, and then it’s like, a tidal wave of disappointment from everyone around me. My boss keeps giving me these little sighs, and it's like he *knows* I’m failing, even though I'm trying so hard. I just want to disappear, honestly. It’s not even about the job, it’s just… this constant feeling like I’m not good enough. Everyone else seems to just *get* it, but I’m always scrambling, always behind. I keep thinking about just… not showing up tomorrow. Just… not.

Okay, seriously, what is happening to me? Lost my job last week, which was… fine, I guess? Until I realized I don’t even *care* anymore. Like, it’s not sadness, it’s just… nothing. Then the nights started. Just staring at the ceiling, replaying everything over and over. Like, what did I do wrong? Was it me? I keep telling myself it’s the unemployment, it's just stress, but then I feel this… emptiness. Everything feels pointless. My room is a mess, I haven't showered in two days, and I just want to curl up and disappear. It's like my brain just shut off.  I keep thinking about just… not getting up.  Just staying in bed.  It's not a plan, I swear, but it’s just… there.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay so like… this is probably dumb, but i just feel… completely adrift, you know? It's not even like, a specific thing. It’s just… everything. I went out with Liam last night, which was… fine. He’s nice, really. But afterwards, like, I just wanted to crawl into bed and not talk to anyone. Which, like, isn’t *new*, but it felt… heavier. He asked if I was okay and I just kinda mumbled something about being tired, which is my default setting now, i think.

I keep thinking about how everyone else seems to have this… normal life. Like, they’re posting pictures of their weekends, their dinners, their friends. And i'm just… here. Scrolling. Watching. Feeling like i’m observing a movie about someone else’s life. It’s weird.

I tried to text Sarah today, just to say hi, you know? And she replied with a link to a concert she's going to. Which is cool for *her*, obviously. But it just… highlighted how alone i am. It’s not that she’s ignoring me, it’s just… she’s living her thing, and i’m just… existing. Maybe i’m overthinking it? Probably. I don't know. I just… i don’t really have anyone to talk to, honestly. My family is… busy. They don’t really get it, you know? They just tell me to "snap out of it." Like it's that easy.

I keep telling myself it's just a phase, a weird slump. But it’s been going on for weeks, and it feels… deep. Like it’s not just sadness. It’s more like… a lack. A lack of connection. A lack of… something. I dunno. Maybe i just need a really big cup of coffee and a cheesy movie. Or maybe i just need to accept that this is… just how things are for a while. idk. Just… feeling really lost, i guess.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, just… staring at the wall again. It’s so quiet here. Like, really quiet. I don’t even know what to do with myself anymore. Maybe I should just order takeout and watch TV? idk. It feels… pointless, you know?

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, another graveyard shift. Seriously, I don’t even know why I bother sometimes. It’s just…grey. Like everything. I keep thinking about the rent, you know? It’s due next week and I *maybe* have enough. Like, maybe. I ordered takeout tonight, just a small thing, but it felt…wrong, I guess? Like I was just throwing money away. Everyone says I should just get a real job, but what *is* a real job anymore? I just feel…lost, I think. Like I’m constantly switching faces, trying to be someone else.  Mom would be so disappointed. I just… I don’t even know what I’m doing, honestly. I feel like a ghost sometimes, just drifting through the days. It’s probably nothing, right? Just stressed. I just wish things were simpler.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, seriously, I don’t even know anymore. Like, I applied for this internship – it was supposed to be *the* thing, you know? The one that would finally prove I’m not a complete failure, that I can actually do something with my degree. And it was… intense. They wanted me to basically build an entire system from scratch in a week, and everyone kept saying “think outside the box” which is just code for “completely overwhelm this poor kid.” I tried, I really did, I stayed up until 3 am every night fueled by lukewarm coffee and the sheer terror of disappointing everyone. My mom keeps asking if I’m sleeping, which is like, a ridiculously obvious question, but she just… she *looks* at me like I’m slowly dissolving.

Then, yesterday, Mr. Henderson – he’s the supervisor, and he’s basically a grey, judgmental cloud – told me my presentation was "adequate" but not "exceptional." Not exceptional! Like I’m some kind of underperforming robot. It just… it felt like a punch to the gut. And then I started thinking about all the things I *couldn't* do, all the things I’m supposed to be, and I just… I just wanted to disappear, you know? Not like, permanently, but just… somewhere quiet where no one could judge me. It’s like I’m constantly trying to hold this really fragile thing together, like a bunch of mismatched puzzle pieces, and every time I try to force them together, they just crumble. I keep telling myself it's just pressure, it’s just a job, it’s just a stupid internship, but it feels like it's *everything*. I feel like I'm failing at everything, even breathing.  I just... I just wish things would just *stop* being so complicated.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: He said I was too much. Like I was some overflowing vase about to burst. Guess I am. Everything feels…wrong now. Just staring at the ceiling, honestly. It’s stupid, but I can’t stop thinking about how messy it all was.

okay okay so the doctor said it's probably nothing serious but my chest just felt...weird you know? like a tightness that won't go away. i keep checking myself in the mirror, just staring. it’s stupid i guess but i feel like if i look close enough i’ll see something wrong, something *wrong* that everyone else is missing.

i just… i don’t even know who i am anymore. like i’m wearing a mask and it’s cracking a little more each day. my roommate’s been nice, brought me some tea, but it doesn't really help. it's like everything feels magnified, everything is a disaster, even when it’s not.  i keep replaying everything, every stupid thing i’ve ever said, every mistake i’ve ever made. is this it? is this how it ends just…feeling like this?

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so… this is weird, right? Like, seriously weird. I don’t even know where to start. It’s just… everything feels so fragile lately. The doctor said it’s probably nothing, just stress, you know? But it feels like *everything* is stress. Especially since… well, since Mark. It’s been six months now, and I still… I still feel like I’m standing on eggshells. Every text, every call, every *thought* about him. I try to be okay, genuinely, I do. I really do. But then I look in the mirror and I just… I don’t recognize myself. Like, not in a dramatic, “oh my god, I’ve aged ten years” kind of way. It’s more like… the edges are blurry. Like a watercolor that’s been left out in the rain.

And this health thing… it’s just amplified everything. My heart rate is all over the place, I’m exhausted all the time, and I keep having these… panic attacks? I don’t even know. They just come out of nowhere. I just… I just want to disappear, honestly. Like maybe if I’m not here, nothing bad can happen. It’s stupid, I know. Mark always said I was dramatic, and maybe he was right. I dunno. It’s just… I feel like I’m constantly failing at being an adult. At being *okay*. I keep replaying everything, you know? Every fight, every argument, every time I said the wrong thing. Maybe I should have just… let it go. But then what if I missed something? What if I should have done something differently? It’s just… a lot. I just need a nap. Or a really, really strong coffee. Or maybe just to not feel anything at all. Idk.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: everything feels so pointless honestly. like i'm just stuck here, watching everyone else move on and i’m just... waiting. bills are piling up and mom keeps asking about jobs but i just can’t even. it's like i’m not even real, you know?
