text: Okay, seriously, this week is just… a mess. Like, a gigantic, swirling, chaotic mess and I can’t seem to stop it. Mom keeps saying I’m not pulling my weight with the family finances, which is ridiculous, I’m drowning in textbooks and trying to pass all these exams – it's not like I’m just chilling, you know? But then Dad just… sighs and looks at me like I’m a disappointment, like I’m failing at being a good son, and it just makes everything worse. I keep thinking about how much better things would be if I could just disappear, honestly, just vanish and not have to deal with any of this. It's like I’m constantly oscillating between feeling completely worthless and then suddenly thinking I'm the most important person in the world, and it's exhausting. I just want to scream, but I don’t even know *why* I’m so angry, it's just… a pressure building up, you know?

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, this essay is *killing* me. It’s due tomorrow and I haven’t even really started. I keep switching between thinking it's amazing and thinking it's totally garbage. I feel like everyone else in my class just *gets* this stuff, you know? Like, they're just…understanding. I'm probably just being dramatic, right? Maybe. I keep rewriting the intro, then deleting it, then rewriting it again. It’s just… a mess. I feel this weird pull to just, like, not do it at all. Just disappear for a bit, I guess? It’s not that I *want* to fail, it's just... everything feels so overwhelming. Idk. I should probably try to write something, but... ugh.

everything feels so pointless now, like he took all the color with him. i keep staring at the bills and it's just... a blur. i don't even know who i am without him and the money, it’s just… a mess. i just want to disappear, honestly.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
Okay, seriously, this is a disaster. Like, a huge one. I just… I don’t even know anymore. Moving to this town was supposed to be *good*. Fresh start, you know? Get away from everyone and everything that’s been dragging me down. Mom kept saying it was a ‘wonderful opportunity’ and Dad just nodded and smiled, like he actually believed it. But it’s just… louder. Everything is just louder and brighter and it’s making me want to curl up in a ball and disappear.

I tried going for a walk today, like I told myself I would. Just to get some air. Ended up just staring at people, you know? Like they were all judging me. Which, let’s be honest, probably they were. I keep thinking about how stupid I was to think I could just *fix* myself by moving. It’s not fixing anything. It’s just… shifting the problem. 

I feel like I’m constantly on the verge of something. Like a balloon about to pop. Except it’s not even a real balloon, it’s just this weird tightness in my chest that won’t go away. I keep expecting something bad to happen, and it never does, but the anticipation is awful. I keep replaying everything, every stupid thing I’ve ever said, every time I messed up. Like if I just change enough of those things, maybe it’ll all be okay. But it won't, will it? It never is. I just… I feel so lost. And I don’t even know where to start looking for anything.  Just… so tired.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, this city is HUGE and everyone just seems to *know* each other, you know? I tried to say hi to the guy at the coffee shop yesterday and he just stared right through me, like i wasn’t even there. It’s making me feel… really lost, like i’m just a ghost floating through everything and it’s terrifying honestly.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: okay seriously this city is just… overwhelming. like everyone here is so confident and put together and i just feel like a mess. i keep rearranging my little apartment trying to make it feel like *mine* but it just feels… temporary. it’s stupid, i know, everyone moves, but it's like i’m failing at being an adult or something. i keep expecting someone to just, like, *know* i’m struggling, you know? but they don’t. they just smile and ask if i need help finding a good coffee shop. coffee shops aren’t the problem. it's everything else. i just want to go back home but i can't. it feels like i’m stuck here watching everyone else live their lives and i'm just... waiting for something to happen, but nothing ever does.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: everything feels so…wrong. like i’m wearing someone else’s clothes and they’re itchy and don’t fit. this exam is just a giant mess, and i keep thinking everyone’s judging me even though they probably aren’t. i just want it to stop.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is embarrassing even to type, but I’m just… spiraling a little. My bank account is looking really sad, you know? I thought I was doing okay, managing my freelance work and all, but it’s just not enough, I guess. I keep spending, just little things, like coffee or takeout, and then it adds up so fast. My roommate keeps saying I need to budget, which is totally fair, but it just feels… overwhelming.

I don't even know *why* I'm spending it. It’s like a reflex, or maybe I’m trying to fill something? I feel so stupid. Like I’m failing at being an adult. I keep telling myself it’s just a temporary thing, that things will get better, but… idk. Maybe I should just quit everything and move to a cabin in the woods. Seriously. It’s probably my fault, just a messy brain, I guess.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so this is weird. I just…feel like I’m constantly changing. Like, one minute I’m totally okay, and the next I’m just…overwhelmed. It's not like anything specific happened, you know? Just…everything. I keep looking at my phone and thinking, “Wait, did I just say that differently last week?” It's messing with my head. My roommate says I'm being dramatic, but maybe she's right? I don’t even know. I just feel like I'm floating, you know? Like I’m observing myself but not *being* myself. It’s exhausting. I keep trying to tell myself it’s just the shift, the sleep deprivation, idk. Maybe I just need a vacation, or a new hobby, or…something. Just…something to hold onto, I guess. It's just...a lot.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay so like… this is probably going to sound completely insane, but I’m kinda freaking out. Liam had this rash yesterday, just a little one, you know? Like a tiny red spot on his leg. I thought it was just a bug bite or something, I’d been playing outside in the grass and all. But then it just… kept spreading. It’s everywhere now, on his arms, his cheeks, even a little bit on his tummy. And he’s been so fussy, just crying and pulling at his blankets. I called the doctor, of course, but they said it could be anything – eczema, allergies, who knows? They said to just keep him cool and moist, and watch for fever. But I just… I can’t shake this feeling. Like, what if it’s something really bad? I keep looking at him and thinking, “God, is he okay?”

It's just… everything feels so overwhelming lately. Becoming a parent is, like, the hardest thing I’ve ever done, and I’m just constantly second-guessing myself. Did I feed him enough? Is he getting enough sleep? Am I doing everything right? I just want to protect him, you know? It’s stupid, I guess, to be this anxious about a rash, but it feels like it’s connected to everything else. Like if he’s not okay, then… then I’m not okay. I just… I don’t know. Maybe I’m just tired. Seriously, I’m so exhausted. I think I need a nap. Or maybe a coffee. Or maybe I just need to breathe. I just wish I knew what was going on. I feel like I'm watching a movie and I don't know the plot. It's just... a lot.

Ugh. Just… ugh. Another night. Feels like everything’s spinning, you know? Mom’s yelling again, about the dishes. Seriously, dishes. Like that’s the end of the world. She says I never do anything right, that I’m just drifting. Like I don't *try*. But it’s like… trying just makes it worse. She doesn’t even *listen*. It’s always the same thing. I tell her I'm exhausted, working double shifts, barely sleeping, and she just… scoffs. Says I’m lazy.

My brother called, too. Just… rambling. About his job, his girlfriend, how *everyone* is judging him. He sounds so… fragile. Like he needs me to fix it, like I can just make everything okay. I can’t. I honestly can’t. I just feel this heavy feeling, like I’m drowning in everything that’s wrong, and I can’t even breathe. It's like I'm watching myself from far away, just… existing. It’s exhausting. I keep thinking about just… disappearing, you know? Not that I *want* to, but like maybe if I just wasn’t here, it wouldn't hurt so much. It's just... a thought, I guess. A stupid, awful thought. I need coffee. Lots of coffee. And then probably just... nothing.

Seriously, this whole thing… the hospital, the tests, it’s like my body just decided to throw a tantrum. Like, why now? Why *this*? He said he’d always be there, you know? Always. And now this. It’s stupid, I know, to be so upset about a weird pain in my chest when, like, everyone else is freaking out. But it feels…personal. Like it's saying something about me, about how I’m just… falling apart. He probably knew, didn’t he? That I’d crumble the second he was gone. Just… doesn’t make it any less awful.  I keep thinking about everything we used to do, and it's like it’s all just… fading. Like a badly photocopied picture.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, my sister just texted again. About Mom. It’s like, everything is always about Mom, isn’t it? I don’t even know what I want anymore. I just… I don't get why it has to be so hard. Maybe I should just block her. idk. It feels messy.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Ugh, just… everything feels so wrong. Like, I’m supposed to be studying for these finals and I just… can’t. I keep switching between subjects, like, one minute I’m staring at calculus and the next I’m just scrolling through old pictures of my family. It’s weird, right? I don’t even *like* looking at them sometimes, but it just… happens.

And then I feel guilty, obviously. Like, I *should* be concentrating. But then I feel like everyone else is just effortlessly crushing it and I’m completely failing. I don’t know, maybe I’m just overthinking things? It’s probably nothing. I just… feel a little lost, I guess. Like a part of me is missing or something. Idk. It’s just… a lot.

Okay, seriously, I don’t even know anymore. It’s just… a constant hum, you know? Like a low-level static in my brain that never really goes away. Everything feels… fragile. Like if I breathe too hard, it’ll all just shatter. I keep rearranging things, just… moving things. The furniture, the pictures, even the way I fold the towels. It's stupid, I know, but it makes me feel like I’m *doing* something. Like I’m in control. Which I’m definitely not.

He’s… he's good. Really good. And that’s the problem, isn't it? He doesn’t *need* me to be in control. He just needs… someone to make sure he takes his pills. And the tea’s hot. And the lights are on. I used to do so much more. I used to *paint*. Remember that? God, that feels like a lifetime ago.

I keep thinking about all the things I should have said, all the things I should have done differently. It’s not that I blame myself, exactly. It's just... a weight. A heavy, constant weight. I feel like I’m drowning in all the *should haves*. And I keep trying to grab at something, anything, to hold onto, but it all slips through my fingers. Like sand. Just… slipping. I feel so tired. Just so incredibly, bone-deep tired. It’s not a good tired. It’s the kind of tired that makes you question everything. Like, what’s the point of even getting out of bed? Honestly, I don’t even know.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
It’s the anniversary. Feels stupid even typing that. Like marking a stain on a tablecloth that’s already covered in others. Liam’s asleep, finally. He’s a good baby, honestly, a really good one. But it doesn't...fill anything. Just a hollow space next to the grief. I keep expecting him to yell, to need something, anything to pull me back to that other version of me. The one who *did* something.

I just…I keep thinking about him. And it’s not a good thought. It’s not a sad thought, not really. It’s just…there. Like a rock in my stomach. I told Sarah I was fine, of course. Just tired. It’s easier than explaining. Easier than admitting this little guy is just amplifying everything. Like a spotlight on a mess.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay seriously, this is all just… a lot. like, a *massive* lot. he called, you know, like two days ago, just to say… he’s fine. Fine. That’s it. Just ‘fine’ after, like, six months of barely a text, of ignoring me, of just… disappearing. And he’s fine. It's almost insulting, you know? Like he’s trying to pretend everything was normal, like the last six months didn’t involve me feeling like a ghost, like a half-remembered dream. I keep replaying everything in my head, you know? The arguments, the silences, the way he used to look at me like I was… an inconvenience. It’s not fair, it just isn’t fair.

And then, then the doctor called. About my blood pressure. Apparently, it’s through the roof. Stress, they said. Of course, they said stress. Like it’s that simple. It’s not *just* stress. It's… it's everything. It's the divorce, it’s the feeling that I’ve lost a part of myself, it's the constant, low-level hum of anxiety that’s just… *there*. It’s like my body is screaming at me, and my brain is just… stuck. I keep thinking about all the things I should have done differently, the chances I missed, the words I didn’t say. I just… I feel so hollow, like someone scooped out all the good stuff and left this empty shell. He said "be happy," right? Like that’s just something to *do*. It's so ridiculous. I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear, honestly. Or maybe just… not feel anything at all. It’s exhausting, you know? This constant, churning, overwhelming feeling. I don’t even know what I’m trying to fix, or if anything *can* be fixed anymore.  It feels like everything is just… slipping away.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, so like, this is ridiculous, seriously. I moved here six months ago, right? Just…poof. Packed up my life and drove across the country to, like, *nothing*. Everyone says it’s a great city, full of opportunities, and…it is, I guess. But it’s also terrifyingly empty. I don’t know anyone, and honestly, most people just seem to ignore you, like you’re a ghost or something. And now this exam is looming, this huge history paper on the French Revolution, and I’m just…stuck. I started it, like, three weeks ago and it's just…a mess. A really, really messy, rambling mess. It's supposed to be like, 15 pages, and I’ve got maybe six? It doesn't even make sense. I keep thinking about how everyone else probably knows everything about this, they've been studying for months, and I just…I just feel like I’m drowning. Like I’m trying to build a sandcastle in a hurricane. 

I keep imagining my mom calling, asking how it’s going, and I just want to scream. Not a big, dramatic scream, just…a quiet, desperate one. She always knew how to do things, how to organize, how to *be*. I used to think I was okay, you know? That I was, like, pretty solid. But then I got here, and suddenly, everything feels…fragile. Like if I breathe too hard, it’ll all just shatter. It’s not even the exam, though, it’s…it’s everything. This whole feeling of being adrift. Like I'm watching my life slip through my fingers and I can't grab onto anything. I keep trying to write, to force myself to focus, but my brain just…loops. It’s like a record skipping, playing the same anxious thoughts over and over.  I just… I don't know what to do.  Maybe I should just, like, disappear. Just vanish.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay so like, this is just… a lot. seriously. I went to Sarah’s birthday dinner last night and I think I completely ruined it. or maybe it just… felt ruined. everyone was laughing, you know? normal birthday stuff, cake, presents, the whole shebang. and I just… I couldn't. I couldn't really *do* anything. I kept staring at my plate, just this weird, uncomfortable feeling like I was being watched, even though everyone was looking at the food.

And then I started rambling about, I don’t even know, about how everyone always asks me about my job and it’s like, a *huge* deal to me, obviously, but it’s also completely pointless because it’s *gone*. Just… gone. Like, poof. And I started going on about how everyone else seems to just *know* what they’re doing, you know? Like they have this whole plan and I’m just… drifting. I think I even said something about how I’m pretty sure everyone else’s lives are meticulously crafted and mine is just… a mess. Which is probably awful, right?

Sarah kept trying to change the subject and everyone was giving me these really polite smiles, but I could tell they were uncomfortable. I felt this huge wave of shame, just this overwhelming, sticky shame. Like I was dripping it all over the table. I wanted to disappear, honestly. Just melt into the tablecloth. I kept picking at my food, which I always do when I’m nervous, and I think I actually made a face and didn't even realize it. It’s just… I don’t even know how to be around people anymore. I feel like I’m constantly messing things up, like I’m a walking disaster. It’s exhausting. It’s just… exhausting. Maybe I should have just stayed home. Or, like, sent a gift. But I didn’t. I just… showed up. And then I probably made everyone miserable. Ugh.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay, so like, everything feels…weird. Liam just…stopped texting. Didn’t say anything, just gone. it’s probably my fault, right? I always mess things up, I know that. I overthink *everything*. He said he needed space, which is, like, the most vague thing ever. I spent the whole night scrolling through our old texts, just re-reading them over and over. It's stupid, I guess. He probably doesn’t even remember half of what we did.

I keep expecting him to just… reappear, you know? Like a glitch in the system. But he doesn’t. And I don’t even know what to *do* anymore. I just feel…hollow. Like a part of me just vanished with him. Maybe I'm being dramatic, idk. But I just… I don’t know who I am without him. Maybe I’m just lonely. Ugh. This sucks.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so this is…weird. I just moved to this tiny apartment in this new city and it’s, like, completely overwhelming. I thought I’d be excited, you know? A fresh start, right? But honestly, everything feels…wrong. Like, I keep rearranging the furniture and then hating it five minutes later. I don't even *know* what I’m looking for, I guess? Maybe a feeling of belonging? It’s just…a lot. Everyone seems so chill and normal here, and I feel like I'm constantly on the edge of something, like I'm about to say or do the wrong thing. I keep wondering if I’m even *capable* of being normal. Idk. I just feel…off. I'm trying to be okay, but it’s hard. Maybe it’s just the move, but it feels deeper than that.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay, so like, tonight was… a thing. Mom made lasagna, which is always nice, you know? Comfort food and all that. But then Aunt Carol started talking about her pottery class, and honestly, it just spiraled. She was going on and on about the *perfect* curve of a vase, and how she’d finally found her ‘thing,’ her *passion*, and I just… I felt this weird tightness in my chest. Like she was so sure of herself, so completely *there*, and I’m just… here. Floating, I guess? Like a ghost at a dinner party.

Everyone else seemed to be genuinely interested, nodding and asking questions about her glaze techniques, and I just kept scrolling through my phone, which is probably rude, I know, but it felt like the only way to breathe. Dad tried to pull me into the conversation, asked if I was enjoying my gap year, and I just mumbled something about “figuring things out,” which is, like, the most cliché thing ever, right? And it’s *true*, but it doesn’t actually *mean* anything, does it? It’s just a word I’m using to avoid saying what I actually feel.

I keep thinking about how everyone else has a plan, a direction, a *job*. I graduated, which is… good, I guess? But I don't *want* a job. Not really. I just want to… disappear, maybe? Or maybe not disappear, but just… fade into the background. Like I’m not even real, you know? Like I'm just an extra in everyone else's movie and I’m watching it all happen from the bleachers, completely detached. It’s exhausting. It's like I'm constantly trying to be someone else, someone who can handle this, someone who isn’t… this. And I just fail, every single time.  I just wish I could just… be okay.  It’s stupid, I know, to even think that.  Just… stupid.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
okay so like seriously, i don't even know why i'm typing this but my phone is just… buzzing and i feel this weird pressure behind my eyes and it’s like everything’s just…wrong, you know? not wrong like, ‘oh god something bad happened’ wrong, but like fundamentally wrong like the colors are slightly off and the air smells funny and i keep expecting someone to be standing behind me even though i’m completely alone in this breakroom at 3 am. it’s always like this, you know? a little bit of…offness. i used to try to fix it, you know? make things *right* but it just gets worse, like trying to glue broken glass back together.

and then i think about my mom and how she always said i was too sensitive, too much, like i couldn’t just… be normal. normal. what even *is* normal? is it just pretending everything’s okay when it’s not? i feel like i’m wearing someone else’s clothes, like they’re itchy and uncomfortable and don’t quite fit me, and i’m constantly adjusting them but they never actually change. i keep rearranging the furniture in my head, trying to find a space where i can just…settle, but it’s never right. everything feels… unstable. like a sandcastle about to get washed away. i keep starting things and then i stop halfway through, just because i don't think i can finish them, or because i don't even *want* to. it's exhausting. really exhausting. i just… I wish i knew who i was supposed to be, or if there even *is* a ‘supposed to be’ anymore. it’s just a lot, you know? just…a lot.

Okay, seriously, this bio exam is going to be the end of me. Like, I’ve been staring at these diagrams for three days straight and it’s just… swirling. It’s not even that hard, I think, but my brain just completely shuts down when it comes to remembering the details. My mom keeps saying “just focus” but it’s like she doesn’t *get* it. It’s not a lack of focus, it’s… everything. Like, I know the concepts, I *know* them, but the information just won’t stick.

I keep rereading the chapter and it’s like it’s written in another language. Or maybe I’m just failing to understand it, which then makes me feel like I’m failing at everything. Everyone else seems to be, like, totally nailing it. Liam was just talking about xylem and phloem last night and I was just thinking about whether I remembered to feed Mr. Snuggles, my hamster. Seriously.

I tried to study with Chloe, but we just ended up arguing about what to order for pizza. It’s stupid, I know, but it felt… important. Like, if I can’t even agree on pizza, how am I supposed to understand cellular transport? It’s just… a lot. A huge, overwhelming, suffocating *lot*. I feel like I’m drowning, and this bio exam is just the final, really ugly wave. Maybe if I just don’t even try, it’ll all go away. But then I’ll fail, and then it’ll just get worse, right? Ugh. I just need to… breathe. Or something.
Ugh, this deadline is a joke. Like, seriously, why does everything have to be so urgent? I just… I don’t even know if I can do this. It's all just falling apart, you know? Feels like I’m drowning.

Seriously, it’s just… too much. Like, I can’t even think straight anymore. My boss is breathing down my neck about this report, and it’s due tomorrow and it’s a disaster. I keep staring at the screen and it just blurs. I feel like I’m going to crack, you know? Everything’s just… shaky. I keep picturing myself failing, completely failing, and then just… disappearing. It’s not even a big deal, really, just a stupid report, but it feels like the whole world is piling on. I just want to curl up and not feel anything. Everyone expects so much, and I'm just… not enough. Ugh.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, this is weird. I don’t even know *why* I’m posting this, honestly. Just…feeling really shaky today. It’s been, like, six months since Liam left and it hasn’t gotten easier, you know? It’s just… a constant weirdness. I keep expecting him to text, or show up at the door, or something. It's probably stupid, right? I just… I feel like I’m supposed to be *over* it, but I'm not. Maybe I’m just clinging on to something that’s already gone. I keep rearranging my apartment, trying to make it feel like *his* place again, even though he doesn't live here anymore. I dunno. It's just a mess. Maybe I should just delete everything and start over? Or maybe that's just more confusing.

they just don't *get* it. everyone's yelling, deadlines are insane, and i feel like i'm drowning. it's like i'm not even there, a ghost just shuffling papers and nodding. i keep thinking i'm failing, failing at everything.

okay seriously i don’t even know where to start. it’s just… a lot. like, a *massive* amount. everything feels pointless, you know? i keep staring at the bills and they just swim before my eyes. he said he’d take care of things, he promised, he *knew* i wasn't good with money. and now… now it's all on me. and i feel like i’m drowning.

i keep thinking about the wedding, like, how much it cost. it feels like a huge, stupid mistake now. all those decorations, the fancy food, everyone flying in… it was just… a lot. and now it's just a weight. a really heavy, suffocating weight.

i tried to call my mom, but i couldn't even get the words out. she just kept saying "be strong" which is the worst thing anyone could say right now. it’s not strong to be this stressed, it's just… exhausting. like i’m running on fumes and i don’t even know where the gas station is.

i keep replaying everything that happened, little things mostly. the way he sighed when i asked him to help with the budget, the way he rolled his eyes when i talked about wanting to save up for something nice. did i just… did i push him away? i don’t even know anymore. it’s like my brain is just a tangled mess of regret and worry. and i just keep scrolling through old photos, looking at us smiling, and it hurts so much. it feels like i’m watching a movie about someone else’s life. this isn’t me. this isn’t how things should be. i just… i just want it to stop. i want to feel normal again. but i don't even know how.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
it’s… it’s today. october twenty-seventh. feels like a punch to the gut. two years. two years since he said he was leaving. i keep expecting to hear his voice, you know? like it’s just a bad phone call or he’ll walk through the door and it’ll all be okay. but it’s not. it never is. i tried to be normal, i really did. went to that pottery class like we used to, bought a stupid little plant that’s probably already dying. my mom keeps sending me these cards with inspirational quotes about moving on, and honestly, i just throw them in a drawer. they feel…wrong. like they don't understand. he just…left. no explanation, no goodbye, just a text that said he needed space. space? like i was an inconvenience.

i keep replaying everything in my head, you know? every fight, every little thing. maybe if i’d said something different, maybe if i’d been nicer, maybe if i’d just…held on tighter. it’s stupid, i know. it’s always my fault, isn’t it? i hate that. i hate feeling like a failure. everyone else seems to have figured it out, you know? their lives are moving forward, and i’m stuck here, just…existing. like a ghost. i keep telling myself it’ll get better, that i’ll find someone new, but it feels like a lie. a really, really sad lie. and the worst part is, i don't even know who i am without him anymore. it's like a part of me just vanished. a big, important part. i just…i just want it to stop feeling like this. i want it to stop hurting so much.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, Mom just yelled again. It’s always something, you know? Like, I just… I don’t even know why she does it. Maybe she’s just lonely? idk. It’s just… exhausting. I feel like I’m constantly walking on eggshells.

okay seriously, this is just… a lot. everyone keeps saying ‘it’s a beautiful thing’ and ‘you’re a great mom’ and i just… i don’t feel it. not really. like, i love him, obviously, he’s my baby, but it’s like i’m watching someone else live my life. and it's exhausting. we’re drowning in bills. seriously drowning. the daycare is bleeding us dry, and i keep thinking about the rent, and the electricity, and everything. i told my husband, and he just… sighed. Like, a big, heavy sigh. Like i’m being dramatic. But i’m not being dramatic! We’re barely eating anything that isn’t ramen. He said we need to “budget” again. Budget?! We’ve been budgeting for six months! It’s like he doesn’t even *see* it. He’s always got his head in his phone, scrolling through whatever. I keep trying to tell him how i feel, how it’s like i’m constantly on edge, like something bad is *always* about to happen, but he just nods and says “you’re stressed.” Yeah, i'm stressed! It’s more than stressed. It’s like… like i’m failing. Like i’m not a good mom. Like i’m not a good wife. I just… I need a break. A real break. Not just when he’s finally looking at me.

text: Ugh, the hospital… it just felt…wrong, like everyone was judging me even though they weren’t, you know? Mom kept saying it’s nothing serious but I feel like everything *is* serious, like this whole life is just one giant, messy, collapsing thing and i’m just standing here watching it fall apart and i don’t even know why.  I just... I just feel so lost.

Okay, seriously. This sucks. Like, a *really* sucks kind of suck. I’m staring at this pile of bills and it’s just… growing. It’s not even a big pile, it’s just *there*. Every. Single. Day. Another one. I keep telling myself it’ll be okay, that I’ll figure something out, but it’s like trying to hold onto smoke. It just slips through my fingers.

My boss, Mark, he’s nice, I guess, but he doesn’t get it. He just says “work harder,” like that’s the answer. Like I can just, you know, *make* more money. I’m tired. So, so tired. This shift is brutal, and I’m already running on fumes. I feel like I’m failing at everything. Just… failing.

I keep thinking about selling my old guitar. It’s stupid, I know, but it’s the only thing I have that’s really *mine*. It’s just sitting there, collecting dust. I don’t even play it anymore. It feels… pointless. Like everything feels pointless lately. I tried asking Sarah for a loan, but she just said she’s struggling too. It’s like everyone’s drowning and I’m just… floating. And I don’t even know how to swim. This whole thing just feels… heavy. Like I'm carrying something I can't even name. I just want it to stop. Just… stop.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: it’s just… everything feels so blurry you know? like i’m watching a movie but the colors are all wrong and the sound keeps cutting out. i keep changing my mind about everything, about what i want, about who i even am, it’s exhausting and i just want to disappear sometimes, i guess?

Okay, seriously, this is just…a mess. I haven’t slept properly in like, weeks. Or maybe months? Time is blurring, you know? It’s always blurring. I shift starts at 11, finishes at 7, and it’s just…grey. Everything’s grey. And I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing, or things I *could* be doing, but I just can’t. Like, I should be cleaning my apartment, which is a disaster, but even the thought of actually doing it feels…heavy. It’s not even a big deal, really, just stuff piled up, dirty dishes, but it feels like a mountain.

And then there are the faces. All the faces. The customers, the other guys on the shift, even the security guard. They all seem to be judging me, I swear. Like I’m a disappointment. Or maybe I’m judging *myself* even harder. It’s a loop. A horrible, spinning loop. I keep telling myself I’m just tired, that it’s the shift, that it’ll pass, but it doesn’t. It just…shifts. Like the gears in a broken machine.

I tried to call Mom yesterday, but I just…couldn’t. It felt like a huge effort, like I was exposing something. Something fragile and broken. I don't even remember what I said. I just hung up. And now I feel even worse. Like I’m failing at being a decent human being. Everything feels so…unstable. Like if I blink too hard, I’ll just disappear. Or worse, that everything is just…wrong. Just fundamentally wrong. I just want to lie down, but I can't even lie down without feeling like I'm drowning.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: it’s just… so loud here. like, constantly. they don’t get it, you know? my mom keeps saying i need to “move on” from everything, like it’s that easy. like i just erase the last ten years and pretend it never happened. it’s not that simple. it’s like… a part of me still lives back there, with them, even though i’m supposed to be building something new. i feel like a ghost, haunting a place that doesn’t want me. i keep replaying their arguments in my head, trying to figure out where i went wrong, but it’s just a mess. a big, tangled, awful mess and i don't even know how to pick at it without everything falling apart again. i just wish they’d just… understand that this isn’t about *them*, it’s about me being completely lost.

I just feel…disconnected, I guess? Like I’m watching myself try to write, but it’s not really *me*. Idk, this whole adulting thing is just… exhausting.

text:
okay so like... this is probably stupid to even write but i need to get it out somehow, right? it’s just… everything feels so wrong. i keep replaying everything, you know? like, every stupid thing i said, every time i didn’t say something, every little glance. and it’s not even like i *wanted* to do any of those things, it's just... a constant loop. i don't even know *why* i’m feeling this way, honestly. it's like a weird pressure in my chest all the time.

he said he needed space, which, like, fine, i get that. but space feels like… oblivion. it feels like he's just erasing me. and i keep thinking about all the things we talked about, all the plans we made – stupid little things like that trip to the coast we were saving for – and it's like they never actually happened. they’re just… ghosts now, aren’t they?

i tried to call him, like, three times yesterday. left voicemails. just… to hear his voice, i guess. but of course he didn't answer. i feel like i'm the only one who remembers any of this, you know? like he’s already moved on and i'm stuck here in this echo chamber.

maybe i’m being dramatic. maybe it’s just the grief and the shock and the fact that i haven’t slept properly in days. idk. i just… i keep expecting him to show up at the door, like a bad movie scene. and then he won't. and it’s just… crushing. i'm trying to be okay, i really am, but it feels like i’m constantly on the verge of just… falling apart. maybe i should just block his number and pretend he never existed. but then i’d feel even worse, wouldn't i? it's just… a mess. a huge, tangled mess.

Ugh, this deadline is a joke. Like, seriously, it's just... piling up. He always said I couldn’t handle anything, right? Guess he was right. I just... I don't even know why I bother anymore. It’s all pointless.

text:
Ugh, I just… I don’t even know what’s happening anymore. Like, I’m supposed to be finishing this huge project for Mr. Henderson, and it’s just… piling up. And then I have to work at the bakery tomorrow, right? Mrs. Davison’s breathing down my neck about everything, saying I need to step up, that I’m “not pulling my weight.” I know she means well, I guess, but it just feels… crushing.

I keep switching between wanting to just curl up in my room and disappear and wanting to, like, actually do something, you know? It's a really weird feeling. Part of me thinks I should just tell her I can't handle it, but then what would happen? I’d probably just get in more trouble. Maybe I’m just overreacting. I really hope so. It's just… a lot. I don’t even know why I’m typing this out, tbh.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this happened and I don't even know what to do. I went to the nurse at school today because my chest was just… hurting. Really bad. Like, a tight feeling, and it felt like my heart was beating super fast, but it wasn't, I think. I just… panicked. She checked me out, and she said I probably just had a weird muscle spasm or something, but she gave me this really serious look and said I needed to go see a doctor.

And I *did*. My mom took me, and the doctor was… nice, I guess? But he kept asking a lot of questions, like, really intense questions about how I feel all the time. About if I ever feel like I’m… falling apart. I just kinda mumbled and said I’ve been stressed, you know? About school and stuff. But he kept saying things about how I seem to get overwhelmed easily and that my moods change really fast.

It's just… it feels weird. Like, I don't *want* to be overwhelmed, obviously, but it's not like I’m, like, *always* sad or anything. Sometimes I’m good, and then suddenly I’m just… not. And then I feel guilty for feeling that way. I don’t even know *why* I feel guilty! I just do.

My mom keeps saying it’s probably nothing serious, that I’m just a worrier. But maybe… maybe she’s wrong. Maybe there *is* something wrong. I just feel like everything is so fragile, you know? Like, if I mess up one thing, the whole world will just… crumble. I just want to be normal, I guess. Like, just be okay. Idk. Maybe this is just a big overreaction. But it feels… really big. I just keep thinking about it, and it’s making my chest hurt again.

okay seriously i’m losing it. the report’s due tomorrow and i haven't even started. it’s like my brain just… shuts off. everyone keeps saying “just focus” but it’s not that simple, you know? like, i *want* to focus but then i just spiral. i keep changing my mind about everything, about the formatting, about the data, about whether i even *understand* what i'm writing. it’s just… a mess. my sister keeps texting, asking if i’m okay, but she doesn’t get it. she thinks it's just stress, but it feels… bigger. like i’m unraveling a little bit more with every passing hour. i just… i can't. i really can't. maybe if i just stare at the ceiling for a while…

it’s just… everything’s falling apart again. like, i know i should ask for help but it feels so stupid. my mom’s yelling about bills and i just… i don’t even know what i’m supposed to do. i feel like a burden, you know? a total failure.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
it's… it’s the anniversary today. i didn't even realize. just a little notification popped up on my phone, a reminder, and suddenly everything feels… thick. like wading through honey. i keep staring at little leo, his tiny hand gripping my finger, and i just want to scream. not at him, of course, never at him. just… to make the noise go. it’s like a constant, low hum in my chest, you know? like the memory itself is vibrating.

i thought having a baby would fix it. i really did. i thought if i just poured all my energy into *him*, into this tiny, perfect little life, it would somehow… erase everything. it doesn't. it just layers it on top, this new, beautiful, terrifying thing. like adding another blanket to a bed that’s already overflowing.

yesterday, i found myself scrolling through old photos, the ones from… you know. and i just… froze. it felt like looking at a ghost. not a sad ghost, not exactly. more like a phantom limb. i keep expecting to feel *something*, a sharp pain, a specific emotion, but it’s just… muted. distant. like i’m watching a movie about someone else’s grief.

leo’s smiling at me right now, a gummy, wide-mouthed smile. and i want to hug him so tight i think i might break. but then i remember… and i just… pull away. i don’t know how to hold onto anything anymore. i don’t even know who i am anymore. is this what it feels like to be constantly fractured? like you’re pieced together from broken shards? i just… i just need to breathe. and hold leo. but it’s not enough, is it? it's never enough.

okay seriously this is just… a lot. i just got here to the apartment and it smells like bleach and new carpet and it’s supposed to be comforting but it’s not. it’s just… sterile. like everything’s trying to be clean and perfect and i feel like i’m supposed to be too. i spent the whole morning unpacking boxes filled with his things, you know? his favorite mug, the one with the little blue bird, and i almost cried. it just felt wrong, like i was stealing him again. i keep thinking about him and how he hated moving, how he always said it felt like losing a part of himself. i don't want to lose anything else. i just... i just don’t know how to do this alone. it's too quiet here.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city

text: Just… everything feels wrong. Like I’m wearing someone else’s clothes and it’s itchy and doesn't fit. This city is loud, and I don’t even know why I thought it would fix anything. Just… empty.

But like, what’s the point if I fail? I feel like everyone else gets it, they just… know what to do. I’m just… a mess, I guess. I don't even know who I am anymore, it's all just spinning. I’m probably overthinking it, right? But I feel like I’m always overthinking everything. Idk. Just… ugh.

Just moved, feels…weird. Like everyone here is a version of someone I used to know, but not. I keep expecting them to say something, anything, but they just look at me like I’m an error. It’s exhausting.

text: Ugh, I just... I don't even know where to start, honestly. Everything feels so heavy lately, like I’m carrying this invisible weight around all the time. Work is just… a constant pressure, you know? Like, I’m trying to do good, really I am, but it just feels like I’m always one step behind, one email away from a disaster. And then there's, like, everything else, right? Little things piling up, appointments I keep forgetting, just the general feeling that I’m not doing anything *right*. I keep scrolling through my feed and seeing everyone else’s lives look so… easy? It's making me feel even worse, I think. Just a general low-grade panic, a constant hum of anxiety that won’t just go away. I just want to curl up and disappear, if that makes any sense.

text: Okay so like, I just finished my shift and it’s… it’s just hitting me now, you know? It’s always been there, a low hum, a sort of grayness, but tonight it’s just… louder. Everyone else went home, of course, like, who even *wants* to be here at 3 am? It's just me and the flickering fluorescent lights and the echoing drips from the bathroom. I keep staring at the security camera footage on the monitor, watching people sleep, or just… existing, and I feel like I’m watching a movie about someone else’s life and I’m just a blurry extra in the corner. I tried to text Liam, my roommate, but he’s probably asleep and won't see it until noon. It feels pointless, honestly. He’s good, he’s really good, but he doesn’t *get* it. He doesn’t get this constant feeling of being… adrift. Like I'm orbiting something, but I don’t know what it is or if it even cares if I’m here. It's stupid, I know, just a night shift, but it’s isolating, you know? Like, I talk to the machines sometimes, just to hear a voice, even if it’s just the printer whirring. I wish I could just… turn it off. Not like, literally, obviously, but the feeling. The feeling of just being *here*, watching everything else happen while I'm stuck in this silent, empty space. I just keep thinking about how much I miss just… normal conversations. Like, you know, about anything. Anything at all.  I just feel so… alone. Really, really alone.  I should probably go make some coffee, I guess, but it doesn't really matter, does it?

text: okay seriously, i don’t even know where to start, like, everything feels so…fuzzy and heavy. i keep waking up in the middle of the night, heart pounding, just staring at the ceiling, you know? it’s not even scary, just…empty. like my brain is just…gone. and then i try to go back to sleep and it’s just this awful spiral of thoughts, mostly about, like, bills and interviews and just…not having anything to do. it’s stupid, i know, but it’s like my brain is screaming about needing to *do* something, anything, and i can’t even remember what that something is supposed to be. i keep replaying the interview in my head, that one question about why i left my last job, and i just…freeze. i mumbled something about needing a change, i think, and it sounded so weak and pathetic. i just feel so useless, like i’m a failure, you know? it’s hard to explain, but it’s like the whole world is just…gray. and i’m just…sitting in it. i tried to watch a movie last night, but i just couldn't focus, i kept zoning out, and then i just turned it off and cried. i feel like i’m losing it, honestly. i don’t even want to get out of bed some days, it's just... exhausting. i keep telling myself it’ll get better, that i’ll find something, but it’s so hard to believe right now. i just want to disappear, i guess, but then i think about my mom and i can’t do that. so i just keep lying here, staring at the ceiling, hoping for morning to just…arrive.

text: Okay, seriously, I haven’t slept properly in like, weeks. It’s just…a constant buzzing in my head, you know? Like, I’ll be trying to read something, or even just staring at the wall and my brain just starts firing off, going over everything. Work is insane, deadlines are piling up, and my boss keeps emailing me at all hours, which is totally not helping, obviously. Then there's this weird feeling, too, like I’m forgetting things, or missing details, and it’s freaking me out. I keep waking up in the middle of the night, just staring at the ceiling, feeling completely exhausted but also like I haven’t actually *done* anything. I tried chamomile tea, and counting sheep, and meditation apps – absolutely nothing works. It's just...a weight, a really heavy, prickly weight on my chest. I just want to feel normal again, you know? Just...sleep.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, I don't even know where to start. This week has been…a lot. I usually handle the night shift pretty okay, you know? It’s mostly just quiet, and I can zone out and read or whatever. But last night, Sarah from accounting asked me to go grab coffee after work, and then Mark from IT wanted to bounce ideas off me about his new project. And then Brenda from HR just…stopped by my station to chat about the company picnic. Like, it’s not that I *hate* talking to people, honestly. It's just...everything.

I feel like I’m supposed to be friendly, you know? Like, a team player. And I want to be, I really do. But every time someone says hello, or asks a question, or just *looks* at me, my brain just freezes. It’s like a little panic button goes off. I try to respond, but my words feel all jumbled and stupid, and I just end up saying something awkward and then wanting to disappear.

I think I mumbled something about the weather to Brenda. I seriously don’t remember. I just wanted to escape. I keep thinking about just pretending I didn’t hear them, which, like, isn't really a sustainable strategy, is it? I’m just so tired of feeling this way. It’s not that I’m *difficult*, I just…I don’t know how to navigate these social things anymore. Maybe I should just stick to the vending machine and my book. Maybe that's the best solution. I’m probably overthinking it, though. Ugh. I just wish people would just...leave me alone for a little bit. I don’t even know why I’m typing this out. It feels pointless, but I needed to get it off my chest, i guess.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a really weird thing to post, but i just… i don’t even know where to start. Exams are, you know, *bad*. Really bad. But then, like, everything else is just… amplified. I mean, I’m supposed to be studying organic chemistry – which is already a nightmare – and I keep just staring at the textbook and it’s just… blurring. I’m getting, like, zero out of it. And then, like, yesterday happened. It was… awful. Just a really, really awful day. It’s been a week since Liam and I broke up, and honestly, it feels like it happened yesterday. We just… drifted apart, i guess? No huge fight or anything, just… quiet. And it’s not even that I *wanted* it to end, you know? I genuinely liked him a lot. But it’s just… a lot.

Everything feels heavier. Like, I’m walking around in molasses. I keep thinking about him, about all the stupid little things, like the way he used to make me coffee in the morning, or how he always knew when I was feeling down. It’s just… a constant ache. I keep telling myself I should focus on the exams, that I need to pass these, that my future depends on it. But it’s so hard. Like, maybe I should just drop out. Seriously. That's a thought I had, like, five minutes ago. I don't even know what I'd do, though. I just… i feel completely lost. I think I just need to lie down. Maybe. Or maybe just cry. I don’t know. Ugh. This is a mess, isn’t it?

everything feels so…wrong. he just left. i don’t even know where he is. i’m supposed to be picking up his meds tomorrow and i just…can’t. the house is too quiet and it’s all just too much right now. i just want to disappear.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, it’s been a year. A whole freaking year since… well, you know. And I just… I keep thinking about it. It’s not supposed to be this hard, right? Like, anniversaries are supposed to be, you know, sentimental, maybe a little sad but like, in a nostalgic way? But it’s just… awful. Everything feels muted, like someone turned down the volume on my life. I keep replaying everything in my head, little details I probably should have forgotten, stupid things he said, the way the light was hitting his face. It’s ridiculous, I know, obsessing over all of it, but I can’t help it. My mom keeps trying to be supportive, saying “it gets easier,” but it doesn't feel like it. It just feels… heavier. Like I’m carrying this giant, invisible stone around with me all the time. School is just… a blur. I can’t focus on anything, even the stuff I usually like. My grades are slipping, my friends are starting to ask if I'm okay and I just… I don’t want to tell them. It feels like I'm failing at being okay, at just moving on. I keep telling myself to be strong, to be resilient, but it’s exhausting. It's like a constant battle against this feeling of just being completely overwhelmed and like I'm stuck in a loop. I just want it to be over, you know? Like, just let me forget. Seriously, I just want to forget.  I feel like a broken record, just repeating the same sad song over and over. I’m trying to be normal, I really am, but it's so hard.

Seriously, I just can’t. Little Finn had a fever spike last night and I was up all night bouncing him, trying to get him to drink something. The doctor said it’s probably just a virus, but I swear my chest is tight and I can’t breathe properly. I feel like I’m failing him, like I’m not even a good parent. My husband is trying to be helpful, bless him, but he doesn’t *get* it. It’s just… a lot. Like, a massive, constant weight. I keep staring at him sleeping and then I just want to cry. I just need a minute. Seriously, just a minute to not feel this.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
okay so, like, this is embarrassing to even type out but i’m just… completely fried. it’s not even one big thing, you know? it’s just… everything. last night was awful. we had carol and mark over for dinner – they’re good people, really genuinely good, but honestly, just *being* around them felt like climbing a mountain. they kept asking about my husband, david, and how he was doing, and i just… i don’t know how to talk about it. i don't want to talk about it. i just want to be quiet. and they kept offering advice, like, “you should try this,” “have you considered that?” and it just felt… invasive. i swear i’m not trying to be difficult, i really just need space.

Then this morning, my sister called. she’s always so relentlessly cheerful, you know? and she asked if i was “enjoying” taking care of david. enjoying?! Like, I’m exhausted and he needs help with *everything* – dressing, eating, even just going to the bathroom. and she just kept saying how lucky i am to have him, and how grateful i should be. I just… i wanted to scream. i didn't, of course, i just mumbled something about being busy. i don't even know why i bother answering her calls sometimes.

I just feel so… exposed, i guess? Like everyone is watching me and judging me for not being… better. better at managing, better at being happy, better at pretending everything is okay. idk. maybe i should just stay in bed all day. seriously. maybe that's the best thing to do. i just feel so overwhelmed by everything. it’s like a constant pressure, building and building. and i’m just… not sure how much longer i can keep doing this.

Okay, seriously, I need to just vent for a sec. It’s not even like I *hate* people, or anything. It’s just…everything. Last night was a disaster. Sarah’s birthday dinner, and I felt like I was performing a play the whole time. Like, everyone was telling stories about their amazing vacations and promotions and new dogs and I was just nodding and smiling and trying to remember what I had for breakfast. It's exhausting. And then Mark asked me about my weekend, and I just blurted out that I mostly just reorganized my closet. He asked if I needed help, and I just said no, really quickly. I just wanted it to be over.

And today? Ugh. Liam invited me to a networking thing, which, fine, I go to those, but everyone is just *so* confident and knows exactly what they’re doing. They’re talking about strategies and connections and ‘building your brand’ and I’m just standing there feeling like a complete fraud. I actually started sweating. Like, a full-blown panic sweat. I just wanted to disappear. I keep replaying every interaction in my head, analyzing every awkward pause and every stupid thing I said. It’s getting ridiculous. I just wish I could be normal, you know? Just…not feel this way all the time when I’m around other people. It’s like I'm constantly on edge, waiting for someone to judge me or realize I'm a complete mess. I’m just…over it. Seriously, over it.

okay seriously i don’t even know what’s happening anymore. it’s just… a lot. like a constant, low-grade burn that never really goes away. i haven’t slept properly in days, mostly just fitful naps when the baby’s finally asleep and leo is… well, leo is being leo. he just doesn't get it, you know? he keeps saying i’m being too hard on myself, that everyone’s stressed, but it’s not the same. it’s not just a little bit stressed. it’s like… everything is piling up and i can’t seem to move it.

we had a fight last night, a really stupid fight about the laundry. he just kept saying i was being unreasonable and demanding, and i just… snapped. i yelled, i cried, i don’t even remember what i actually said, but it was awful. and then he stormed off to his room and slammed the door. now he won’t talk to me. and the baby’s just… well, the baby's screaming, which isn’t helping, obviously.

i feel like i’m failing at everything. i’m failing as a mom, i’m failing as a wife, i’m failing as a person. i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. i keep thinking about how exhausted i am, how completely drained. it's not even the physical exhaustion, though that’s bad too, it’s more like… a soul-weariness. like i’ve just run a marathon and then someone keeps throwing things at me. i feel so alone with this. i just wish someone could just… understand. i don’t know how much longer i can keep doing this.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s the anniversary today… feels like yesterday and a million years ago all at once. just… so much. the baby’s sleeping finally, but i can’t shake this heavy feeling. honestly just want to disappear for a minute.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: okay seriously i don’t even know how i’m supposed to do this like i’m completely losing it. exams are next week and i’m behind on everything. i swear i’ve been staring at the same textbook for like six hours straight and i haven’t actually learned anything. it’s just…words. a blur. and then top of that i just moved into this tiny apartment downtown it’s supposed to be “cool” and “urban” but it’s just…loud. and cramped. and the fridge is basically a shoebox. i unpacked everything yesterday and it took me like ten hours and i’m still missing a box of socks. my roommate, elara, is nice i think but she has this really intense obsession with collecting vintage teacups and she talks about them constantly. like, for an hour straight sometimes. i tried to study last night but i just ended up zoning out listening to her monologue about the provenance of a chipped porcelain rose. i feel like i’m failing at everything. like i’m just not cut out for this adulting thing. i keep thinking about just going home packing everything up and driving back to my parents’ place it would be so much easier. but then i’d just be falling behind too, right? and then i’d feel even worse. i just need a nap. a really long nap. maybe two. seriously though, this is too much. i just want to disappear for a little while. i’m so tired.

it’s just… quiet here. like, really quiet. everyone else seems to be doing something, hanging out, laughing. i saw liam and chloe on instagram at that new arcade yesterday and i just… froze. i don't even know what to say to them anymore. it’s like i’m on a different planet. my phone’s been buzzing with notifications but i just keep scrolling past them. it's stupid i know, but i feel this huge pressure, like i'm supposed to be *doing* something, connecting, but i just can’t seem to find the words or the energy. i wish i wasn't so invisible. everyone else seems to have their tribes and i'm just… floating. it’s getting harder to even get out of bed some days. i just want someone to notice me, even just a little bit.

seriously i can’t even think straight tonight. like my brain is just… buzzing. i tried to study for that history test but i just kept staring at the textbook and then falling asleep. then i’d wake up and be even more tired. it’s just a loop. my mom keeps saying “just get some sleep” but it’s not that simple you know? it’s like my body just refuses. i feel like i’m running on fumes and i’m going to completely fail everything. everyone else seems to be handling this whole senior year thing so much better and i’m just… here. completely lost and exhausted. i just want to crawl into bed and not wake up but then i’d have to go to school tomorrow. ugh. this is awful.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so… this is weird to even type, like, putting it out there feels…wrong? I don’t even know where to start, honestly. It's just… everything. It’s been, like, a month since, you know… well, since it ended. And it feels like a year. I keep expecting him to just… show up, or call, or something. But he doesn’t. And that’s the worst part, I think. Not the shouting, or the tears, or anything specific – though those were definitely awful. It’s just the quiet. The complete, echoing quiet where there used to be… him.

I’m supposed to be “moving on,” everyone keeps saying it. My sister sends me articles about self-care and finding new hobbies. My mom keeps suggesting I redecorate. But I just… I don’t *do* anything. I scroll through Instagram, which is awful, because everyone’s lives look so perfect and shiny and I feel like I’m just stuck in a gray puddle. I tried to go back to my pottery class, but I just couldn’t focus. It felt… disrespectful, somehow, to be making something beautiful when I feel like I’m falling apart.

I keep telling myself it’s normal to feel this way, that grief is a process, whatever. But it’s not *just* grief, is it? It’s like… a low-level hum of anxiety that’s always there. Like I’m perpetually waiting for something bad to happen. I don’t even know why I’m feeling this way, honestly. There wasn’t one big fight, no dramatic declaration. It just… faded. Like a slowly leaking balloon. Maybe I should call someone? But who do you even talk to about… this? Just feeling… lost. Seriously, idk what’s wrong with me. I just… need a nap, I guess. Or maybe just to not think about anything at all.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: it’s just… quiet here. too quiet. everyone else is moving on, getting jobs, building lives and i’m just… back in my childhood bedroom. feels like i’m watching everyone else live their lives through a screen. honestly, i don’t even know what to do anymore.

okay seriously i don't even know why i'm typing this but i just… i need to get it out. it's been like six weeks since i lost my job and honestly i’m just… avoiding everything. like i keep seeing people on instagram or tiktok just out, doing stuff, laughing, and it just hits me like a brick wall. it’s not that i *want* to go out, it’s just that i feel like i'm supposed to. everyone expects me to be okay, to be “moving on” or whatever, and i’m not. i feel like a ghost.

last night my sister invited me to dinner with her and her boyfriend and i just… i couldn't. i texted her back saying i wasn't feeling well and honestly i just felt sick to my stomach thinking about it. she seemed a little confused, which is probably fair, but it’s just… everything feels awkward. like i’m an outsider looking in on a life that's still happening.

i tried to call my mom the other day too, just to chat, but i froze. i kept rambling about the weather and then i just stopped. she asked if i was okay and i just mumbled something about being tired. i hate that. i hate that i can’t even bring myself to say how awful i feel. it’s like the effort of even pretending to be normal is just too much. i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. i don’t know what to do anymore. it’s just… a lot. a really, really big lot. i just wish it would stop feeling like this. i just want to feel… normal again, i guess. but i don’t even know what that is anymore.

it’s just… quiet here. everyone’s at parties or hanging out with their friends and i’m just… staring at my phone. feels like everyone else is living their best life and i’m just stuck in slow motion. wish i could just disappear for a little bit.

it’s just… so much. i keep switching shifts and it’s like i’m always playing catch up but never actually getting anywhere. the kids are screaming, not like, all the time but enough that it’s just a low hum of chaos constantly in the background. and mom… god, mom. she’s been calling every night, mostly just to complain about something, anything really. the grocery store being out of her favorite brand of tea, the neighbor’s dog barking, the weather – it’s relentless. i try to be patient, i really do, but it’s like she just wants to suck the life out of me. and then my husband… he's just… distant. like he’s operating on a different frequency. i asked him about it last night, and he just said he's tired. i am *so* tired. i just want to disappear sometimes, you know? like, not actually disappear, just… not be here. it's like i'm running on fumes, fueled by lukewarm coffee and the sheer terror of messing up something else. i keep thinking about all the things i forgot to do at work today, all the paperwork piling up. it’s just… a lot. i feel this tightness in my chest all the time now, and my head is always buzzing. i just want a moment of quiet, a single, stupid moment of quiet. is that too much to ask?

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text:
Okay seriously, I don’t even know where to start. Like, everything feels… off. It’s been a week since Liam and I, you know, broke up and it’s not like a clean break or anything, more like a slow, agonizing unraveling. I keep replaying everything in my head, you know? Little things, big things, stupid things – it’s just a constant loop of “what if” and “should I have.” I thought we were good, really good, I mean we’d been together for almost two years and he said he loved me, like, really loved me. And now... nothing. Just this huge, gaping hole where everything used to be.

And on top of that, exams are starting next week. Organic Chem is going to be the death of me, honestly. I haven't even started the practice problems because I just… I can't focus. I keep staring at my textbook and it just looks like a meaningless jumble of equations. It's like my brain has just completely switched off. I tried to study last night but I just ended up crying for like an hour. It’s embarrassing, I know, but it’s just… so much. I feel like I’m failing at everything. Like I’m failing at being a student, failing at being a girlfriend, failing at just… existing. My roommate keeps asking if I’m okay, which is nice, I guess, but it’s hard to explain. She just doesn’t get it, you know? It's not like I can just "snap out of it." It’s just… heavy. This whole thing is just making me feel so utterly depleted, like I have nothing left to give. I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. Maybe I should just drop out of everything. Ugh, I’m so tired.

Ugh, seriously, I don't even know where to start. This project at work is just…a monster. It’s supposed to be “streamlined,” but it’s just a tangled mess of requirements and expectations that keep changing. My boss keeps saying “urgent” but then doesn’t actually tell me *what* is urgent. Like, is it the presentation next week? The report due tomorrow? I’m spending my entire life staring at a screen, fueled by caffeine and anxiety. I keep redoing things, obsessing over tiny details, and then I just feel completely paralyzed.

And then there’s the online course. I'm already behind, and I'm pretty sure half the stuff is just designed to make us feel stupid. I just want to throw my laptop out the window. My roommate keeps asking me if I'm okay, and I just give her a weak smile and say “fine.” It’s exhausting pretending everything’s normal. I keep telling myself to just take it one step at a time, but it feels like climbing a mountain with weights tied to my ankles. I haven’t slept properly in days, and I’m pretty sure I’m starting to forget what real food tastes like. I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. I feel like I'm failing at everything, and I don’t even know how to fix it. Honestly, I just need a nap. A really, really long nap.

text: okay seriously i don’t even know where to start. it’s just… so much. i feel like i’m constantly running on fumes. i finished my degree like, six weeks ago and everyone keeps saying “now you’re an adult” and “it’s time to find a job” and i’m just sitting here staring at my bedroom wall. mom keeps asking about applications and uncle mark keeps telling me about his company which is, like, a plumbing supply warehouse. plumbing. i just… i wanted to write. i wanted to make things. i don’t even know if i *want* to work a normal job, honestly. this internship at the gallery is supposed to be “stepping stone” but it’s mostly just endless packing and unpacking and trying to keep the paint from drying out before i can finish a piece. and the deadlines are insane. the curator keeps saying “push yourself” which is basically code for “collapse under the weight of your ambition”. i haven’t slept properly in days, i'm just scrolling through tiktok and eating cold pizza. i keep thinking about all the things i *should* be doing, all the steps i need to take, and it just… it feels like a brick wall. like if i even breathe too hard i'm going to crumble. i just need a break. a real one. not just scrolling through memes. i just wish i knew how to make this all stop. it’s not even fun anymore, it's just… exhausting. i feel like i'm failing at being an adult even though i just *graduated*. ugh.

Okay, so like, this is really hard to even type, but I need to vent i guess? It’s just… everything is so messed up lately, and it's all connected to sleep, I think. Or maybe it’s just that I can’t sleep, and that’s making everything else feel worse? I honestly don't even know anymore. I've been trying to get to bed at a decent time, like 10 or 11, but then my brain just… goes. It’s like a record skipping, or a broken toy that just keeps repeating the same annoying noise. I’ll be staring at the ceiling, and suddenly I’m thinking about that stupid history test next week, and then the fact that I haven't talked to Liam in like, three days, and then my mom's been giving me the silent treatment because I didn’t do my chores, and then I'm just… panicking.

I tried reading, but my eyes just glaze over. I tried meditating, but I just ended up thinking about how bad I am at meditating. Seriously, I’m a terrible meditator. Maybe I should just give up on trying to sleep. But then I’ll be exhausted tomorrow and even *worse* at school. It's a really awful cycle, you know? I feel like I'm constantly failing at something, and it’s not even important. I keep telling myself to just relax, but it feels impossible. I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear, like, maybe it would be easier if i just didn't wake up. It's probably just the stress, right? Or maybe I'm just really, really tired. I’m so tired of feeling this way. I don’t know what to do. I just… I wish I could just feel normal again, i guess.

text:
Okay, so like, this is… a lot. I don’t even know where to start. It's just… everything’s piling up, you know? The divorce was awful, obviously, it was a mess and I’m still processing it, honestly. It feels like yesterday and like a lifetime ago all at the same time. I keep replaying things, stupid things, things I shouldn’t have said, things I should have done differently. But it’s pointless, right? It’s done. Or, at least, supposed to be.

Then, last week, Mom had that scare. Just a little dizzy spell, they said, probably just stress, but… it got me thinking. Seriously, like, the *worst* kind of thinking. I’ve always been a worrier, always imagining the worst-case scenario. And now it’s just… amplified. I keep checking her, constantly. I called her three times yesterday just to hear her voice, and she was totally fine, just a bit annoyed. I feel so guilty about that.

I’m supposed to be focusing on getting back on my feet, finding a new place, trying to be okay on my own. But it's so hard. It's like I'm trying to build a new life while simultaneously trying to hold onto the fragments of the old one. And now this... Mom. It's just... a lot. I’m trying to be strong, I really am, but some days, I just feel like I’m barely keeping it together. I don’t even know how I’m going to make it through the weekend. Maybe I’ll just stay in bed. I don’t know. Idk what to do. Just... overwhelmed, I guess.  It’s like a weight, constantly pressing down. Maybe it will pass, but right now, it just feels… permanent.

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a dumb thing to be posting, but I just… I feel really, really stuck. It's not even a huge, dramatic thing, you know? It’s just… everything. I graduated like, six weeks ago? And everyone else seems to be *doing* something. Sarah’s already got this amazing job at that marketing firm, Liam’s traveling through Europe, even Chloe’s just, like, starting a pottery class and posting cute pictures. And me? I’m back here in my childhood bedroom, staring at the ceiling. My parents are, of course, super supportive, which is great, truly, but it’s also… intense. They keep asking about job applications, and “what’s next?”, and I just… idk. I’ve applied to a few things, mostly just admin stuff, nothing really exciting.

It’s just… lonely. I miss having people to, like, *do* things with. We used to hang out all the time in college, you know? Board game nights, late-night study sessions, just generally being a chaotic mess together. Now it’s just me and the TV, and I’m starting to feel like I’m just… fading. I try to call people, but it’s awkward, right? Like, I don’t want to just complain about being bored and unemployed. Maybe I should just volunteer somewhere? But even *that* feels like a big commitment, and honestly, I just don't have the energy. I keep telling myself it’s temporary, that things will get better, that I’ll find something, anything. But a part of me… maybe it’s a small part, but it’s there… just worries that this is it. Like, this is my new normal. Just… existing. I don’t know. Sorry for rambling, I just needed to say something.

okay seriously i don't even know where to start. it's just... a lot. like a tidal wave of everything. i keep staring at little leo and just... crying. not like sobbing, just these quiet little streams. he's the best thing ever you know? the cutest little guy with his little button nose and the way he grabs my finger. but then... then i look at elias and everything just feels so heavy. we talked last night, you know? really talked. or i tried to. it was like trying to build a sandcastle against the tide. he said he needs space. space? after six months? after we promised forever? it feels like he’s just... leaving. not like he’s actively hurting me, it’s just... he’s not *here* anymore. not really. and leo needs both of us. he needs a dad, a partner, someone to read him bedtime stories and change his diapers and just… be. i feel like i'm failing him already. and failing myself. i'm exhausted. physically, emotionally, mentally. i haven't slept properly in days. i keep waking up in the middle of the night just staring at the ceiling, feeling this awful, hollow ache. i just want it to stop. i want things to go back to how they were, but i know that's impossible, isn't it? it's just... so much. so much to handle alone. i just need a hug. a real hug. not just leo's little sticky hugs. ugh. i just don’t know what i’m going to do.

Just...can't sleep. Everything feels so loud. Like my brain is just spinning and I can't stop it. Used to have him to fall asleep with, now it's just me and the silence and this awful, empty feeling. Ugh.

okay so like this is just… a lot. seriously a *lot*. i haven't slept properly in days i think. it’s mostly just the… the fallout. you know? it’s not even really about him anymore, not really. it’s about my mom. and my brother. they’re both… they’re both just completely unreasonable. it started with a phone call last week, a really nasty one, about who gets what from the house. which is ridiculous, it was supposed to be split fairly, right? but then my mom started bringing up *everything*. every single argument we ever had, every perceived slight, every stupid thing i ever said. she just kept piling it on. and my brother, he’s just… mirroring her. just agreeing with everything she says, making it sound like it’s all my fault. it's like a loop. a horrible, endless loop of accusations and disappointment. i keep trying to explain that i’m just trying to move on, that i need some space, but it’s like talking to a brick wall. i feel like i'm constantly walking on eggshells, terrified of saying the wrong thing and setting them off again. i just want to disappear, honestly. just vanish and not have to deal with any of this. i keep replaying everything in my head, wondering what i could have done differently, what i could have said. it's exhausting. i feel so utterly alone, like nobody understands how completely drained i am. i just wish they'd just… leave me alone. please.

text:
Okay seriously, I don’t even know how to start this. It’s just… so much. Everyone keeps telling me to “just breathe” like it’s some magic cure, but it’s not, you know? It’s like trying to hold back a tidal wave with a teacup. My family… it’s just been awful lately. Mom’s been on this constant rant about me not calling enough, and Dad’s just… silent, which is worse, honestly. Like he’s deliberately ignoring me. And then my little brother, Leo, he's just… being Leo, you know? Asking a million questions, needing constant attention, and then getting frustrated when I can’t give it. It's like I’m being pulled in a million different directions.

I have these huge exams coming up, like, the final exams for everything, and I’m barely sleeping, and I’m eating way too much ramen, and I just keep staring at my notes and they just… blur. I tried to talk to Sarah about it, but she just said “look, you always stress about everything” and honestly, it just makes me feel worse. Like she doesn’t even get it. It’s not that I *always* stress, it's just... *right now* it's this massive, overwhelming pressure. I feel like I’m failing at everything. Like I'm not good enough at anything, not even being a decent daughter or a student. I just want to disappear, you know? Just vanish for a little while and not have to deal with any of it. It’s exhausting. I just need a break, a real, honest to God break, but how do you even take a break when everything is screaming at you all at once?
