text:
Okay, so like, this is probably weird to write about, but I just… I don’t even know where to start. Mom called last night, and she was freaking out. Said she’d noticed I hadn’t been eating much, and that my skin looked really pale, and that I seemed… distant. Which, you know, is totally fair. I haven’t been exactly radiating sunshine lately. She said she was worried about me, and honestly, I kind of was too. Not like, a panicked, screaming worried, but more like a quiet, sinking feeling.

I went to the doctor today, and they did some blood work. I'm just waiting for the results, and honestly, I’m a mess. It’s probably nothing, right? It *has* to be nothing. I’ve been so stressed, obviously, you know? Graduating was a huge deal, and now I’m just… stuck. Back here with my parents, still trying to figure out what to do with my degree, and feeling like I'm failing at everything. I’m working part-time at the bookstore, which is fine, but it's not exactly setting the world on fire.

And then this. This blood test. It just feels like one more thing, you know? Like if something *is* wrong, it’s just… piling on. I keep thinking about all the things I haven’t done, all the people I haven’t called, all the things I *should* be doing, and it’s just… exhausting. I feel like I'm carrying the weight of the world on my shoulders, and it's just... heavy. I don’t even know what I’m feeling, really. Just… overwhelmed. I just want everything to go back to normal, even if “normal” was a really bad idea to begin with. I just wish I could feel *okay*. Maybe I just need a nap. Or a really, really big coffee.

text: okay, so like, the exam is next week and i’m just… completely lost. i don’t even know where to start, idk if i’ll be able to finish studying properly. it’s just so much, maybe i should just drop out, ugh. feeling totally overwhelmed, honestly.

Ugh, tonight was brutal. Seriously, I just need to disappear. I work the graveyard shift, you know, and it’s already bad enough, but last night… everyone just wanted to talk. Like, *really* talk. Or at least, they wanted me to pretend I was listening. Sarah from accounting cornered me for twenty minutes about her cat’s weird eating habits – seriously? And then Mark from IT started rambling about his fantasy football league, which, okay, fine, whatever, but he kept asking me if *I* had any strategies. Like I have time for fantasy football! I’m trying to process the fact that I haven’t seen sunlight in 36 hours.

And then Brenda from HR wanted to “catch up.” Catch up on what? My existential dread? I just gave her a polite nod and mumbled something about needing to refill the coffee machine. It's always the same. Everyone assumes because I’m quiet and work alone that I’m okay. They don’t see the way my chest feels like it's shrinking, or how my brain just… shuts down after a while. It’s not that I don’t *want* to be social, it’s that just… existing around people when I’m this exhausted feels like climbing a mountain. I feel like I’m constantly failing at being a normal person. Just… please leave me alone. I just want to go home and stare at the wall. Seriously, I think I might just start wearing a hoodie and sunglasses to work tomorrow and hope people take the hint.

text: okay so like seriously, I don’t even know why I’m typing this but I just… I feel like I’m going to implode. It’s not even the exams, not really, though they’re obviously a huge part of it, it’s everything *around* the exams. Like, last night I had to go to Sarah’s birthday party and it was… a disaster. A complete, utter disaster. I tried to be normal, you know? I smiled, I laughed, I asked about her new job, which is great, actually, she totally deserves it, and I even managed a semi-coherent conversation about… I don’t even remember what we talked about, something about cats probably. But the whole time, the whole entire time, I felt this overwhelming pressure, like I was wearing a suit made of lead or something. Everyone was just… talking, and laughing, and it felt like I was observing it all from behind a thick pane of glass, you know? Like I was a ghost. And then Liam started talking about his trip to Iceland and I just… blanked. Completely blanked. He kept trying to draw me in, asking if I wanted to hear about the glaciers, and I just mumbled something about being tired and had to leave. Which is true, obviously, but it felt so… rude. And then I spent the rest of the night just scrolling through my phone, avoiding eye contact, just desperately trying to disappear. I just wish I could be present, like, actually *be* there with people, but it’s just… too much. It's like every social interaction is this huge, exhausting performance and I’m just failing miserably. I just want to curl up in my room and never leave again, honestly, but I have to keep going, right? I *have* to study. Ugh. This is so stupid. I'm just so stressed about everything, even the little things, like going to a party. It’s ridiculous, I know, but it’s just… a lot.

text: Okay, seriously, I haven’t slept properly in, like, weeks. Not really slept. Just… been staring at the ceiling, you know? Trying to count sheep, which is ridiculous because I can’t even *do* that without thinking about everything that needs to be done. It’s just… a constant loop. He needs me to make sure he takes his meds, and then I have to check if he’s eaten, and then I have to make sure he doesn’t wander off, and then I need to do laundry, and then the bills are piling up and I swear I’m forgetting things, like where I put my keys, or sometimes even his glasses. It's not even the big things, it’s the little stuff, the tiny details that just keep spinning around in my head until I can’t even think straight. I keep telling myself it's just temporary, that he’ll get better, and that *I’ll* get better, but it feels like it’s just… settling in. Like a damp, heavy blanket. I feel so exhausted all the time, like I'm running on fumes, and I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. I know I shouldn’t complain, he’s going through so much too, but it’s just… a lot. I keep thinking about how much easier things were before, before this happened, before he needed me this much. I just wish I could just… breathe. Seriously, just one normal night's sleep. That’s all I’m asking for, isn’t it? It feels impossible though, just utterly impossible. I’m starting to feel like a failure, like I’m not even capable of taking care of him, and it’s just… overwhelming. I just want to be okay.

text:
Okay seriously, I’m losing it. This paper is due Monday and I haven’t even really started properly. I thought I was being smart, like, spreading it out, you know? But now it’s just… looming. Everything feels blurry. I keep staring at the screen and just… nothing. Like my brain just completely shuts off. It's stupid, right? Everyone else seems to be handling it better. My roommate back home was telling me about her finals and it just felt like a competition I’m already losing.

I moved here for a fresh start, right? To get away from everything. And it *was* good for a little while, I really liked the coffee shops and the weird bookstores. But now, all I can think about is this paper. And the looming feeling that I'm going to fail. My landlord just sent another notice about rent, and honestly, I’m already behind. I don’t even have the energy to look for a new job, let alone finish this assignment. I feel like I’m drowning, just slowly sinking under all this work and this feeling of…pointlessness, I guess? I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. It's not even a big paper, just a history thing, but it feels huge. Like it’s going to define everything. I just...need to sleep, I think. Or maybe just stop looking at my laptop.

text: okay so like seriously this is a mess i moved into this tiny apartment last week and it’s just…a lot. i thought it would be this cool, fresh start, you know? like finally getting out of my parents’ basement and being an adult. but it’s not. it’s just…more. everything is harder than i thought it would be. setting up the internet was a nightmare, i’ve spent three days just trying to get the thermostat to work and it’s still freezing in here. and i keep forgetting to pay bills which is terrifying, i don’t even know where to start with that. i called my mom and she just kept saying “you’re doing great” which is like, the last thing i need right now. i miss having her just, i don’t know, tell me to take a deep breath or something. it’s just so quiet here too. back home there was always something going on, people talking, music playing, even just the TV. now it’s just…me and the silence and this weird feeling that i’m failing at being independent. i keep looking at my old room and feeling this pang of sadness, like i’m leaving something good behind. i’m trying to be positive, i really am, but i just feel like i’m constantly running on empty. i just wanted a fresh start, not a whole new set of problems. i just wish i could go back to just…existing. it's overwhelming honestly, like trying to build a whole new life out of mismatched Lego bricks.

text: Ugh, seriously, this is just... a lot. I hate being back here. Like, really hate it. Mom keeps saying I need to “find a job” but she doesn’t even *try* to help me look. She just critiques everything I do, every application, every conversation I have. It’s like she’s actively trying to make me feel like a failure. And Dad… don’t even get me started. He just sits there, silent, judging. It’s like I’m a disappointment to them both. I just wanted a little space, you know? A chance to breathe after graduation, to figure things out without everyone’s opinions constantly swirling around me. But no, it’s always “you’re not doing enough,” “you’re wasting your potential,” “why can’t you just be happy?” Seriously, can people just *leave* me alone for five minutes? I feel like I’m suffocating. I keep snapping at everyone, and I know it’s my fault, but it's just... exhausting. I keep replaying everything in my head, every argument, every sideways glance, and it just gets worse. I just want to disappear, honestly. Not like, permanently, but just... somewhere quiet where they can't bother me. I keep telling myself things will get better, but right now, it just feels like a huge, messy weight on my chest. I’m so tired.

text: just... everything feels wrong. like the color's been drained out of the world. he just stopped responding and i don't know what to do. mom keeps asking if i'm eating enough, it's so stupid and i just want to hide under the covers.

Okay seriously, I don’t even know where to start. Mom had a dizzy spell yesterday. Just…fell over. Luckily she wasn’t hurt, just really shaken up, but the doctor said it could be something serious. Something about her heart, he was being really vague but all I heard was “potential arrhythmia” and “need to run more tests.” Tests. More tests. It's like this is all she ever is – needing tests. 

I’m exhausted. I’ve been taking her everywhere, making sure she eats, reminding her to take her meds, just…holding her hand sometimes when she gets scared. It’s been like this for years, but lately it’s just…constant. I used to have a life, you know? A job, hobbies, friends. Now it’s just me and her. And honestly, I’m starting to feel like I’m disappearing. 

I tried to go to the grocery store today and I just… froze. Like a deer in headlights. I couldn’t even remember what I needed, let alone get through the checkout. I had to go back later, just to get milk and bread. It’s stupid, I know, but it just feels like everything is piling up and I'm completely overwhelmed. I just need a minute. Just five. To breathe. I keep thinking about all the things I didn’t do, all the places I didn’t go, and it’s just… heavy. I just wish someone else could help. Seriously. Just one thing.

text: okay so like seriously everything is just... too much lately i don't even know where to start. my grandma she's been in the hospital, like, for the past week and it's just been a constant barrage of calls and texts and feeling like i should be doing *something* but i don’t even know what. she tripped, apparently, and hit her head really hard and they’re saying she might have a concussion or something worse, and honestly i'm just terrified. my mom's been a wreck, obviously, trying to coordinate everything with my grandpa and the hospital and the nurses and it’s just this huge weight on her shoulders and i feel like i should be helping more, but i’m just… paralyzed. i keep staring at my history textbook and it just looks like a bunch of meaningless words and formulas and i can’t even focus on it. i have this huge history test coming up next week and i haven't even started studying because i just keep thinking about my grandma and if she’s okay and if they’re going to tell us something awful. i tried to eat dinner tonight, but i just pushed it all around my plate. it felt like i couldn't even enjoy something as simple as food. and then my best friend, Liam, he texted me saying he's going to that concert we've been talking about for months and i just... immediately felt awful. like i was letting him down, and then i felt awful for feeling like i was letting my grandma down, and then i just felt… numb. i just want it to stop, you know? just for like, five minutes, i just want five minutes of peace. i keep replaying everything in my head and worrying about everything that could possibly go wrong. it’s exhausting. i just feel so incredibly stressed and overwhelmed and like i’m failing at everything.

Ugh. Just… ugh. Another night, another shift, another blurry face staring back at me. It’s weird, like the world just keeps spinning even when everything inside me feels like it’s grinding to a halt. Liam… he just… left. No big fight, no screaming, just… quiet. Packed his bags and walked out. Said he needed space. Space from *me*. Honestly, I don't even know why I’m upset. It just… hurts. Like a dull ache behind my ribs. I keep replaying stupid things I said, things I didn’t say. This job is already a nightmare, and now this. Just want to curl up and disappear. Coffee’s not helping. Seriously, not helping at all.

Okay, seriously. Just...wow. It's like someone flipped a switch and everything is suddenly wrong. Liam just… left. No big fight, no dramatic yelling, just…gone. He said he needed space, which is code for “I found someone shinier.” I don’t get it. We were fine. Well, as fine as they ever get, I guess. I’ve been doing this caregiving thing for fifteen years, you know? Since he was a kid. Raised him, kept him on track, made sure he had everything. And now? Now I’m just… leftover. Like an old sweater someone threw out.

I keep replaying everything in my head, trying to find where I went wrong. Did I not do enough? Did I smother him? I always just wanted him to be happy, you know? To have a good life. And now he’s running off to have a ‘good life’ without me. It’s stupid. It's really, really stupid.

The house is too quiet. Always too quiet. He used to be home by six, always. Now…nothing. I keep expecting him to walk in, to tell me he’s sorry and he’ll be back. But he won’t. I’m exhausted. Physically, emotionally, everything. I don’t even have the energy to be angry. Just… numb. I keep staring at his side of the bed and just… wishing he was there. It’s just… a lot. More than I can handle, honestly. I need a coffee. A really, really strong coffee.

okay just… ugh. it’s been a week and it still feels like someone punched me in the gut. i keep replaying everything, you know? stupid little things i said, things i didn't say. it’s like my brain just keeps looping. my mom keeps calling, saying “you’ll find someone new,” and i just… i don’t even want to talk about it. i cancelled all my plans with my friends too, which is awful because i miss them so much but i just don’t have the energy to pretend. everything feels gray and heavy. i’m just trying to get through each day, honestly. i keep staring at the empty space where his things used to be and it just… hurts. i don’t even know what to do anymore.

text: Okay, so like, this is insane. Seriously, I don’t even know where to start. I lost my job last month, which was bad enough, you know? It wasn't even a *great* job, honestly, but it was a job and I needed it. And then, like, two weeks later, Mom started having these weird pains, she went to the doctor and it turns out she has something, some kind of thing with her gallbladder, and she needs surgery. The surgery’s scheduled for next month, which is, like, terrifying. The hospital bills are going to be astronomical, I can just tell. Dad’s already stressed about everything, and he's got his own health problems simmering under the surface too, you know? He's just...quiet lately, like really quiet. And I feel this huge weight, this pressure just crushing me, like I have to fix everything, solve all the problems, be the strong one, but I’m completely depleted. I keep replaying everything that happened at work, all the stupid little mistakes I made, wondering if I could have done something differently, you know? It's just...too much. I haven't been sleeping, I’m eating takeout constantly, and I just feel this constant, dull ache in my chest. I tried calling my sister, but she's got a baby and a new job and she just said, “Oh, honey, that sucks,” and hung up. It's not that she doesn’t care, I know she does, it’s just…it feels like no one really *gets* it. I just want to curl up and disappear, honestly. It's like a constant low-level hum of anxiety, just vibrating through my whole body. I don't even know how I'm going to manage this whole thing.  Just...overwhelmed. Completely and utterly overwhelmed.

Ugh, Mom's been acting weird all day. She’s been coughing *a lot* and just…down. Like, really down. Dad keeps saying it’s probably nothing, allergies or something, but I just feel like something’s seriously wrong. I've been trying to help her with stuff, making her tea, calling her doctor’s office (they won’t answer!), but it’s just making me feel even more useless and stressed. I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing – homework, studying for the history test – but I can’t even focus. It's just...heavy. I just wish I knew what was happening and how to actually *help* her. Everything feels so fragile right now. I just want it to be okay.

text: Okay so this is just… a lot, seriously. I moved here like, six weeks ago and it feels like everyone already has their lives figured out, you know? Like they're all at these parties and going to these cool restaurants and just… thriving and i'm just sitting here in my tiny apartment staring at the wall. I tried to join a hiking group but everyone already knew each other and it was just awkward, super awkward. I made a friend online, Liam, but we just messaged back and forth for a few days and then he stopped replying. It’s not even like I said anything weird, I just… exist, i guess? It's hard to explain, it’s like being a ghost, you know? Just watching everyone else live and not being part of it. I keep telling myself it’ll get better, that I’ll make friends, but tonight feels particularly heavy, like a weight in my chest just refusing to let go. I just want someone to… you know… just be here.

text: Just… everything is just *so much* right now, you know? Like, all the readings, all the practice questions, the looming deadlines, and just this constant feeling of being behind and not even sure what I’m doing wrong, it’s just a huge weight and i feel like i can’t even breathe properly, honestly.

Seriously, it’s just… exhausting. Mom’s convinced she knows best about everything, even after I’ve explained it five times. And Dad? He just sits there, nodding and agreeing with her, like I’m the crazy one. It's like they’re deliberately trying to make this harder than it already is. I’m constantly mediating, explaining, apologizing – for what, I don’t even know half the time. I just want a few minutes of peace, you know? Just to breathe. I feel like I’m walking on eggshells all the time, terrified of saying the wrong thing and starting another fight. I just wish they’d listen to me for once. It's not about being difficult, it’s about… just trying to keep the family together, I guess.

text: Okay seriously, I don’t even know where to start. Like, everything is just... a mess. Liam is screaming for milk, again, and I swear he does it just to annoy me. My mom keeps sending over casseroles which is so sweet but it’s just… more to deal with. And my own mom keeps saying "you need to rest!" like it's that simple. It's not simple. It’s just… exhausting. I just wish someone would just… understand that it’s not about *doing* everything perfectly, it’s just about getting through the day, and right now, getting through the day feels impossible. I keep thinking about just curling up and sleeping for a week, but then I’d be letting Liam down, right? It's just a loop. A really, really tiring loop.

text: Okay, so this is just… a lot. It’s not like I *didn’t* want to do this, you know? I mean, I volunteered, right? Said I’d help. And I did, I really did. But it’s… it’s not what I pictured. He’s… he’s a good man, really, really good, but he just… doesn’t talk much anymore. It’s been, what, five years now since… well, since things started to change. And I’m here, mostly. Just… here. Making sure he eats, taking him to his appointments, helping with the bills, trying to remember when he last took his medication – honestly, it’s a blur sometimes. My friends, they don’t understand. They say, “You need to take care of yourself!” And I know they're right, I *do*, but it’s hard to even remember what ‘me’ looks like anymore. I used to paint, you know? Landscapes, mostly. Bright, sunny landscapes. Now, I just stare at the walls.  It’s quiet here, terribly quiet. Except for him, of course. And the TV. Always the news. It’s depressing. I feel like I’m slowly fading away, just… blending into the wallpaper. Like I’m watching my own life happen, but I’m not really *in* it. I try to call my sister, but she’s busy with her kids, and honestly, I don't want to burden her with this. It’s just… a heavy weight, you know? Just a constant, dull ache. I just wish someone would see me. Really see me. Not just the caregiver, not just the helper, but… me.  I just feel so utterly, completely alone.

text: Okay, so like, seriously, this is a mess. I moved here six weeks ago, completely alone, and everything’s just…heavy. I thought being in a new city would be exciting, you know? Like, a fresh start and everything. But it’s just…loud. And expensive. And I keep feeling like I’m forgetting something important, like a key or a bill or…God, I don’t even know. But it’s always something. This physics exam is looming, this huge, monstrous thing, and I’m just, I’m just not getting it. I’ve been studying, honestly, like *hours* every day, but it’s just swirling around in my head and I can’t seem to grasp any of it. It’s like trying to hold water in my hands, just slipping right through. My roommate back home, Liam, he’d be laughing, he’d be telling me to just chill, but he doesn't understand, does he? He doesn’t understand this feeling of being totally adrift. I keep replaying everything I missed in lectures, and then I feel even worse, like I’m starting from zero. I keep staring at the textbook and it just…blurs. I keep wanting to just curl up and disappear, which is probably stupid, I know, but it feels like the only option sometimes. I just need to finish this exam, I really do, but it feels like climbing a mountain made of sand.  I just... I just wish I could breathe normally.  I'm so tired of feeling like I'm failing at everything, even just existing here.

It’s a vicious cycle. I feel like I’m going to forget everything. My brain is just… fuzzy.

I haven’t slept properly in days, just little bursts of four hours here and there. The graveyard shift is already brutal, but then I have to come home and *do this*? It's not fair. I just want to curl up and disappear. I keep thinking about failing, about letting everyone down, about Mom being disappointed. She worked so hard to help me get here. And honestly, I just feel completely exhausted. Like my battery is dead and I’m running on fumes.  I keep saying to myself “just finish one section,” but then I see another one and... ugh. I just need to breathe, I think. Or maybe just shut the laptop down and stare at the wall. I don't even know anymore. Just...overwhelmed. Really, really overwhelmed.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: It’s… it’s the anniversary. I can’t believe it’s actually been a year. A whole year since… well, since everything. I keep replaying it in my head, you know? Like a broken record, just the same awful scene over and over. I tried to, like, distract myself all day, went for a long walk, made a huge dinner, even called my mom, but it’s just… there. It’s always there, buzzing under everything. I feel this pressure, this tightness in my chest, and honestly, I just want to curl up and disappear. Work is insane too, deadlines looming, everyone’s stressed, and it’s like my brain just can’t handle it. It’s not that I'm *not* working, it’s just… everything feels amplified, like the volume’s turned up to eleven and I can't turn it down. I just need a nap, or maybe just… silence.

just finished my shift and i feel like my brain is scrambled eggs. honestly i don’t even remember half the stuff i did tonight. it’s just… too much. everyone expects so much and i’m just running on fumes. seriously need a nap.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
It's the anniversary. Feels like yesterday, honestly. Like a bad dream I can't quite shake. Everything’s…bright. Too bright. The flowers are gorgeous, the cake looks amazing, and everyone’s smiling like they’ve never seen a sad face. Which, you know, they haven’t. They just don't *know*. Liam keeps asking me how I’m feeling, and I just say “fine.” Fine. That’s all I can manage. It’s exhausting.

He’s got little Milo, our boy, in his arms, chubby and happy, and I just… I feel this weird knot in my stomach. It’s not hate, not really. It’s just… a heavy weight. Like I’m supposed to be overflowing with joy, with this new life, and I just can’t. It's like a switch is broken. I look at Milo and I want to protect him fiercely, of course, I do, but I also feel this overwhelming guilt. Like I should be *better*. Like I should be bouncing off the walls with excitement. 

I keep thinking about…before. Before Milo. It doesn't help. Just flashes. And then I'm back here, trying to be a good mom, trying to be present, but constantly pulled back to that awful moment. I just want to curl up in a dark room and disappear. I feel like I'm failing at everything. Failing at being a good partner, failing at being a good mom, failing at even just…existing. I just want it to be over. And then, I have to smile and pretend everything is okay. God, it’s a lot. Just...a lot.
Ugh, this is just… a lot. Lost my job, then my mom had to go to the hospital. Just feels like everything’s piling up and I can’t even breathe. Seriously, how am I supposed to deal with this?

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: It’s… it’s the anniversary. Stupid anniversary. I keep expecting to wake up and it’ll all be a bad dream, like it was for a while, you know? A really long, blurry dream where everything just… faded. But it doesn’t. It’s still here, sitting right beside me, this heavy blanket of remembering. I haven’t even bothered to change my shift tonight, just dragged myself in here to the warehouse, mostly because the quiet feels… calmer than the house. The silence is just as awful, though. It’s like the air itself is holding its breath. I keep staring at the conveyor belts, watching the boxes go by, and it’s like they’re representing everything I’ve been carrying around, all these little bits and pieces of what happened, just moving, moving, moving, never stopping. Honestly, I feel like I’m stuck in slow motion, just existing. Everyone else is just… normal. Like they don't have this thing, this constant hum of sadness that just won’t shut off. I tried to make a cake, a stupid, little chocolate cake, for… for them. Just to do something, you know? Something productive. It’s burnt, of course. Completely burnt. I didn't even bother to take the foil off properly, I just slammed it in the oven and left it. It’s symbolic, I guess. Everything feels symbolic now. Like I'm trying to build something solid out of smoke and regret. I just want it to stop. I just want to not feel this.  It’s exhausting, you know? Just… exhausting.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so this is… weird. I guess. I haven’t really talked to anyone in like, days? Seriously, days. It’s just… quiet. Really quiet. I keep checking my phone, but like, there’s nothing. No messages, no calls. I know everyone’s busy, and I totally get that, but it just feels… heavy. Like I’m just… floating. I don’t even know what to do with myself. I applied for a few jobs, but idk, it’s just exhausting. The thought of going out, even just to the grocery store, is exhausting. Maybe I should just order takeout again. Or maybe I shouldn't. It just feels pointless, you know? I just… miss having something to do, someone to talk to. It's stupid, I know, to feel this way. I just… I just feel so alone.

text: okay so like, seriously, this is a disaster. I just… I don’t even know where to start. I quit my job, which was, you know, a good job, a *really* good job, and then I thought moving to Oakhaven would be this fresh start, this chance to breathe, right? But it’s just… overwhelming. The apartment is tiny and smells faintly of mildew and the boxes are still everywhere, like a monument to my bad decisions. I keep staring at the blank walls and feeling this huge, hollow space inside me, like all the energy just drained out of me. I keep forgetting to eat, and I haven’t slept properly in days. It’s not even the moving itself, it’s… everything. Just the sheer weight of it all, you know? I feel like I'm constantly failing at something, even if I don’t know what that something is.

Seriously, this new town is just...a lot. Like, everything feels unfamiliar and I can't even find a decent coffee shop. Just trying to unpack and it's already making me want to hide under the covers. Ugh.

text: ugh i can't even. it’s like… a week ago today was the day everything changed, you know? and it just hit me like a brick. i keep replaying it in my head, like a broken record. i have these huge exams coming up, and i'm supposed to be studying but i just can’t focus. it’s like my brain is full of cotton wool. everyone else seems to be okay, just grinding away, but i feel like i’m just… stuck. the pressure is insane. i keep scrolling through my feed and everyone’s posting about happy things and it just makes it worse. i just want to disappear for a little bit, like, just breathe. i keep telling myself it’ll get better but honestly, it feels like it’s just getting heavier and heavier. i don’t even know how to get through this week.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, seriously, I just… I don’t even know why I bother going to parties anymore, like, it’s just everyone yelling and trying to talk to you all at once and it’s so loud and everyone seems to know everyone and I just feel so awkward, you know? I spent like an hour tonight just scrolling through my phone trying to find an excuse to leave, and then I felt guilty because I didn’t even want to leave *completely* – I don’t want to look like a total weirdo, but it’s just… a lot. My brain is buzzing afterwards, like static, and I just want to curl up in my room and disappear for the rest of the weekend, honestly. It's not that I *like* being around people, it's just... exhausting trying to keep up, to say the right thing, to not seem like I’m judging them or something. I wish I could just be invisible sometimes, just... not.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Ugh, just... everything feels heavier now. Moving back home wasn’t the plan, and it’s just a constant reminder. Like, I’m supposed to be building something, but all i’m doing is staring at the ceiling. It’s stupid.

Okay, seriously, this is a mess. Just…everything is a mess. Boxes everywhere, the apartment smells faintly of someone else’s cleaning products, and I keep finding things that were *his*. It’s not even the sentimental stuff, it’s just… reminders. Like a chipped mug or a book he used to read. I keep expecting him to be here, you know? Just standing in the doorway, saying something stupid. It’s quiet here, too quiet. I thought a fresh start would be…good. But it’s just loud with the sound of me trying to figure out how to be alone. I just need to unpack one drawer, that’s all. One drawer. Then maybe I can breathe.

Ugh, I just got back from Sarah’s birthday dinner. Seriously, don't even ask. It was... a lot. Everyone was so happy, laughing, talking about their lives, their trips, their promotions... and me? I just sat there nodding and smiling like a freaking robot. Like I’m not completely exhausted just *existing* around all that. Honestly, I felt like a prop. Like I was supposed to be genuinely thrilled for them, but I just... wasn’t. I think I’ve lost the ability to fake it.

My daughter, Leo, she’s brilliant, you know? She’s got this amazing art project due next week, and she’s stressing about it, of course. But she’s so focused, so passionate. And I'm just… trying to keep the apartment from falling apart and making sure she has dinner. It’s hard to be excited about anything when you’re constantly running on fumes. 

And then there’s Mark. He’s been quiet lately, just... staring. I asked if he was okay, and he just said he was tired. I know what that means. It means he’s carrying a weight too, and I’m not sure how to help him without adding to it. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop, constantly juggling everyone else’s problems and completely forgetting to take care of myself. I just need a minute. A real, quiet minute. Just to breathe. God, I hate these things. People and their happiness. It’s exhausting.

it's just…so much. staring at these monitors all night, the hum of the machines, the flickering lights, it's all blurring together. and then i get home and it’s the same thing. bills piled up, the electricity company just sent another threatening email, and i swear i’m working overtime just to keep the lights on. i tried to make a budget today but i just ended up crying. it feels like i’m running on fumes, like if i just take one more shift i’m going to completely break. i keep thinking about how much easier things would be if i didn’t have to worry about paying rent, about whether we can afford groceries. it’s stupid, i know, but it’s just… suffocating. i just wish i could breathe for a second.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: okay seriously i don't know how anyone does this anymore. it's just... a lot. i swear my boss is breathing down my neck every five minutes and the assignments are piling up faster than i can finish them. i keep falling behind on everything and i feel like i'm just constantly failing. mom keeps asking if i'm sleeping and eating properly which is just making it worse, like i'm already a mess. i tried to study last night but i just kept zoning out, thinking about all the deadlines and the pressure. i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. it's like i'm drowning in coursework and pointless meetings and i don't even know how to swim. i just need a break, a real one, not just scrolling through my phone.
Ugh, another late shift. Bills are piling up, and I just… I don’t know how much longer I can keep this up. Feels like I’m just spinning my wheels, going nowhere fast. Seriously stressed about it all.

ugh seriously i don’t even know how i’m gonna pay for this semester’s books. my parents are already stressed about everything and i feel like i’m just adding to it. it’s like a weight on my chest all the time just thinking about it. i wish i could just magically have more money.

okay so like seriously i don’t even know where to start. the boxes are still everywhere and i swear they’re multiplying. it’s been three days since the movers left and i haven’t really unpacked anything useful. mostly just stared at the walls and felt…heavy. i keep expecting to wake up and realize it’s all a really weird dream, you know? this new place is…fine i guess. it’s just…empty. and quiet. too quiet. like the walls are listening. my brain is just buzzing with a million little things i need to do, like, seriously a million. i’m supposed to be setting up the internet, finding a grocery store, figuring out the garbage schedule, and honestly i just keep scrolling through my phone. it’s pathetic i know. my boss is probably wondering why i haven't checked in, which is stupid because he doesn’t need to know. he just sees a shift worker who shows up. but it’s hard to show up when you feel like you’re underwater. i miss my old apartment, even though it was tiny and kinda depressing. at least it felt familiar. here everything is…sterile. and i keep catching myself just…breathing too loudly, like i’m disturbing the silence. i had a coffee this morning, like, five, and i just feel jittery and exhausted. i just want to curl up in a ball and not move for a week. maybe two. maybe forever. i keep thinking about calling mom but then i’m worried about bothering her and she's probably already stressed about her own stuff. it’s just a lot, you know? a massive, overwhelming, slightly terrifying amount of a lot.

text: it's the anniversary today… feels like yesterday it all happened. i keep replaying it in my head, stupidly. just… so much pressure and now it's just… here. i don’t even know what to do with myself. everything feels heavy.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Ugh, it’s… it’s the anniversary today. Of everything. I don’t even know why I’m typing this, honestly. It just keeps coming back to me, you know? Like a really bad song stuck on repeat. Just… flashes. I feel like I should be doing something, like, a proper memorial or something, but I just… I just can’t. It’s just… a lot. My daughter keeps asking if I’m okay, which is sweet but also makes it worse, I guess. I just feel… heavy. Like I’m carrying this weight that never goes away. Maybe I should call my sister? No, probably not. She’d just want to talk about happy things. I just want to… be numb, I think. Idk. I just need a quiet night. Maybe a cup of tea.

Okay seriously, I don’t even know where to start. It just… happened. Like, one minute we were arguing about something stupid – again – and the next he’s just… gone. Packed his things and left a note. A really short note. “It’s not working.” That’s it. No explanation, no anything. I’ve spent the last six hours just staring at the wall, and I’m pretty sure I’ve aged about ten years. My boss called, asking about that presentation, and I just mumbled something about being “busy.” Busy with what? With trying to figure out how to breathe?

Everything feels wrong. My phone keeps buzzing with notifications from him, and I automatically reach for it, like I still need to know what he’s doing, or if he’s… okay? Which is ridiculous, obviously. He’s not okay. And neither am I. I keep replaying everything in my head, trying to find where we went wrong, but it’s like a tangled mess of bad decisions and unspoken words. We were good, weren’t we? Or at least, we *seemed* good. I thought we were building something.

I just… I feel so utterly lost. Like I’ve lost my anchor, my north star, everything. I keep expecting him to walk through the door, to apologize, to say he made a mistake. But he won’t. And I know he won’t. It’s just… a lot. Like, a massive, crushing amount of a lot. I don't even want to look at my apartment anymore. It’s just full of reminders of him. I just need to sleep, I think. Or maybe just disappear for a while. Seriously, this is awful.

text: Ugh, seriously, this week is just… a lot. Exams are looming, obviously, everyone’s freaking out, and I’m just trying to keep my head above water. But then, like, my mom keeps calling about the weird argument with my brother last week. It’s not even really about what happened, it’s just… she’s just *on* me about it. I don’t even know why she’s bringing it up now, months later. I keep trying to tell her it's done, that it's not worth rehashing, but she just keeps saying "you need to understand how your actions affected everyone." I just feel so overwhelmed, like I can’t win at anything. Maybe I should just avoid her calls, but then that would probably make things worse, right? Idk. I’m just completely drained, honestly. Just want to curl up and disappear for a week.

text: Okay seriously, this is just… too much. Like, I haven’t slept properly in a week and I just feel… heavy. Not like, sad heavy, more like everything is just weighing down on me. It's not one specific thing, you know? It's just… everything. The readings, the assignments, trying to actually *live* a little, the constant feeling of needing to be doing something productive. I keep scrolling through my feed and seeing everyone else’s highlight reels and it just makes me feel like I'm failing at being a student, at being a human. I keep telling myself it’s just exam season, it’ll pass, but my brain is just a loop of ‘need to do more’ and ‘why can’t I just relax?’ Ugh.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is… a lot. I don’t even know where to start, honestly. Everything feels so blurry and… heavy. I just got laid off last week, which was bad enough, you know? It wasn’t like a huge, dramatic firing or anything, just… they said “restructuring” and “streamlining” and I just kinda… went home. And then, like, two days later, Liam… he just said he needed space. Said he wasn’t feeling it anymore. I mean, I *knew* things weren’t great lately, we’d been arguing a lot, and honestly, I’d been feeling pretty distant myself, but… this is just… brutal.

It’s weird because I thought we were okay, or at least, I *wanted* to think we were. We’d been together for, like, five years, and it just feels like… the foundation just crumbled. I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering what I could have done differently, if I could have fixed it somehow. Maybe if I’d been more understanding, or less critical, or just… more present. I don’t even know. It’s so stupid, isn’t it? Feeling like I completely messed everything up.

I'm just scrolling through old photos, mostly, and it’s just… painful. Like, remembering all the good times, knowing they’re over. I keep wondering if he’s seeing other people, if he’s happy, if he even *misses* me. It’s probably ridiculous to think about, but it’s just… consuming me. I’m supposed to be looking for jobs, dealing with the unemployment stuff, but honestly, all I can think about is him. I’m trying to be okay, I really am. I’m going for walks sometimes, and listening to music, but it's just… a distraction, I think. A really, really weak one. I just feel… lost. Like a ship without a rudder. Maybe tomorrow will be better. I hope. I really, really hope.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is weird to even write down, but I think I might be really stressed. Seriously stressed. It started yesterday with this weird chest pain, you know? Just a dull ache, but it kept coming back. I thought it was just, like, maybe I hadn’t eaten enough or something, or I was just super tense. I’ve been moving to this city – completely alone – and it's been… a lot. Finding a job is harder than I thought, and my apartment is tiny and feels really isolating. I’m mostly just eating takeout and staring at the walls.

Then, I went to urgent care, and they did all these tests, and they said it could be anxiety-related, or maybe just a weird muscle spasm. They ran some bloodwork, and idk, it just felt… intense. The nurse kept saying things like "we need to keep an eye on this" and "it's important to get enough rest." Like, I *am* trying to rest! I'm just... lying in bed most of the time. I feel like I should be doing something, but I just can’t. It's like my brain is just full of static.

My mom keeps calling, which is lovely, but also super stressful because she’s constantly asking if I’m okay and telling me I need to take care of myself. She doesn’t understand that moving to a new city and trying to build a life from scratch is, like, a monumental thing. Maybe I'm just overreacting? Or maybe I'm not. I just feel… overwhelmed. Everything feels like it’s piling up, and I’m just not sure how to handle it. I just wish I could feel…normal, i guess. Like, not constantly on edge. It’s exhausting.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text: okay seriously i don’t even know how i’m going to get through this week i have like four exams crammed into seven days and it’s just… a lot. i keep staring at my notes and they just swim before my eyes you know? like trying to read a billboard from a mile away. and on top of that the electricity bill was insane this month i swear the meter is broken or something i’m already rationing the fridge and i can’t even afford to buy decent coffee to, like, keep myself awake. mom keeps saying “just study harder” but it’s not that simple it’s like trying to build a skyscraper with only a spoon and a handful of sand. i had to skip lunch today because i didn’t have enough money for anything besides ramen and even that felt like a luxury. i keep thinking about all the late nights i’ve pulled just to keep up and it feels… pointless honestly. everyone else seems to be handling it so much better, posting about their study groups and stuff and i’m just… here. staring at my textbook and feeling like i’m falling apart. i just need a break but i can’t even take a break because i need to pass these exams to, like, actually pay rent. it’s just a neverending cycle, isn’t it? i just… i don't know what to do. feeling completely paralyzed and exhausted and like i'm failing at everything.  i just want it to be over.

text: Okay, so this new apartment… it’s…smaller than I thought. And Dad’s getting really confused about the layout, you know? I just… idk. It’s a lot to manage, moving everything, and him. Maybe I should have just stayed where we were. Ugh, just feeling totally swamped.

okay seriously i don’t even know how i’m gonna get through this week it just keeps piling up you know? i started this new accounting course online and it's brutal like honestly i thought i was good at numbers but these spreadsheets are a nightmare i’ve been staring at them for hours just trying to understand depreciation schedules and it's making my head spin. and then i have to work my shift at the diner again tonight which is already stressful enough with the usual grumpy customers and the kitchen being a complete disaster zone last night. my manager was yelling at me about the slow tables again it’s just… constant pressure.

it's not even a huge deal like, it’s not like i'm failing or anything but the feeling of being behind is just eating me alive. i keep thinking about all the assignments due next week and i’m just paralyzed i can't even start them because i'm so exhausted just *thinking* about it. i tried to study last night but i just kept zoning out and scrolling through my phone it’s like my brain just shuts down when i try to focus. i feel like i’m constantly playing catch up and it’s impossible to win. i keep replaying everything in my head what i should have done differently, what i could have done differently, just endlessly agonizing over mistakes. i just want to curl up in a ball and disappear for a few days but i can’t i have to keep going. i just… i just feel so overwhelmed. honestly i just need a nap. a really long nap.

Ugh, just… wow. Dad had another episode last night. Woke up to him yelling and confused, like he doesn’t even know where he is. The doctor said it’s probably just a flare-up of the memory thing, but it’s exhausting. I feel like I’m running on fumes, constantly checking on him, making sure he’s okay, trying to remember everything he told me last week. It's like my brain is just a sponge and it’s all soaking up his confusion and distress. I barely slept last night, just sitting in the living room. I just need a minute. Seriously, just a minute to breathe. I feel like I'm losing it, honestly.  I wish someone else could help, but there’s no one.  Just me.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay so like, this whole new city is just...loud and lonely and everything I thought would be amazing is just feeling really, really heavy, you know? It’s just…hard to breathe sometimes, especially when I think about him, and how everything just kind of fell apart, like a really messy jigsaw puzzle with no picture on the box.

Okay, seriously, I’m losing it. It’s just… a lot. Like, a *massive* amount. I thought having a baby was hard. I was wrong. It’s like, everything is harder now. My husband, Liam, he just… doesn’t get it. He’s always “helping,” but he just makes things worse. He’ll try to pick up the baby, and then he’ll drop him, or he’ll try to change a diaper and just leave it sitting there half-open. And then he’ll say, “Just relax, it’s fine!” Relax?! How am I supposed to relax when I’m running on three hours of sleep, trying to feed a screaming human, and feeling like I’m constantly failing at everything?

Last night, we had this huge fight over the dishes. Seriously, dishes. It spiraled. He said I was being unreasonable, that I was “overreacting,” and I just… snapped. I told him I felt completely unsupported, like he wasn’t even trying. He said I was being dramatic and that I needed to appreciate what he does. What he *does* is leave piles of laundry and doesn’t ask if I need help. It’s not about the dishes, it's about feeling like I’m doing this alone. It’s just... exhausting. I just want a quiet moment, a normal conversation, but it feels impossible. I’m just… tired. So, so tired.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text: Okay, so like, this is really hard to even write about. I don’t even know where to start. It’s just… everything’s piling up. text: Okay so like, this whole "making friends" thing in a new city is just… exhausting. I went to that open mic night last week, the one I was so excited about, and it was just…awkward. This city feels huge and empty, and I’m just…lost.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is really hard to even write about, but I need to say something. It's just... everything is piling up. School's been insane, obviously, you know? Mr. Henderson’s history class is suddenly obsessed with this super complicated stuff about the War of 1812 and I genuinely don't get it. I've been staying up ridiculously late trying to cram, and I’m pretty sure I’m just memorizing facts instead of actually understanding them. My grades are probably slipping, which is, like, the last thing I need.

And then there's the job at the cafe. I thought it would be, you know, chill, just a way to earn some extra cash and maybe get a little independence. But it’s actually *really* stressful. There’s this one manager, Beatrice, and she’s… intense. She’s constantly rearranging things, yelling about customer service, and she always seems to find something to complain about, even when everything is going smoothly. I’m constantly messing up orders, or forgetting things, or just generally feeling like I’m not doing anything right. I asked her for a little help with the new espresso machine the other day and she just kinda glared at me and said "figure it out."

I just… I don't know how I'm supposed to juggle all of this. I feel like I’m drowning, honestly. I keep telling myself I'm being dramatic, that everyone feels stressed sometimes, but it’s just… constant. I feel so tired all the time, and I don't even really want to go to class or to work. Maybe I should just drop everything and not go to school at all? I don’t even know. I just feel so overwhelmed. I just wish someone would understand. It’s probably nothing, really. It's just a lot. I dunno what to do.

just... ugh. everyone keeps asking about baby and i just want to disappear. it's so exhausting trying to smile and say something nice when i’m honestly just completely wiped out. i feel like i’m failing at being a decent person, honestly.

text: It's… it’s the anniversary today. Of everything. I just… idk. It feels heavier than usual, you know? Like maybe I should have done something different, or maybe I’m just being stupid. Just a really bad feeling, honestly.

okay seriously this is the worst. like, the absolute worst. i just moved, like, two weeks ago and i already want to pack up and leave. this new town is… nothing. it’s just beige houses and a weird, empty park. and everyone already knows everyone, which is terrifying. i tried to talk to some kids at lunch today and they just stared at me like i was an alien. i don't get their jokes or anything. it’s not even funny, it’s just… isolating.

my mom keeps saying “it’ll get better” and “you’ll make friends” but it doesn’t feel like it. i miss my old friends so much. we had this whole thing going, you know? inside jokes and everything. here, i'm just… invisible. i feel like i’m failing at being a student too. i’m constantly behind on assignments because i’m just… zoning out. it’s hard to concentrate when my brain is just screaming about how much i hate this place.

and the house is awful. it smells like old potpourri and my bedroom is tiny. i keep tripping over stuff. it’s just… everything is wrong. i’m trying to be positive, i really am, but it's exhausting. i just want to go home. i don’t know how much longer i can do this. i’m just… so tired. like, bone-deep tired. it feels like carrying a boulder everywhere i go. i just want it to stop.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s… it’s today. ten years. i can’t believe it’s actually here. i keep expecting him to just… be here. like he always was. it’s quiet though, really quiet. too quiet. i made his favorite stew, you know, the one with the potatoes and the carrots and a little bit of thyme. it smells good, it really does, but it just sits there on the stove. i haven’t eaten much. just a few bites. everything tastes like ash. everyone keeps saying it’s okay to move on, to remember the good times, but how do you ‘move on’ from a hole like this? it’s not like a broken bone, you know? you can heal a bone. this… this just keeps getting bigger. i tried to find his old photo albums, you know, the ones with the goofy pictures from when he was younger. i just… i couldn't. it’s too much. i keep replaying everything in my head. the last conversation we had. the way he looked at me. all the things i wish i’d said. it's like a broken record stuck on repeat. i feel so utterly exhausted. like i’ve been running a marathon for a decade and i don’t even know where the finish line is. i just want it to stop. i want to go back to when things were… simpler. even if it was hard. honestly, i don’t know how i’m going to get through today. or tomorrow. or… i just need a nap. a really long one.

text: Ugh, honestly, my mom is just... relentless, you know? She keeps calling, texting, leaving voicemails about how i should be "moving on" and "seeing people" but it just feels like she doesn't actually *listen* to anything i say about how messed up things are with my sister and dad, it's just so exhausting, like a constant pressure i can’t escape.

text: Okay, so, like, this is weird. It's… it’s the anniversary. Of, you know. The thing. And I didn't even really *plan* on thinking about it today, seriously. I was just trying to get Leo to eat his broccoli and, like, manage the chaos of a toddler and laundry, which is basically my entire life right now. But then it just… hit me. Like a wave. I don't even know. It’s not even…sad, exactly. More like…heavy. Just a really thick, heavy feeling in my chest. I keep thinking about… well, you know. And it’s not that I’m *angry* about it, not really. More like…just…resentful, I guess? At the universe, maybe? I keep replaying things in my head, little snippets, and it’s just… exhausting. Leo’s so cute, he doesn’t understand anything, and it’s supposed to be a happy time, you know? A new chapter, a fresh start. But it feels…wrong. Like I’m pretending. I’m trying to be happy for him, for us, but it's hard. I keep telling myself it’s okay to feel this way, that it’s normal, but it just *feels* like I should be over it. Like I should be smiling and posting adorable pictures of him and not… this. I just… idk. Maybe I just need a really long bath and a giant cup of tea. Or maybe I just need to cry. Honestly, I don’t even know what I need. It’s just...a lot.  I just wish it hadn't happened, you know? And I wish I could just…move on, but I feel stuck. Like I’m constantly looking over my shoulder.

text: Ugh, seriously, I don't even know how to start this. It’s like my brain just… refuses to shut off. I’m working the graveyard shift again, which is already awful, but then my sleep schedule is completely wrecked. I keep waking up, like, three, four times a night, just staring at the ceiling, heart pounding, and then trying to fall back asleep and failing miserably. It’s this awful, gray exhaustion, you know? Like I’m running on fumes and just… everything feels harder. I tried counting sheep, I tried chamomile tea, I even downloaded a white noise app – nothing works. My boss keeps asking if I’m okay, which is nice, I guess, but it’s hard to explain that it’s not just being tired, it's this constant, low-level panic that just won't go away. I just need a few hours of solid sleep, is that too much to ask? I feel like I'm going to completely lose it.

text: It’s…it’s just today. Honestly, I didn’t even realize it was today until like, an hour ago. I’ve been just…existing, you know? Going through the motions, making coffee, staring at the rain, scrolling through my phone like a zombie. It’s weird, because it’s the anniversary. Not *the* anniversary, you know? The one…the really awful one. It feels like yesterday and a million years ago all at once. Like a distorted memory, a faded photograph that keeps shifting and blurring. I keep expecting to hear his voice, just a little thing, like he’s about to tell me about the weather or something, and then…nothing. Just the silence. And it’s not a peaceful silence, it’s…heavy. Like there’s a weight pressing down on my chest. I tried to distract myself, I really did, I baked a stupid cake – chocolate, his favorite – and made myself a fancy cup of tea, but it just felt…wrong. Like I was going through the motions of celebrating something that isn’t real anymore. I keep replaying everything in my head, little snippets of conversations, moments that seemed insignificant at the time but now feel so loaded with meaning. It’s exhausting, you know? Just…exhausting. I don't even know why I’m writing this, it just kind of spilled out. It’s like a dam breaking, just a whole bunch of feelings, all tangled up and messy. I just feel…overwhelmed. Like I’m carrying this enormous, invisible weight and I don’t know how much longer I can keep going.  Just…ugh.

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably going to sound really stupid, but I’m… I’m totally freaking out. It’s just this presentation, right? For the Peterson account. Mr. Henderson specifically asked for it to be *polished*, and I’ve been working on it for, like, a week now? And it still feels… wrong. I’ve gone over the numbers a million times, I swear, but I keep finding these little discrepancies, and idk, they just keep popping up. It’s not huge, probably, but Henderson *really* emphasized how important it is to him, and I just… I don’t want to mess it up.

I tried to tell Sarah about it, but she just said I was overreacting, which, yeah, maybe I am. She’s always so chill. But it’s not *just* the presentation. There’s this whole other thing with the report due next week too, and honestly, I'm behind on that already. It’s like, a domino effect, you know? One thing stresses me out, and then the next thing just… piles on. I keep thinking about all the possible ways it could go wrong. I could stumble over my words, the data could be wrong, Henderson could hate it and… well, I don’t even want to think about that.

I’m trying to stay positive, I really am. I've made a schedule, a ridiculously detailed schedule, with breaks and everything. But even with the schedule, I just feel this constant, low-level panic. Like I’m running on a hamster wheel and I’m not even getting anywhere. Maybe I should just ask for an extension? But that would look bad, right? It's probably a dumb idea anyway. I just need to breathe, I guess. Just… breathe. I’m probably just being dramatic. Ugh.

ugh i just wanna disappear honestly. going out with the other moms feels like climbing a mountain. everyone’s so put together and i just… i feel so awkward and tired. like i’m failing at being a decent human being.
I just can't sleep. He needs me to check on him every hour, every two hours, and I'm exhausted. My head is pounding and I feel like I'm going to fall apart. It's just... too much, honestly.

Seriously, I don’t even know why I’m arguing anymore. Liam just… he just doesn't get it. Like, he thinks I’m deliberately trying to make things harder for him. It’s not that, okay? It’s just… everything is a battle. Getting the baby to sleep, getting him fed, getting *him* to do *anything* without a meltdown. And then my mom keeps calling, saying she wants to “help,” but she just complicates things. She criticizes my choices, tells me I’m doing it all wrong. I just want a moment. Just five minutes. To breathe. I feel like I’m constantly walking on eggshells, and honestly, I’m starting to crack. I just need a break. A real one.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text:
it’s…it’s october 14th. i can’t believe it. exactly two years. two years since…everything. i haven’t really talked about it, not really, just little bits here and there to my sister, mostly just mumbled apologies and “it’s okay” even though it’s absolutely not okay. it’s just…a lot. i keep expecting to wake up and realize it’s all a bad dream, you know? like, he’ll walk through the door and say “surprise!” but he won't. he’s not. and i’m still here. alone.

i tried to, like, do something. i went to that little cafe we used to go to, the one with the awful coffee but the comfy booths. i ordered a latte and just sat there staring at the rain. it felt…wrong. like i was deliberately trying to remember, to feel. but it just brought everything back. the way he used to laugh, the way he’d always steal the blankets, the way he’d just…be.

i feel this weird tightness in my chest all day. it’s not sadness, not really. it’s more like…a constant low-level hum of anxiety. and it’s getting worse. i keep replaying things in my head, little snippets of conversations, arguments, moments of…peace. and then it just spirals. i don’t even want to look at old photos. i deleted most of them, honestly. it’s too painful. i just want to disappear, you know? not like he did, just…fade away. i just…i don’t know how to do this. i just don’t. it’s just… exhausting.

everything feels so…loud. like constantly walking on eggshells with everyone. i just want some peace, you know? little elara’s screaming and my husband’s just…tired. i don’t even know how to breathe sometimes. it’s just too much.

text: Ugh, I don't even know where to start. Liam...it's just... a lot. I thought we were building something, you know? Maybe I was wrong. Everything feels so shaky now, like a house of cards. Just... trying to breathe, honestly. I just miss him.

text:
okay so like, this is probably a really stupid thing to be posting about, but i’m just… a mess. it’s the dissertation, you know? the one i’ve been working on forever and ever and it’s due in, like, a week. and i haven’t really gotten past chapter three. seriously, chapter three. i keep staring at the screen and just… nothing. it feels so huge. like, a mountain of words i can’t even begin to climb.

i know i should be, like, totally focused and productive and stuff, but i just… can’t. it’s like my brain is full of cotton wool. i tried to start writing this morning and i just ended up scrolling through old photos of Liam and me. just… remembering. it’s awful. it’s not even sad, just… nostalgic, i guess? and then i felt this huge wave of just, like, everything. the divorce, the dissertation, the fact that i’m basically living in my parents’ basement now. it’s just… a lot.

i’m supposed to be meeting with my advisor on Tuesday, and i’m terrified. what am i even going to say? "Hi, i’m failing spectacularly"? i don't even know. i just feel so incredibly behind and like i’m going to completely drop the ball. maybe i should just email him and tell him i’m taking a break? idk. that feels like admitting defeat, but also, maybe it’s the only sensible thing to do. i just… i wish i could just magically have all the answers. or, you know, just be able to *write* something. even if it’s terrible. it’s just… so much. maybe i’ll just order pizza and watch something mindless. that's probably the best i can do right now, honestly.

okay seriously i don’t know how much more of this i can take. history test tomorrow and i haven’t even started studying like, at all. my boss at the ice cream shop is being a nightmare too, everyone’s demanding and the line is insane. i feel like i'm constantly running and never actually getting anywhere. it's just… a lot. like, a *really* a lot. i keep staring at my notes and they just blur together. i just want to disappear for a few hours, just like, vanish. i'm so tired all the time, even when i sleep i just wake up feeling even more exhausted. i just wish things would slow down, even just a little bit. i just feel so completely overwhelmed and like i'm failing at everything.

text: Okay, so like, this is just... a lot, you know? They’re saying it could be something serious, like early-onset vertigo or something, and honestly, I’m just completely freaking out. I keep replaying everything, all the times she pushed herself too hard, all the little things I didn't say, and it's just this awful, heavy feeling in my chest. I haven’t been sleeping, and I'm just staring at the ceiling most nights, just… waiting. Trying to be strong for her, which is stupid because I’m barely holding it together myself. It's just a constant worry, a buzzing in the back of my head. I just wish I knew what was going on, seriously.

text: it's… it's the anniversary. i almost forgot honestly. just a few days ago i was staring at the calendar, trying to remember what today even *was*. it feels like a lifetime ago, you know? like a really, really dark lifetime. and now it’s just… here. another year. i keep expecting to wake up and realize it’s all a bad dream, but it’s not. it’s just… quieter now. i mean, the baby’s asleep, finally, bless his little heart. he’s so good, so peaceful, and i just… i feel like i’m supposed to be happy, right? like this should be the easy part, this new chapter. but it’s not. it’s not easy at all. i keep thinking about… about everything that happened before him. it’s like a constant hum in the background, a low thrum of sadness that i can’t quite shake. i tried to make his favorite puree tonight, the one with the peaches and a little bit of banana. it smelled good, i think. but i just… i couldn’t really enjoy it. i just felt this overwhelming wave of everything. i keep wanting to call someone, anyone, but i don't want to burden them. i don’t want to talk about it. i just want to hold him and try to be a good mom, but it's so hard. it's just so, so hard. and i feel like i’m failing at everything. like i’m not even present, just going through the motions. i just need a minute. just a minute to breathe.

text:
Okay seriously, I don’t even know how to start this because it’s just…a lot. I’m supposed to be finishing my online marketing certificate, you know, finally getting something useful under my belt after all these years, and it’s just…impossible. The assignments are piling up, and Professor Davies keeps emailing me about deadlines, and it’s like a tidal wave just constantly crashing down. I keep staring at the screen, just blank, you know? Like my brain has just completely shut off. I keep telling myself I'll just do one more task, just one, and then I’ll take a break, but then I’m just scrolling through social media and then suddenly it’s 3 am and I’ve done absolutely nothing. And then it’s back to the assignments. It’s just... exhausting.

And then there’s work. Mrs. Henderson called this morning, practically in tears, because her grandson, little Leo, he’s been having a really hard time at school, just not wanting to go, and she wants me to talk to his teacher, again. I’m already juggling everything, you know, my own little anxieties about not being good at this whole “adulting” thing, and then I’m stuck fielding everyone else’s problems. It feels like I’m constantly patching things up, holding everything together, and honestly, I don’t know how much longer I can do this. I feel so stretched thin, like I'm about to snap. I just need a moment, just a little quiet space, but it's never quiet, is it? It’s always something. I keep thinking about just...stopping. Not stopping completely, but just... slowing down, letting things go, but how do you even do that when you feel like you’re drowning? I just wish someone would see how completely overwhelmed I am.

text:
Okay, seriously, I can’t even. It's like my brain just… won’t shut off. I moved back home after graduation, which, like, should be a good thing right? Fresh start, you know? But it’s just… not. I keep staring at the ceiling at 3 AM just thinking about everything I *should* be doing. Job applications, finding an apartment that doesn’t smell faintly of mothballs and regret, figuring out what the hell I’m even supposed to *do* with my degree. My parents keep asking me about “progress” and it just makes me want to scream. They don't get it. They think just *existing* is progress.

And the sleep! Ugh. I’m exhausted. Like, bone-deep tired. I tried counting sheep, I tried warm milk, I even tried that meditation app everyone keeps raving about. Nothing works. Every time I try to drift off, my mind just jumps back to all the things I'm failing at. Or the things I’m not doing. It’s a loop. A really, really annoying loop. I feel like I'm constantly wired, even when I’m supposed to be asleep. I keep nodding off at dinner, which is embarrassing. Mom keeps giving me these pitying looks. Honestly, I just want to disappear. Not like, actually disappear, but just... vanish from this room, from this house, from this whole situation. It's just... too much. I just wish I could just… breathe. Seriously, just breathe.

My advisor said it needs to be submitted next week, which, idk, feels ridiculously soon. I’m pretty sure I’m just staring at the screen, typing nonsense, hoping something will magically appear. I keep starting and stopping, going back and re-writing paragraphs until they’re unrecognizable. Maybe I should just email him and ask for an extension? That feels admitting defeat, though, doesn't it? I just... I need to get this done, but I feel like I’m completely losing it. I just want to curl up in a ball and not do anything. It's just so much.

attrs.persona: a person living alone in a new city
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, so this is officially the worst. Moving to Oakhaven was supposed to be, like, this huge fresh start, right? A chance to just breathe and figure things out on my own. But honestly, it’s just… exhausting. I keep staring at the ceiling at 3 am, just thinking, you know? Trying to fall asleep but it’s like my brain is just this buzzing, frantic thing. Every little thing feels magnified – the rain, the distant sirens, even just the creak of the floorboards. I keep telling myself to relax, to just let it go, but it’s like trying to hold onto sand. I feel so utterly alone here, like I'm floating in this big, empty room and nothing feels real. It’s not even that I’m *doing* anything, just… existing, and it’s draining all my energy. I just want to sleep, like a normal person, but it’s just not happening.

okay seriously i don't even know where to start. it just… happened. like, one minute we were talking about, i don't even remember what, and the next he was just… gone. packed his things and left. no fight, no screaming, just quiet and gone. it's so weirdly anticlimactic you know? i keep expecting him to just walk back in and say he made a mistake but he hasn't. it's been like, three days and i still feel like i’m in some sort of terrible dream. everything feels wrong, like the colors are muted or the air is thick. i keep replaying everything in my head, little things we said, things i should have said, things i didn't say at all. it's exhausting. my apartment feels so empty, like a shell of what it was. i keep staring at the space where his side of the bed used to be and just… nothing. it's just blank. and my friends keep texting me, asking how i am, and i just want to send them a string of emojis or something, anything to avoid actually talking about it. i just keep feeling this heavy weight in my chest, like i’m carrying the whole world on my shoulders. i can’t sleep, i can’t eat, i just… exist. it's like a slow, constant drizzle of sadness and i can’t seem to shake it off. i just wish it was all over. i don’t even know what i’m going to do. i just… i just want it to stop.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
Seriously, I just… I don’t even know anymore. It’s like every time I try to do something, something else explodes. Mom’s constantly on my case about, like, everything. My job, my eating habits, what I’m wearing, who I’m talking to. It’s exhausting. And Dad… don’t even get me started. He just sighs and says “you should be further along by now” like I’m some kind of failing project. He doesn't *get* it. He thinks I just need to “pull myself together.” Like it’s that simple.

I moved back home after graduation and it was supposed to be a fresh start, right? A chance to decompress, figure things out. Instead it’s just… louder. More critical. They just keep reminding me that I haven't ‘achieved’ anything. It's like they're measuring my worth by some ridiculous, impossible standard. I applied for a million jobs and I'm getting nothing but automated rejection emails. It’s not like I’m *not* trying. I’m just… stuck. Feeling like I'm walking through mud, just trying to keep moving forward. I keep picturing myself just… disappearing. Not like, permanently, but just… shutting down. Just needing to be somewhere quiet where they can’t tell me what to do. I just want a moment of peace, you know? A single, uninterrupted moment. Is that too much to ask? I’m so tired of feeling like I’m constantly disappointing everyone.

it’s just… too much. i keep checking on him, you know? mr. henderson, the guy who always orders two black coffees and reads the newspaper. he had that weird cough last night, like a little rattle and he looked…pale. i called the nurse, twice, and she said it was probably just a cold, but it’s been going on for days. and now i’m up here again, staring at the monitors, listening for anything, anything at all. everyone’s so quiet, it’s almost worse than the chaos during the day. i feel like i’m failing him, failing everyone, just because i’m stuck on this shift. i keep thinking about his wife, she must be so worried. i just wish i could make it better, even if it's just a little bit. this shift is eating me alive honestly.

text: Okay, so like, this is hard. Really hard. I just… I don’t know what to do anymore. Liam is, you know, a good baby. He sleeps a lot, mostly. But it’s just… me. All the time. My partner, Silas, he works so much, and he’s exhausted too, I get it, but it’s still lonely. I keep staring at him when he’s asleep and just thinking, “Is this it?” I tried calling my mom yesterday, but I just… couldn’t. It felt like a huge effort. Maybe I should just stay in tonight, order takeout, and watch something mindless. I don't even know if that would help, honestly. I feel like I’m failing at being a good mom, and a good partner, and a good person. I just… miss talking to someone who actually *gets* it, you know? I don't think I've had a proper conversation in days.

text: it's just... everything is piling up you know? lost my job last month and now my sister is constantly calling, saying i'm not taking it seriously enough, like i'm just wallowing and it's making things so much worse, i just don’t know what to do anymore honestly, it feels like everyone’s judging me and i’m just so tired.

Ugh, seriously, I don't even know why I bother. Mom’s just…constant. It’s like she actively *wants* to argue about everything. The thermostat, the TV, what to eat, if I’m using enough soap. It’s exhausting. And Dad just sits there, nodding along, agreeing with everything she says. Like he doesn’t even see it. I feel like I’m the only one who cares about keeping things even remotely peaceful around here. I just want a single, quiet afternoon. Is that too much to ask? I’m starting to feel like I’m losing my mind trying to manage it all. It's just...a lot.

Ugh, I just had the worst party. Like, seriously. Everyone was just…loud. And all the couples were, like, *so* close. It’s not that I don’t want to be with anyone, it’s just…everything feels so overwhelming lately. I spent the whole time trying to find a corner to just, like, melt into. And then Liam asked me if I was okay, and I just mumbled something about being tired and bolted outside. Seriously, why do people always assume I’m sad? I’m not *sad*, exactly. It’s more like…everything is just a little too bright. Like, I can’t even look at my phone without feeling this weird tightness in my chest.

My mom keeps saying I need to “get out there more,” like it’s that simple. But it’s not! It's like I'm watching a movie of everyone else's lives and I'm stuck behind a really thick pane of glass. I feel this constant pressure to, like, *be* something, to *do* something, to be *fun*. And I just...don't feel it. I don’t even know what I *want* to feel. Just…quiet. Please. I just want to be quiet. I keep replaying everything that happened, trying to figure out where I went wrong, even though I didn’t *do* anything wrong. It just feels like everyone else knows how to navigate this whole social thing and I'm completely lost. I just wanna disappear for a little bit, you know? Just fade away for a bit.
