text: Okay, seriously, I don’t even know how to start this. Everything feels…off. Like, I’m supposed to be studying for these finals, right? Huge ones, like, everything hinges on them. And I’m failing, just spectacularly. I keep staring at the textbook and it’s just…noise. Like static. I tried to read a chapter yesterday and I literally had to close it because my head was pounding and I felt like I was going to throw up. It's not just the studying, it’s… Grandma. She called last night, said she's having chest pains again. And it's not the first time this month. The doctor just said "manage her anxiety," which is, like, what?! She's eighty-seven! I feel like I should be doing something, like, *anything*, but I don’t even know where to begin. I just keep thinking about her and it’s like a weight on my chest. I keep forgetting to eat, or drink water. Just...existing feels exhausting. I keep snapping at Liam, and I hate that. He's trying to help, I know he is, but I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. I know I *should* be prioritizing my studies, but it feels pointless. Like, what's the point of getting a good grade if Grandma is hurting? God, this is a mess. I just…I need to breathe. Seriously. Just breathe.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, so I don’t even know where to start, seriously. Mom had a dizzy spell yesterday, like a really bad one, and they took her to the hospital. It was just… chaotic. The waiting room was packed, everyone looking so worried and clutching their phones, and the nurses were running around like crazy, shouting orders or something. I just felt… completely useless, you know? I kept asking if she was okay, a million times, and they just kept saying they were running tests and trying to figure out what was wrong. It's just… she's been so good to me, you know? Always there, always making sure I was okay, and now *I’m* the one completely freaked out. And the doctor… he was so clinical, just rattling off terms I didn’t understand – arrhythmia, elevated blood pressure, something about electrolytes. It just felt like he wasn’t really *seeing* her, seeing *Mom*. She's 78, she's had a few bumps and bruises in her life, but this felt different. And the thought of something seriously wrong… it’s just sitting there, heavy in my chest. I'm exhausted, honestly. I’ve been driving back and forth to the hospital all day, trying to keep her comfortable, getting her some water, just holding her hand. I barely ate anything. I just keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong, and all the things I could be doing differently, and it’s just… a lot. I just want everything to be okay, I really do. I just… I feel like I'm failing her, and failing myself. I just need a nap, a really long nap.

text: Okay, so like, this is… a lot. I don’t even know where to start. I lost my job last month, which was bad enough, obviously. Just… gone. No warning, just a really awkward call from Brenda in HR. I thought it was a mistake, honestly. I was doing okay, I *thought* I was doing okay. I was keeping busy, you know? But then, like, two weeks later, Mom started complaining about her chest. okay wow i just… i don’t even know where to start. it’s like my brain is permanently stuck on repeat, just playing this awful loop of everything that went wrong. and then i try to sleep and it’s just… nothing. absolutely nothing. i toss and turn for hours staring at the ceiling like i’m waiting for an announcement or something. my eyes are so heavy but my mind is just buzzing. it’s like every little thing that happened with mark, every stupid argument, every time i felt like i was failing, it’s just… there. amplified. i keep replaying conversations in my head, picking at them, trying to figure out where i could have done things differently, but there's no ‘undo’ button for relationships you know? and now i’m just… alone. and exhausted. i tried reading but my brain just wouldn’t cooperate, it kept flashing back to him saying he didn’t understand. it’s ridiculous i know, i’m letting this one guy ruin my whole life, but it feels like he’s just… everywhere. i feel like i’m constantly on edge, waiting for something to go wrong, or worse, something to remind me of everything that’s wrong. i just want to feel normal again, to sleep through the night without waking up in a cold sweat. honestly i just want to disappear for a little bit, just quiet everything down, but i know that's not an option. i need to, like, actually do something. but what? i just feel so… overwhelmed. everything feels so heavy. it’s like carrying rocks in my pockets all day. seriously i’m so tired.

text: It’s just… it’s today. Seven years. Feels like a lifetime ago, but also like it just happened. Dad’s birthday would have been today, you know? And it’s just… heavy. I keep expecting to hear his voice, to see him sitting in his armchair reading, or just… being. I tried to do something nice, made his favorite stew – apple and cinnamon, he loved that – but it just sits there in the fridge, mocking me, really. I keep going over everything, every little thing we did, every silly argument we had, and it’s just a blur of regret and wanting to rewind time. I just wish I’d said more, done more. Honestly, I feel like I’m carrying the weight of the world on my shoulders, and it’s just getting heavier and heavier. I don’t even know what to do with myself today, or any day really.

Okay, seriously, what is even happening? I just… I don’t even know where to start. It’s been weeks since, well, you know. And it’s not like I’m actively searching, it's just… this heavy feeling. Like I’m supposed to be *doing* something, but I don't even know *what*. My phone just feels like a constant reminder of all the things I’m not doing, all the people who are probably moving on and I'm just… stuck. I keep replaying everything, trying to figure out where I went wrong, but honestly, it’s just a blur. I just feel… empty. And tired. Like a battery that’s been drained completely. Just trying to get through the day, that’s all.

I feel like I’m just existing, not living. Everyone keeps saying "you'll get over it," but it doesn’t feel like it. It’s just...heavy. Like a weight on my chest. I just want to go back to sleep, but it's impossible.

seriously can’t sleep. it’s like my brain is just… buzzing. everything feels so heavy, you know? like i’m carrying this giant weight around all night. i keep replaying everything that happened at the office, just obsessing over small things, stupid things. it’s not even that it was a bad job, exactly, but now that it’s gone… i just can’t switch off. i keep thinking about the interviews, the questions i should have answered differently, the things i said. and then i just lie there staring at the ceiling and i can’t shut it off. i just want to be asleep but my thoughts just keep swirling. it’s exhausting. i feel so useless, like i’m just… failing at even sleeping.  ugh.

text:
Okay so, this is embarrassing but like, I can’t sleep. Seriously. I know, I know, it’s a cliche, right? Fresh grad, back in mom’s basement, spiral-ing. But it’s actually happening. It started a few weeks ago, I think? Or maybe it’s been longer? I honestly don’t even keep track anymore. I just… lie there. Like, staring at the ceiling, thinking about, you know, everything. Which is always a bad idea, I’ve learned that the hard way.

My job search is going… okay? I mean, I’ve gotten a few interviews, which is good, right? But they're all for things I'm not really qualified for, and I just feel so awkward talking about my limited experience. Like, how do you even *sell* yourself when you basically just graduated and haven’t done much besides, like, read books and eat cereal? I keep telling myself to just be confident, but it feels like a really big lie.

And then there’s… everything else. Mom keeps asking about my plans, which is fine, but it just makes me feel more anxious. She’s just worried, I get it, but it's like, I’m 23! I should know what I’m doing, shouldn't I? Maybe I should just tell her I'm feeling really down, but what if she gets upset? She already worries so much. It's exhausting. I tried meditating, and yoga, and everything I saw on TikTok, but nothing works. I keep waking up in the middle of the night, heart racing, just… overwhelmed. Idk, maybe it’s just the transition? Moving back home was supposed to be temporary, but it feels like I’m stuck. I just need a nap, a really long nap, but then I’m worried about falling *further* behind. Ugh. I just feel so… useless.

text: Okay, seriously, I hate this. Moved to this city three weeks ago and everyone just…expects me to be social. Like, I’m not stupid. I’m not invisible, but I haven’t exactly built a network or anything. Last night was awful. Tried to go to that bar everyone keeps recommending, just stood in the corner nursing a water, felt like everyone was staring. It's not that I *want* to be alone, it's just...hard. I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering if I said the wrong thing, if I looked awkward. Just feeling so disconnected, like a ghost. I don’t get it. I just want to… exist, you know? Maybe I'm just not cut out for this. Ugh.

Seriously, who even knows what they’re doing anymore? It’s just… me and the baby. All day. Every day. I keep expecting someone to, like, ask how I am, or if I need anything, but nobody does. My partner works insane hours and when they get home they just... sleep. I try to talk to them about it, about feeling so utterly alone, and they just mumble something about being tired. It’s not helpful. I feel like I'm losing myself in this, you know? Like I’m slowly fading away because there's no one to see me. Just this little tiny person who needs me, and honestly, I’m just... exhausted. And lonely. Really, really lonely.

text:
Ugh, I just… I don’t even know where to start. It's like this constant low-level hum of anxiety, you know? Like, it’s not a *panic*, not really, but it’s just… always there. I’ve been working these night shifts at the warehouse for, like, six months now and I thought I’d be used to it by now, but honestly, it's just getting worse. I keep telling myself it’s the sleep deprivation, obviously, that's a huge factor, right? But it feels… deeper than that. Like, my brain just doesn't switch off. I try to read before bed, but I can't focus on anything. Everything just feels… sharp.

And then there’s the quiet. It’s so quiet here, especially when it's supposed to be busy. Just the whirring of the machines and the occasional forklift, and it’s… unsettling. It makes me think about everything. About all the stuff I *should* be doing, all the things I’m putting off. I haven't called my mom in a week, which is awful, I know. She worries. I just… I don’t want to talk about it. It’s hard to explain, you know? It’s not like there’s one big thing that’s triggering this, it’s just… everything all at once, I guess. Maybe I’m just a worrier. I don’t know. Maybe I need a new job. Or a hobby. Or a nap. Seriously, just a really long nap. I feel like I'm constantly running on fumes. I just want to be okay, but I don’t even know how. It's just... heavy.

Ugh. Just… ugh. Another night. Feels like I’m running on fumes and caffeine. Everything’s just… piling up, you know? Like, I don’t even know *why* it’s piling up. Just is. Work’s okay, I guess? But then I get home and it’s just… more. Dishes, laundry, trying to remember to eat something that isn't a granola bar. My head just feels fuzzy all the time. I keep snapping at Liam, and he just looks tired too. I feel like I'm failing at everything, even though I'm not *trying* to fail. It's just... heavy. Like a weight I can't shake off. I just want to sleep, but then I’m worried about falling behind. Seriously, what’s the point of anything right now?

text: Okay, so like, this is really weird to even write about, but I just... I don't know what to do. It's been a week since, you know, the thing with Mom. The doctor said it was nothing serious, just a weird blood test result and some inflammation, but it's just... hanging over everything. I'm in this tiny apartment in this city I've only been here six months for, completely alone, and now this. It feels like a weight, a really heavy, dull weight. I keep expecting a call, or a text, or *something*, but nothing. Just silence. I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering if I should have pushed her to see a specialist sooner, or if maybe I'm just being a neurotic worrywart. I know, I know, it's probably nothing, but my brain just won’t shut off. I tried to distract myself, went to that new coffee shop downtown, and it was okay, but then I kept thinking about how far I was from her, from everyone. I just… I feel so incredibly isolated. Maybe I should call my sister, but she’s always so busy. Or maybe not. It just feels like adding to the chaos, you know? I'm just… tired. Like bone-deep tired. I don't even know why I'm posting this, it’s probably just a rambling mess, but I needed to say something. I just wish things weren't so… intense. Everything feels amplified, like a bad movie soundtrack playing on repeat. I just want it to be normal again, I guess.

text: Okay so like, this is just… a lot. I keep thinking about Grandma, you know? She had that thing with her heart, and it just hit me yesterday, like a brick, that it’s not just something that happens to *other* people, right? Like, it could happen to me, and honestly, I’m terrified. I haven’t been sleeping properly at all, just tossing and turning, replaying everything she said, every time she coughed, every worried look she gave me. And then I look around here, back at my parents' house, and it’s just… suffocating. I thought moving back home would be, like, a nice break, a chance to just breathe and figure things out after graduation, which was supposed to be this huge, amazing thing, but now it just feels… heavy. All I do is stare at the TV or scroll through my phone until I’m exhausted, and even then, my brain is just buzzing with worries. Mom keeps asking me about job applications, and Dad just keeps making meatloaf – which, honestly, is lovely, but doesn't solve anything. I feel so useless, like I should be doing something productive, but I can't even get out of bed some days. It's just this constant, low-level hum of anxiety that never really goes away. I miss my apartment, my friends, the idea of actually *having* a job. It’s all just too much, you know? I just wish I could feel… normal again, I guess. Or at least less like I’m constantly waiting for something bad to happen. I don’t even know what to do anymore.

I moved here six months ago, completely alone, trying to build a life, and now this. It’s like the universe is just actively trying to throw a wrench in everything. I keep staring at my apartment and it just feels… small, you know? Like a tiny box when everything feels so huge. I keep ordering takeout, which is great, but also makes me feel even more pathetic, like I can’t even take care of myself. Seriously, I just want it to be over, but it’s not, is it? Just… a mess.

text: It's the anniversary. Feels like yesterday everything just… imploded. Mom keeps asking if I’m okay, but it’s hard to explain how heavy it still feels. Just scrolling, honestly. Can't look at anything.

Ugh. Seriously, I don’t even know where to start. It’s just… constant. My sister keeps calling, demanding to know why I didn’t talk to Mom last week. Like, seriously? She’s acting like I deliberately ignored her, like I *wanted* to cause a rift. And Mom? Don’t even get me started. She’s twisting everything, making me the bad guy, saying I'm obviously struggling and need to reach out more. Which, okay, yeah, I am struggling! But it's not that simple. It’s not like I'm enjoying this whole mess. Honestly, it feels like everyone is just waiting for me to crack, to say something stupid, to make it worse.

I tried to explain, you know, that I need space, that I’m still processing everything, that talking feels… awful right now. But it just gets met with judgment. “You should be stronger,” “You need to move on,” “Don't be so sensitive.” Like I chose this! Like this divorce didn’t completely upend my entire life! It’s exhausting. I feel like I'm walking on eggshells everywhere I go. I just want a few minutes of peace, a moment to breathe without being interrogated about my family dynamics. I keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out what I could have done differently, if I could have prevented this whole thing. It’s just… too much. I just want it to stop. I just want to not feel this way.

text: Okay seriously, I don’t even know where to start, like, everything feels so… heavy. It's just… a constant weight, you know? The bills are piling up, like, *really* piling up, and it’s not even like I’m spending money on anything crazy, just the essentials, which are somehow still impossible to afford. I keep looking at my bank account and it’s just… red. A big, angry red. I know I should be doing something about it, making a budget or something, but honestly, I just feel paralyzed. It's like I’m stuck in quicksand and the more I try to move, the deeper I sink. I keep thinking about how much easier things were before, and it just feels… unfair, you know? Like the universe is just actively trying to make me miserable. I just want a normal day, you know? A day where I don’t have to think about money.

Okay, seriously, I need a break. Like a real one. Not just staring at the ceiling for an hour while the baby sleeps. Last night was… something. We went to Sarah and Mark’s birthday party, and I felt like I was just… observing. Everyone was laughing, chatting about work and their summer vacations, and I just kept nodding and smiling, trying not to look like a total zombie. I felt so awkward. Like I didn’t even *know* how to be around people anymore. 

And then there was the playing with the baby. He’s cute, don’t get me wrong, but he’s also… a lot. He screamed for half an hour because he wanted a banana, and then promptly threw it everywhere. I cleaned it up, of course, but it just felt like a constant, exhausting effort. I wanted to tell someone, *anyone*, that I'm completely fried. That I haven't had a proper shower in three days and I'm pretty sure my hair is permanently sticky.

I feel like I’m failing at being a good mom, a good partner, and a functioning adult all at once. Everyone seems to be handling this new life so effortlessly. They're posting pictures of happy, smiling families, and I'm just… here. Trying to keep the baby from throwing himself off the couch. It's not a glamorous life, you know? I just… I just wish I could turn it down a notch, just for a little while. Just to breathe. Seriously, someone send coffee. And maybe a very large hug.

okay seriously i don't even know how i'm managing right now like i thought having a baby would be…nice? peaceful? i was so wrong. it’s just…constant. everything is just a frantic scramble to keep little elara happy and fed and somehow not covered in poop. and then there’s work. professor moran just sent another email about the paper due next week and i swear he’s personally trying to ruin my life. he wants it like yesterday and i'm already behind, and honestly i’m just staring at the blank screen and my brain is just…gone. i keep trying to write, but it's all just a jumbled mess of half-formed sentences and panic. i feel like i'm failing at everything. like i'm supposed to be this amazing mom and a brilliant student and a functioning adult but i’m just…this exhausted, overwhelmed blob. my husband tries to help, he really does, but it’s like he’s operating on a different wavelength. he’s doing his thing and i'm just spiraling. i keep thinking about just calling in sick to both, just disappearing for a day, but then elara would be crying and my husband would be worried and it would just make everything worse. it’s this awful loop. i just need a break. a real break. not just a five-minute shower. i just want to breathe. i keep replaying everything in my head, what i should have done differently, what i’m doing wrong, and it just gets louder and louder and i can't shut it off. i just wish someone understood how completely and utterly drained i feel.

text:
Okay, so like, this is really hard to even write about, honestly. I just… I haven’t been sleeping. At all. Like, properly. It’s been, idk, maybe a week or two now? It started after I moved back in with my parents, which, you know, was supposed to be a good thing? Fresh start, right? But it’s just… a lot. I keep tossing and turning, staring at the ceiling, and then I just end up lying there awake for hours.

My mom keeps asking if I’m okay, and I just say “fine,” which is totally a lie, obviously. I feel so stupid. Like, I graduated college, I have a degree, I *should* be able to handle this, but I can’t even get out of bed some days. It’s not even like I’m anxious or anything, I don't think. I just… feel this constant pressure, you know? Like there's this weight on my chest, and I can't shake it off.

I keep thinking about all the things I *should* be doing – job applications, figuring out my next steps, paying bills (which are piling up, ugh). But the thought of actually *doing* anything just makes me want to hide under the covers. Maybe it's the quiet? I'm so used to the chaos of university, and now it's just… silence. A really unsettling silence. I feel like I’m failing at being an adult, even though I just finished school. It's embarrassing. I just want to go back to sleep, but then I know I’ll just end up feeling even worse. I just don't know what to do. Seriously, idk. Maybe I should just call my sister, but that feels… dramatic, somehow?

text:
okay so like, this is probably a weird thing to post, but i just… i don’t know what to do anymore. i moved here six months ago, completely alone, on a whim, you know? figured a fresh start would be good, and it kinda was, for a little while. i even made some friends, like, actual friends, not just people i met at the coffee shop. but then… then Liam. it was good, really good, for a while. we were laughing a lot, and he was, like, really sweet. i thought maybe, just maybe, i could actually build something here.

and now… nothing. We just kinda… stopped. Didn’t really talk about it, just… quiet. He said he needed space, and i guess i do too, honestly. i just feel so lost. this whole city feels huge and empty without him. everything reminds me of something we did, or something we *could* have done. i keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out where we went wrong, but idk. it just felt… natural, you know? like we were just growing apart, but it’s still hitting me harder than i expected.

i'm trying to be okay, i really am. i’m going for walks, trying to cook actual meals instead of just ordering takeout (which is probably making things worse, lol). but it’s hard. it’s *really* hard to pretend everything's fine when it feels like my whole world just shifted on its axis. maybe i'm overreacting, maybe i’ll be laughing about this in a month, but right now? it just hurts. a lot. i just wish i could rewind. or maybe just… not have moved in the first place. i’m seriously questioning everything. i don’t even know who i am without him. it’s just… a mess.

okay seriously this is a disaster i thought we were being so careful about packing and everything but it's just… a mess. like a giant, beige, heartbreaking mess. we moved like three days ago and i still feel like i'm standing in the middle of it all. little elara just wants her toys everywhere and i swear she's actively trying to dismantle the boxes. and dad’s trying to “help” which mostly means he’s moving things from one pile to another and making louder noises. i just want to sit down. honestly, i just want to sit down and not think about anything.

the apartment is…fine i guess. it’s just…empty. we have the furniture but it doesn’t *feel* like home yet. like it’s just…waiting. and elara keeps staring at the walls like she’s expecting someone to appear. i keep wanting to hug her so tight she’ll just scream. i miss our old place so much it physically hurts. it wasn’t perfect but it was *ours*. and now we’re just…here.

i'm so tired. not just physically, though that’s a whole other level of exhaustion, but mentally. i keep replaying everything in my head, wondering if we made the right decision, if we packed enough, if i’m doing this right. everyone keeps saying “it’ll get better” but it just feels like a constant uphill battle. i feel like i’m failing at being a mom and a partner and a person. i just need a minute. a real minute to breathe and not feel like i’m constantly running on fumes. i just…ugh. i just want things to be normal again. like before.

text: okay so like seriously my bank account is a disaster zone and i just keep staring at it, you know? i’m trying to be responsible, i really am, but bills are piling up and rent is due and i don't even know where to start, it just feels so overwhelming and i’m pretty sure i’m going to have a panic attack later, ugh.

Okay, seriously, this is a mess. Just… a huge, chaotic mess. I thought I’d be thrilled, you know? New city, fresh start, right? Everyone keeps saying that. But it’s just… loud. And expensive. And I’m living out of a box in a storage unit. Like, a *real* storage unit. With a padlock. It’s depressing.

I didn’t even unpack half of the boxes I brought. Just… stuff. Clothes I don’t wear, books I won’t read, memories wrapped up in cardboard. It’s like I’m deliberately avoiding everything. Which is probably stupid, but I can’t seem to shake this feeling that if I just stare at it all, it’ll all come crashing down. 

Getting the utilities set up was a nightmare. The internet keeps cutting out, and the electricity bill is insane. I keep expecting a notice, like I’m going to be evicted or something. It’s not funny. It's just... constant. This low-level hum of anxiety that’s always there. I keep scrolling through job postings, but it feels pointless. Everyone wants experience, and I don't have any *new* experience, just… the experience of being unemployed.

My mom keeps calling, asking how I'm doing, and I just say “fine.” Which is a lie. A big, fat, embarrassing lie. I don’t want to talk about it. I don't want anyone to see how completely lost I feel. Just… overwhelmed, I guess. Just completely overwhelmed. And I don't know how to stop it. It's like a weight, just sitting on my chest, getting heavier and heavier every day. I just want to go home. But I can't.

Ugh, seriously, this week is just… a lot. I’m pulling these triple shifts at the warehouse again, and Mr. Henderson is breathing down my neck about the inventory. He keeps saying “efficiency, efficiency, efficiency,” like I’m not already running on fumes. It's not even *that* much busier, it’s just that he’s constantly hovering and making little comments about how I’m “not meeting expectations.” I know he’s stressed too, probably has his own problems, but it’s just… relentless.

And then there's the online course. It's supposed to be a quick thing, just a supplemental class for my degree, but the professor posts like, a million assignments a week. And they’re all due at 11:59 PM. Seriously, who writes a fifteen-page paper at 11:45 PM after twelve hours on my feet hauling boxes? I haven’t slept properly in days. I keep staring at the screen, just trying to force the words out, and my brain feels like it's wading through mud.

I tried to explain to my roommate that I'm completely wiped, but she just said, "You should have planned better." Like, duh! Easier said than done when you're working 12-hour shifts and trying to keep up with a demanding course. I just feel this constant weight, you know? Like I’m carrying the entire world on my shoulders and it’s slowly crushing me. I just want to curl up in a ball and disappear. Maybe if I just stop responding to texts… just for a little while.

text: Okay seriously, I don’t even know where to start. It’s just…everything. Like, I thought I could handle it, you know? Like, everyone said having a baby was amazing and full of joy and it *is*…sometimes. But then there’s this constant, low-level hum of anxiety that’s just…there. It’s not like a big, dramatic thing, it’s just… a pressure, you know? Like I’m holding this giant, warm, demanding ball and I’m constantly worried I’m going to drop it, or that it’s not getting enough, or that I’m not doing anything right. And then Leo just…he cries. Constantly. Not like a baby who’s hungry, or tired, it’s just…a wail. A really loud wail. And I feel this overwhelming guilt, like I’m failing him, like I’m not a good mom, and then I just want to hide under the covers and not come out for like, a week. My husband tries to help, he really does, but he works long hours and I feel like I’m just burdening him. We barely eat together, we barely talk, and I feel so alone. I keep staring at him sleeping and it’s so beautiful, but then I remember all the sleepless nights and the constant worry and it just…crushes me. I just wish I knew how to turn it down, this volume inside my head. It’s not that I don’t love him, I do, so much, it’s just…this feeling, this weight, it’s exhausting. I feel like I’m constantly running on fumes and I’m just…waiting for it to end, or for it to get easier, or for *something* to change.  I just need a nap. Seriously, just one nap.

Ugh, just saw like, three people I vaguely know from work. Felt like I had to smile and say something stupid. Seriously considering just hiding under a rock for a while. Everyone seems to be moving on and having a good time, and I'm just... here.

just… so much. deadlines are breathing down my neck and i swear i haven’t slept properly in days. everything feels urgent and important all at once, like i’m trying to hold onto sand. i just want to disappear for a minute, honestly.

Seriously, I don't even know where to start. It just… happened. Like, one minute we were talking about stupid stuff, the next it was over. Everything feels so weird, you know? My apartment is still half his, and I keep expecting him to just walk in. It’s like a ghost is hanging around. I keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out where I messed up, but there’s nothing to figure out, is there? Just… a mess. I feel like I’m underwater, just trying to keep my head above the waves of sadness and disbelief. I’m exhausted. Just… utterly exhausted.  I keep staring at the ceiling and it’s all I can see.

text: Okay so like, I haven't slept properly in weeks and I just... I don't even know what to do anymore. Moving here was supposed to be this amazing fresh start, you know? attrs.persona: a university student in exam season
attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, seriously, I don't even know why I'm even bothering to type this. It’s just… everything feels so heavy, like I’m carrying this giant rock around and it’s crushing me. These exams are insane, I've been up for like, 40 hours straight just trying to keep up, and my boss at the cafe is being completely unreasonable, demanding I work double shifts even though I’m barely sleeping. I just keep thinking about how pointless it all is, you know? Like, what's the point of getting a degree if I'm just going to end up stressed and exhausted and miserable? I just want to... just disappear, honestly. It’s not that I *want* to hurt anyone, it’s just... the thought of just not being here anymore feels... less awful than everything else, I guess. I don't even know what I'm saying, I'm just... feeling so lost and numb.

Ugh, just got back from Sarah’s birthday dinner. Seriously, don’t even get me STARTED. Everyone was just…laughing. Like, genuinely happy. And it felt like I was watching a movie about a life I don’t even recognize anymore. She’s got a new guy, a cute puppy, and a whole weekend trip planned. It's...fine. For her. I just...exist. Like a ghost at a party. I tried to talk about the divorce, you know, just a little bit, but it just landed wrong. People kept changing the subject. “Oh, you’re doing so well!” “It’s a fresh start!” Fresh start my ass. It's like every single person around me is trying to convince me I’m *fine* when I feel like I’m actively dissolving. I kept staring at my plate, just picking at the pasta. Felt like everyone was looking at me, judging me, wondering why I wasn't having a good time. Like I was supposed to be ecstatic about this...this nothing. Honestly, I think I just need to disappear. Not like, permanently, but just…away. Somewhere I can’t see anyone, hear anyone. Somewhere the noise just fades out. I don't want to be a burden. It’s just…too much. Everything is too much. I just want to not feel this heavy. I keep thinking about just...not showing up. To things. To life. It's a stupid thought, I know, but it’s there. A persistent little voice that just wants everything to stop.

Okay seriously, this is a mess. Just… a complete, utter mess. Packed everything up, drove three hours, found a tiny apartment in a town I’ve never even heard of. It was supposed to be *better*. A fresh start, right? Like something out of a cheesy movie. Except it's not. It’s just… more lonely. I told my mom I needed space, needed to figure things out after losing my job, and she just keeps saying “you’ll find something.” Like it's that easy. Like I just magically sprout a new career.

Everything costs more here. The apartment is cramped, the internet is slow, and the only grocery store is a mile away. I feel like I’m drowning in boxes and bad decisions. I keep staring out the window and just… wishing I wasn't here. Like this whole thing was a mistake. I keep thinking about just… not going back. Not facing anything. It would be so simple, wouldn’t it? Just… not.

I don't even know anyone. There’s this old lady who walks her dog past my window every morning and she smiles at me, but it doesn’t help. It just makes me feel… heavier. Like I’m a weight nobody wants to carry. I just want to sleep. All the time. And maybe… maybe not wake up. It doesn’t really matter, does it? This place, this life… it’s just not for me. I don't even know why I bothered.

text: it’s… it’s the anniversary today. you wouldn’t understand, honestly. it just feels like everything’s pressing down, like the weight of it all is just crushing me. i keep replaying it in my head, the way it happened, the faces… ugh. everyone else is probably celebrating something, going out, having fun, and i’m just… here. staring at my textbook, trying to cram for these exams and it’s completely pointless, really. what’s the point of grades, of anything, when this just… lingers? i don’t even want to go to bed, like, the thought of facing another day, another reminder, it’s just too much. i keep thinking about just… not. just letting it all go. i just wish it would stop hurting so much. i feel so empty. i don’t know what to do anymore, seriously.

Ugh, the doctor said it’s just a stupid virus, but I feel like I’m gonna die. Little Leo's been so clingy and I'm exhausted. Like, bone-deep tired. Everything just feels…heavy. I keep staring at his little face and just wanting to, like, not do this anymore. I know that’s stupid, right? It’s just…the pressure, the sleepless nights, the constant worry about him, about us. It’s too much. I don't even know how to breathe sometimes. I just want the quiet. Just…gone. It would be so easy, wouldn’t it? Just to not have to carry this anymore.

text: okay so, like, this is really hard to even write. my mom called me yesterday, and she was freaking out about my bloodwork. nothing *terrible*, they said, just… high. like, really high. and it’s just… everything. I mean, the divorce was bad enough, a real mess honestly, but now this? it feels like my body is just… failing me, you know? i don’t even know what to do. i keep thinking about just… not dealing with any of it. i don't know, maybe that’s dramatic, but it's just... a lot. i just want it to stop. i just want to disappear, honestly. it’s probably silly, i’m probably overreacting, but... maybe it would be easier if i didn't exist. idk. just… feeling really lost, i guess.
Just... can't. Seriously. This baby won't sleep and I'm pretty sure I'm losing my mind. It's just... too much. Like, maybe it would be easier if i wasn't here.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: it’s just… everything is piling up, you know? the divorce papers, this stupid thesis deadline looming over me like a weight, and honestly, what's the point of even trying anymore? i just feel so numb and like i'm drowning, just… wouldn’t it be easier if i wasn't here?

Just… everything’s just too much. Like, I don’t even know why it’s too much, it just is. Used to have a job, a routine, you know? Now it's just this gray blur. Everyone keeps asking what happened, like it's some dramatic story. It’s not. It just… ended. And now I’m just existing. I keep staring at the ceiling, trying to remember what it feels like to actually *want* to do something. Honestly, the thought of just not having to *feel* anymore is… appealing. I don't want to bother anyone. Just… quiet.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: Okay seriously, I'm done. Like, completely and utterly done. This whole “fresh start” thing was a massive lie. I thought moving back here after graduation would be…good. A chance to breathe, you know? Get a job, actually *do* something with my degree instead of staring at it collecting dust. But it’s just…more of the same. Everyone here acts like I’m the one who messed up, like I should have just gone to California or something. Mom keeps saying "it’ll take time," but it doesn’t. It's been six months. Six months of applying for jobs that don’t even look at my resume, of eating dinner with the same tired faces, of feeling like I’m drowning in beige. I just... I don't know how to be happy here anymore. It’s like a permanent gray cloud. I keep thinking about just…leaving. Not like, packing up and going somewhere else, but just…not. Not waking up. It’s not a plan, not really, but it’s…comforting, I guess? Like the loudest, quietest option. I feel so utterly pointless. Everyone else seems to have it figured out, you know? Their lives are moving forward, and mine is just…stuck. I feel like a broken record, repeating the same sad song over and over. I just want the record to skip.

Just got the call. Mom's in the hospital. Again. Doesn't matter what it is this time, just… everything feels pointless. Like, what’s the point of even getting out of bed? Seriously thinking about just… not.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: I can’t even think straight anymore, just staring at the monitors, the numbers blurring together like everything else, it’s like the darkness just keeps spreading and spreading and i just want to…not wake up, honestly, i just don’t know how to fix this feeling, it’s crushing me, and the sleep…it’s just gone.

okay im not even sure how to say this but im just… i just feel like i cant keep going. little elara has been so sick this week really bad. shes been in the hospital twice now and the doctors just keep saying “viral” and “watchful waiting” but shes just… fading. she looks so small and fragile and shes barely eating anything. my husband is trying to be strong but i can see it in his eyes too. he keeps saying “we’ll get through this” but it feels like a lie. i just keep picturing her getting worse and worse and i dont know what to do. i feel like i’m failing her, like i’m not a good enough mom. i just want her to be okay, i just want her to be happy. i feel so utterly exhausted and hopeless. the nurses are nice, they keep asking if i need anything but i just want to disappear. i keep thinking about how it would be easier if she wasnt here, not like… end it all, just… not have to watch her suffer. i know thats awful i know but it's just... it’s just so hard. i just want to sleep and not wake up. is this what it feels like to just not want to exist anymore? i don’t want to burden anyone with this. i’m sorry. i just… i need to just breathe but i can’t even breathe right.

Ugh, just staring at my bank account again. It’s like a black hole, sucking everything in. I thought I was doing okay, you know? Like, a decent job, rent paid... but then the car needed fixing and then the fridge died and now I’m eating ramen for the third day in a row. Everyone else seems to be fine, going out, buying stuff. It’s just… crushing. I keep telling myself it's temporary, that things will turn around, but honestly? It feels like I'm just sinking deeper and deeper. I don’t even want to bother getting out of bed some days. Like, what’s the point? Just… numb.

just staring at the flickering monitors again. everyone's asleep, all the customers gone. feels like the whole world is sleeping and i'm just stuck here in the dark. wish i could just disappear, honestly. it's too quiet, too empty.

text: Okay, okay, just… everything is just too much, you know? Like, I've been trying to finish this grant proposal for weeks now, it's due tomorrow and it’s just… a mess. It’s supposed to be about supporting elderly patients and their families, and honestly, I feel like I'm failing at everything. My mom’s getting worse, the doctor said she needs more help with her meds and I can’t even keep track of it half the time, and it's just this constant weight, this pressure. I keep staring at the screen and just… everything feels pointless. Like, what’s the point of writing about helping people when I can barely manage my own life? I don’t even want to get out of bed, I just want to lie here and… forget it all. It’s just… so heavy.

Ugh, seriously, just… gone. Like he vanished. It's stupid, right? He didn’t even *say* anything, just stopped answering my texts. I keep replaying everything in my head, trying to figure out what I did wrong, but there *was* nothing. Just… us. And now it’s nothing. Feels like a part of me just died too. Everyone keeps saying it’ll get better, that I’ll move on, but it just feels… heavy. Like I'm carrying this giant rock around. I don’t even want to go to school tomorrow. Everything feels gray and pointless. I just… wish it would all just stop. I don’t know what to do anymore.

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a really bad idea to even type this out, but… I don’t know what else to do. Everything feels… blurry. It’s been, like, a week since Liam left and I just keep replaying everything in my head. It’s stupid, right? It was a long time coming, everyone said it was, but it doesn’t actually *feel* like it was coming. It just… happened. And now I’m just… here.

I keep checking his Insta, even though I know he probably doesn’t even look at it anymore. Just to see if, like, maybe he posted something, anything, that would tell me he’s okay. Or that he’s not regretting it. Which I don’t even blame him for, honestly. I was… a mess. I think we both knew that. I just didn’t want to admit it.

I lost my job too, you know? Two things at once. It’s just… piling up. Like, I’m already feeling so useless and worthless, and now I can’t even afford groceries. I'm pretty sure I’ve eaten ramen for the last five days. I just... I don’t see the point in doing anything anymore. Maybe if I just stopped trying, it would all go away. Maybe it would be easier. I don't know. I’m just… tired. So, so tired. It feels like a weight, a constant, heavy weight on my chest. It's hard to even breathe sometimes. I don't even want to talk to anyone. It feels like I'm just burdening them with my mess.  I just want to disappear, honestly.  Like, just fade away.  I don’t even know why I’m writing this, it's probably just making things worse.  Ugh.

attrs.register: hesitant, lots of qualifiers like "idk" and "maybe"

text:
Okay, so like, this is really hard to even write down, you know? It’s just… everything feels so empty. Liam left, like, six weeks ago and it’s just… been a blur. I keep expecting him to just, like, show up at the door or something, you know? A stupid little surprise. But he didn’t. And he hasn’t called. Not once. I’ve been trying to reach him through mutual friends, but they’re being really, really careful about not saying anything. Which, I get it, I guess. It’s probably for the best. But it just makes me feel even more… invisible.

I'm just sitting here staring at my phone, scrolling through everyone’s lives and they're all doing things, going places, laughing… and I'm just… here. In my apartment. Eating takeout. It smells good, I guess, but it doesn’t really *matter*. I went for a walk yesterday, like, just a really long walk, and I saw a couple holding hands and it just… hit me. Like a punch to the gut. I don't even know why. I just felt this overwhelming wave of sadness.

I don’t even talk to anyone anymore. My sister texts, like, once a week, and it's all about her new puppy. Which is cute, I guess, but it’s not *me*. I feel like I’ve just… faded. I don't know what to do. Maybe I should just… you know. Just stop doing anything. It’d be quiet, right? No more pretending. I don’t know. I just… I don’t know how to make it stop hurting. I think I'm just going to lie down for a bit. Idk.

text: Everyone here just seems…distant. Like I’m invisible. I moved here to find myself, but all I’ve found is a giant, empty room. Just wanna disappear, honestly. It’s easier than trying to connect with anyone.
my grandma’s in the hospital and it’s just… everything feels too heavy. like i can’t even breathe sometimes. i just wish it would all be over. i don’t even know why i’m typing this.

text: Ugh, another night. Seriously, I don’t even know why I keep doing this. Mom’s on my case again, about the bills, about not calling enough, about just generally being a disappointment. She doesn't *get* it, you know? She thinks if she just nags me hard enough, it’ll magically fix everything. Like I’m a broken machine that needs constant fixing. It’s exhausting, honestly. Dad just sits there, silent, like he’s watching a movie. He used to talk to me, you know? Before... before everything went sideways. I just feel so trapped, like I’m drowning in this grayness and there’s no way out. It's just...so much. I wish I could just disappear, honestly. Not like, dramatically, but just...fade away. Like a ghost. I don't want to be a burden anymore.

Okay seriously, I can’t even. Like, I haven’t slept properly in weeks. Just staring at the ceiling, thinking about… nothing really. Or maybe everything. It’s a mess. Work is awful, my apartment is a disaster, and my mom keeps calling asking if I’m eating. She doesn't get it, obviously. She thinks I'm just stressed. But it's more than that. It’s like… a weight. Just this constant, heavy pressure behind my eyes. I keep trying to distract myself, scrolling through feeds, watching stupid videos, but it never works. It just makes me feel worse. Like I’m actively avoiding something. And I don’t even know what that something is. I just want to… disappear, I guess. Not like, dramatically. Just… fade away. Like a bad photograph. No one would notice. And honestly? Maybe that wouldn’t be so bad. I keep thinking about how pointless everything is. Like, what’s the point of getting out of bed tomorrow? Or next week? Or next year? It's just… this endless cycle of disappointment. I keep telling myself it’s temporary, that things will get better, but... they don’t feel like they will. I just feel so numb, you know? Just… empty. And tired. So, so tired. I’m not even sure why I’m typing this. Just… needed to say it out loud, I think. Or at least, type it.

text: okay seriously, i don’t even know where to start, like it just feels… pointless. everything feels pointless now. he just… left. just vanished like he never even existed. we were supposed to be moving in together, you know? like, a real thing, a future, and now it’s just… gone. it’s not even like there was a fight or anything, he just said he needed “space” which is, like, the most infuriatingly vague thing someone can ever say. and i just… i don’t get it. i thought we were good, i really did. i guess i was wrong, and now i feel like every single thing i’ve ever done was wrong too, like i was a terrible partner, a terrible person, and i just want to disappear into the floorboards, you know? it’s just a constant weight, this heavy feeling in my chest that won’t go away, and it’s getting heavier and heavier every second. i keep replaying everything in my head, every conversation, every little thing we did, and it’s just… painful. it’s like my brain is stuck on repeat, and i can’t turn it off. i keep staring at my phone, scrolling through his old posts, just… looking. and then i just feel even worse. i just want to be somewhere else, anywhere else, not here, not with this. it’s like the world keeps spinning and i’m just stuck, frozen, unable to move, and the colors are just… faded. i don't even care about anything anymore, honestly. like, what’s the point of eating, or sleeping, or even breathing? it all feels so exhausting.
they just don’t get it do they? like, everything is always my fault and they yell and yell and i just want to disappear. it’s too much. i don’t know what to do anymore.

text: okay so like… this is probably a really weird thing to say on, like, whatever app this is. but i don’t know… i just feel… really, really heavy. i moved like, two weeks ago. to this tiny apartment in… well, it’s a town i’ve never been to before. supposed to be a fresh start, right? everyone keeps saying that. a blank slate. but it just feels… gray. i picked it because it was cheap, and close to the woods, which, okay, that’s nice, i guess? but it’s just… quiet. too quiet. and i keep expecting someone to call, you know? like, my ex. but he hasn’t. and i’m starting to think… maybe he doesn’t *want* to be contacted. which is… fine. i mean, it’s good, in a way. but then i just sit here, staring at the walls, and it’s like… what’s the point? i tried to unpack everything, but i just… couldn’t. i left a lot of his stuff. just… things. and i keep thinking about how everything is *his* now. everything. and i don’t have anything. i don’t have anywhere. i don’t even have a proper cup. it’s stupid, i know. but sometimes i just… i just want to stop feeling. like, just… fade away. i don’t know if i’m even sad, exactly. just… empty. maybe that’s worse. idk. i just… i’m really tired.

everything feels so heavy like i’m drowning just trying to keep this baby alive honestly i don’t know how much longer i can do this. just want it to stop the screaming the tears the constant exhaustion.  i just… i just don't want to be here anymore.

text:
okay seriously what is happening to me? i just got the results back from the doc and it’s… not good. they found something in my blood work, some weird thing they don’t really know what it is yet. just said it needs further testing and basically told me to “take it easy” like that’s even possible. i’m in this tiny apartment in this city i’ve hated since i moved here and i feel completely alone. it's just… a lot.

i haven't eaten anything decent in days, just grabbing granola bars and trying not to think about it. my phone’s been blowing up with messages from my mom, offering to come get me, which is… nice i guess, but also just makes me feel worse. like i’m a failure. why did i even move here? why did i think i could just uproot my entire life and start over?

everything feels so heavy. the air, my chest, my thoughts. i keep staring at the rain and just… wanting it to wash everything away. i know that’s stupid, dramatic, whatever, but it’s just… the only thing that feels remotely… clear right now. i just want to disappear, you know? not like, vanish completely, just… fade into nothing. it’s getting harder to even get out of bed, let alone deal with anything. i just feel numb most of the time, like i'm watching my life happen to someone else.  this city feels so big and empty and i'm just... lost.  i don’t even know what i’m doing anymore.

text: It's the anniversary today... of everything. I don't even know why I'm still here, honestly. Maybe it's just… easier to not be. I just feel so numb, like a ghost. idk what to do, maybe just sleep forever?

Just... everything feels pointless now. Like, seriously pointless. He said it was 'irreconcilable differences.' That's code for 'I found someone shinier,' right? It's always about them, isn't it? I haven't slept properly in days. Just staring at the ceiling in this awful, flickering fluorescent light. This job is a nightmare anyway, working nights and seeing nothing but grey. It’s like the world just turned off the color. I keep replaying everything we said, every stupid argument, every little thing. It’s stuck in my head, a broken record. I don't even know why I bother getting out of bed. Maybe tomorrow will be different, but honestly? Probably not. Just...tired. So, so tired of everything.

Dad just… ignores everything. It’s like they both think I *choose* to be miserable. Like I enjoy being alone, which is ridiculous. It’s not a choice, okay? It’s just… heavy. Everything feels so heavy. I keep thinking about just… not answering the phone. Not showing up. It’s not that I want anything bad to happen to them, just… less. Less noise. Less disappointment. I don’t even know why I’m typing this out. Just… feeling numb, i guess.

text: Ugh, just… everything feels so heavy today, like I’m carrying a brick in my chest. I don’t even know why. It’s not like anything *happened*, you know? Like, nothing bad. But it’s just… constant. This dull ache, this feeling that nothing really matters. I tried to do my history homework, but I just stared at the page for an hour and then closed the book. I don’t even remember what we were supposed to be doing. I think I just… felt numb. My mom asked me if I was okay, and I just said “fine,” which is, like, the most useless thing ever. She seemed worried, but I don’t want to bother her. It’s probably just me being dramatic. Maybe. I don’t know. I keep thinking about just… not getting out of bed tomorrow. It doesn’t have to be a big thing, you know? Just… staying in bed. All day. Maybe if I just stayed in bed, the feeling would go away. I wish I could just switch it off, like a light. Everything’s just gray lately, really. And loud, but not a loud that you *hear*. More like a loud that you *feel* inside. I feel so lost. Like I'm drifting, and there’s no anchor. I’m just… tired. So unbelievably tired. I just… I don't know what to do.

i’m back at my parents’ house, which is… awful. it’s so boring here. i keep scrolling through his old stuff on my phone, like, pictures we took, stupid little videos he made. it just makes it worse, i think. it reminds me of how happy we *were*, or at least, how i thought we were. maybe i was just deluding myself. idk. i feel so stupid for getting so invested, for thinking it would last. i guess it’s just… a lot to deal with. i’m just… tired. so unbelievably tired. i don't even want to go to bed, honestly. maybe i should just… just stay up all night. it would be easier, maybe. i don't know. i just… i don’t know what to do anymore. i just want it to stop hurting.

text: okay so like, this is probably a really weird thing to post but i just… i don’t even know anymore. my grandpa, you know, he had this scare last week, like a really bad one at the hospital and they were talking about… about a stroke or something. and it just hit me so hard, you know? like, he's eighty-two, he’s lived a really long life, but the thought of him just… not being here, or being like, *different*, it’s just… terrifying. and i keep thinking about how fragile everything is, like one tiny thing could just… snap and then it’s all over. i mean, i’m only seventeen, i should be worrying about college applications and stupid drama with chloe, but it's like, this huge weight just dropped on me and i can't even breathe properly. i keep replaying it in my head, the doctors, the machines, the worried faces... it's just constant. and i hate it. i hate feeling like this, like everything is slipping away, and it's all just random and unpredictable. i just want things to be… stable, i guess? like, permanently. and maybe… maybe it would be easier if i wasn't here to watch it all go wrong. it's not that i *want* anything to happen to him, of course, but the thought of him being gone, or of *me* being gone, or of just… not being able to cope with it all, it’s just… too much. i feel so useless, so heavy, like i’m just dragging everyone down with me. i should probably just go to sleep, but i don’t think i can. it just keeps looping.

Seriously, what's the point anymore? Everything just feels…gray. Like a really, really faded photograph. Lost my job last week, and then she just…left. Said it wasn’t working, that I was “too much.” Too much of what? Just existing? I don’t even know why I bother getting out of bed some days. The silence is the worst, you know? Just this endless, heavy silence. I keep replaying everything, every stupid thing I said, every time I messed up. It’s like a broken record stuck on repeat. Thinking about just…stopping it all. Not like a dramatic thing, just… fading away. Like the TV when you unplug it. No drama, no mess. Just…gone.

text:
Okay, so like, this is really hard to even type. I just… I don’t know what to do anymore. I went to the clinic today, you know, the one I randomly found when I was just wandering around looking for a pharmacy? And they ran some tests, and apparently my blood pressure is, like, *really* high. They said I need to, like, seriously watch what I’m eating and maybe get some exercise, which is… laughable, honestly. I haven’t left my apartment in days. It’s just… everything feels pointless, you know? I moved here six months ago, to start fresh, to get away from everything, and it’s just made things worse. I thought this would be it, this would be *better*.

I miss my mom, actually. A lot. And my dog, Buster. He probably hates me being here all alone. I keep replaying everything in my head, all the bad things, all the stupid mistakes. Maybe it’s the blood pressure, maybe it’s just… me. I just feel so heavy, like I’m carrying the weight of the world on my shoulders. I tried calling Liam, but I just couldn’t. I don’t want to bother him with this. He's doing okay, I think, but I feel like a burden. I just… maybe if I wasn’t here, things wouldn’t be so bad. I don't know. It's just… a thought. A really persistent one. I don't want to be a problem. I just... I don't want to feel this way anymore.

Ugh, I can’t even. This history project is just… crushing me. Mr. Henderson keeps saying it needs to be “groundbreaking” and like, seriously detailed. I’m drowning in sources and I haven’t slept properly in days. And then I have to work at the diner on weekends, just to help out with bills. Mom’s stressing about everything and I feel like I’m failing at everything. It’s just… too much. Like, a constant weight. I keep staring at the ceiling and thinking maybe it would be easier if I just didn't have to wake up tomorrow. Not like I want to *do* anything, just… not having to deal with this is the only thing that seems appealing right now.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, another shift done. Honestly, I just… I just don’t get how everyone else can just *do* things, you know? Like, go out, laugh, talk about their days. I try, I really do, but it’s like there’s this wall, this thick fog in my brain and I can’t even manage a simple “good morning.” It’s exhausting, always feeling like I’m watching a movie of someone else’s life and I’m just… a ghost in the corner. Everyone seems to have these easy connections, these little inside jokes and I’m just… blank. I keep thinking about just not going to the grocery store tomorrow, or not answering my phone. It’s stupid, right? But it’s just… easier, I guess. Just easier to disappear into the quiet.  I feel so unbelievably alone, and it’s just... heavy. Like a weight I can't even lift.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: it's just… everything feels so pointless now, you know? like the lights just switched off and i'm stuck in this gray haze, just watching the monitors flicker and the numbers go up and down. she left, just like that, a text message and then nothing, and i keep replaying it in my head, trying to figure out where i went wrong, even though it doesn’t matter, does it? it doesn't matter if i was stupid or if she was just… gone. i keep thinking about just closing my eyes and not opening them again, not having to deal with the silence, or the exhaustion, or the feeling like i'm just a ghost haunting this place. i don't even know what time it is anymore, honestly, i just keep shifting my weight and staring at the same patch of wall. it’s like this endless shift, this endless… nothing.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Ugh, I just... I don't even know why I'm typing this but it feels like I have to say something, even if it's just to the void, you know? Everything just feels so heavy lately, like I’m carrying this giant invisible weight all the time. School is awful, my parents don’t get it at all, they just tell me to “try harder” which is like, the most useless thing anyone could ever say. I scroll through Instagram and everyone seems to be having the best time, laughing and traveling and just… happy. And I’m just here, staring at my phone, feeling like I’m trapped in a really blurry, grey movie. I keep thinking about just… not doing anything anymore, just lying down and letting it all just… fade away. It's not like anything specific happened, it's just… everything. It’s just too much, I guess.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text:
Ugh, family dinner tonight. Seriously, it's always the same. My mom keeps asking about my job search, like it's just gonna magically happen. My brother's bragging about his promotion, like that's something to be proud of. And my dad… he just looks at me like I'm a disappointment. It’s like they don’t even *see* me. I tried to talk about feeling stuck, about how everything feels pointless, but they just nodded and said something about "pulling myself together." Like it's that easy.

I just want to disappear, you know? Not like, dramatically. Just... fade away. I went out with some old college friends last week and it was awful. Everyone’s moving on, getting married, buying houses. They’re talking about their futures and I feel like I’m stuck in quicksand. I barely said two words the whole time. I just stared at my phone, scrolling through endless feeds of people living lives I don't have.

I keep telling myself it’s just temporary, that things will get better. But they don't *feel* like they will. It's this heavy, dull ache all the time. Like a constant pressure. I don’t want to be a burden. I just want the noise to stop. The constant feeling of not belonging, not being good enough. Just… quiet.

attrs.persona: a recent graduate living back home

text: ugh i just… i don’t think i can do this anymore. everyone’s so happy here, moving to this tiny town feels like i’m drowning. my parents are thrilled, they keep saying it’s a fresh start and that i’ll love it but it just feels wrong. like i’m supposed to be excited about unpacking boxes and figuring out the bus schedule when honestly i just want to disappear. i keep thinking about just… not showing up to the apartment tomorrow. it’s stupid, i know, but it’s a constant little voice in my head that just won’t shut up. i feel so alone and like i'm failing at everything. i just wish i could go back to how things were, even if they weren’t great. i’m so tired.

it’s just… too much. like, i can’t even think straight. mom’s been yelling about the bills again, and i haven’t even told her i’m failing algebra. i just… i don’t know how much more of this i can take. everyone else seems to have it together, going to parties, having friends, worrying about prom stuff. i’m just stuck here, working at the diner all day and barely eating. i keep thinking about just… not showing up tomorrow. it wouldn’t be my fault, would it? like, the school wouldn’t even care. it’s just easier, you know? i just want the noise to stop. the yelling, the worrying, the feeling of being completely useless. i just wish i could disappear.

just...everything is too much. the job is a mess, the kids are screaming, and i don't even know why i'm trying anymore. it would be so easy to just… not wake up. honestly i feel like i just can’t keep going.

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: okay seriously like, what’s the point anymore? i just… i don’t even know. i keep replaying everything in my head, you know? all the stupid things i said, all the times i messed up, all the chances i didn't take. and it’s just… it’s relentless. this job, or what *was* this job, it was just… crushing. like, they kept piling on the work, deadlines were impossible, and Mr. Henderson just kept breathing down my neck about every little detail. he didn't even *try* to understand, just yelled and demanded perfection. i was working myself to the bone, sleeping maybe four hours a night, fueled by coffee and anxiety. and then they let me go. just like that. “restructuring,” they called it. restructuring my entire life, apparently. and now i'm here, staring at the ceiling, feeling completely useless. i keep thinking about all the expectations, all the pressure i put on myself, and it's just so overwhelming. i just wanted to do well, to prove myself, but it never felt like enough. everyone else seemed to be moving forward, climbing the ladder, and i was just… stuck. a broken cog. i just wish i could just disappear, honestly. not like, dramatically, but just… quietly. fade away. it’s not that i have a plan or anything, it’s just… this constant ache, this heavy feeling, it’s just too much to carry. i just want it to stop. i just want to not feel this. i don't even know why i'm typing this out, i guess i just needed to say it, even if no one understands.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: It’s the anniversary. Feels like yesterday, honestly. Like a bad dream I can’t wake up from. Everything just… hurts. Like a constant, dull ache behind my eyes. I keep staring at the monitors, all those blinking lights, and it’s just… overwhelming. They don’t even notice I’m staring, they’re too busy with their own shifts. I don't even care anymore, I just want it to stop. Just… nothing. I keep thinking about how easy it would be to just… not wake up tomorrow. Not have to deal with any of this. Just… gone. Doesn’t even feel sad, just… quiet. A really, really quiet, empty quiet.

text: It’s… it’s the anniversary today. Of everything. Of him leaving, of us arguing that night, of the way the rain was just *pouring* down and felt like it was mirroring how I felt inside. Honestly, I don't even know why I’m typing this, it just… keeps coming out. It’s like a pressure in my chest, this constant dull ache that just won’t go away. Everything reminds me of him, you know? The coffee shop we used to go to, the stupid little keychain he gave me, even just the smell of rain. I keep replaying it all in my head, you know? Trying to figure out where we went wrong, but it’s useless, completely pointless. I just… I wish it hadn't happened. I just want it to stop feeling like this weight.

text: Just… everything feels so heavy. Like I’m wading through mud and it’s getting deeper every second. Exams are a disaster, my roommates are annoying, my bank account is screaming, and honestly? It doesn’t even matter. It just doesn't. I keep staring at my bed and thinking about just… not getting up. Like, not even for breakfast. Just… staying there. It’s not like I have a reason, or anything. It's just... a constant, dull ache that’s building into something else. I don't want to talk to anyone, I don't want to do anything. Just… quiet. Please just leave me alone.

okay seriously i don’t know how anyone does this. like everyone else is just…fine. they’re laughing and chatting and holding hands and i’m just standing here holding leo and feeling like a complete failure. it’s always like this isn’t it? the playgroup the grocery store even just walking down the street. everyone seems to know each other and have these easy little connections and i feel like i’m watching a movie i wasn’t invited to. i tried to say hi to sarah yesterday and she just kinda glanced at leo and then looked back at her phone. it wasn’t mean i guess but it just…confirmed everything. like i’m not part of it.

i keep thinking about how much easier it would be if i didn’t have him. i don’t want to be a bad parent obviously i love leo so much it’s insane but the thought of just not having this constant pressure this weight on my shoulders…it’s tempting. i feel like i’m drowning and everyone else is swimming effortlessly. i feel so utterly alone even when i’m surrounded by people. i just want to disappear sometimes you know? just fade away and not have to face this. it’s exhausting being this tired and this sad and this…invisible. i just wish i could just be normal. is that too much to ask? i feel like i’m failing at everything. maybe i am just a terrible person.

text: I don’t even know what to do anymore. Little Leo had a fever spike last night, and the doctor said he might need tests. It’s just… so much. I feel like I'm failing him, failing everyone. Maybe it would just be easier if… idk. I just… I’m so tired.

The walls, the furniture, even the light. Like I’m just fading into the background. I keep staring out the window at the streetlights and just... wishing they’d go out. It’s so quiet here, except for the hum of the fridge. I don’t even know if anyone will come visit. Just me and this gray box. I just… don't want to keep doing this. It’s just… exhausting. attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it’s the anniversary today. the one where it all… happened. feels like yesterday and a lifetime ago at the same time. everyone keeps saying it’ll get easier, that time heals all wounds, but it doesn’t. it just… shifts. like the pain moves somewhere else, or gets quieter, but it’s *still there*. mostly just a dull throb under everything. i keep expecting to wake up and it’s all a horrible dream, but it’s not. she’s here, little Leo, and he’s… perfect. ridiculously, impossibly perfect. and that’s the worst part, maybe. i look at him and i just… feel this wave of grief, this overwhelming sadness that isn’t even *for* her anymore, it’s just… everything. like i’m supposed to be happy, and i’m trying, i really am, but it’s like there’s this weight on my chest, this constant ache. i don't know how to be a good dad, i don't know how to be a good husband, i just… exist. and it's suffocating. i just want it to stop. i just want to not feel this.  i don’t even know why i’m typing this, just needed to say it out loud, i guess. it doesn't help, does it?

attrs.register: long and rambling with run-on sentences

text: Okay, so I just… I can’t sleep. Seriously, I haven’t been able to sleep properly in weeks, maybe months, honestly it feels like forever. It started after… well, after Harold. It’s just… the silence, you know? He used to hum, always humming some old tune, even when he was just sitting there reading. Now it's just…empty. And the nights are the worst. I keep thinking about him, about how much he needed me, and I feel like I failed him somehow. It’s not like it was a dramatic thing, not really. Just… gradual. More confused, more tired, more needing help with everything. And I was there, I was always there. But maybe it wasn’t enough. Maybe I should have gotten him more help, maybe I should have… I don’t know. It’s just a constant, dull ache, this feeling that I should have done something differently. And then the sleep comes, and it’s just a jumble of everything – his last words, the way his hand felt in mine, the smell of his pipe tobacco… it’s overwhelming. I toss and turn, and I just want to disappear, honestly. Like if I could just fade away, just stop feeling this weight, this constant, heavy sadness. I just wish… I just wish it would all be over. I keep telling myself tomorrow will be better, but it never is, does it? Just more of the same. More silence. More sleepless nights. More feeling like I'm drowning.

text:
Okay, so like, this is probably a really bad time to be posting, honestly. I just… I don’t even know what to say. It’s just… everything feels so heavy, you know? I’m working the night shift again, which is already awful, but this deadline at work is just… crushing me. It's for this huge project, and if I don’t finish it by Friday, I’m screwed. My boss is already breathing down my neck, and I’m pretty sure he’s judging me, even though he doesn’t actually *say* anything. It’s just this…look.

And then there’s the exam next week. A huge one. I studied, I really did, I think. But I keep replaying everything in my head, and I just feel like I’m going to fail. Like, spectacularly. It’s probably stupid to worry this much, I guess? It’s just… everything feels pointless. Like I’m just pushing myself to do these things, to meet these deadlines, and it’s never enough. It never *feels* enough.

I just… I keep thinking about just not showing up. Just… not. Like, maybe if I just didn’t go to work, maybe if I just didn’t go to the exam, it would all just… stop. It sounds so dramatic, I know. But it’s not a dramatic thought, it’s just… a constant, dull ache. I don’t want to be a burden. I don’t want to disappoint anyone. But honestly, I just feel so tired. So overwhelmingly tired. Maybe I should just call in sick. Or maybe... maybe it wouldn't even matter. I don’t know. I really don’t know. I just need to sleep, I think. Or maybe just… not.

attrs.persona: a university student in exam season

text:
Okay seriously, I can’t even. This exam is going to ruin me. Like, completely. It’s for Professor Thorne’s Ancient Civilizations class and it’s worth, like, 50% of my grade. And I’ve just… I haven’t even started properly. I’ve been skimming the textbook, mostly just staring at the pages, you know? Trying to look busy. My roommate keeps asking if I’m okay, but what does she even *know*? She’s doing fine with her graphic design project, which is, like, actually fun.

I keep thinking about just not showing up. Just skipping it. Like, walking out and not looking back. It would be so easy. Everyone would just assume I failed, and honestly, at this point, it wouldn’t even matter. It’s all just… pointless. All this studying, all this pressure, it’s just building up and building up until I feel like I’m going to explode. I keep replaying everything I’ve done wrong, every stupid mistake I’ve made. It’s like the exam is the only thing that matters, and I’m failing at everything else.

I just feel… empty. Just a huge, heavy emptiness. I keep wanting to just lie down and not move. Like, just shut down completely. It's not even about the grade, it’s about… everything. This feeling, this constant weight, it's just too much. I don’t know how much longer I can keep going. I just wish it would all just… stop.

text: okay so like, this is probably a really weird thing to post but i just… i don't even know why i'm typing this, honestly. it's just… everything feels so heavy, you know? i lost my job last week, just like that, and it’s not even like a *bad* job, it was just… a job. and now it’s just… gone. and it’s not even that i hated it, or that I was miserable, it’s just… it was *something*. it was a routine, a purpose, even if it wasn't a *good* one. and now there's nothing. nothing to get up for, nothing to do, nothing to *be*. my apartment just feels… enormous, like a giant empty box filled with all my things and my sadness. i keep staring at the walls and they just… stare back. my phone keeps buzzing with notifications from people i haven’t talked to in ages, and it’s just… it's a reminder that i'm still here, still existing, and it's awful. it's like i'm watching my own life from the outside, and it's just a blurry, grey mess. i don't even want to eat, i don't want to shower, i don't want to talk to anyone. i just want to… disappear, i guess. not like, dramatically, you know? just… fade away. like a smudge on a photograph. i don’t want to hurt anyone, i just... i just feel so incredibly, hopelessly tired. and like maybe it would be easier if i wasn’t here anymore. i don’t even know what i’m saying, i just needed to type *something* out.

attrs.trigger: an anniversary of a hard event

text: it's… it’s the anniversary. three years. three years since everything just… shattered. i keep expecting to see him, you know? just a flicker of movement in the hallway, a car pulling up. but it's just me. always just me. and the silence. god the silence is deafening. i tried to make dinner, a stupid little pasta thing, like we used to. it tasted like ash. everything tastes like ash now. i deleted all the photos. every single one. even the stupid ones from our trip to the beach. i don't want to remember the good times, not really. they just make it worse. they remind me of what i’ve lost. of what *we* lost. i keep thinking about how easy it would be to just… not wake up. like, a really deep, peaceful sleep. not like this, not with the constant ache. i don’t even know why i’m typing this. i just needed to say it out loud, i guess. to have someone – even if it's just the void – know that i’m here. drowning. just… drowning. it’s getting harder to breathe, honestly. harder and harder. i just wish it would end. seriously. just… end.

text: Ugh, this exam season is just… so much. Mom keeps yelling about my grades, like I’m failing everything. Dad just ignores me. I don't even know what to do anymore, idk. Maybe it would just be easier if i didn’t exist, honestly. It’s probably stupid, but…

I just...can't sleep. Like, ever. My brain is just buzzing with static and everything feels so heavy. I toss and turn for hours and then wake up exhausted, and it just starts all over again. It's like there's this constant, dull ache behind my eyes, and it’s getting worse. I keep thinking about just closing my eyes and not waking up. Not having to deal with anything.
